Genesis1:1 In the beginning, God made the heavens and the earth." 
Genesis1:2 The earth was without shape and it was empty. Deep water covered the earth and everywhere was dark. The Spirit of God moved above the water. 
Genesis1:3 God said, ‘There will be light!’ And there was light." 
Genesis1:4 God saw that the light was good. He caused the light and the dark to be separate. 
Genesis1:5 God called the light ‘day’. He called the dark ‘night’. Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the first day. 
Genesis1:6 Then God said, ‘A wide space will appear between the waters. So the waters will be in two separate places.’" 
Genesis1:7 So God made this wide space. He made the water under the space separate from the water that was above it. What God said happened. 
Genesis1:8 God called the wide space ‘sky’. Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the second day. 
Genesis1:9 God said, ‘The water that is under the sky will come together in one place. Then dry ground will appear.’ And what God said happened." 
Genesis1:10 God called the dry ground ‘land’. He called the water that had come together ‘sea’. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good. 
Genesis1:11 Then God said, ‘The land will cause plants to grow. There will be plants with their seeds and trees with their fruits. Each kind of plant and tree will have its own seeds and fruits.’ And what God said happened." 
Genesis1:12 All kinds of plants and trees began to grow in the ground. The plants made seeds. The trees made fruits with seeds in them. Each plant made its own kind of seeds. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good. 
Genesis1:13 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the third day. 
Genesis1:14 God said, ‘There will be lights all across the sky. They will make the day different from the night. They will show the seasons, days and years." 
Genesis1:15 The lights in the sky will give light to the earth.’ And what God said happened. 
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights. The brighter light ruled over the day. The less bright light ruled over the night. God also made the stars. 
Genesis1:17 God put all these lights in the sky to shine their light on the earth. 
Genesis1:18 They were to rule over the day and night. They were to make the time of light separate from the time of dark. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good. 
Genesis1:19 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the fourth day. 
Genesis1:20 God said, ‘The waters will become full with many living things. Birds will appear and fly above the earth, all across the sky.’" 
Genesis1:21 God made big animals to live in the sea. He made every different kind of living thing that filled the sea. He made every different kind of bird to fly in the sky. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good. 
Genesis1:22 God blessed the animals and the birds. He said to them, ‘Give birth to many young ones, so that you grow in number. Fill all the water in the seas. The birds also should become very many, all across the earth.’" 
Genesis1:23 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the fifth day. 
Genesis1:24 God said, ‘Different kinds of animals will now appear on the land. There will be farm animals and wild animals. There will also be other small animals that move along the ground. There will be many kinds of animals and each kind will be different.’ And what God said happened." 
Genesis1:25 God made the different kinds of wild animals and the different kinds of farm animals. He made all the different kinds of animals that move along the ground. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good. 
Genesis1:26 Then God said, ‘We will make humans so that they are very much like us. They will rule over the fish in the sea and over the birds in the sky. They will rule over the farm animals. They will rule over the whole earth and all the animals that move along the ground.’" 
Genesis1:27 God made humans. He made them to be like himself. He made them to be male and female. 
Genesis1:28 God blessed them. He said to them, ‘Give birth to children. Grow in number. Fill the earth and rule over it. Rule over the fish in the sea. Rule over the birds in the sky. Rule over every different kind of living animal that moves along the ground.’" 
Genesis1:29 Then God said to them, ‘Listen! I now give to you every plant on the earth as your food. I give you every plant that has seeds in it. I give you every tree that has fruit with a seed in it. I give them to you for your food." 
Genesis1:30 I give to the animals green plants for their food. It will be food for all the animals on the earth, all the birds in the sky and all the animals that move along the ground. Everything that has life may eat every kind of green plant.’ And what God said happened." 
Genesis1:31 God looked at everything that he had made. He saw that it was very good. Evening passed. And then it was morning. That was the sixth day. 
Genesis2:1 In that way God finished making the heavens and the earth, and everything that was in them." 
Genesis2:2 The seventh day came. Then God stopped the work that he had been doing. He had finished his work and he rested on the seventh day. 
Genesis2:3 God blessed the seventh day. He made it a special day, because he had finished making everything. He did not work on that day." 
Genesis2:4 This is what happened when God made the heavens and the earth. When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth," 
Genesis2:5 there were no plants or grasses growing on the earth. That was because the Lord God had not sent any rain on the earth. Also, there was nobody to dig the soil so that plants would grow." 
Genesis2:6 But streams of water were coming up from the earth. The water made all the top of the ground wet. 
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God took some soil from the ground and he made a man. He breathed air into the nose of the man to give him life. So the man became alive. 
Genesis2:8 The Lord God planted a garden. It was in the east, in a place called Eden. God took the man that he had made and he put him in the garden." 
Genesis2:9 The Lord God made all kinds of trees grow from the ground there. The trees were beautiful to look at. The fruit from the trees was good for people to eat. In the middle of the garden were two special trees. One was the tree that gives life. The other was the tree that gives knowledge of what is good and what is evil. 
Genesis2:10 A river began in Eden. It went through the garden and it gave water for the ground. After that, it became four separate rivers." 
Genesis2:11 The name of the first river is the Pishon. It goes through the whole land of Havilah. There is gold in that land. 
Genesis2:12 The gold there is very good. Bdellium and onyx stone are also in Havilah. 
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is the Gihon. It goes through the whole land of Cush. 
Genesis2:14 The third river is called the Tigris. It goes along the east side of Assyria. The fourth river is called the Euphrates. 
Genesis2:15 The Lord God took the man and he put him in the Garden of Eden. God wanted the man to work in the garden and to take care of it. 
Genesis2:16 Then the Lord God told the man, ‘You may eat the fruit from any tree in the garden, as much as you like." 
Genesis2:17 But you must not eat any fruit from the tree that gives knowledge of good and evil. If you do eat fruit from that tree, that day you will certainly die.’" 
Genesis2:18 Then the Lord God said, ‘It is not good for the man to live alone. I will make a helper who is right for him.’" 
Genesis2:19 The Lord God took soil from the ground and he made all the animals and birds. He brought them to the man. God wanted to know what names the man would call them. Whatever the man called each living thing, that became its name." 
Genesis2:20 So the man gave names to the farm animals, to the birds in the sky and to all the other animals. But none of these animals was the right helper for the man." 
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused the man to sleep. While the man was sleeping, God took a rib from the man. Then he closed up the place in his body and he covered it again." 
Genesis2:22 The Lord God used the man's rib to make a woman. He brought the woman to the man. 
Genesis2:23 Then the man said, ‘Finally, this is someone who is right for me. She has bones that are taken from my bones. Her body comes from my body. I will call her “woman” because God used me, a man, to make her.’" 
Genesis2:24 Because of this, when a man marries, he leaves his father and his mother. Instead, God joins the man and his wife together. The two people become as one body." 
Genesis2:25 The man and his wife were not wearing any clothes. But they did not feel ashamed. 
Genesis3:1 The Lord God made many wild animals. But the snake was the most clever of them all. The snake asked the woman, ‘Did God say, “You must not eat the fruit from any tree in the garden”? Is that really true?’" 
Genesis3:2 The woman replied, ‘We may eat fruit from the trees in the garden." 
Genesis3:3 But God said, “You must not eat fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden. You must not even touch it. If you do that, you will die.” ’" 
Genesis3:4 Then the snake said to the woman, ‘No, you will not die." 
Genesis3:5 God knows that when you eat the fruit from this tree, you will understand things. You will become like God himself. You will know about good things and evil things.’" 
Genesis3:6 The woman looked at the fruit on the tree. She saw that it would be good to eat, and it was beautiful to look at. She wanted to eat it because it would make her become wise. So she took some fruit and she ate it. Then she gave some of the fruit to her husband who was with her. He also ate it." 
Genesis3:7 Then they understood what they had done. They realized that they were not wearing any clothes. So they took some leaves from fig trees. They tied them together to cover their bodies. 
Genesis3:8 In the evening there was a nice cool wind. The Lord God walked in the garden. The man and the woman heard the sound of the Lord God. They hid themselves behind some trees, so that God would not see them." 
Genesis3:9 The Lord God called out to the man. He said to him, ‘Where are you?’" 
Genesis3:10 The man replied, ‘I heard you in the garden. I was afraid because I had no clothes. So I hid myself from you.’" 
Genesis3:11 The Lord God said, ‘Who told you that you had no clothes? Have you eaten fruit from the tree that I said you must not eat?’" 
Genesis3:12 The man said, ‘It was the woman that you put here with me. She gave me some fruit from the tree. So I ate it.’" 
Genesis3:13 Then the Lord God said to the woman, ‘Why have you done a thing like that?’ The woman replied, ‘It was the snake. The snake deceived me with a lie. So I ate the fruit.’" 
Genesis3:14 The Lord God said to the snake, ‘Because you have done this, I will curse you. Among all the farm animals and wild animals, you are the one that I will curse. From now on, you will move across the ground on your stomach. You will eat dust from the ground for your whole life." 
Genesis3:15 I will cause you and the woman to become enemies. Your descendants and her descendants will always be enemies. One of her descendants will attack your head. You will attack his heel.’ 
Genesis3:16 God said to the woman, ‘I will cause you to have great pain when you give birth to children. You will want to please your husband. But he will rule over you as your master.’" 
Genesis3:17 Then God said to Adam, ‘You listened to your wife and you did what she said. You ate fruit from the tree after I told you, “You must not eat fruit from this tree.” Because you did that, I will curse the ground. You will have to work very hard to make plants grow in it for your food. It will be like this for your whole life." 
Genesis3:18 Thorn bushes and thistles will grow in the ground. But you will eat plants that grow in the fields. 
Genesis3:19 You will have to work hard for a long time before you have any food to eat. You will do this for your whole life until you die. Then you will return into the ground. That is where you came from. I made you from the soil of the ground, and you will become soil again.’" 
Genesis3:20 Adam gave his wife a name. He called her Eve. This was because she would become the mother of all people. 
Genesis3:21 The Lord God made clothes for Adam and Eve to wear. He used the skins from animals to make them. 
Genesis3:22 The Lord God said, ‘The man has now become like one of us because he understands good and evil. So we must not let him take fruit from the tree that gives life. If he eats that fruit, he will live for ever.’" 
Genesis3:23 So the Lord God sent Adam out of the Garden of Eden. To get his food, Adam had to dig the ground that God had used to make him." 
Genesis3:24 Then God put cherubs to be guards for the garden. God put them on the east side of the garden. There was also a sword of fire that moved quickly from side to side. As a result, nobody could go near to the tree that gives life." 
Genesis4:1 Adam had sex with his wife, Eve. She became pregnant. She gave birth to Cain. She said, ‘With the Lord 's help, I have given birth to a son.’" 
Genesis4:2 Some time later, Eve gave birth to Cain's brother. She called him Abel. When they had grown to become men, Abel took care of sheep. Cain worked on the land as a farmer." 
Genesis4:3 One day, it was time for the harvest. Cain brought to the Lord some of his crops as an offering." 
Genesis4:4 But Abel brought to God some of the first lambs that his sheep had given birth to. He brought pieces of fat from the best of his lambs to offer them to God. Abel and his gift pleased the Lord. 
Genesis4:5 But God was not pleased with Cain and his gift. So Cain became very angry. His face looked sad. 
Genesis4:6 Then the Lord said to Cain, ‘Why are you angry? Why do you look sad?" 
Genesis4:7 If you do what is right, I will accept you. But if you do not do the right thing, sin will be very near to you. It will be like a wild animal at your door that is ready to eat you. But you must become its master.’" 
Genesis4:8 One day Cain said to his brother Abel, ‘We should go out to the fields.’ While they were in the fields, Cain attacked his brother Abel. He killed Abel." 
Genesis4:9 Then the Lord said to Cain, ‘Where is Abel, your brother?’ Cain replied, ‘I do not know where he is. Must I always keep my brother safe?’" 
Genesis4:10 Then the Lord God said, ‘Why have you done this bad thing? Now your brother's blood is lying there on the ground. That is like his voice which is shouting to me for help." 
Genesis4:11 The ground received your brother's blood when you killed him. As a result, the ground no longer accepts you. It has brought a curse on you." 
Genesis4:12 When you work hard to dig the ground, no plants will grow in it for your food. You will travel from place to place. You will not have your own home.’" 
Genesis4:13 Cain said to the Lord, ‘That punishment is too much! It will give me too much pain." 
Genesis4:14 Now you are making me leave my home here on the land. I will be far away from where you are. I will be a traveller without a home. If someone sees me, they will kill me.’" 
Genesis4:15 But the Lord said to Cain, ‘No! If anyone kills Cain, I will punish that person seven times more than I have punished you.’ Then the Lord put a special mark on Cain. This was to tell other people that they must not kill Cain." 
Genesis4:16 Cain went away from the Lord. He went to live in the land called Nod, which is east of Eden." 
Genesis4:17 Cain had sex with his wife. She became pregnant and she gave birth to Enoch. Cain started to build a city. He called the city Enoch because that was his son's name. 
Genesis4:18 Enoch became the father of Irad. Irad became the father of Mehujael. Mehujael became the father of Methushael. Methushael became the father of Lamech. 
Genesis4:19 Lamech married two women. One was called Adah. The other was called Zillah. 
Genesis4:20 Adah gave birth to Jabal. Jabal became the father of people who live in tents and they take care of animals. 
Genesis4:21 Jabal had a brother called Jubal. Jubal became the father of people who make music with harps and flutes. 
Genesis4:22 Zillah gave birth to a son. His name was Tubal-Cain. He used bronze and iron to make tools. Tubal-Cain had a sister. Her name was Naamah. 
Genesis4:23 Lamech said to his two wives, ‘Adah and Zillah, listen to me! My wives, hear my words! A man attacked me, so I killed him. Yes, I killed a young man because he hurt me." 
Genesis4:24 If someone kills Cain, God will punish that person seven times more. But if someone attacks me, I will punish that person 77 times more!’" 
Genesis4:25 Adam again had sex with his wife, Eve. She gave birth to another son. She called him Seth. Eve said, ‘God has given me another child to take the place of Abel, because Cain killed Abel.’" 
Genesis4:26 Later Seth had a son and he called him Enosh. At that time, people began to use the name of the Lord to worship him." 
Genesis5:1 This is the report about Adam and his family. When God made men and women, he made them like himself." 
Genesis5:2 God made male and female people and he called them ‘human’. He blessed them. 
Genesis5:3 When Adam was 130 years old, he had a son who was like him. Adam called him Seth." 
Genesis5:4 After Seth was born, Adam lived for 800 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:5 Adam was 930 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:6 When Seth was 105 years old, he had a son. He called him Enosh." 
Genesis5:7 After Enosh was born, Seth lived for 807 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:8 Seth was 912 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:9 When Enosh was 90 years old, he had a son. He called him Kenan." 
Genesis5:10 After Kenan was born, Enosh lived for 815 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:11 Enosh was 905 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:12 When Kenan was 70 years old, he had a son. He called him Mahalalel." 
Genesis5:13 After Mahalalel was born, Kenan lived for 840 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:14 Kenan was 910 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel was 65 years old, he had a son. He called him Jared." 
Genesis5:16 After Jared was born, Mahalalel lived for 830 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:17 Mahalalel was 895 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:18 When Jared was 162 years old, he had a son. He called him Enoch." 
Genesis5:19 After Enoch was born, Jared lived for 800 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:20 Jared was 962 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:21 When Enoch was 65 years old, he had a son. He called him Methuselah." 
Genesis5:22 After Methuselah was born, Enoch lived as God wanted for 300 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:23 Enoch lived on earth for 365 years. 
Genesis5:24 Enoch lived to please God all this time, then Enoch was not there any more. God took Enoch to be with him." 
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah was 187 years old, he had a son. He called him Lamech." 
Genesis5:26 After Lamech was born, Methuselah lived for 782 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:27 Methuselah was 969 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:28 When Lamech was 182 years old, he had a son." 
Genesis5:29 He called his son Noah. Lamech said, ‘He will help us in our difficult work on the land. We have pain when we work on the land to get food. That is because the Lord God cursed the ground.’" 
Genesis5:30 After Noah was born, Lamech lived for 595 years. During this time, he had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis5:31 Lamech was 777 years old when he died. 
Genesis5:32 After Noah was 500 years old, he became the father of Shem, Ham and Japheth." 
Genesis6:1 People began to grow in number all over the earth. They gave birth to daughters. 
Genesis6:2 The sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful. They chose for themselves any of them that they wanted to become their wives. 
Genesis6:3 The Lord said, ‘My Spirit will not give life to humans for ever. One day they will die. I will not let them live for more than 120 years. Then they will die.’" 
Genesis6:4 The Nephilites lived on the earth at that time, and for some time after that. During that time, the sons of God were having sex with the daughters of men, who gave birth to their children. The Nephilites were famous great men and fighters." 
Genesis6:5 The Lord saw that men and women on the earth had become very bad. They thought only of evil things all the time. 
Genesis6:6 The Lord was sorry that he had made humans and put them on the earth. He became very upset. 
Genesis6:7 So the Lord said, ‘I made humans, but now I will destroy them. I will completely take them off the earth. As well as human people, I will also destroy the animals, the living things that move on the ground, and the birds. I am sorry that I made them.’" 
Genesis6:8 But as for Noah, the Lord was pleased with him." 
Genesis6:9 This is the report about Noah and his family. Noah was a good man. He was the only person at that time who lived in a completely right way. He stayed near to God in his life. 
Genesis6:10 Noah became the father of Shem, Ham and Japheth." 
Genesis6:11 God saw that everything on the earth had become spoiled. Everywhere, people were attacking each other." 
Genesis6:12 God saw how much the earth had become spoiled. Everyone was doing bad things. 
Genesis6:13 God said to Noah, ‘I will destroy everyone. That is what I have decided to do. Everywhere on the earth, people are attacking each other. Now I will destroy the people and also the earth." 
Genesis6:14 So make a ship for yourself. Use wood from cypress trees to make it. The ship must have rooms in it. Cover the inside and the outside of the ship with tar. 
Genesis6:15 This is how you will build the ship: It must be 150 metres long, 25 metres wide and 15 metres high." 
Genesis6:16 Make a roof for the ship. Leave a space 45 centimetres high all around under the roof. Put a door in the side of the ship. Inside the ship, make three floors above each other." 
Genesis6:17 I will cover the earth with deep water. I will destroy all living things, wherever they live. Everything that breathes will die." 
Genesis6:18 But I will make a covenant with you, Noah. You will go into the ship. You will take your wife with you, and also your three sons and their wives." 
Genesis6:19 You must also take two of every different kind of animal into the ship. One will be male and the other will be female. Then they will stay alive with you. 
Genesis6:20 Bring two of every different kind of bird, two of every different animal and two of every different living thing that moves along the ground. All these will come to you. You will take care of them so that they will stay alive." 
Genesis6:21 You must also take every different kind of food. That will be food for you and food for the animals.’ 
Genesis6:22 Noah obeyed God. He did everything that God had told him to do. 
Genesis7:1 Then the Lord said to Noah, ‘Go into the ship. Take your wife and family with you. I can see that you alone do what is right, among all the people on the earth." 
Genesis7:2 Take seven pairs of every different kind of clean animal. Each pair will be a male and a female. Take two of every different kind of unclean animal. Take a male and a female together. 
Genesis7:3 Take seven pairs of every kind of bird, male and female together. Do this so that they will give birth to young ones. Then each different kind of animal and bird will continue to live on the earth." 
Genesis7:4 In seven days, I will cause rain to fall on the earth. It will rain for 40 days and 40 nights. In that way, I will destroy every living thing that I have made on the earth.’" 
Genesis7:5 Noah did everything that God told him to do. 
Genesis7:6 Noah was 600 years old when the deep water covered the earth. 
Genesis7:7 Noah and his wife went into the ship. His sons and their wives also went in. They all went into the ship to be safe from the deep water. 
Genesis7:8 Pairs of clean animals and unclean animals came to Noah in the ship. Also, pairs of birds and pairs of all the living things that move across the ground came to him." 
Genesis7:9 Each pair was one male and one female. They all came to Noah in the ship. Everything happened as God had said to Noah. 
Genesis7:10 Seven days later, God sent the deep water to cover the earth." 
Genesis7:11 When Noah was 600 years old, on the 17th day of the second month, streams of water came up everywhere from below the earth. Also, the water in the heavens poured down on the earth." 
Genesis7:12 Rain fell on the earth for 40 days and 40 nights. 
Genesis7:13 On the day that the rain began to fall, Noah and his wife went into the ship. Noah's three sons, Shem, Ham and Japheth, and their three wives also went in." 
Genesis7:14 With them was every different kind of wild animal and every different kind of farm animal. They also had with them every kind of living thing that moves along the ground. Every different kind of bird and other flying things were there too. 
Genesis7:15 Pairs of every kind of animal that lived came to Noah and went into the ship. 
Genesis7:16 Each pair was one male and one female. This happened in the way that God had told Noah. When they were all in the ship, the Lord shut the door." 
Genesis7:17 For 40 days, the water on the earth became deeper. The water lifted the ship high above the ground." 
Genesis7:18 The water became even deeper and completely covered the earth. The ship went safely on top of the water. 
Genesis7:19 More water came until it covered all the high mountains on the earth. 
Genesis7:20 The water still became deeper so that it was more than 6 metres higher than the tops of the mountains. 
Genesis7:21 As a result, everything that had lived on the earth now died. The birds, the farm animals and the wild animals died. Every living thing that moved across the ground died, and so did all the people." 
Genesis7:22 Everything that lived on the dry land died. Nothing could breathe any more. 
Genesis7:23 God destroyed everything that lived on the earth. He destroyed people, animals, living things that move across the ground, and birds. God removed them from the earth. Only Noah and his family, together with the animals that were in the ship, stayed alive." 
Genesis7:24 The deep water covered the earth for 150 days. 
Genesis8:1 Then God thought of Noah and all the animals that were with him in the ship. He caused a wind to blow over the earth. As a result, the water began to go down." 
Genesis8:2 Streams of water stopped coming up from below the earth. Rain stopped coming down from the sky. 
Genesis8:3 The deep water continued to go down for 150 days. 
Genesis8:4 On the 17th day of the seventh month, the ship stopped on the ground. It was sitting on the top of Ararat mountains." 
Genesis8:5 The water continued to go down until the tenth month. On the first day of the tenth month, the tops of the mountains appeared above the water." 
Genesis8:6 After 40 more days, Noah opened a window in the ship." 
Genesis8:7 He sent a raven to fly away. The raven flew away from the ship and then it returned. It continued to do this until the earth was dry. 
Genesis8:8 Then Noah sent out a dove. He wanted to see if the water had gone away from the top of the ground. 
Genesis8:9 But the dove could not find anywhere to stand. Water still covered all the ground. So it returned to Noah in the ship. Noah put out his hand and took the dove back into the ship. 
Genesis8:10 He waited for seven days. Then he sent the dove out of the ship again. 
Genesis8:11 The dove returned to Noah in the evening. It carried a fresh leaf from an olive tree in its mouth. Then Noah knew that the water had gone down. 
Genesis8:12 He waited for seven more days. Then he sent the dove out again. This time, it did not return to Noah." 
Genesis8:13 Noah was now 601 years old. On the first day of the first month, the water that had covered the earth was now gone. The ground had become dry. Noah made a hole in the roof of the ship and he looked around. He saw that the top of the ground was dry." 
Genesis8:14 By the 27th day of the second month, the earth had become completely dry." 
Genesis8:15 God said to Noah," 
Genesis8:16 ‘Come out of the ship. Bring your wife, your sons and their wives with you." 
Genesis8:17 Bring every different kind of living animal out of the ship. Bring out the birds, the animals, and the living things that move across the ground. Now they may give birth to young ones again. They can grow in number all over the earth.’" 
Genesis8:18 Noah went out of the ship, together with his wife, his sons and their wives." 
Genesis8:19 All the living animals also went out of the ship. The birds and the living things that move across the ground all went out. Every different kind of animal came out of the ship, each of them in its own group." 
Genesis8:20 Then Noah built an altar to make sacrifices to the Lord. He took one from each kind of clean animal and clean bird. He offered them to the Lord as sacrifices which he burned on the altar. 
Genesis8:21 The Lord smelled the sacrifice and it pleased him. The Lord said to himself, ‘I will never curse the ground again because of the bad things that people do. From the time that they are children, they want to do bad things. But I will never again destroy everything that breathes, as I have done this time." 
Genesis8:22 As long as the earth continues to be here, the time for people to plant seeds will come each year. The time for harvest will come each year. Times of cold and heat will always come. Summer and winter will always come. Day and night will never stop.’" 
Genesis9:1 God blessed Noah and his sons. He said to them, ‘Give birth to many children so that you become very many people. Your descendants will live all over the earth." 
Genesis9:2 All the animals and all the birds will be afraid of you. You have authority over them. You also have authority over all the living things that move across the ground and all the fish in the sea. 
Genesis9:3 You may now eat anything that lives and moves. Before, I gave you the green plants to eat. Now I give you everything to eat as your food." 
Genesis9:4 But you must not eat meat that still has the blood of life in it. 
Genesis9:5 If any person or animal kills a human, I will certainly punish them. Their punishment will be death." 
Genesis9:6 God made people so that they were like himself. So if anyone kills a human, another human must kill him." 
Genesis9:7 But as for you, you should give birth to many children so that you become very many people. There will be many people and they will live all over the earth.’" 
Genesis9:8 God said to Noah and to his sons," 
Genesis9:9 ‘I promise you that I will obey the covenant that I am making with you. I promise this to you and to all your descendants. 
Genesis9:10 I also promise this to all the animals and birds that came out of the ship with you. That is everything that lives on the earth. 
Genesis9:11 This is the covenant that I promise to keep: I will never cause water to destroy all living things on the earth again. Deep water will never again come again to destroy the earth.’ 
Genesis9:12 Then God said, ‘I will show you clearly that I have made this covenant with you, and with every living animal. I will keep this promise for ever, for as long as people live on the earth." 
Genesis9:13 I have put my rainbow among the clouds in the sky. It will show you clearly that I have made this covenant with everything on the earth. 
Genesis9:14 When I bring clouds over the earth, a rainbow will sometimes appear." 
Genesis9:15 Then I will remember the covenant that I have made with you and with all the different kinds of living things. Never again will the water become so deep that it destroys everything that lives. 
Genesis9:16 When I see the rainbow in the clouds, I will remember my covenant. It is a promise that I have made with every kind of living thing on the earth.’" 
Genesis9:17 Then God said to Noah, ‘The rainbow will show you that I will certainly do what I have promised. I will keep my covenant that is between me and everything that lives on the earth.’" 
Genesis9:18 The names of Noah's sons were Shem, Ham and Japheth. They all came out of the ship with Noah. Ham later became the father of a son called Canaan." 
Genesis9:19 All the people who live on the earth are descendants of Noah's three sons. 
Genesis9:20 Noah was a farmer. He was the first person to plant vines. 
Genesis9:21 He used the grapes to make wine. One day, he drank some of the wine and he became drunk. He lay inside his tent and he slept. He was not wearing any clothes." 
Genesis9:22 Canaan's father, Ham, saw that Noah was not wearing any clothes. He went out of the tent. He told his two brothers what he had seen." 
Genesis9:23 Then Shem and Japheth took Noah's coat and they put it between their shoulders. They walked into Noah's tent with their backs to Noah. In this way they covered their father with the coat. They looked away from their father so that they did not see him when he was not wearing any clothes. 
Genesis9:24 Later, when he was no longer drunk with the wine, Noah woke up. He found out what his youngest son, Ham, had done." 
Genesis9:25 He said, ‘I curse Canaan! He will become a slave who must serve his brothers.’" 
Genesis9:26 Noah also said, ‘I praise the Lord, the God of Shem! Let Canaan become Shem's slave!" 
Genesis9:27 I pray that God will make the land of Japheth and his descendants grow bigger. I pray that the descendants of Japheth will share good things with Shem. I pray that Canaan will become Japheth's slave.’ 
Genesis9:28 Noah lived for 350 years after God covered the earth with deep water. 
Genesis9:29 In his whole life, Noah lived for 950 years. Then he died." 
Genesis10:1 This is the report about Noah's sons, Shem, Ham and Japheth, and their families. After the deep water went away, they all had children themselves." 
Genesis10:2 These are the sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech and Tiras." 
Genesis10:3 These are the sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath and Togarmah." 
Genesis10:4 These are the sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittim people and the Rodanim people." 
Genesis10:5 These people lived by the sea. They lived in groups. Each group went to live in a different place. Each group spoke its own language. 
Genesis10:6 These are the sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Put and Canaan." 
Genesis10:7 These are the sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah and Sabteca. These are the sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan." 
Genesis10:8 Cush was also the father of Nimrod. Nimrod became the first brave soldier in the world. 
Genesis10:9 The Lord helped Nimrod to be a great hunter. That is why people say, ‘Like Nimrod, a great hunter that the Lord helped.’" 
Genesis10:10 The first of Nimrod's lands were Babylon, Erech, Akkad and Calneh, in Shinar." 
Genesis10:11 From that land he went to Assyria. This is where he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir and Calah." 
Genesis10:12 He also built Resen. Resen is between Nineveh and Calah, which is a large city." 
Genesis10:13 Mizraim was the ancestor of the Ludites, Anamites, Lehabites, Naphtuhites," 
Genesis10:14 Pathrusites, Kasluhites and Caphtorites. The Philistines were descendants of the Kasluhites." 
Genesis10:15 Canaan was the father of Sidon, his first son. Canaan was also the ancestor of the Hittites," 
Genesis10:16 Jebusites, Amorites, Girgashites," 
Genesis10:17 Hivites, Arkites, Sinites," 
Genesis10:18 Arvadites, Zemarites and Hamathites. After some time had passed, the Canaanite families went to live in different places." 
Genesis10:19 The land of Canaan went from Sidon to Gerar, as far as Gaza. It went to Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha." 
Genesis10:20 These are all descendants of Ham. They lived in groups in their own parts of the land. Each group spoke their own language. 
Genesis10:21 Shem also had sons. Shem was Japheth's older brother. All the sons of Eber are descendants of Shem. 
Genesis10:22 These are the sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud and Aram." 
Genesis10:23 These are the sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether and Meshech." 
Genesis10:24 Arphaxad was the father of Shelah. Shelah was the father of Eber. 
Genesis10:25 Eber had two sons. The name of one son was Peleg. When he was alive, people began to live in different places on the earth. That is why his name was Peleg. Peleg's brother's name was Joktan." 
Genesis10:26 Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah," 
Genesis10:27 Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah," 
Genesis10:28 Obal, Abimael, Sheba," 
Genesis10:29 Ophir, Havilah and Jobab. All these men were Joktan's sons." 
Genesis10:29 Ophir, Havilah and Jobab. All these men were Joktan's sons." 
Genesis10:30 The land where they lived went from Mesha to Sephar. This is in the land with hills in the east. 
Genesis10:31 These are the descendants of Shem. They lived in groups in their own parts of the land. Each group spoke their own language. 
Genesis10:32 All these people are descendants of Noah's sons. They went to live in different parts of the earth and their children became different nations. This happened after the time that deep water covered the earth. 
Genesis11:1 At this time, everyone who lived on the earth spoke one language. They used the same words." 
Genesis11:2 People moved to the east. They reached a large flat piece of land called Shinar. They stayed there to live. 
Genesis11:3 They said to each other, ‘We should make bricks and cook them until they are hard.’ These people used bricks instead of stones to build houses. They used tar to hold the bricks together, instead of cement." 
Genesis11:4 They said, ‘Let us build a city to live in. We should also build a very tall tower. The tower will go up into the heavens. Then we will always be famous. If we do that, we will not have to go and live in different places on the earth.’" 
Genesis11:5 But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower. He saw that the people had started to build them. 
Genesis11:6 The Lord said, ‘Because they all speak the same language, they are able to do this. If they can do this together, they may decide to do whatever they want." 
Genesis11:7 Now we must go down to them. We must cause their language to become confused. Then they will not be able to understand each other.’ 
Genesis11:8 So the Lord divided the people from one another. They went to live in different places all over the earth. They stopped building the city for themselves. 
Genesis11:9 That is why people called the city Babel. It was because the Lord confused all the people of the world and he gave them different languages to speak. From that time, the Lord caused the people to live in different groups all over the earth." 
Genesis11:10 This is the report about Shem and his family. Two years after the deep water had covered the earth, Shem was 100 years old. He became the father of Arphaxad." 
Genesis11:11 After Arphaxad was born, Shem lived for 500 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:12 When Arphaxad was 35 years old, he became the father of Shelah." 
Genesis11:13 After Shelah was born, Arphaxad lived for 403 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:14 When Shelah was 30 years old, he became the father of Eber." 
Genesis11:15 After Eber was born, Shelah lived for 403 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:16 When Eber was 34 years old, he became the father of Peleg." 
Genesis11:17 After Peleg was born, Eber lived for 430 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:18 When Peleg was 30 years old, he became the father of Reu." 
Genesis11:19 After Reu was born, Peleg lived for 209 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:20 When Reu was 32 years old, he became the father of Serug." 
Genesis11:21 After Serug was born, Reu lived for 207 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:22 When Serug was 30 years old, he became the father of Nahor." 
Genesis11:23 After Nahor was born, Serug lived for 200 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:24 When Nahor was 29 years old, he became the father of Terah." 
Genesis11:25 After Terah was born, Nahor lived for 119 years. He had other sons and daughters." 
Genesis11:26 After Terah was 70 years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran." 
Genesis11:27 This is the report about Terah and his family. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran. Haran became the father of Lot." 
Genesis11:28 While Terah was still alive, his son, Haran, died. Haran died in Ur, the land of the Chaldeans. That was where Haran had been born." 
Genesis11:29 Abram married Sarai. Nahor married Milcah. Milcah was Haran's daughter. Haran was the father of Milcah and Iscah. 
Genesis11:30 Sarai could not give birth to children. She did not have any children. 
Genesis11:31 Terah left Ur, the land of the Chaldeans. He took with him his son Abram and his grandson Lot. (Lot was Haran's son.) Terah also took Abram's wife Sarai. They began to go to Canaan. But when they came to Haran, they stayed there." 
Genesis11:32 Terah died in Haran when he was 205 years old. 
Genesis12:1 The Lord had said to Abram, ‘Leave your country and the people of your father's family. Go to the land that I will show you." 
Genesis12:2 I will cause your descendants to become a great nation. I will bless you. Everyone will know your name. You will bring my blessing to other people. 
Genesis12:3 I will bless those people who bless you. But I will curse anyone who insults you. I will bless all the families of people on the earth because of you.’ 
Genesis12:4 Abram did what the Lord had told him. He left Haran. Abram was 75 years old when he left Haran. Lot went with him. 
Genesis12:5 Abram took his wife Sarai and his nephew Lot. He took everything that belonged to them. He also took the people who worked for them in Haran. They all left to go to the land of Canaan. When they arrived in Canaan," 
Genesis12:6 Abram walked through the land. He went as far as Shechem, to the special oak tree of Moreh. At that time, Canaanites lived in this land." 
Genesis12:7 The Lord appeared to Abram there. He said, ‘I will give this land to your descendants.’ So Abram built an altar in that place to worship the Lord. He did that because the Lord had appeared to him." 
Genesis12:8 Then Abram left Shechem. He went to the hills that are on the east of Bethel. He put up his tent in that place. Bethel was towards the west, and Ai was towards the east. Abram also built an altar there to worship the Lord." 
Genesis12:9 Then Abram took his tent and he left that place. He continued to travel towards the Negev. 
Genesis12:10 There was a famine in the land of Canaan. So Abram went to live in Egypt for some time, because the famine was very bad." 
Genesis12:11 When Abram came near to Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, ‘Listen to me. I know that you are a very beautiful woman." 
Genesis12:12 When the Egyptians see you they will say, “This is Abram's wife.” Then they will kill me, but they will let you live." 
Genesis12:13 So tell them that you are my sister. Then they will do good things to me, because they want to please you. They will not kill me because they will think that you are my sister.’" 
Genesis12:14 When Abram arrived in Egypt, the people saw that Sarai was a very beautiful woman." 
Genesis12:15 The king's officers saw her. They told Pharaoh that she was very beautiful. They took her to the king's palace. 
Genesis12:16 Pharaoh did good things to help Abram because of Sarai. He gave Abram sheep, cows, donkeys and camels. He also gave Abram male servants and female servants." 
Genesis12:17 But the Lord made Pharaoh and the people in his palace very ill. The Lord did this because the king had taken Abram's wife Sarai. 
Genesis12:18 So Pharaoh called Abram to come to him. Pharaoh said, ‘You have done this bad thing to me! You did not tell me that Sarai is your wife! Why not?" 
Genesis12:19 You told me, “She is my sister.” As a result, I took her to be my wife. Now, here is your wife. Take her and go away!’" 
Genesis12:20 Pharaoh told his officers what to do with Abram. They sent Abram away, with his wife and everything that belonged to them." 
Genesis13:1 Abram left Egypt and he travelled north to the Negev. He took his wife and everything that belonged to him. Lot also went with him. 
Genesis13:2 Abram had many valuable things. He had many cows, and much silver and gold." 
Genesis13:3 Then Abram left the Negev and he went to different places. He arrived at Bethel. He returned to the place where he had put up his tent before. That place was between Bethel and Ai. 
Genesis13:4 It was where Abram had first built an altar to worship the Lord. 
Genesis13:5 Lot was travelling with Abram. Lot also had many cows, sheep and tents." 
Genesis13:6 But the land could not grow enough food for all of them to eat. They could not all live together in the same place, because they had so many animals and people with them." 
Genesis13:7 Abram's shepherds and Lot's shepherds began to quarrel with each other. (At that time, Canaanites and Perizzites also lived in the land.)" 
Genesis13:8 Abram said to Lot, ‘We must not quarrel with each other. Your shepherds and my shepherds must not quarrel with each other. Remember that we belong to the same family." 
Genesis13:9 Look everywhere! There is enough land for all of us. We must go different ways. If you go to the left, I will go to the right. If you go to the right, I will go to the left.’" 
Genesis13:10 Lot looked up and he saw the valley of the Jordan River, as far as Zoar. He saw that it had lots of water, like the garden of the Lord. It was good land, like the land of Egypt. (This was before the Lord destroyed the cities there called Sodom and Gomorrah.)" 
Genesis13:11 So Lot chose for himself the valley of the Jordan River. He travelled towards the east. 
Genesis13:12 Abram stayed in the land of Canaan and he lived there. But Lot lived near the cities that were in the Jordan Valley. He put up his tents near the city of Sodom. 
Genesis13:13 The people of Sodom were very wicked. They did not obey the Lord at all. 
Genesis13:14 After Lot had gone away, the Lord said to Abram, ‘Stand where you are and look all around! Look to the north, the south, the east and the west." 
Genesis13:15 I will give you all the land that you can see. I will give it to you and your descendants. It will belong to them for ever. 
Genesis13:16 I will make your descendants become very many. Like the dust of the ground, people will not be able to count them all." 
Genesis13:17 Go and travel round all this land. Look at it, because I am giving it to you!’" 
Genesis13:18 So Abram moved his tents. He went to live near the special oak trees of Mamre. That place is at Hebron. Abram built an altar to worship the Lord there. 
Genesis14:1 Now there were many kings living in that land, at that time. They fought against each other. One fight happened when Kedorlaomer had ruled over the other kings for 12 years. And in the next year, five kings made one group and they fought against him. Kedorlaomer joined with three other kings. So it was four kings against five other kings. The four kings were Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Kedorlaomer king of Elam and Tidal king of Goyim. Those kings fought a war against Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim and the king of Bela. (Bela is another name for the town of Zoar.) They fought in Siddim Valley. (That is the Valley of the Salt Sea.)" 
Genesis14:2  
Genesis14:3  
Genesis14:4  
Genesis14:5 One year later, Kedorlaomer and the kings with him won against the Rephaites. This was in Ashteroth Karnaim. They won against the Zuzites in Ham. They won against the Emites in Shaveh Kiriathaim." 
Genesis14:6 And they won against the Horites in the country of Seir. This was as far as El Paran near the desert. 
Genesis14:7 They then went back. They went to En Mishpat. (That is Kadesh.) They took the whole land of the Amalekites. They also took the land of the Amorites who were living in Hazezon Tamar. 
Genesis14:8 Then, the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim and the king of Bela (that is Zoar) went to fight. That was in Siddim Valley." 
Genesis14:9 They fought against Kedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goyim, Amraphel king of Shinar and Arioch king of Ellasar. There were four kings fighting against five kings." 
Genesis14:10 Siddim Valley had many holes in the ground. The holes were filled with tar. The kings of Sodom and Gomorrah ran away. Some of their men fell into the deep tar. The other men ran away to the hills. 
Genesis14:11 The four kings took everything in Sodom and Gomorrah, including all the food. Then they went away." 
Genesis14:12 Abram's nephew, Lot, was living in Sodom at that time. They took him away from there as their prisoner. They also took everything that belonged to him." 
Genesis14:13 At that time, Abram, the Hebrew man, was living near the oak trees of Mamre. Mamre was an Amorite. He was the brother of Eshcol and Aner. These men had agreed to be Abram's friends. A man had escaped from Sodom. He told Abram everything that had happened." 
Genesis14:14 Abram understood that the kings had taken his nephew as their prisoner. So he called 318 men to come together. These men had been born in Abram's camp and they knew how to fight. Abram took his men with him. They went to attack the men who had caught Lot. They went as far as Dan. 
Genesis14:15 During the night, Abram separated his men into two groups. Then they attacked Kedorlaomer and his men who had taken Lot. Those men ran away and Abram followed them as far as Hobah. Hobah is north of Damascus." 
Genesis14:16 Abram took back everything that those men had taken from Sodom. He also brought back his nephew Lot, together with the women and the other people." 
Genesis14:17 In that way, Abram won against Kedorlaomer and the kings with him. As Abram was returning home, the king of Sodom came out to meet him. They met in Shaveh Valley, which people call the King's Valley." 
Genesis14:18 Melchizedek was the king of Salem, and he was a priest of the Most High God. He brought out bread and wine for Abram." 
Genesis14:19 He blessed Abram. He said, ‘I pray that the Most High God who made heaven and earth will bless Abram." 
Genesis14:20 Praise the Most High God! He has given you power over your enemies.’ After that, Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth part of everything." 
Genesis14:21 Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, ‘Give back to me all my people. Take everything else for yourself.’" 
Genesis14:22 But Abram said to the king of Sodom, ‘I have made a strong promise to the Lord, the Most High God who made heaven and earth." 
Genesis14:23 I promised that I would not take anything that belonged to you. I will not take the smallest thing, not even a piece of string or part of a shoe. Then you will never be able to say, “I made Abram become rich.”" 
Genesis14:24 I myself will accept only the food that my young men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol and Mamre went with me to help me to fight. They should take their part of the things that we brought back with us.’" 
Genesis15:1 After this, the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision. God said, ‘Do not be afraid Abram. I will keep you safe. I myself will give you many good gifts.’" 
Genesis15:2 But Abram said, ‘ Almighty Lord, there is one gift that I want. I still have no children. When I die, Eliezer of Damascus will receive everything that I have." 
Genesis15:3 Because you have not given me any children, all my things will one day belong to a servant in my house.’" 
Genesis15:4 Then the Lord gave Abram this message: ‘No! Eliezer will not be the one who receives your things. You yourself will be the father of a son. He will be the one who receives everything that you have.’ 
Genesis15:5 The Lord took Abram outside. He said to Abram, ‘Look up at the sky. Count the stars. They are too many for you to count!’ Then God said, ‘That is how many descendants you will have.’" 
Genesis15:6 Abram believed the Lord. As a result, the Lord accepted Abram as right with him." 
Genesis15:7 The Lord also said to Abram, ‘I am the Lord. I brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to bring you to this land. I am giving it to you. It will belong to you.’" 
Genesis15:8 But Abram said, ‘Almighty Lord, how can I be sure that this land will belong to me?’" 
Genesis15:9 The Lord said to Abram, ‘Bring a young cow, a goat and a male sheep to offer them to me. Each animal must be three years old. Also bring a dove and a young pigeon.’" 
Genesis15:10 Abram brought the three animals and the two birds to offer to God. Abram cut each animal in two pieces. He put the halves opposite each other. But he did not cut the birds in two pieces. 
Genesis15:11 Some birds flew down to eat the animals' dead bodies. But Abram sent them away. 
Genesis15:12 When the sun was going down, Abram started to sleep. Suddenly he became very afraid because it was dark all around him." 
Genesis15:13 Then the Lord said to Abram, ‘Be sure of this: Your descendants will live in a foreign country. They will be strangers there. People will do bad things to them and they will become slaves. This will continue for 400 years." 
Genesis15:14 But I will punish the people of that country who give them trouble. After this, your descendants will leave that country. They will take many valuable things with them." 
Genesis15:15 But as for you, Abram, you will have a long life. When you die, you will have peace in your mind." 
Genesis15:16 After four generations, your descendants will come back here to Canaan. At that time, I will punish the Amorites because they do very bad things. But the time has not yet arrived that I will punish them.’" 
Genesis15:17 Then the sun went down and it became dark. Abram saw a pot that had coals in it. The coals were burning and making smoke. There was also a branch that burned with bright fire. These passed between the halves of the animals that Abram had cut into two pieces. 
Genesis15:18 On that day, the Lord made a covenant with Abram. The Lord promised, ‘I give this land to your descendants. The land starts from the river of Egypt and continues as far as the Euphrates River." 
Genesis15:19 These people live in the land: Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites," 
Genesis15:20 Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites," 
Genesis15:21 Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites and Jebusites.’" 
Genesis16:1 Abram's wife, Sarai, had not given birth to any children. She had an Egyptian servant. The servant's name was Hagar." 
Genesis16:2 Sarai said to Abram, ‘The Lord has not let me have any children. Go and sleep with my servant. Then if she gives birth, her children will be my family.’ Abram agreed to do this." 
Genesis16:3 Abram had now been living in Canaan for ten years. Sarai gave her Egyptian servant, Hagar, to him. She became like another wife for Abram." 
Genesis16:4 Abram had sex with Hagar and she became pregnant. When Hagar knew that she was pregnant, she no longer respected Sarai." 
Genesis16:5 Then Sarai said to Abram, ‘It is because of you that Hagar is now insulting me. I gave her to you so that you could have sex with her. Now she is pregnant and she does not respect me. The Lord will decide who is right, you or me.’" 
Genesis16:6 Abram said to Sarai, ‘Hagar is your servant and you have authority over her. Do to her whatever you think is right.’ Then Sarai caused trouble for Hagar. So Hagar ran away from Sarai." 
Genesis16:7 The angel of the found Hagar. She was by a spring of water in the desert. The spring was near the road to Shur. 
Genesis16:8 The angel said, ‘Sarai's servant, Hagar, where have you come from? Where are you going?’ Hagar replied, ‘I am Sarai's servant but I am running away from her.’" 
Genesis16:9 The angel of the Lord told Hagar, ‘Go back to Sarai. You are her servant and you must obey her.’" 
Genesis16:10 The angel also said, ‘I will make the number of your descendants become very many. They will be too many for people to count.’" 
Genesis16:11 The angel of the Lord said to Hagar, ‘Now you are pregnant and you will give birth to a son. When you cried in pain, the Lord heard you, so you must give your son the name “Ishmael”." 
Genesis16:12 Like a wild donkey, nobody will be able to rule him. He will fight against everyone, and everyone will fight against him. He will think that everyone is his enemy. He will live far away from his brothers.’" 
Genesis16:13 When Hagar heard what the angel said, she said to herself, ‘I have seen the God who sees me!’ So she called the Lord ‘The God who sees me.’" 
Genesis16:14 That is why people called the well in that place ‘Beer Lahai Roi’. The well is between Kadesh and Bered. 
Genesis16:15 After some time Hagar gave birth to a son. Abram was his father. Abram gave his son the name ‘Ishmael’. 
Genesis16:16 Abram was 86 years old when Hagar gave birth to Ishmael. 
Genesis17:1 When Abram was 99 years old, the Lord appeared to him. The Lord said, ‘I am God Almighty. In all your life, obey me and do nothing wrong." 
Genesis17:2 Then I will certainly keep my covenant with you. I will make your descendants grow in number.’ 
Genesis17:3 Abram fell down so that his face touched the ground. God said to him," 
Genesis17:4 ‘Listen to me. This is my covenant that I have made with you. You will become the ancestor of many nations of people. 
Genesis17:5 Your name will not be Abram any more. Instead your name will be Abraham, because I will cause you to become the ancestor of many nations." 
Genesis17:6 I will give you many descendants. These will make many nations of people. Some of your descendants will become kings. 
Genesis17:7 I will make this covenant with you and with your descendants. It will continue for many generations of your descendants, for all time. I will be your God. I will also be the God of all your descendants." 
Genesis17:8 You are now living here in Canaan as a foreign person. But I will give all of this land to you and to your descendants. It will belong to them for ever. I will be their God.’ 
Genesis17:9 God continued to speak to Abraham. He said, ‘As for you, you must agree to obey me. You must obey the rules of my covenant. You and all your descendants after you must obey it." 
Genesis17:10 This is what you and all your descendants must do to obey my covenant. Every male person in your family must be circumcised. 
Genesis17:11 Your circumcision will show that you have agreed to my covenant with you. 
Genesis17:12 Every male child among you must be circumcised when he is eight days old. You must continue to do this for every generation of your descendants. Circumcise every male that lives with you. Circumcise your own family. Circumcise servants that have been born in your house. Also circumcise foreign servants that you have bought with money. 
Genesis17:13 You must circumcise all the male servants who work for you in your house. Circumcision will be a mark in your bodies which shows that you accept my covenant. It will continue for all time. 
Genesis17:14 If any male person among you has not been circumcised, I will not accept him as one of my people. He must become separate from his people. He has not obeyed my covenant.’" 
Genesis17:15 Then God said to Abraham, ‘As for your wife, do not call her Sarai any more. Instead, her name will be Sarah." 
Genesis17:16 I will bless her. She will give birth to a son for you. Many nations of people will be her descendants. Some of them will become kings. That is how much I will bless her!’ 
Genesis17:17 Abraham fell down so that his face touched the ground. He laughed and he said to himself, ‘I am now 100 years old. That is too old to have a son. Sarah is 90 years old, so she could never give birth to a child.’" 
Genesis17:18 So Abraham said to God, ‘I would be happy if you would bless Ishmael as my son.’" 
Genesis17:19 God said to Abraham, ‘No, your wife Sarah will give birth to a son for you. You must give him the name “Isaac”. I will make my covenant with him and with his descendants for all time." 
Genesis17:20 I have heard what you want me to do for Ishmael. I will bless him too. I will cause him to have many descendants. Among his descendants there will be 12 rulers. His descendants will become a great nation. 
Genesis17:21 But I will make my covenant with Isaac, not with Ishmael. At about this time next year, Sarah will give birth to your son, Isaac.’" 
Genesis17:22 When God had finished speaking to Abraham, he went away." 
Genesis17:23 On that same day, Abraham circumcised Ishmael and every male person in his house. He circumcised every male servant that was born in his house, and also those that he had bought with money. He did this in the way that God had told him." 
Genesis17:24 Abraham himself was circumcised when he was 99 years old. 
Genesis17:25 His son, Ishmael, was 13 years old when he was circumcised." 
Genesis17:26 Abraham and Ishmael were circumcised on the same day. 
Genesis17:27 As well as them, all the male people living in Abraham's house were circumcised. This included the male servants that had been born in his house and those that he had bought with money." 
Genesis18:1 The Lord appeared to Abraham near the oak trees of Mamre. It was the very hot time of the day and Abraham was sitting by the door of his tent. 
Genesis18:2 Abraham looked up and he saw three men. He ran in a hurry to meet them. He bent down to the ground to welcome them. 
Genesis18:3 Abraham said, ‘Please sir, stop here and let me serve you. That would make me very happy." 
Genesis18:4 Let us bring some water so that you can all wash your feet. Then you can rest under this tree. 
Genesis18:5 Let me also bring some food for you to eat. Now that you have come to my home, you should rest and become strong again. Then you will be ready to continue with your journey.’ The men replied, ‘That is good. Please do what you have said.’" 
Genesis18:6 Abraham went quickly to the tent. He said to Sarah, ‘Be quick! Get plenty of good flour and make bread with it.’" 
Genesis18:7 Then Abraham ran to his cows. He chose a very nice young cow. He gave it to his servant. The servant quickly prepared it for the men to eat. 
Genesis18:8 Then Abraham also brought some cream and some milk, as well as the meat from the cow. He put all the food in front of the men. While they ate it, Abraham stood near them, under a tree." 
Genesis18:9 Then the men asked Abraham, ‘Where is Sarah, your wife?’ Abraham replied, ‘She is there in the tent.’" 
Genesis18:10 Then one of the men said, ‘I will certainly return to you at about this time next year. At that time Sarah, your wife, will have a son!’ Sarah was listening to all this. She was at the door of the tent, near to where they were talking." 
Genesis18:11 Abraham and Sarah were now very old. Sarah was past the age when she could give birth to a child. 
Genesis18:12 So Sarah laughed to herself. She thought, ‘I have become old and weak. My husband is also old. I will never have a baby and be happy like that.’" 
Genesis18:13 Then the Lord said to Abraham, ‘Why did Sarah laugh like that? She said, “I am too old to have a child.”" 
Genesis18:14 But surely, nothing is too difficult for the Lord to do. At this time next year, I will return to you and Sarah will have a son.’" 
Genesis18:15 Sarah was afraid, so she told a lie. She said, ‘I did not laugh.’ But the Lord said, ‘Yes, you did laugh.’" 
Genesis18:16 Then Abraham's visitors started to leave. They looked down towards Sodom in the valley. Abraham was walking with them to say ‘goodbye’ to them. 
Genesis18:17 The Lord said to himself, ‘I should tell Abraham what I have decided to do." 
Genesis18:18 Abraham's descendants will become a great nation of people. They will be very powerful. I will bless all the nations of the earth because of him. 
Genesis18:19 I have chosen him so that he will teach my message to his children and their families. He will teach them to do what is right and what is fair. Then I, the Lord, will do for Abraham everything that I have promised to him.’" 
Genesis18:20 So the Lord said to Abraham, ‘I have heard how wicked the people in Sodom and Gomorrah are. Everybody says that their sins are very bad." 
Genesis18:21 So I must go down there to see if it is true. I will see if those people are as bad as everybody says they are. If it is not true, I need to know about it.’" 
Genesis18:22 The other two men then turned away and they went towards Sodom. But Abraham stayed there to talk with the Lord. 
Genesis18:23 Then Abraham went near to the Lord and he asked, ‘Will you destroy the good people in Sodom as well as the bad people?" 
Genesis18:24 There might be 50 people in the city who live in a good way. Will you still destroy the whole city? Or will you leave it there, because there are 50 good people in it?" 
Genesis18:25 Will you kill them all, the good people and the bad people? Surely you would not do anything like that! You would not punish good people in the same way as bad people. You are the great judge of everyone on the earth. You will surely do what is right!’" 
Genesis18:26 The Lord replied, ‘If I see that there are 50 good people in the city of Sodom, I will not destroy that place. Because of those 50 good people, I will leave the city there.’" 
Genesis18:27 Then Abraham spoke again. He said, ‘I have been brave enough to speak to the Lord. I know that I am nothing more than dust and ashes." 
Genesis18:28 But let me ask you this. What will you do if there are 45 good people in the city? Will you destroy the city, because there are only five fewer good people there?’ The Lord replied, ‘If I see that there are 45 good people in the city, I will not destroy it.’" 
Genesis18:29 Abraham spoke to God again. He said, ‘What will you do if only 40 good people are there?’ The Lord said, ‘Because of 40 good people, I will not destroy the city.’" 
Genesis18:30 Then Abraham said, ‘Please do not be angry, Lord. Let me speak again. What will you do if there are only 30 good people there?’ The Lord answered, ‘I will not destroy the city if I find 30 good people there.’" 
Genesis18:31 Abraham said, ‘I have been brave to speak to you, Lord. What will you do if only 20 good people are there?’ The Lord said, ‘Because of 20 good people, I will not destroy the city.’" 
Genesis18:32 After all that, Abraham said, ‘Please do not be angry, Lord. Let me speak one more time. If there are only ten good people there, what will you do?’ The Lord replied, ‘Because of ten good people, I will not destroy the city.’" 
Genesis18:33 The Lord had finished speaking with Abraham. So he continued on his journey. Abraham returned home. 
Genesis19:1 The two angels arrived at Sodom. It was evening and Lot was sitting near the city gate. When Lot saw the angels, he got up to meet them. He turned his face towards the ground to welcome them." 
Genesis19:2 Lot said, ‘My lords, please come to stay in my house. You can wash your feet and stay the night there. Then you can continue your journey early in the morning.’ The angels replied, ‘No, we will stay the night here in this public place.’" 
Genesis19:3 But Lot continued to ask them to stay with him. So the angels went with Lot to his house. Lot prepared a big meal for them, with some bread that he had baked without yeast. The angels ate the food." 
Genesis19:4 After that, they were preparing to go to bed. Then all the men who lived in Sodom came to Lot's house. There were old men and young men who came from every part of the city. They stood all around the house." 
Genesis19:5 The men shouted to Lot, ‘Where are the men who are staying with you tonight? Bring them out here to us. We want to have sex with them.’" 
Genesis19:6 Lot went outside to talk to the men. He shut the door of his house behind him. 
Genesis19:7 He said, ‘No, my friends. Please do not do this evil thing." 
Genesis19:8 See here! I have two daughters. They have never had sex with a man. Let me bring them out to you. Then you can do whatever you want with them. But do not do anything to these men. They are my visitors and I cannot let anyone hurt them.’ 
Genesis19:9 The men from the city said, ‘Do not try to stop us! You live here as a stranger here in our city. You cannot tell us what to do! Be careful or we will do even worse things to you.’ They pushed against Lot and tried to reach the door of his house. They wanted to break the door and go into the house." 
Genesis19:10 The two visitors who were inside the house opened the door. They pulled Lot back into his house and they shut the door quickly. 
Genesis19:11 Then they caused all the men outside to become blind, the young men of the city as well as the old men. As a result, they could not find the door of the house, even though they tried for some time." 
Genesis19:12 The two visitors asked Lot, ‘Do you have any of your family here in the city? Do you have any sons or daughters, or husbands for your daughters? If you have any family, you must take them away from here." 
Genesis19:13 We will soon destroy this city. Everybody knows how bad the people who live here are. As a result, the Lord has sent us to destroy this place.’" 
Genesis19:14 So Lot went out of his house. He spoke to the men who would marry his daughters. He said to them, ‘Hurry! Leave this city now, because the Lord has decided to destroy it.’ But the men did not believe Lot. They thought he was not being serious." 
Genesis19:15 At dawn, the two angels told Lot he must leave quickly. They said to him, ‘Hurry! Take your wife and take your two daughters who are here. If you do not go quickly, you will all die when the Lord destroys the city.’" 
Genesis19:16 But Lot did not move. So the angels took hold of Lot's hand, and also the hands of his wife and his two daughters. The Lord was very kind to Lot and his family and the angels led them away from the city." 
Genesis19:17 When they had reached a place outside the city, one of the angels said, ‘Now run! Your lives are in danger. Do not look behind you! Do not stop anywhere in this valley. Run up into the mountains or you will die.’" 
Genesis19:18 But Lot said, ‘No! Please, my lords, I cannot do that!" 
Genesis19:19 I am your humble servant and you have been very kind to me. You have saved my life. But I cannot run away into the mountains. If I try to do that, this punishment will catch me before I reach there. Then I will surely die." 
Genesis19:20 Look! See that town over there. It is near and I can run to it safely. And it is a small town. So please let me run there. You can see that it is only a small place. If I go there, I will stay alive.’" 
Genesis19:21 The angel said to Lot, ‘I will let you do what you have asked. I will not destroy that town." 
Genesis19:22 But run there quickly. I cannot do anything until you arrive in the town.’ (The name of the town became ‘Zoar’, because it was small.)" 
Genesis19:23 Lot reached Zoar at the time when the sun was rising that morning. 
Genesis19:24 Then the Lord poured sulphur with fire down on Sodom and Gomorrah. It fell from the sky like rain. 
Genesis19:25 In that way God destroyed those cities and everything in the valley. He killed all the people who lived in those cities. And he killed all the plants that grew on the land. 
Genesis19:26 But as they ran away, Lot's wife looked back at the city. When she did that, she became a large piece of salt, like a pillar." 
Genesis19:27 Early in the morning, Abraham returned to the place where he had spoken with the Lord." 
Genesis19:28 He looked towards Sodom and Gomorrah and the whole valley. He saw thick dark smoke that was rising from the land. It was like the smoke from a hot fire. 
Genesis19:29 When God destroyed the cities of the valley, he saved Lot from that punishment. God remembered what he had promised to Abraham. God took Lot away from the cities where he had lived. Then he destroyed those cities." 
Genesis19:30 Lot was afraid to live in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and they went up into the mountains. They lived together in a cave. 
Genesis19:31 One day, the older daughter said to her sister, ‘Our father is now old. There are no men who live near here, so there is nobody to marry us. We cannot have sex like everyone on the earth wants to do." 
Genesis19:32 So we should give our father a lot of wine to drink. When he becomes drunk, we will have sex with him. Then our father's family will continue to have descendants.’" 
Genesis19:33 That night they caused their father to become drunk with wine. The older daughter had sex with him. Lot was very drunk. He did not know when she came to him. And he did not know when she left him. 
Genesis19:34 The next day the older daughter said to her younger sister, ‘Last night I had sex with my father. We should make him drunk with wine again tonight. Then you can have sex with him. As a result, our father's family will continue.’" 
Genesis19:35 So that night, they caused their father to become drunk again. The younger daughter had sex with her father. He did not know when she came to him. And he did not know when she left him." 
Genesis19:36 In that way, Lot caused both of his daughters to become pregnant." 
Genesis19:37 The older daughter gave birth to a son. She called him Moab. He became the ancestor of the Moabites. 
Genesis19:38 The younger daughter also gave birth to a son. She called him Ben-Ammi. He became the ancestor of the Ammonites. 
Genesis20:1 Abraham travelled south to the Negev Desert. He lived between Kadesh and Shur. For a time he stayed in Gerar. 
Genesis20:2 While he was there, Abraham told people that his wife, Sarah, was his sister. Because of this, the king of Gerar took Sarah so that she would become his wife. The king's name was Abimelech." 
Genesis20:3 But God appeared to Abimelech in a dream. He said to Abimelech, ‘Now you will die! The woman that you have taken to be your wife is already a married woman.’" 
Genesis20:4 But Abimelech had not yet touched Sarah. He said to God, ‘ Lord, I have not done anything wrong. Surely you will not destroy me and my people." 
Genesis20:5 Abraham told me that Sarah was his sister. And she herself said, “I am his sister.” So I am not guilty! I did not think that I was doing a bad thing.’" 
Genesis20:6 God spoke to Abimelech again in a dream. He said, ‘That is true. You did not think that you were doing something wrong. I know that. So I did not let you touch her. I stopped you from doing anything wrong against me." 
Genesis20:7 You must now give her back to her husband. He is a prophet and he will pray for you. Because of that, you will not die. But if you do not give her back, you and all your people will surely die.’" 
Genesis20:8 Early the next morning, Abimelech called together his officers. He told them what had happened. When the officers heard about it, they were very afraid." 
Genesis20:9 Abimelech called Abraham to come to him. He said to Abraham, ‘Why have you done this bad thing against us? I have not done anything wrong against you. Now you have made me and the people in my kingdom guilty of a very bad thing. Nobody should ever do the things that you have done to me.’" 
Genesis20:10 Abimelech asked Abraham, ‘What caused you to do this?’" 
Genesis20:11 Abraham replied, ‘I did it because I was afraid. I thought, “The people here do not respect God. They will kill me so that they can take my wife from me.”" 
Genesis20:12 And also, she really is my sister. She is the daughter of my father. But she is not the daughter of my mother. And she became my wife." 
Genesis20:13 God told me to leave my father's house and to travel. At that time I said to Sarah, “This is how you can show that you love me. Everywhere we go, tell people that I am your brother.” ’" 
Genesis20:14 Then Abimelech brought sheep and cows to give to Abraham. He also gave to Abraham male and female servants. And he gave Sarah back to Abraham. 
Genesis20:15 Abimelech said, ‘Look! See my land all around you. Go and live anywhere that you want to live.’" 
Genesis20:16 Abimelech said to Sarah, ‘I have given 1,000 pieces of silver to your brother. This is to show everyone that you yourself did nothing wrong. It will pay you for any trouble that you have received.’" 
Genesis20:17 Then Abraham prayed to God. As a result, God made Abimelech become well again. He also made Abimelech's wife and his female slaves become well, so that they could have children again." 
Genesis20:18 The Lord had made them unable to have children. He did this because of what happened to Abraham's wife, Sarah." 
Genesis21:1 The Lord was kind to Sarah. He did what he had promised to do for her. 
Genesis21:2 Sarah became pregnant. She gave birth to a son for Abraham when he was very old. This happened at the time that God promised Abraham it would happen. 
Genesis21:3 Abraham called his son ‘Isaac’. This was the son that Sarah gave birth to. 
Genesis21:4 When Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, as God had told him to do." 
Genesis21:5 Abraham was 100 years old when his son, Isaac, was born." 
Genesis21:6 Sarah said, ‘Now God has made me laugh. Everyone who hears about what has happened will be happy with me.’" 
Genesis21:7 She also said, ‘Nobody would ever have said to Abraham that I would feed a child at my breasts. But I have given birth to a son for him, even when he is old.’" 
Genesis21:8 The child grew stronger, and he began to eat food. On that day, Abraham prepared a big party meal." 
Genesis21:9 But Sarah saw that Ishmael was laughing at Isaac. (Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the Egyptian servant.)" 
Genesis21:10 So Sarah said to Abraham, ‘Send that slave woman and her son away from here. Ishmael must never receive any of our family's things. Everything must belong to my own son, Isaac.’" 
Genesis21:11 This made Abraham very sad, because Ishmael was his own son." 
Genesis21:12 God said to Abraham, ‘Do not be upset about Ishmael or about Hagar. Do whatever Sarah tells you. Your family's descendants will come through Isaac, not Ishmael." 
Genesis21:13 But because Ishmael is also your son, I will make his descendants become a great nation of people too.’" 
Genesis21:14 Abraham woke up early the next morning. He took some food and some water in a bottle that was made from animal skin. He gave them to Hagar and he put them on her shoulders. Then he sent Hagar away, with their son, Ishmael. She went and she travelled through the desert near Beersheba." 
Genesis21:15 After they had drunk all the water, Hagar put her son in the shade under a bush." 
Genesis21:16 Then she went and sat down about 100 metres away from him. She thought, ‘I cannot watch the boy die.’ As she sat there, she began to cry." 
Genesis21:17 Ishmael was crying and God heard him. The angel of God spoke to Hagar from heaven. He said, ‘What is the trouble, Hagar? Do not be afraid. God has heard the boy while he is crying there." 
Genesis21:18 Go over to him and lift him up. Take hold of his hand and help him to stand. I will make him become a great nation of people.’ 
Genesis21:19 Then God helped Hagar to see clearly. She saw a well with water in it. She went to the well and she filled the bottle with water. She gave the boy some water to drink. 
Genesis21:20 God took care of Ishmael while he grew. The boy lived in the wilderness. He became a hunter of wild animals. 
Genesis21:21 He lived in the Paran wilderness. Hagar, his mother, found an Egyptian woman for him to marry."
