Genesis21:22 At that time Phicol was the leader of King Abimelech's army. Abimelech and Phicol said to Abraham, ‘We see that God helps you in everything that you do." 
Genesis21:23 So please make a promise to me in front of God. Promise that you will never deceive me, or my children, or my descendants. You are living here in this land as a stranger. I have been kind to you, so please show that you will also be kind to us.’" 
Genesis21:24 Abraham said, ‘I promise to do all that.’" 
Genesis21:25 One day, Abraham complained to Abimelech about a certain well of water. Abimelech's servants had taken the well from Abraham. They said that the well belonged to them." 
Genesis21:26 Abimelech said, ‘I do not know who has done this. You did not tell me before. I did not hear about it until today.’" 
Genesis21:27 So Abraham brought some sheep and some cows and he gave them to Abimelech. The two men made a promise to help each other. 
Genesis21:28 Abraham took seven female lambs from the sheep. He put them in a different place from the other animals. 
Genesis21:29 Abimelech asked Abraham, ‘Why have you put these seven lambs in a different place?’" 
Genesis21:30 Abraham replied, ‘You must accept these seven lambs as a gift from me. That will show that you agree that I dug this well. Everyone will know that it belongs to me.’" 
Genesis21:31 Because of that, the name of the place became ‘Beersheba’, because the two men made a promise there." 
Genesis21:32 In that way, they made an agreement at Beersheba to help one another. Then Abimelech and Phicol, the leader of his army, returned to the land of the Philistines." 
Genesis21:33 Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba. In that place, he worshipped the Lord who is God for ever." 
Genesis21:34 Abraham lived in the land of the Philistines for a long time. 
Genesis22:1 Some time later, God wanted to see if Abraham really trusted him. God said to him, ‘Abraham!’ Abraham replied, ‘Here I am.’" 
Genesis22:2 God said, ‘Take your son and go to the land of Moriah. Take your only son Isaac, whom you love. You must offer him to me there as a burnt offering. I will show you the mountain where you must do this.’" 
Genesis22:3 Abraham got up early the next morning. He made his donkey ready for the journey. He took two servants with him, and also his son Isaac. First, he cut wood for the fire to make the burnt offering. Then he started on his journey to the place that God had told him." 
Genesis22:4 On the third day of the journey, Abraham could see the place. It was not very far away." 
Genesis22:5 He said to his servants, ‘Stay here with the donkey. I will take the boy and we will go over there. We will worship God in that place and then we will come back to you.’" 
Genesis22:6 Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering. He gave it to Isaac to carry. Abraham himself carried the fire and the knife. The two of them walked on together. 
Genesis22:7 Isaac spoke to his father, Abraham. He said, ‘Father?’ Abraham replied ‘Yes my son, what is it?’ Isaac said, ‘I see that the fire and the wood are here. But where is the lamb so that we can make the burnt offering?’" 
Genesis22:8 Abraham replied, ‘My son, God himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering.’ The two of them continued to walk on together." 
Genesis22:9 They came to the place that God told them about. Abraham built an altar there. He put the wood on it, ready for the fire. Then he tied his son Isaac. He lifted him up and he put him on top of the wood on the altar." 
Genesis22:10 Then Abraham took hold of the knife. He was ready to kill his son as a sacrifice. 
Genesis22:11 But then the angel of the spoke to Abraham from heaven. He said, ‘Abraham! Abraham!’ Abraham replied, ‘Here I am.’" 
Genesis22:12 The angel said, ‘Do not hurt the boy! Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you respect God and you obey him. Isaac is your only son and you agreed to offer him to me as a sacrifice. You did not try to keep him safe.’" 
Genesis22:13 Just then, Abraham looked round and he saw a male sheep behind him. Its horns were caught in some bushes. So he went and he took hold of the sheep. Abraham killed the sheep on the altar as a burnt offering to God. In that way, he killed the sheep as a sacrifice, instead of his son." 
Genesis22:14 Abraham called that place ‘The Lord provides.’ People still say today, ‘On the mountain of the Lord, he will provide what we need.’" 
Genesis22:15 The angel of the Lord called from heaven to Abraham again. 
Genesis22:16 He said, ‘This is what the Lord says. I promise that this is what I will do for you. You did not try to keep your son safe, but you offered him to me. And he was your only son." 
Genesis22:17 Because you have done this, I will bless you. I will cause your descendants to be so many that people cannot count them. They will be as many as the stars that are in the sky. They will be as many as the pieces of sand on the shore by the sea. Your descendants will win against their enemies and take their enemies' cities for themselves." 
Genesis22:18 I will bless all the nations on the earth through your descendants. I will do this because you have obeyed me.’ 
Genesis22:19 Then Abraham returned to his servants, with Isaac. Together they all left to go to Beersheba, where Abraham lived." 
Genesis22:20 Later, someone told Abraham ‘Milcah also has given birth to children. Your brother Nahor is their father.’" 
Genesis22:21 The names of the children were: Uz, the son who was born first. Then his brother Buz. Then Kemuel, who is the father of Aram." 
Genesis22:22 Then five more sons: Kesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph and Bethuel." 
Genesis22:23 Bethuel later became the father of Rebekah. Those were the eight sons of Milcah and Abraham's brother, Nahor." 
Genesis22:24 Nahor also had a slave wife. Her name was Reumah. She also gave birth to sons for Nahor. Their names were: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash and Maakah." 
Genesis23:1 Sarah lived for 127 years. 
Genesis23:2 She died in the land of Canaan, at Kiriath Arba. That place is also called Hebron. Abraham was very sad. He went to her tent and he cried very much, because she was dead." 
Genesis23:3 Then Abraham went to the Hittite people and he said to them," 
Genesis23:4 ‘I am living as a stranger among you. Please, sell me some land here, so that it belongs to me. Then I can use it to bury my wife properly.’" 
Genesis23:5 The Hittites replied," 
Genesis23:6 ‘Sir, please listen to what we say. We respect you as a great and powerful person. We have good places where we bury our people who have died. Choose the best one that you like. None of us will refuse to give you the place that you choose. Then you will be able to bury your dead wife there.’" 
Genesis23:7 Abraham stood up. He turned his face towards the ground to respect the Hittites who lived in that place. 
Genesis23:8 He said to them, ‘You have agreed to help me bury my wife here. So please speak to Zohar's son, Ephron, for me." 
Genesis23:9 Ask him to sell the cave of Machpelah to me. It belongs to Ephron. It is at the edge of his field. You will see that I pay the proper price to him. Then it will belong to me. I can bury my family there when they die.’ 
Genesis23:10 Ephron was sitting there with his people, at the city gate. He spoke so that all the Hittites could hear him." 
Genesis23:11 Ephron said, ‘No sir. Please listen to me. You may take my field and the cave. I promise in front of my people that I will give all this to you. Then you can bury your dead wife there.’" 
Genesis23:12 Abraham again turned his face towards the ground to respect the people who lived in that place. 
Genesis23:13 While they were listening, he said to Ephron, ‘Let me say this. I will pay you the price to buy the field. Please accept the money from me. Then I can bury my dead wife there.’" 
Genesis23:14 Ephron replied to Abraham," 
Genesis23:15 ‘Listen to me, sir. The land is worth 400 shekels of silver. But the price is not important, because we are friends. Now bury your dead wife there.’" 
Genesis23:16 Abraham agreed to the price that Ephron had asked for. He weighed the correct amount of silver to give to Ephron. The Hittites there knew that it had been done properly. The weight of 400 shekels agreed with the weights that people used at that time. 
Genesis23:17 In that way, Ephron's field in Machpelah, near Mamre, now belonged to Abraham. This included the field, the cave in the field and all the trees in the field as far as its edge." 
Genesis23:18 All the Hittite people who were sitting there at the city gate agreed that Abraham was the new owner. 
Genesis23:19 After this, Abraham buried his wife, Sarah, in the cave in the field of Machpelah. This field was near Mamre, in the land of Canaan. That place is also called Hebron." 
Genesis23:20 That is how Abraham bought the field and the cave that was in it from the Hittite people. He bought it as a place for his family's graves. 
Genesis24:1 Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had blessed him in every way. 
Genesis24:2 Abraham had an important servant in his house. He had authority over everything that belonged to Abraham. Abraham said to him, ‘Come here and make a promise to me. Put your hand between my legs to show that I can trust you." 
Genesis24:3 You must make a serious promise to me in front of the Lord. He is the God who rules both heaven and earth. I am living among the Canaanite people, but you must promise this to me: Do not get a Canaanite woman to be a wife for my son." 
Genesis24:4 Instead, you must go to my own country. Go to my family there to find a wife for my son Isaac.’" 
Genesis24:5 The servant asked Abraham, ‘What should I do if the woman will not agree? Maybe she will not come back with me to this land? Must I then take your son to the country that you came from?’" 
Genesis24:6 Abraham replied, ‘No! You must never take my son back there." 
Genesis24:7 The Lord, the God of heaven, took me away from there. He took me out of my father's house. He took me away from the land where my family lives. God made a serious promise to me. He said, “I will give this land to your descendants.” So he will send his angel to go in front of you. When you arrive there, you will find a woman to marry my son." 
Genesis24:8 But perhaps the woman will not agree to come back here with you. If that happens, you will not have to do what you have promised. But do not take my son back to that land.’" 
Genesis24:9 So the servant put his hand between Abraham's legs. He promised that he would do everything that Abraham had told him. 
Genesis24:10 After that, Abraham's servant left to go on his journey. He took with him ten of his master's camels. He also took many different kinds of valuable gifts that Abraham had given to him. He travelled towards Aram Naharaim. He arrived in the town of Nahor." 
Genesis24:11 Abraham's servant stopped outside the town. He made the camels go down on their knees near a well. It was the evening time, when women came to the well to get water." 
Genesis24:12 The servant prayed to God. He prayed, ‘ Lord, God of Abraham, my master, please be my guide today. Be kind to my master Abraham, as you have promised him." 
Genesis24:13 See, I am standing here near this well. The daughters of the people who live in the town will come to get water from the well." 
Genesis24:14 I will say to one of the young women, “Please let me drink some water from your pot.” If she is the right woman for your servant Isaac to marry, please may she say, “Yes, drink. Then I will give water to your camels also.” If that happens, I will know that you have been kind to my master, as you promised.’" 
Genesis24:15 The servant was still praying when Rebekah came to the well. She had her pot on her shoulder. Rebekah was the daughter of Bethuel, who was the son of Milcah. Milcah was the wife of Abraham's brother, Nahor." 
Genesis24:16 The young woman was very beautiful. She had not had sex with any man. She went down to the well. She filled her pot with water and she came back from the well again. 
Genesis24:17 Abraham's servant hurried to meet her. He said, ‘Please give me a little water to drink from your pot.’" 
Genesis24:18 She replied, ‘Yes sir, please drink.’ She quickly took the pot down from her shoulders. She held the pot with her hands and gave him a drink." 
Genesis24:19 After she had done that, she said, ‘Now I will get some water for your camels too. I will do this until they have drunk as much water as they want.’" 
Genesis24:20 So Rebekah quickly poured the water from her pot into the place where the animals drank. She ran back to the well to get more water. She did this until the camels had drunk enough. 
Genesis24:21 Abraham's servant watched the girl, but he did not say anything. He wanted to know if the Lord had helped him to find a wife for Isaac." 
Genesis24:22 The camels finished drinking. Then Abraham's servant took out a valuable gold nose ring. It weighed one half shekel. He also took out two large gold rings for Rebekah's arms. They weighed 10 shekels each. He gave them to Rebekah. 
Genesis24:23 Then he asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you? Please tell me. Is there a room in your father's house for me and my men to sleep there tonight?’" 
Genesis24:24 Rebekah replied ‘I am the daughter of Bethuel. Nahor is his father, and Milcah is his mother." 
Genesis24:25 We have plenty of grass and food for the camels. And there is a room for you to stay with us for the night.’ 
Genesis24:26 Then the servant turned his face towards the ground. He worshipped the Lord. 
Genesis24:27 He said, ‘I praise the Lord who is the God of my master, Abraham. He has been very kind to my master. He has done what he promised to do for him. The Lord has been kind to me too. He has led me here to the house of my master's family.’" 
Genesis24:28 Rebekah quickly ran back home. She told her mother and her family about what had happened. 
Genesis24:29 Rebekah had a brother. His name was Laban. 
Genesis24:30 Laban saw the nose ring and the arm rings that Rebekah was wearing. Rebekah told him what the man had said to her. So Laban went out quickly to the well to meet the servant. He found him as he stood near the well, with his camels." 
Genesis24:31 Laban said to him, ‘The Lord has blessed you! You should not continue to stand out here. Come with me. I have prepared a room for you in my house. There is also a place for your camels.’" 
Genesis24:32 So Abraham's servant went with Laban to the house. They took the luggage off the camels. They brought grass and food for the camels. They also brought water so that Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet. 
Genesis24:33 Laban's family prepared food for the servant but he said, ‘I will not eat yet. First I must tell you why I have come here.’ Laban said, ‘Please tell us.’" 
Genesis24:34 So the servant said, ‘I am Abraham's servant." 
Genesis24:35 The Lord has blessed my master very much, so that he has become very rich. The Lord has given him sheep and cows, and silver and gold. He has also given him male servants and female servants, camels and donkeys." 
Genesis24:36 Sarah is my master's wife. She has given birth to a son for him, even when she was very old. My master has given his son everything that belongs to him." 
Genesis24:37 My master told me to make a serious promise to him. He said, “I live as a stranger among the Canaanite people. But you must not look for a Canaanite woman to be a wife for my son." 
Genesis24:38 Instead, go to the land where my father lived and find a wife for my son from among my family there.”" 
Genesis24:39 So I asked my master, “What should I do if the woman will not return with me?”" 
Genesis24:40 My master said “I have lived to please the Lord. He will send his angel to go with you. You will find a wife for my son, because the Lord will help you. You will find a wife for him from among my own family." 
Genesis24:41 Go to the place where my relatives live. If they refuse to let you take her, you will be free from your promise to me.” " 
Genesis24:42 I arrived at the town well today. I prayed, “ Lord, God of my master Abraham, please help me to find a wife for my master's son. This is why I have travelled here." 
Genesis24:43 See, I am standing here near this well. If a young woman comes to get water, I will say to her, ‘Please let me drink some water from your pot.’" 
Genesis24:44 If she is the woman that the Lord has chosen for my master's son, please may she say to me, ‘Yes, drink. Then I will also get some water for your camels to drink.’ ”" 
Genesis24:45 While I was still praying quietly, Rebekah arrived at the well. She carried her pot on her shoulder. She went down to the well, and she got some water. Then I said to her “Please give me some water to drink.”" 
Genesis24:46 She quickly took her pot down from her shoulder. She said, “Drink. Then I will get water for your camels too.” So I drank. And she also gave water to the camels." 
Genesis24:47 I asked her, “Whose daughter are you?” She said, “I am the daughter of Bethuel. Nahor is his father and Milcah is his mother.” When I heard that, I put the ring in her nose. I put the rings on her arms." 
Genesis24:48 I turned my face towards the ground and I worshipped the Lord. I praised the Lord, the God of my master, Abraham. The Lord led me to the right place so that I met the granddaughter of my master's brother. The Lord has led me to her, for my master's son." 
Genesis24:49 So tell me what you will say. If you will show faithful love to my master, then please say, “Yes.” But if not, then tell me. Then I will know what to do.’" 
Genesis24:50 Laban and Bethuel replied, ‘We know that the Lord has caused all this to happen. So what we ourselves say is not important." 
Genesis24:51 Here is Rebekah. Take her with you to become the wife of your master's son. That is what the Lord has shown to be right.’ 
Genesis24:52 When Abraham's servant heard their answer, he turned his face towards the ground to thank the Lord." 
Genesis24:53 He brought his master's gifts to give them to Rebekah. They were valuable things made from gold and silver, as well as beautiful clothes. He also gave valuable things to her brother and to her mother." 
Genesis24:54 After that, the servant ate a meal. The men who were with him also ate and drank. They stayed there for the night. When they woke up the next morning, the servant said, ‘Let me leave now so that I can go back to my master.’" 
Genesis24:55 Rebekah's brother and her mother replied, ‘Please let Rebekah stay with us for a few more days. After about ten days, she can go with you.’" 
Genesis24:56 But the servant said, ‘Do not make me stay longer. The Lord has given me what I came here for. Let me leave now so that I can go back to my master.’" 
Genesis24:57 Rebekah's brother and mother said, ‘Let us call the girl. We can ask her what she wants to do.’" 
Genesis24:58 So they called Rebekah to come. They asked her, ‘Will you go with this man now?’ Rebekah said, ‘Yes, I will go.’" 
Genesis24:59 So they agreed to let their sister Rebekah go. Her family servant went with her. They left there with Abraham's servant and his men. 
Genesis24:60 As Rebekah was leaving, her brother and her mother blessed her. They said, ‘Our sister, may you become the mother of millions of descendants. May your descendants win against their enemies. May they take their enemies' cities for themselves.’" 
Genesis24:61 Then Rebekah and her female servants left there, together with Abraham's servant. They took camels to ride on. That was how Abraham's servant took Rebekah and left." 
Genesis24:62 At this time Isaac had returned from Beer Lahai Roi. He was now living in the Negev. 
Genesis24:63 It was evening time. Isaac went out to walk in the fields. He looked up and he saw some camels. They were coming towards him. 
Genesis24:64 Rebekah also looked up and she saw Isaac. She got down from her camel. 
Genesis24:65 She asked Abraham's servant, ‘I see a man in the field who is coming towards us. Who is he?’ The servant replied, ‘He is my master.’ Rebekah covered her face with a piece of cloth." 
Genesis24:66 Then the servant told Isaac everything that had happened. 
Genesis24:67 Isaac took Rebekah into the tent that his mother Sarah had lived in. Rebekah became Isaac's wife. And Isaac loved Rebekah. So Isaac was happy again, after the death of his mother." 
Genesis25:1 Abraham then married another woman. Her name was Keturah. 
Genesis25:2 She gave birth to Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak and Shuah." 
Genesis25:3 Jokshan later became the father of Sheba and Dedan. The descendants of Dedan were the Asshurites, the Letushites and the Leummites." 
Genesis25:4 Midian had sons who were called Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida and Eldaah. All these men were descendants of Abraham's wife, Keturah." 
Genesis25:5 When Abraham died, he left everything that belonged to him to Isaac." 
Genesis25:6 But while he was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of his slave wives. He sent these sons away to the land of the east. He wanted to keep them far away from Isaac." 
Genesis25:7 Abraham lived for 175 years. 
Genesis25:8 He died after a good and long life, when he was very old. He joined his ancestors who had died before him." 
Genesis25:9 His sons, Isaac and Ishmael, buried his body in the cave of Machpelah, near Mamre. The cave is in the field that belonged to Zohar's son, Ephron. He was a Hittite." 
Genesis25:10 Abraham had bought the field from the Hittites. So they buried Abraham there, where his wife Sarah had also been buried." 
Genesis25:11 After Abraham's death, God blessed Isaac, his son. Isaac was living near Beer Lahai Roi." 
Genesis25:12 This is the report about Abraham's son, Ishmael, and his family. Sarah's female servant gave birth to Ishmael. Her name was Hagar. She was from Egypt." 
Genesis25:13 These are the names of Ishmael's sons. The list starts from the firstborn son and ends with the last son. Nebaioth was the first son of Ishmael. Then there were Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam," 
Genesis25:14 Mishma, Dumah, Massa," 
Genesis25:15 Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish and Kedemah." 
Genesis25:16 These were all Ishmael's sons. Their names became the names of 12 groups of people. They went and lived in different places. Ishmael's sons ruled over the 12 groups of people. 
Genesis25:17 Ishmael lived for 137 years then he died. He joined his ancestors who had died before him. 
Genesis25:18 Ishmael's descendants lived in the lands from Havilah to Shur. These places are near Egypt, towards Asshur. They were always at war with each other." 
Genesis25:19 This is the report about Abraham's son, Isaac, and his family. Abraham became the father of Isaac." 
Genesis25:20 When Isaac was 40 years old, he married Rebekah. Rebekah was the daughter of Bethuel. Bethuel was an Aramean from Paddan Aram. She was the sister of Laban the Aramean." 
Genesis25:21 Rebekah could not have children. So Isaac prayed to the Lord for Rebekah. The Lord answered Isaac's prayer. Isaac's wife, Rebekah, became pregnant." 
Genesis25:22 The babies inside her were fighting with each other. Rebekah said, ‘Why is this happening to me?’ So Rebekah went to ask the Lord." 
Genesis25:23 The Lord said to Rebekah, ‘The two children who are in your body will become two separate nations of people. One group will be stronger than the other. The older son will become a servant to the younger son.’" 
Genesis25:24 The time came for Rebekah to give birth. There were two babies inside her. 
Genesis25:25 The first baby to come out had a red body. Hair covered the whole of his body. They called him Esau. 
Genesis25:26 After this, his brother came out. His hand was holding the back of Esau's foot. They called him Jacob. Isaac was 60 years old when Rebekah gave birth to them." 
Genesis25:27 When the boys became men, Esau was a good hunter, out in the fields. Jacob was a quiet man. He stayed near to the tents." 
Genesis25:28 Isaac liked to eat the meat from the wild animals that Esau killed. So he loved Esau. But Rebekah loved Jacob. 
Genesis25:29 One day, Jacob was cooking a meal. Esau came back from the country. He was very hungry." 
Genesis25:30 He said to Jacob ‘Quick, let me have some of that red food! I am very hungry.’ (That is why Esau was also called Edom.)" 
Genesis25:31 Jacob said, ‘You must first sell me your birthright.’" 
Genesis25:32 Esau said, ‘Look, I am so hungry that I will die. Then my birthright will not help me at all!’" 
Genesis25:33 Jacob said, ‘First, make a serious promise to me.’ So Esau promised to sell his birthright to Jacob." 
Genesis25:34 Then Jacob gave Esau some bread and the soup made from lentils. Esau ate the food and he drank. Then he got up and he left. In that way, Esau showed that he did not think that his birthright was important." 
Genesis26:1 There was another famine in the land. This had happened before, when Abraham was alive. Now Isaac went to Gerar to visit Abimelech, the king of the Philistines." 
Genesis26:2 The Lord appeared to Isaac. He said, ‘Do not go to Egypt. Instead, live in the land that I will show you." 
Genesis26:3 Stay in this land, and I will be with you. I will bless you. I will give these lands to you and to your descendants. I made a strong promise to your father Abraham. I will make that promise become true." 
Genesis26:4 I will give you many descendants. They will be as many as the stars in the sky. And I will give these lands to them. Because of your descendants, I will bless everyone on the earth." 
Genesis26:5 This will happen because Abraham obeyed me. He obeyed all my laws and rules, and he did what I told him to do.’" 
Genesis26:6 So Isaac stayed in Gerar. 
Genesis26:7 The men of Gerar asked Isaac about his wife. He told them, ‘She is my sister.’ He was afraid to say, ‘She is my wife.’ He thought that the men of Gerar might kill him because Rebekah was very beautiful." 
Genesis26:8 Isaac stayed in Gerar for a long time. One day Abimelech, the king of the Philistines, looked down from his window. He saw that Isaac was kissing Rebekah." 
Genesis26:9 Abimelech called Isaac to come to him. He said, ‘Rebekah is your wife! Why did you say, “She is my sister”?’ Isaac replied, ‘I thought that someone might kill me because of her.’" 
Genesis26:10 Abimelech said, ‘You have done a bad thing against us! One of my men might have had sex with your wife. Then we would have been guilty of a bad sin.’" 
Genesis26:11 So Abimelech told his people, ‘I will kill anyone who hurts this man, or his wife.’" 
Genesis26:12 Isaac planted crops in the land and they grew very well. They made 100 times the amount of food that he had planted. This was because the Lord blessed him. 
Genesis26:13 Isaac became rich. His riches continued to grow so that he became a very important person. 
Genesis26:14 He had many sheep, goats and cows. He also had many servants in his house. He had so many servants that the Philistines became jealous of him." 
Genesis26:15 So they took dirt from the ground and they filled up Isaac's wells. When Abraham was alive, his servants had dug these wells to get water." 
Genesis26:16 Abimelech said to Isaac ‘You have become too powerful for us. Go and live somewhere else.’ 
Genesis26:17 So Isaac moved away from that place. He put his tents in Gerar Valley and he stayed there. 
Genesis26:18 Isaac dug the dirt out of the wells that Abraham had dug. After Abraham died the Philistines had filled them with dirt. Isaac gave these wells the same names as his father had given them. 
Genesis26:19 Isaac's servants went to dig in the valley. They found another well there. It was full of fresh water. 
Genesis26:20 But the shepherds of Gerar quarrelled with Isaac's shepherds. They said, ‘The water belongs to us!’ So Isaac called that well Esek, because they quarrelled with him there." 
Genesis26:21 So Isaac's servants dug another well. But the shepherds of Gerar quarrelled about that one too. Isaac called that well Sitnah. 
Genesis26:22 Isaac moved away from Sitnah and he dug another well. No one quarrelled with him about this well. So he called it Rehoboth. He said, ‘Now the Lord has given us a place to live. We will have many good things in this land.’" 
Genesis26:23 From that place, Isaac went to Beersheba." 
Genesis26:24 That night the Lord appeared to him. The Lord said, ‘I am the God of your father Abraham. Do not be afraid, because I am with you. I will bless you. I will give you many descendants because of my servant Abraham.’" 
Genesis26:25 Isaac built an altar there and he worshipped the Lord. He put up his tent there. His servants dug a well. 
Genesis26:26 During that time, Abimelech had come from Gerar to visit Isaac. He came with Ahuzzath his officer and with Phicol the leader of his army." 
Genesis26:27 Isaac asked them, ‘Why have you come to me? You hated me and you sent me away from you.’" 
Genesis26:28 They replied, ‘We saw that the Lord is with you. So we said to one another, “There should be an agreement between us and you.” So let us promise to be friends." 
Genesis26:29 Promise that you will not hurt us. We did not hurt you. We did only good things for you. We sent you away as friends. Now the Lord is blessing you.’ 
Genesis26:30 Then Isaac made a large meal for them. And they all ate happily together. 
Genesis26:31 Early the next morning, they promised each other to be friends. Then Isaac said ‘goodbye’ to them. They left as his friends." 
Genesis26:32 The same day, Isaac's servants came to him. They told him about a well that they had dug. They said, ‘We have found water!’" 
Genesis26:33 Isaac called the well Shibah. So the town is still called Beersheba. 
Genesis26:34 When Esau was 40 years old, he married Judith. She was the daughter of Beer the Hittite. He also married Basemath. She was the daughter of Elon the Hittite." 
Genesis26:35 Esau's wives brought a lot of trouble to Isaac and Rebekah. 
Genesis27:1 Isaac became old. He could not see anything because his eyes were weak. He called for his older son, Esau. Isaac said, ‘My son.’ Esau replied, ‘Yes, I am here.’" 
Genesis27:2 Isaac said, ‘I am an old man. I may die very soon." 
Genesis27:3 Get your bow and your arrows. Go out into the country and kill some wild animals for me. 
Genesis27:4 Then prepare the food that I like to eat. Bring it to me so that I can eat it. Then I will give you my blessing before I die.’ 
Genesis27:5 Rebekah had listened to what Isaac said to Esau. She waited until Esau went out to the country to kill an animal and bring back the meat. 
Genesis27:6 Then she said to Jacob, ‘I heard your father say this to Esau:" 
Genesis27:7 “Bring me some meat and prepare the food that I like to eat. When I have eaten it, I will give you my blessing. I will bless you in front of the Lord before I die.” " 
Genesis27:8 Now my son, listen carefully and do as I tell you." 
Genesis27:9 Go out to the farm animals and bring two goats to me. They must be very good young goats. Then I will prepare some food that your father likes to eat. I will cook the meat in the way that he likes. 
Genesis27:10 Then take the food to your father. When he has eaten it, he will give you his blessing before he dies.’" 
Genesis27:11 Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, ‘That will be difficult. Hair covers my brother's skin. But my skin does not have any hair." 
Genesis27:12 My father may touch me. Then he will know that I am deceiving him. He will not bless me. He will curse me instead.’ 
Genesis27:13 Jacob's mother said, ‘My son, the curse will happen to me, not to you. Do what I say. Go and get the goats for me.’" 
Genesis27:14 Jacob went and he got the goats. He brought them to his mother. She prepared some food in the way that Isaac liked to eat it. 
Genesis27:15 Then Rebekah took some of Esau's best clothes that were in the house. She put them on Jacob, her younger son." 
Genesis27:16 She covered Jacob's hands with the skin from the goats. She also covered the part of his neck that had no hair. 
Genesis27:17 Then she gave Jacob the food that Isaac liked to eat. And she gave him some bread that she had made. 
Genesis27:18 Jacob went to his father. He said, ‘My father.’ Isaac replied ‘Yes, my son. Who are you, Esau or Jacob?’" 
Genesis27:19 Jacob said, ‘I am Esau. I am your firstborn son. I have done as you told me. Please sit up. Eat some of the meat from the wild animal that I killed. Then you can bless me.’" 
Genesis27:20 Isaac asked his son, ‘My son, how did you find it so quickly?’ Jacob replied, ‘The Lord your God helped me to find it.’" 
Genesis27:21 Isaac said to Jacob, ‘Come near to me so that I can touch you, my son. Then I will know if you really are my son Esau.’" 
Genesis27:22 Jacob went near to his father. Isaac touched him and he said, ‘The voice is Jacob's voice. But the hands are Esau's hands.’" 
Genesis27:23 Isaac did not know that it was Jacob because he could feel the hair on Jacob's hands. They felt like Esau's hands. So Isaac blessed Jacob. 
Genesis27:24 Isaac asked again, ‘Are you really my son Esau?’ Jacob replied, ‘I am.’" 
Genesis27:25 Isaac said, ‘My son, bring me some of your meat. I will eat it. Then I will bless you.’ So Jacob brought the food to him and Isaac ate it. Jacob also brought some wine and Isaac drank it." 
Genesis27:26 Then Isaac said, ‘Come here my son and kiss me.’" 
Genesis27:27 So Jacob went to Isaac and kissed him. Isaac smelled the clothes that Jacob wore. Then Isaac blessed him. He said, ‘The smell of my son is like the smell of a field. It is like a field that the Lord has blessed." 
Genesis27:28 May God give you rain for your crops, so that they grow well in the good ground. Then you will have plenty of grain for food. And you will have grapes to make wine." 
Genesis27:29 May the people of many nations serve you. May they respect you as their master. You will rule over your brothers. Yes, your mother's sons will bend down to respect you. May God curse anyone who curses you. And may he bless anyone who blesses you.’" 
Genesis27:30 When Isaac had blessed Jacob, Jacob left him. At that moment, Esau came back from the country where he had killed an animal." 
Genesis27:31 He prepared nice food that Isaac liked to eat. Then he took the food to his father, Isaac. Esau said, ‘My father, please sit up. Eat some of the meat from the wild animal that I have killed. Then you can bless me.’" 
Genesis27:32 His father Isaac asked, ‘Who are you?’ Esau replied, ‘I am your firstborn son, Esau.’" 
Genesis27:33 Isaac's body shook very strongly. He asked, ‘Who was it who killed an animal and brought the meat to me? Just before you came, I ate all of it. And then I blessed him. And God will certainly bless him!’" 
Genesis27:34 Esau heard what his father had said and he cried aloud. He was very upset. He said, ‘My father, please bless me too!’" 
Genesis27:35 But Isaac said, ‘Your brother came to me. He deceived me and he took your blessing away from you.’" 
Genesis27:36 Esau said, ‘Yes, “Jacob” is the right name for him. He has cheated me twice. First he took my birthright. Now he has taken my blessing as well!’ Then Esau said to his father, ‘You must surely still have some blessing for me.’" 
Genesis27:37 Isaac said to Esau, ‘I have made Jacob master over you. All his relatives will now be his servants. I have given him crops and wine. So what can I still do to bless you, my son?’" 
Genesis27:38 Esau said, ‘My father, you surely still have one blessing for me. Please bless me too!’ Then Esau wept loudly." 
Genesis27:39 Isaac replied, ‘You will not live in a place where the ground gives good food. You will not have rain to make your crops grow." 
Genesis27:40 You will have to fight to get the things that you need. You will serve your brother as your master. But when you choose to turn against him, you will become free from his power over you.’" 
Genesis27:41 Because Isaac had blessed Jacob, Esau hated his younger brother Jacob. Esau said quietly, ‘My father will die soon. When we have buried his body, I will kill my brother.’" 
Genesis27:42 Someone told Rebekah what her older son, Esau, had said. So she told her younger son, Jacob, to come to her. She said to him, ‘Your brother Esau wants to kill you, because of what you did to him." 
Genesis27:43 Now my son, do what I tell you. Quickly go away from here. Go to my brother Laban who lives in Haran." 
Genesis27:44 Live with him for a time. Stay there until your brother is no longer angry with you. 
Genesis27:45 He may forget what you did to him. Then I will send a message to you, so that you can come back from Haran. I do not want to lose both of my sons in one day.’" 
Genesis27:46 Then Rebekah said to Isaac, ‘I do not like living in the same place as Esau's wives. It makes me very upset, because they are Hittites. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women who live in this land, I will be very sad. I would rather die!’" 
Genesis28:1 Isaac called Jacob to come to him. He blessed Jacob. He told him, ‘Do not marry a Canaanite woman." 
Genesis28:2 Go now to Paddan Aram. Go to the house of your mother's father, Bethuel. Find a wife for yourself from there. Choose one of the daughters of your mother's brother, Laban." 
Genesis28:3 I pray that God Almighty will bless you. I pray that he will give you many children, so that your descendants become a large nation of people." 
Genesis28:4 May God bless you and your descendants with the good things that he promised to Abraham. The land that he promised to give to Abraham will then belong to you. That is the land where you now live as a foreign person.’ 
Genesis28:5 Isaac sent Jacob away and Jacob went to Paddan Aram. He went to stay with Laban, who was the son of Bethuel, the Aramean. Laban was the brother of Rebekah. Rebekah was the mother of Jacob and Esau." 
Genesis28:6 Esau learned that Isaac had blessed Jacob and he had sent him away to Paddan Aram. Isaac had told Jacob to find a woman from Paddan Aram to be his wife. Isaac had said to Jacob, ‘Do not marry a Canaanite woman.’" 
Genesis28:7 Esau realized that Jacob had obeyed his father and mother, and that he had gone to Paddan Aram." 
Genesis28:8 So Esau then understood that his father, Isaac, did not like the Canaanite women." 
Genesis28:9 So Esau went to visit Abraham's son, Ishmael. He married Ishmael's daughter, Mahalath, the sister of Nebaioth. When Esau married Mahalath, he kept his Canaanite wives as well." 
Genesis28:10 Jacob left Beersheba and he went towards Haran. 
Genesis28:11 He came to a place where he stayed for the night, because the sun had gone down. He took a stone and he put it under his head. Then he lay down and he went to sleep." 
Genesis28:12 He had a dream. In the dream, he saw some steps. The steps went up from the earth into heaven. God's angels were going up and down the steps." 
Genesis28:13 The Lord stood at the top of the steps. He said, ‘I am the Lord. I am the God of your grandfather Abraham, and the God of Isaac, your father. I will give the land that you are lying on to you and to your descendants." 
Genesis28:14 Your descendants will be as many as the dust of the earth. They will go to the west and to the east. They will go to the north and to the south. Through you and your descendants I will bless all the families of people on the earth. 
Genesis28:15 I am with you. I will take care of you wherever you go. I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you. I will do what I have promised to do for you.’ 
Genesis28:16 Jacob woke from his sleep. He thought, ‘The Lord is in this place and I did not know it.’" 
Genesis28:17 Jacob was afraid. He said, ‘How great this place is! This must be the house of God. This is the gate of heaven.’" 
Genesis28:18 Early in the morning Jacob got up. He took the stone that he had put under his head. He made it stand up straight in the ground. He poured olive oil over it. 
Genesis28:19 Jacob called that place Bethel. Before that, the name of the place had been Luz." 
Genesis28:20 Then Jacob made a promise to God. He said, ‘While I am on this journey, I want God to be with me. I want him to protect me. I want him to give me food to eat and clothes to wear." 
Genesis28:21 Then I want to return to my father's house. If God keeps me safe, then the Lord will be my God." 
Genesis28:22 This special stone that I have put here will be God's house. And from everything that God gives me, I will give him one tenth.’" 
Genesis29:1 Jacob continued on his journey. He came to the land where the people from the east live. 
Genesis29:2 He saw a well in a field. Three groups of sheep lay near it. Shepherds were taking water from the well to give to their sheep. There was a large stone that covered the top of the well. 
Genesis29:3 The shepherds waited until all the sheep were there together. Then they removed the stone from the top of the well. They gave their sheep water to drink, and then they put the stone back on the top of the well." 
Genesis29:4 Jacob asked the shepherds, ‘My brothers, where is your home?’ The shepherds replied, ‘We are from Haran.’" 
Genesis29:5 Jacob said, ‘Do you know Laban? He is Nahor's grandson.’ The shepherds replied, ‘Yes, we know him.’" 
Genesis29:6 Jacob asked them, ‘Is he in good health?’ They replied, ‘Yes, he is. Look. Here comes his daughter now, with her father's sheep. Her name is Rachel.’" 
Genesis29:7 Jacob said to them, ‘The sun is still high in the sky. It is not yet the right time to bring all the sheep together. So give them some water now. Then they can go back to the fields and eat more grass.’" 
Genesis29:8 The shepherds said, ‘We cannot do that. We have to wait until all the sheep are here together. Then we can remove the big stone from the well and we can give water to the sheep.’" 
Genesis29:9 While Jacob was speaking to the shepherds, Rachel arrived there. She brought her father's sheep with her. She was the one who took care of them." 
Genesis29:10 Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his uncle Laban. He saw that she had Laban's sheep with her. So Jacob went to the well and he removed the big stone. He gave water to Laban's sheep." 
Genesis29:11 Then Jacob kissed Rachel and he began to weep aloud. 
Genesis29:12 Jacob told Rachel that he was a relative of her father, Laban. He told her that he was Rebekah's son. So Rachel ran to tell her father." 
Genesis29:13 When Laban had heard what Rachel said, he went quickly to meet Jacob, his sister's son. He put his arms round Jacob and he kissed him. Laban brought Jacob to his home. Jacob told Laban all his news." 
Genesis29:14 Then Laban said, ‘Yes, you really are my own relative.’ So Jacob stayed with Laban for a month." 
Genesis29:15 Then Laban said to Jacob, ‘You should not work for me for nothing because you are my relative. Tell me what I should pay you.’" 
Genesis29:16 Laban had two daughters. The name of the older daughter was Leah. The name of the younger daughter was Rachel. 
Genesis29:17 Leah had weak eyes. But Rachel was very beautiful. 
Genesis29:18 Jacob loved Rachel. He said to Laban, ‘I will work for you for seven years. In return, I want to marry your younger daughter, Rachel.’" 
Genesis29:19 Laban said, ‘It is better for me to give her to you as your wife than to give her to another man. Stay here with me.’" 
Genesis29:20 Jacob worked for seven years to have Rachel as his wife. But the years passed very quickly because Jacob loved Rachel very much. Seven years seemed like only a few days to Jacob. 
Genesis29:21 So finally, Jacob said to Laban, ‘Give me my wife. My time to work for you has finished and I want to marry her.’" 
Genesis29:22 Laban called all the people in that place to come together. He prepared a big meal for them to eat. 
Genesis29:23 When evening came, Laban brought his daughter Leah to Jacob. Jacob slept that night with her as his wife." 
Genesis29:24 Laban gave his female servant to Leah to be her servant. Her name was Zilpah. 
Genesis29:25 When morning came, Jacob saw that he had slept with Leah! He said to Laban, ‘You have done a very bad thing to me! I worked for you to have Rachel as my wife. Why have you deceived me?’" 
Genesis29:26 Laban replied, ‘In our land, we do not give the younger daughter to a man first. We let the older daughter marry first." 
Genesis29:27 So finish this week of Leah's marriage. Then we will give you Rachel to marry too. But you must work for another seven years.’ 
Genesis29:28 So Jacob did what Laban said. He finished the marriage week with Leah. Then Laban gave Rachel to Jacob to be his wife. 
Genesis29:29 Laban gave his female servant Bilhah to Rachel to become her servant. 
Genesis29:30 Jacob also slept with Rachel. He loved Rachel more than he loved Leah. He worked for Laban for seven more years. 
Genesis29:31 The Lord saw that Jacob did not love Leah. So he let her become pregnant. But Rachel did not give birth to any children. 
Genesis29:32 So Leah became pregnant and she gave birth to a son. She called him Reuben. She said, ‘It is because the Lord has seen how sad I am. My husband will love me now because I have given birth to a son.’" 
Genesis29:33 Leah became pregnant again. She gave birth to another son. She said, ‘The Lord has given me another son. He knows that my husband does not love me.’ She called this son Simeon." 
Genesis29:34 Leah became pregnant again. Later, she gave birth to another son. She said, ‘Now I have given my husband three sons, so he will want to stay near to me.’ She called this son Levi." 
Genesis29:35 Leah became pregnant again. She gave birth to another son. She said, ‘This time I will praise the Lord.’ She called this son Judah. Then she stopped giving birth to children." 
Genesis30:1 Rachel now knew that she could not give birth to any children for Jacob. So she became jealous of her sister, Leah. Rachel said to Jacob, ‘Please give me children. If not, I will die.’" 
Genesis30:2 Jacob became angry with Rachel. He said, ‘Am I God? God has not allowed you to give birth to children!’" 
Genesis30:3 Rachel said, ‘Take my servant Bilhah and have sex with her. Then she can have children for me. Through her, I can have a family.’" 
Genesis30:4 So Rachel gave her servant Bilhah to Jacob to become his wife. 
Genesis30:5 Bilhah became pregnant and she gave birth to a son for Jacob. 
Genesis30:6 Rachel said, ‘God has shown that I am right. He has listened to me and he has given a son to me.’ Because of this, she called the boy Dan." 
Genesis30:7 Rachel's servant Bilhah became pregnant again. She gave birth to another son for Jacob. 
Genesis30:8 Rachel said, ‘I have fought with my sister and I have won.’ She called this son Naphtali." 
Genesis30:9 Leah saw that she had stopped having children. So she gave her servant Zilpah to Jacob as a wife. 
Genesis30:10 Zilpah became pregnant and she gave birth to a son for Jacob. 
Genesis30:11 Leah said, ‘This is very good for me.’ She called the boy Gad." 
Genesis30:12 Leah's servant Zilpah gave Jacob another son. 
Genesis30:13 Leah said, ‘I am very happy! Women will call me happy.’ So she called this boy Asher." 
Genesis30:14 It was the time for the harvest of wheat. Reuben went out and he found some mandrake plants in a field. He took them to his mother, Leah. Rachel said to Leah, ‘Please give me some of the mandrakes that your son has given to you.’" 
Genesis30:15 But Leah said to Rachel, ‘You took my husband from me. That was bad enough! Now you want to take my son's mandrakes too!’ Rachel said, ‘If you give me some of your son's mandrakes, Jacob can sleep with you tonight.’" 
Genesis30:16 In the evening, Jacob came in from the fields. Leah went out to meet him. She said, ‘You must sleep with me tonight. I have paid for you with my son's mandrakes.’ So Jacob slept with Leah that night." 
Genesis30:17 God listened to Leah and she became pregnant again. She gave birth to a fifth son for Jacob. 
Genesis30:18 Leah said, ‘God has helped me because I gave my servant to Jacob as a wife.’ She called her son Issachar." 
Genesis30:19 Leah became pregnant again and she gave Jacob a sixth son. 
Genesis30:20 Leah said, ‘God has given me this valuable gift. Now my husband will respect me because I have given him six sons.’ She called this son Zebulun." 
Genesis30:21 After some time, Leah became pregnant again. She gave birth to a daughter. She called her daughter Dinah." 
Genesis30:22 Then God decided to help Rachel. He answered her prayer so that she became pregnant. 
Genesis30:23 She gave birth to a son. Rachel said, ‘I am not ashamed any longer, because God has given me a son.’" 
Genesis30:24 Rachel called her son Joseph. She said, ‘I pray that the Lord will give me another son.’" 
Genesis30:25 After Rachel had given birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, ‘Let me go away now. I want to go home to my own land." 
Genesis30:26 Let me take my wives and my children with me. I have worked for you so that I could have them. You must let me leave, because you know how much I have worked for you.’" 
Genesis30:27 Laban said to Jacob, ‘If you are pleased with me then please stay here. I know that the Lord has blessed me because you are here with me. I have used magic to know that.’" 
Genesis30:28 Laban also said to Jacob, ‘Tell me how much I should pay you for your work. I will pay whatever you want.’" 
Genesis30:29 Jacob replied, ‘You know how much I have worked for you. You can see that you have many more animals now, because I have taken care of them." 
Genesis30:30 Before I came, you had only a few sheep and goats. Now you have many more animals. Wherever I have worked for you, the Lord has blessed you. But now I want to work to help my own family.’" 
Genesis30:31 Laban asked, ‘What must I give to you?’ Jacob replied, ‘You do not need to give me anything. But please do one thing for me. Then I will continue to take care of your animals. I will keep them safe." 
Genesis30:32 Let me go among all your animals today. I will remove any animal that has a mark on it or that has more than one colour on its skin. I will also take any black lambs. That is what you will give me for my work. 
Genesis30:33 In the future, this will show that I am honest. You can check on my animals whenever you want to. I will only have sheep or goats that have marks on their skin, or black lambs. If I have any other animals, you may call me a robber.’" 
Genesis30:34 Laban said, ‘I agree to this. It will be as you have said.’" 
Genesis30:35 On that same day, Laban went to his animals. He quickly removed all the goats that had marks, both male and female goats. He also removed all the black lambs. He gave these animals to his sons to take care of them." 
Genesis30:36 He sent those animals a long way away. It would take Jacob three days to reach them. While this was happening, Jacob was taking care of Laban's other animals." 
Genesis30:37 Jacob took branches that he had cut from trees. He took them from poplar trees, almond trees and plane trees. He cut white lines on the branches. He removed the outside part of the wood to show white lines." 
Genesis30:38 Then he put the branches in the place where there were big bowls of water for the animals to drink. They were in front of the animals when they came to drink. That was where the animals had sex together. 
Genesis30:39 After they had sex in front of the branches, they gave birth to babies that had skin with marks on it." 
Genesis30:40 Jacob kept these lambs separate from Laban's animals. He took the rest of Laban's female animals to join with the animals that had marks or were black. In that way he got more animals that would belong to him. He kept them separate from Laban's animals. 
Genesis30:41 Jacob waited until the stronger female animals were ready to have sex. Then he put the special branches in front of them, near the big bowls of water. Then these animals would see the branches when they had sex together." 
Genesis30:42 But if the female animals were weak, he did not put the branches in front of them when they had sex. As a result, the weak animals would belong to Laban, but Jacob would keep the strong animals." 
Genesis30:43 In this way, Jacob became very rich. Many sheep and goats belonged to him. He also had female servants and male servants, camels and donkeys." 
Genesis31:1 Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining about him. They were saying, ‘Jacob has taken everything that belonged to our father. He has taken things from our father and he has become rich himself.’" 
Genesis31:2 And Jacob could see that Laban was not as nice to him as he had been before. 
Genesis31:3 Then the Lord said to Jacob, ‘Go back to the land where your father and grandfather lived. Go back to your relatives. I will be with you there.’" 
Genesis31:4 Jacob sent a message to Rachel and Leah. He said that they must come to the field where he was taking care of his animals. 
Genesis31:5 Jacob said to them, ‘I see that your father is not as nice to me now as he was before. But the God of my father has been with me." 
Genesis31:6 You know that I have worked very hard for your father with all my strength. 
Genesis31:7 But your father has cheated me many times. He has changed what I receive for my work at least ten times. But God has protected me from him. 
Genesis31:8 Sometimes Laban said, “I will pay you with the animals that have different colours on their skin.” If he said that, all the animals gave birth to babies with different colours on their skin. Sometimes he said, “I will pay you with the animals that have marks on them.” Then all the animals gave birth to babies with marks on them." 
Genesis31:9 In this way God took away your father's animals, and he gave them to me." 
Genesis31:10 One night I had a dream. It was at the time when the animals were becoming pregnant. In the dream, I saw that the male goats had marks and different colours on their skin." 
Genesis31:11 The angel of God spoke to me in the dream. He said, “Jacob.” I replied, “Yes, here I am.”" 
Genesis31:12 The angel said, “Look carefully. See all the male goats that are having sex with the female goats. They all have marks and different colours on their skin. I am helping you because I have seen the bad things that Laban has done to you." 
Genesis31:13 I am the God who appeared to you at Bethel. That is where you poured olive oil on the special stone and you made a promise to me. Now I am telling you to leave this land. Go back to the land where you were born.” ’ 
Genesis31:14 Rachel and Leah replied, ‘Our father will not give us anything more when he dies." 
Genesis31:15 He now thinks of us like foreigners. You worked hard for him so that we could become your wives. He has cheated us as well as you! 
Genesis31:16 So everything that God has taken away from our father really belongs to us and to our children. So you must do everything that God has told you to do.’ 
Genesis31:17 So Jacob put his children and wives on his camels. 
Genesis31:18 He put together all his animals and everything that he had received in Paddan Aram. He took them with him to go to the land of Canaan. He left to go back to his father Isaac. 
Genesis31:19 Before they left, Laban had gone to cut the wool from his sheep. While he was away from the house, Rachel took the idols that Laban worshipped in his house." 
Genesis31:20 Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean. He did not tell Laban that he was going away. 
Genesis31:21 Jacob went away quickly and he took all his things. He went across the Euphrates River. He went towards the hill country of Gilead. 
Genesis31:22 After three days, someone told Laban that Jacob had gone away." 
Genesis31:23 So Laban took his relatives with him and he followed Jacob. After seven days, he found him in the hill country of Gilead." 
Genesis31:24 Then God appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream at night. God warned him, ‘Be careful what you say to Jacob. Do not say anything good or bad to him.’" 
Genesis31:25 Jacob had put up his tent in the hill country of Gilead. That is where Laban found him. So Laban and his relatives also put up their tents in that place. 
Genesis31:26 Laban said to Jacob, ‘Why did you do that? You have deceived me. You have taken my daughters away as if you had caught them in a war." 
Genesis31:27 Why did you go away secretly? Yes, you have deceived me. You should have told me that you were leaving. Then I would have prepared a big meal. We would have been happy together, with songs and music with harps and tambourines." 
Genesis31:28 But you did not even let me say “goodbye” to my daughters or my grandchildren. What you have done is not right. 
Genesis31:29 I have the power to hurt you. But last night the God of your father appeared to me. He told me, “Be careful what you say to Jacob. Do not say anything good or bad to him.”" 
Genesis31:30 I know that you want very much to return to your father's house. That is why you have left my home. But why did you take my idols?’ 
Genesis31:31 Jacob replied, ‘I left secretly because I was afraid. I thought that you might fight me to take your daughters away from me." 
Genesis31:32 But if you find your idols with anyone here, that person must die. While our relatives watch, you may look for anything that belongs to you. If you find anything then take it.’ Jacob did not know that Rachel had taken her father's idols." 
Genesis31:33 So Laban went into Jacob's tent. Then he went into Leah's tent. He also went into the female servants' tent. But he did not find the idols. When he left Leah's tent, he went into Rachel's tent." 
Genesis31:34 Rachel had taken the idols and she had put them inside her camel's seat. Now she was sitting on them. Laban looked everywhere in Rachel's tent but he did not find the idols. 
Genesis31:35 Rachel said to him, ‘Do not be angry with me, sir. I cannot stand up in front of you. It is the time of my monthly blood loss.’ So Laban looked everywhere for the idols, but he did not find them." 
Genesis31:36 Then Jacob became angry and he quarrelled with Laban. He asked Laban, ‘What have I done wrong? What sin have I done against you so that you had to chase me?" 
Genesis31:37 Now you have looked through everything that I have. Did you find anything that belongs to you? If you have found anything, put it here. Then your relatives and my relatives can see it. Our relatives can decide which of us is right, you or me!" 
Genesis31:38 I have worked for you for 20 years. Your female sheep and goats have all safely given birth to young ones. I have not taken any of your male animals as food for myself. 
Genesis31:39 If wild animals attacked your sheep or goats, I did not show them to you. I myself paid you for them. If any animal was lost, in the day or at night, you said that I must pay you for it." 
Genesis31:40 I worked for you in the strong heat of the sun in the day. I had pain from the cold at night. I could not always sleep. 
Genesis31:41 It was like this for 20 years as I worked like a slave for you. I worked for 14 years to pay you for your two daughters. Then I worked for six years for your sheep and goats. You changed what you paid me at least ten times! 
Genesis31:42 The God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac was with me. If he had not helped me, then you would have sent me away with nothing. But God has seen what you have done to hurt me. He has seen how hard I have worked for you. So last night he told you that you had done wrong.’" 
Genesis31:43 Laban replied to Jacob, ‘These women are my daughters. Their children are my grandchildren. The animals are my animals. Everything that you have with you here belongs to me. But there is nothing that I can do today about my daughters or about their children." 
Genesis31:44 So we should be friends. Let us make an agreement together. It will show that we have promised to be friends.’ 
Genesis31:45 So Jacob took a large stone and made it stand up in the ground. 
Genesis31:46 He said to his relatives, ‘Go and get some stones.’ So they brought some stones and they made a heap with them. Then they all ate a meal together, near the heap of stones." 
Genesis31:47 Laban called that place Jegar Sahadutha. Jacob called it Galeed. 
Genesis31:48 Laban said, ‘These stones show that we have made an agreement today.’ That is why the place was called Galeed." 
Genesis31:49 The place was also called Mizpah. That was because Laban said, ‘May the Lord watch you and me when we are far away from each other." 
Genesis31:50 Remember that God is watching you. If you do wrong to my daughters, God will see it. And if you marry any other women, remember this. God is watching you. Even if no one else sees you, God will know.’" 
Genesis31:51 Laban also said, ‘I have put this special stone here, and the heap of stones near it. They stand here between your land and my land." 
Genesis31:52 I will not go past the stones to your side to hurt you. And you must not go past them to my side to hurt me. 
Genesis31:53 Let the God of Abraham, and the god of Nahor and their ancestors decide if one of us is guilty.’ So Jacob made a promise in the name of the God that his father Isaac worshipped." 
Genesis31:54 He offered a sacrifice to God there in the hill country. He asked his relatives to eat a meal with him. After they had eaten, they stayed the night there." 
Genesis31:55 Early the next morning, Laban got up to return home. He kissed his grandchildren and his daughters. He said ‘goodbye’ and he blessed them. Then he left and he went back to his home." 
Genesis32:1 Jacob continued on his journey to his father's house. On his way the angels of God met him. 
Genesis32:2 When Jacob saw them, he said, ‘This is where God's army has put up their tents!’ So he called that place Mahanaim." 
Genesis32:3 Jacob sent men with a message to his brother Esau. Esau was living in the land of Seir, also called Edom." 
Genesis32:4 Jacob told his men, ‘This is what you must say to my master Esau: Your servant Jacob says, “I have stayed with Laban until now." 
Genesis32:5 I have cows, donkeys, sheep and goats. I have male and female servants. Now I am sending this message to your, my master, so that you will be pleased with me.” ’" 
Genesis32:6 When the men returned, they said to Jacob, ‘We went to your brother Esau. Now he is coming to meet you and he has 400 men with him.’" 
Genesis32:7 Jacob was very frightened and upset when he heard this. So he divided his people into two separate groups. He also divided his animals. 
Genesis32:8 He thought, ‘If Esau attacks one group, the other group may run away safely.’" 
Genesis32:9 Then Jacob prayed, ‘God of my grandfather Abraham and my father Isaac, Lord, you said to me, “Go back to your country and to your relatives. I will do good things for you there.”" 
Genesis32:10 You have always loved me. You have continued to be kind to me. I do not deserve this. When I went across the Jordan River to go to Haran, I only had my stick. But now I can make two big groups of people and animals." 
Genesis32:11 I pray that you will save me from the power of my brother Esau. I am afraid that he will come and attack me. He may also attack my family. 
Genesis32:12 But you have said, “I will give you many good things. I will give you so many descendants that nobody will be able to count them. They will be as many as the pieces of sand on the shore of the sea.” ’" 
Genesis32:13 Jacob stayed in that place for the night. He chose some of his animals as a gift for Esau. 
Genesis32:14 He chose 200 female goats, 20 male goats, 200 female sheep and 20 male sheep." 
Genesis32:15 He also chose 30 female camels, with their young ones, 40 cows and ten bulls, 20 female donkeys and ten male donkeys." 
Genesis32:16 He told his servants to take care of them. He divided each group of animals from the other groups. Jacob said to his servants, ‘Go in front of me. Keep some space between each group of animals.’" 
Genesis32:17 He told the servant who led the first group of animals, ‘When my brother Esau meets you, he may ask, “Who do you belong to? Where are you going? Who do all these animals belong to?”" 
Genesis32:18 Then you must say, “They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a gift that he has sent to my lord Esau. Jacob himself is coming behind us.” ’" 
Genesis32:19 Jacob also told the servants who led the other groups of animals, as well as the servants who followed behind them, ‘You are to say the same thing to Esau, when you meet him." 
Genesis32:20 You must also say, “Your servant Jacob is coming behind us.” ’ Jacob thought, ‘Esau will be happy to receive my gifts. He will no longer be angry with me. Then, when I meet him, he will not hurt me.’" 
Genesis32:21 So Jacob sent the men with his gifts to go to meet Esau. But he himself stayed in that place for the night. 
Genesis32:22 During the night Jacob took with him his two wives, his two female servants and his eleven sons. They went across the Jabbok River where the water was not deep." 
Genesis32:23 After Jacob had sent his family across, he also sent all his things across the stream." 
Genesis32:24 Then Jacob was alone. A man came and fought with him for a long time. They continued the fight until dawn. 
Genesis32:25 The man saw that he was not winning the fight against Jacob. So he hit Jacob's hip while they were fighting. As a result, Jacob's hip moved out of its proper place." 
Genesis32:26 Then the man said to Jacob, ‘Now let me go because dawn has come.’ But Jacob said, ‘I will not let you go unless you bless me.’" 
Genesis32:27 The man asked Jacob, ‘What is your name?’ Jacob replied, ‘My name is Jacob.’" 
Genesis32:28 Then the man said, ‘Your name will not be Jacob any longer. Your name will now be Israel, because you have fought with God and you have fought with men. And you have won!’" 
Genesis32:29 Then Jacob said, ‘Please tell me your name.’ But the man said, ‘Why do you want to know my name?’ Then he blessed Jacob in that place." 
Genesis32:30 So Jacob called the place Peniel. He said, ‘I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive!’" 
Genesis32:31 The sun rose in the sky as Jacob passed through Peniel. He could not walk properly because of his hip. 
Genesis32:32 Even today, Israelites do not eat the meat of an animal where it joins to the hip. This is because God touched Jacob's hip." 
Genesis33:1 Jacob looked up. He saw Esau with 400 men coming towards him. So he divided his children into separate groups. Some went with Leah, some went with Rachel, and some went with his two female servants." 
Genesis33:2 He put his female servants and their children at the front of the group. Then came Leah and her children. But Jacob put his son Joseph with Rachel at the back of the group. 
Genesis33:3 Jacob himself went in front of these groups. He bent down to the ground to respect Esau. He did this seven times as he came nearer to his brother. 
Genesis33:4 But Esau ran to meet Jacob and he hugged him. He put his arms around Jacob's neck and he kissed him. They both wept. 
Genesis33:5 Then Esau looked up. He saw the women and children. He asked, ‘Who are these people with you?’ Jacob replied, ‘They are the children that God has given to me, your servant.’" 
Genesis33:6 Then the female servants and their children came to Esau. As they came near to him, they bent down to the ground." 
Genesis33:7 Then Leah and her children came and they bent down in front of Esau. Last of all, Joseph and Rachel came to Esau and they also bent down to the ground." 
Genesis33:8 Esau asked Jacob, ‘Why did you send all those animals in front of you?’ Jacob replied, ‘So that you would be happy to see me, my master.’" 
Genesis33:9 Then Esau said, ‘Keep your animals for yourself, my brother. I have enough animals of my own.’" 
Genesis33:10 But Jacob replied, ‘No! If you are happy to see me, please accept these animals as a gift from me. When you met me and I saw your face, it was as if I saw the face of God himself. I am happy that you have accepted me as a friend." 
Genesis33:11 So please accept the gift that I brought to you. God has been very kind to me so that I have everything that I need.’ So Esau accepted the gifts because Jacob would not agree to keep them for himself. 
Genesis33:12 Then Esau said, ‘Let us continue to go on our way. I will travel with you.’" 
Genesis33:13 But Jacob said, ‘My master, the children are not very strong. Many of the animals have young babies. If we go too far in one day, then all the animals will die." 
Genesis33:14 So you should go in front of me, my master. I will travel slowly with the animals and children, so that they are comfortable. Then, my master, I will come to you in Seir.’" 
Genesis33:15 Esau said, ‘Then let me leave some of my men to travel with you.’ But Jacob replied, ‘You do not need to do that. All I want is for you to be pleased with me.’" 
Genesis33:16 So Esau began his journey back to Seir that same day. 
Genesis33:17 But Jacob went to Succoth instead. He built a house for himself, and he made huts for his animals. That is why the place is called Succoth." 
Genesis33:18 After Jacob left Paddan Aram, he travelled safely to Canaan. He put up his tents there, near the city of Shechem." 
Genesis33:19 He bought the piece of ground where he had put up his tents. He paid 100 pieces of silver to Hamor's sons to buy the land. Hamor was the father of Shechem. 
Genesis33:20 Jacob built an altar in that place. He called the altar ‘The God of Israel is the true God’. 
Genesis34:1 Dinah was the daughter of Leah and Jacob. One day she went out to visit the young women who lived near Shechem city. 
Genesis34:2 Hamor the Hivite ruled that region. When his son, Shechem, saw Dinah, he took hold of her. Dinah could not get free and Shechem had sex with her." 
Genesis34:3 He liked Dinah very much. He loved her. He spoke kind words to her so that she would love him too. 
Genesis34:4 Shechem said to his father, Hamor, ‘I want to marry this girl. Please get her for me.’" 
Genesis34:5 Jacob heard that Shechem had had sex with his daughter. At that time, Jacob's sons were taking care of his animals in the fields. So he did not do anything until his sons came home." 
Genesis34:6 Shechem's father, Hamor, went to talk to Jacob about Dinah." 
Genesis34:7 Jacob's sons now heard what had happened and they came home from the fields. They were very angry because Shechem had done such a bad thing. When he caught Dinah and had sex with her, he had brought shame on Jacob's family. It was a wicked thing that nobody should ever do." 
Genesis34:8 Hamor said to Jacob and his sons, ‘My son Shechem loves your daughter. Please give her to him so that she can be his wife." 
Genesis34:9 There should be more marriages between your people and our people. Our men should marry your daughters and our daughters should become wives for your men. 
Genesis34:10 Then you can live among us. You can live anywhere you want to in this land. You should live here, buy and sell things. Buy land and houses here.’" 
Genesis34:11 Then Shechem said to Dinah's father and brothers, ‘I would like to make you happy. So ask me for whatever you want and I will give it to you." 
Genesis34:12 I will pay you the price that you ask for Dinah to become my wife. I will give you as much as you want. I will pay it all, because I want to have Dinah as my wife.’" 
Genesis34:13 Because of the wicked thing that Shechem had done to Dinah, Jacob's sons decided to deceive him. They deceived his father Hamor too." 
Genesis34:14 They said, ‘We cannot give our sister to a man who is not circumcised. We would be ashamed to do this." 
Genesis34:15 If you want to marry Dinah, you must do one thing. You must circumcise all your males. Then you will become like us and we will agree for you to marry Dinah." 
Genesis34:16 Then we will give you our daughters so that your men can marry them. And our men will marry your daughters. We will live among you, so that we become one people with you." 
Genesis34:17 But your men must agree to be circumcised. If not, we will take our sister with us and we will leave this place.’" 
Genesis34:18 This idea seemed good to Hamor and his son Shechem. 
Genesis34:19 Shechem was the most important person in his family. He decided to do what Jacob's sons told them. Shechem agreed to do it because he wanted very much to marry Dinah. 
Genesis34:20 So Hamor and his son Shechem went to the gate of the city. They spoke to their men there. 
Genesis34:21 They said, ‘Jacob's family are our friends. Let them live in our land Let them buy and sell things here. This land is big enough for them and for us. We can marry their daughters, and they can marry our daughters." 
Genesis34:22 But we must do the one thing that they have asked us to do. We must circumcise all our males in the same way that they do. If we do that, they will agree to live among us." 
Genesis34:23 Then their things and their animals will all become our things. So we should agree to be circumcised, as they ask. Then they will live among us.’" 
Genesis34:24 All the men who were at the city gate agreed with Hamor and Shechem. So they circumcised every male in the city. 
Genesis34:25 Three days after the men were circumcised, they still had a lot of pain. Then two of Jacob's sons attacked Shechem's men with their swords. None of the men were ready to fight. Jacob's two sons were Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers. They killed every male in the city." 
Genesis34:26 They killed Hamor and his son Shechem. Then they took Dinah from Shechem's house and they left. 
Genesis34:27 Jacob's other sons also saw that the men of the city were dead. So they took everything that they wanted from the city. They did that because it was the place where Shechem had done a wicked thing against their sister. 
Genesis34:28 They took all the sheep, goats, cows and donkeys. They took everything that was in the city and in the fields near there." 
Genesis34:29 They carried away every valuable thing for themselves. They also took all the men's wives and their children. They took everything from the houses in the city. 
Genesis34:30 When they returned to Jacob, he said to Simeon and Levi, ‘You have brought bad trouble to me! The people who live in this land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites, will hate me. I will be like a bad smell to them. We do not have many men to fight for us. If the Perizzites and Canaanites join together and they attack us, they will destroy our whole family!’" 
Genesis34:31 But Simeon and Levi replied, ‘Shechem should not have had sex with our sister as if she were a prostitute.’" 
Genesis35:1 After these things had happened, God said to Jacob, ‘Go to Bethel and live there. Build an altar to worship me. I am the God who appeared to you when you were running away from Esau.’" 
Genesis35:2 So Jacob told the people in his house and everyone who was with him, ‘Throw out all the foreign idols that you have with you. Wash your body very well and put on clean clothes." 
Genesis35:3 Then we will go to Bethel. I will build an altar there to worship God. He is the one who answered me when I was in trouble. He has been with me everywhere that I went.’ 
Genesis35:4 So the people gave to Jacob all their idols and the rings that were in their ears. Jacob buried these things under the oak tree near Shechem city. 
Genesis35:5 Then they began their journey. All the people in the towns near there were afraid of God's power. So no one attacked Jacob and his people. 
Genesis35:6 Jacob and everyone with him arrived at Luz, now called Bethel. That is in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis35:7 Jacob built an altar there. He called the place El Bethel, because that was where God appeared to him. God had appeared to him when he was running away from his brother Esau." 
Genesis35:8 Rebekah's personal servant, Deborah, died at that time. Jacob buried her under the oak tree near Bethel. That is why people call the place Allon Bacuth." 
Genesis35:9 After Jacob returned from Paddan Aram, God appeared to him again. God blessed Jacob." 
Genesis35:10 He said to Jacob, ‘Your name is Jacob, but you will no longer be called Jacob. Instead, your name will be Israel.’ So God gave the name Israel to Jacob." 
Genesis35:11 Then God said to him, ‘I am Almighty God. Give birth to many children so that your descendants become very many. They will become many nations and some of them will be kings." 
Genesis35:12 I give to you the land that I gave to Abraham and to Isaac. Yes, I will give this land to your descendants that will come after you.’" 
Genesis35:13 Then God went away from the place where he spoke to Jacob. 
Genesis35:14 Jacob set up a special stone on the ground. It showed the place where God had spoken to him. He poured a drink offering on the stone. He also poured olive oil on it. 
Genesis35:15 Jacob called the place where God spoke to him Bethel. 
Genesis35:16 Then they left Bethel and they continued to travel. When they were still a long way from Ephrath, Rachel began to give birth. She had a lot of pain." 
Genesis35:17 As her pain became worse, the woman who was helping her said, ‘Do not be afraid. You have another son!’" 
Genesis35:18 But Rachel was dying. With her last breath, she gave her son the name Ben-Oni. But Jacob, his father, called him Benjamin." 
Genesis35:19 After Rachel died, they buried her near the road that goes to Ephrath." 
Genesis35:20 Jacob set up a stone as a pillar over the place where they buried Rachel. This stone still shows the place. 
Genesis35:21 Israel then continued to travel. He put up his tents beyond Migdal Eder. 
Genesis35:22 While Israel stayed there, his son Reuben had sex with Bilhah. She was Israel's slave wife, and Israel heard what had happened. Jacob had 12 sons:" 
Genesis35:23 The sons of Leah were Jacob's firstborn, Reuben, then Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar and Zebulun." 
Genesis35:24 The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin. 
Genesis35:25 The sons of Rachel's servant, Bilhah, were Dan and Naphtali." 
Genesis35:26 The sons of Leah's servant, Zilpah, were Gad and Asher. These were Jacob's sons who were born to him in Paddan Aram." 
Genesis35:27 Jacob arrived back at his father Isaac's home. This was in Mamre, also called Kiriath Arba, or Hebron. It was the place where Abraham and Isaac had stayed." 
Genesis35:28 Isaac lived for 180 years. 
Genesis35:29 Then he died and he joined his ancestors. He was an old man who had lived a long life. His sons, Esau and Jacob, buried him." 
Genesis36:1 This is the report about Esau and his family. (Esau is also called Edom.) 
Genesis36:2 Esau took women from Canaan to be his wives. They were: Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite," 
Genesis36:3 and Basemath, the daughter of Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth." 
Genesis36:4 Esau's wife Adah gave birth to a son called Eliphaz. Basemath gave birth to a son called Reuel. 
Genesis36:5 Oholibamah gave birth to three sons: Jeush, Jalam and Korah. These were Esau's sons that were born in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis36:6 Esau went to a land that was a long way from his brother Jacob. He took with him his wives, his sons and daughters and everyone in his house. He took his animals and all the things that he had got while he lived in Canaan." 
Genesis36:7 Esau and Jacob both had too many animals to live together in the same place. There was not enough land to give food to all their animals. 
Genesis36:8 So Esau, who is also called Edom, went to live in the hills of Seir." 
Genesis36:9 This is a list of Esau's descendants, the Edomites. They lived in the hill country of Seir." 
Genesis36:10 These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz, the son of Esau's wife, Adah, and Reuel, the son of Esau's wife, Basemath." 
Genesis36:11 The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam and Kenaz." 
Genesis36:12 Esau's son Eliphaz also had a slave wife. Her name was Timna. She gave birth to a son called Amalek. Those were the grandsons of Esau's wife, Adah."
