Exodus34:20 For a firstborn donkey, you may pay for it with a lamb instead. If you do not pay for it, you must break the donkey's neck. You must pay me back for every firstborn son that you have. Nobody may come to worship me without an offering to give me." 
Exodus34:21 You may work for six days each week, but on the seventh day you must rest. That is true even when it is the time to plough your fields. It is also true at harvest time. You must rest on the seventh day each week." 
Exodus34:22 Eat the Feast of Weeks every year when you cut the first wheat in your fields. Eat the Feast of Harvest at the end of each year. 
Exodus34:23 Three times in every year, all your men must come to worship the Lord God, the God of Israel." 
Exodus34:24 I will chase the nations out of the land as you move in. I will give you more and more land to live in. Three times each year you must come to worship the Lord your God. When you do that, nobody will try to take your land for themselves." 
Exodus34:25 When you offer the blood of an animal to me as your sacrifice, do not also offer any bread with yeast in it. When you kill a lamb for the Passover sacrifice, do not keep any of the meat until the next morning." 
Exodus34:26 When you cut the first crops from your fields, bring the best food to the house of the Lord your God. Do not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.’" 
Exodus34:27 Then the Lord said to Moses, ‘Write down these words. They are the rules of the covenant that I have made with you and with the Israelites.’" 
Exodus34:28 Moses was there on the mountain with the Lord for 40 days and 40 nights. He did not eat anything or drink anything during that time. He wrote the words of God's covenant on the flat stones. Those were God's ten special commands. 
Exodus34:29 Moses came down from Mount Sinai. He carried the two stones of the covenant in his hands. As he came down the mountain, he did not know that his face was shining. His face was shining because he had spoken with the Lord." 
Exodus34:30 When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, they could see that his face was shining. They were afraid to come near him." 
Exodus34:31 But Moses called out to them. So Aaron and the leaders of the people came back to him. Then Moses talked to them. 
Exodus34:32 After this, all the Israelites came near to Moses. He told them all the commands that the Lord had given to him on Mount Sinai." 
Exodus34:33 When Moses had finished speaking to the people, he covered his face with a thin cloth." 
Exodus34:34 But when he went into the special tent to speak with the Lord, he would remove the cloth. When he came out again, he would tell the Israelites what God had commanded him to say." 
Exodus34:35 They could see that his face was shining. So Moses would put the thin cloth over his face again. The cloth would stay on his face until he went into the tent again to speak with the Lord. 
Exodus35:1 Moses brought all the Israelites together. Then he said to them, ‘These are the Lord 's commands to you." 
Exodus35:2 You may work for six days each week, but the seventh day will be a holy day for you. It will be a day of complete rest to give honour to the Lord. If anyone works on that day, you must punish him with death." 
Exodus35:3 Do not light a fire in any of your homes on a Sabbath day.’ 
Exodus35:4 Moses said to all the Israelites, ‘This is what the Lord has commanded:" 
Exodus35:5 Take an offering to give to the Lord from the things that you have. Everyone who wants to give something may choose to give these things: gold, silver, bronze;" 
Exodus35:6 blue, purple and red material; good linen, goats' hair;" 
Exodus35:7 red leather from sheep's skins and other kinds of good leather; acacia wood; 
Exodus35:8 olive oil for the lamps; spices to make the special oil and the sweet incense; 
Exodus35:9 onyx stones and other jewels to fix on the ephod and on the breastpiece. 
Exodus35:10 Every clever person who has special skills must come to help. They must make all these things that the Lord has commanded: 
Exodus35:11 the tabernacle, with the tent and the other material that covers it; the rings, boards, sticks, poles and bases that are part of the tabernacle;" 
Exodus35:12 the Covenant Box, with its poles, its special lid and the curtain that hides it;" 
Exodus35:13 the special table with its poles and all its dishes; the special bread that you will put on the table for the Lord; 
Exodus35:14 the lampstand that will give light and its tools; the lamps and the oil for the lamps; 
Exodus35:15 the altar for incense and its poles; the special oil and the sweet incense; the curtain for the entrance of the tabernacle; 
Exodus35:16 the altar for burnt offerings and its bronze net; the poles for this altar and all its tools; the large bowl for water and its base; 
Exodus35:17 the curtains for the yard, its poles and their bases; the curtain for the entrance of the yard;" 
Exodus35:18 pegs to fix the tent to the ground; pegs and ropes to hold the curtain around the yard; 
Exodus35:19 the special clothes for Aaron and his sons to wear when they serve the Lord as priests in the Holy Place.’ 
Exodus35:20 After Moses had said this, all the Israelites went away." 
Exodus35:21 Everyone who decided to offer a gift to the Lord brought the things that they wanted to give. They brought the things to make the Tent of Meeting and the things to use in it. They also brought materials to make the priests' special clothes. 
Exodus35:22 Both men and women brought beautiful things because they wanted to do that. They brought gold things and jewels that they had worn. They included rings, necklaces and other beautiful things. When they brought their gifts, they lifted them up as an offering of gold to the Lord." 
Exodus35:23 Everyone who had blue, purple or red material, or good linen, brought it. They also brought goats' hair, red leather from sheep's skins and other kinds of good leather." 
Exodus35:24 Those who brought gifts of silver or bronze offered them to the Lord. Everyone who had acacia wood brought it to help with the Lord 's work. 
Exodus35:25 All the women who knew how to make things used their hands to make material. They brought what they had made: blue, purple and red material and good linen." 
Exodus35:26 Other clever women used goats' hair to make cloth. They did that because they wanted to help. 
Exodus35:27 The leaders of the Israelites brought onyx stones and other jewels to fix on the ephod and on the breastpiece. 
Exodus35:28 They also brought spices and olive oil. These things were to burn in the lamps, and to make the special oil and the sweet incense." 
Exodus35:29 Many Israelites chose to offer gifts to the Lord. Men and women all brought the gifts that they had decided to bring. Moses had told them about the work that the Lord had commanded them to do. So they brought these things to help with the work. 
Exodus35:30 Then Moses said to the Israelites, ‘Listen! The Lord has chosen Bezalel, the son of Uri and the grandson of Hur. He belongs to the tribe of Judah." 
Exodus35:31 The Lord has filled him with his Spirit so that he has special skills. He knows how to make many kinds of things. 
Exodus35:32 He can draw pictures of beautiful things that he wants to make. He can use gold, silver and bronze to make them." 
Exodus35:33 He knows how to cut valuable stones and make them look beautiful. He knows how to cut wood into different shapes. He is very clever at all kinds of beautiful work. 
Exodus35:34 The Lord has chosen Oholiab to help Bezalel. Oholiab is the son of Ahisamach. He belongs to the tribe of Dan. Bezalel and Oholiab can teach other people how to make things. 
Exodus35:35 They have special skills to make all kinds of things. They can draw pictures of the things that they want to make. They can cut pictures in metal. They can use blue, purple and red material and good linen to make beautiful things with pictures on them. They are very clever at their work, so that the things that they make look beautiful.’" 
Exodus36:1 Then Moses said, ‘Bezalel and Oholiab will do the work that the Lord has commanded us to do. Everyone that the Lord has helped to have special skills will work with them. They will help to build the tabernacle and all the other special things that the Lord has commanded.’" 
Exodus36:2 Then Moses called Bezalel, Oholiab and all the other people who had special skills to come together. The Lord had given them these skills and they wanted to start the work." 
Exodus36:3 They received from Moses all the gifts that the Israelites had chosen to bring. They had brought gifts that would help to build the tabernacle and the other special things. The Israelites continued to bring more gifts to Moses every morning. 
Exodus36:4 Then all the workers who had special skills stopped doing their work. They left their work on the tabernacle and they went to speak to Moses. 
Exodus36:5 They said to Moses, ‘The people are bringing many more gifts than we need to finish the tabernacle, as the Lord commanded us to do.’" 
Exodus36:6 So Moses sent a message to everyone in the whole camp. He said, ‘No man or woman must offer any more gifts for the work of the tabernacle.’ When the people heard Moses' message, they did not bring any more gifts." 
Exodus36:7 The workers already had more things than they needed to finish all the work. 
Exodus36:8 All the workers who had special skills made the tabernacle with ten curtains. They used very good linen, as well as blue, purple and red material. They made beautiful pictures of cherubs on the curtains." 
Exodus36:9 All the curtains were the same size: 12 metres long and 2 metres wide. 
Exodus36:10 The workers joined five of the curtains together. They did the same thing with the other five curtains. 
Exodus36:11 They made rings of blue material along the edge of the last curtain in each set of five curtains. 
Exodus36:12 They put 50 rings along the edge of the first set. They put another 50 rings along the edge of the second set. The rings on one curtain were opposite to the rings on the other curtain. 
Exodus36:13 They made 50 small gold hooks to join the curtains together. So a single piece of curtain made the whole tabernacle. 
Exodus36:14 The workers used goats' hair to make 11 more curtains to cover the tabernacle, like a tent." 
Exodus36:15 The curtains were all the same size: 13.5 metres long and 2 metres wide. 
Exodus36:16 They joined five of the curtains together to make one set. They joined the other six curtains to make another set. 
Exodus36:17 They used material to make 50 rings along the edge of the end curtain in each set. 
Exodus36:18 They made 50 bronze hooks to join the two sets of curtains together. It made a single tent. 
Exodus36:19 They used red leather from sheep's skins to cover the tent. They used another kind of good leather to cover the red leather. 
Exodus36:20 They used acacia wood to make the boards that would hold up the tabernacle. 
Exodus36:21 Each board was 4 metres high and 65 centimetres wide. 
Exodus36:22 Each board had two wooden pegs, beside each other. They made all the boards for the tabernacle like this." 
Exodus36:23 They made 20 boards for the south side of the tabernacle. 
Exodus36:24 They used silver to make 40 heavy bases to hold up the boards. Two bases would hold up each board. The pegs on the boards fitted into holes in the bases. 
Exodus36:25 They made 20 boards for the north side of the tabernacle. 
Exodus36:26 They made 40 silver bases, two bases under each board." 
Exodus36:27 They made six boards for the west side, at the back of the tabernacle." 
Exodus36:28 They made two boards for the corners at the back of the tabernacle. 
Exodus36:29 They fixed the two boards together, from the bottom to the top. They used a ring to fix them together at the top. They made both corners in the same way." 
Exodus36:30 So there were eight boards at the back of the tabernacle and 16 silver bases, two bases under each board." 
Exodus36:31 They used acacia wood to make bars to fix across the boards. They made five bars for the south side of the tabernacle," 
Exodus36:32 five bars for the north side, and five bars for the west side at the back of the tabernacle." 
Exodus36:33 The bar that would go across the centre of the boards would reach from one end of the tabernacle to the other end. 
Exodus36:34 They used gold to cover the boards and to make rings for the boards. The rings would hold the bars in place across the boards. They also covered the wooden bars with gold. 
Exodus36:35 The workers used blue, purple and red material, and good linen to make a special curtain. A worker used special skills to make pictures of cherubs on it." 
Exodus36:36 They used gold rings to hang the curtain from four poles of acacia wood. They covered the poles with gold. They made four silver bases to hold the poles. 
Exodus36:37 They made a curtain for the entrance of the tent. They used blue, purple and red material and good linen to make it. A worker used special skills to make it, so that it looked beautiful." 
Exodus36:38 They made five poles to hold up this curtain. They fixed gold hooks to the poles. They covered the tops of the poles with gold. But they used bronze to make five bases to hold up the poles. 
Exodus37:1 Bezalel used acacia wood to make the Covenant Box. It was 1 metre long, 75 centimetres wide and 75 centimetres high." 
Exodus37:2 He covered it with gold, inside and outside. He built up the edges of the box with gold." 
Exodus37:3 He made four gold rings for it and he fixed them to its four feet. He fixed two rings on one side and two rings on the other side. 
Exodus37:4 He used acacia wood to make two poles. He also covered them with gold. 
Exodus37:5 He pushed the poles through the rings on the sides of the Covenant Box, to carry it." 
Exodus37:6 He used pure gold to make the special lid for the Covenant Box. It was 1 metre long and 75 centimetres wide. 
Exodus37:7 He used gold to make images of two cherubs. He used a hammer to make them the right shape. He made them to stand at the two ends of the lid. 
Exodus37:8 One cherub stood at one end of the lid and one cherub stood at the other end. He made the two cherubs and the lid from one piece of gold. 
Exodus37:9 The cherubs looked at each other and they watched over the lid. Their wings touched each other over the top of the lid. 
Exodus37:10 Bezalel used acacia wood to make the special table. It was 1 metre long, 50 centimetres wide and 75 centimetres high." 
Exodus37:11 He covered the wood with gold. He built up the edges of the table with gold. 
Exodus37:12 He fixed an extra piece of wood around its edge. It was 7 centimetres wide. He built up the edges of this piece with gold. 
Exodus37:13 He made four gold rings for the table. He fixed them to the four corners, one beside each leg of the table." 
Exodus37:14 The rings were near to the extra piece of wood. They would hold the poles to carry the table. 
Exodus37:15 He used acacia wood to make the poles and he covered them with gold. They would use the poles to carry the table. 
Exodus37:16 He used pure gold to make the things that would be on the table. He made plates, big spoons, jars and bowls. They would use those things to pour out drink offerings." 
Exodus37:17 Bezalel used one piece of pure gold to make the lampstand. He used a hammer to make it the right shape. He used the gold to make its base, its middle pole and the cups to hold the oil. The cups were like open flowers and closed flowers." 
Exodus37:18 There were six branches on the lampstand, three branches on each side of the middle pole." 
Exodus37:19 Three cups in the shape of almond flowers and their leaves were on each branch. Each of the six branches of the lampstand had three cups like that. 
Exodus37:20 On the lampstand itself there were four cups in the shape of almond flowers and their leaves. 
Exodus37:21 There was one closed flower under the first pair of branches. There was a second closed flower under the second pair of branches. There was a third closed flower under the third pair of branches. So there were three closed flowers for the six branches. 
Exodus37:22 He made the flowers and the branches from the same piece of pure gold as the lampstand. He used a hammer to make the gold into the right shape. 
Exodus37:23 He used pure gold to make seven lamps for the lampstand, as well as the small tools and dishes to take care of the lamps." 
Exodus37:24 He used 34 kilograms of pure gold to make the lampstand and all its tools. 
Exodus37:25 Bezalel used acacia wood to make the altar for incense. It was square, 45 centimetres long, 45 centimetres wide and 90 centimetres high. The altar's horns were part of it, made from one piece of wood." 
Exodus37:26 He covered all the altar with gold: its top, its sides and the horns. He built up an edge of gold around the four sides." 
Exodus37:27 He made two gold rings for the altar. He fixed them below the gold edge, on two opposite sides. They would hold the poles to carry the altar." 
Exodus37:28 He used acacia wood to make the poles and he covered them with gold. 
Exodus37:29 He also made the special oil that would make things holy. He also made the incense with a sweet smell, like perfume." 
Exodus38:1 Bezalel used acacia wood to build the altar for burnt offerings. It was square, 2.2 metres long and 2.2 metres wide. It was 1.3 metres high." 
Exodus38:2 He made four horns, one at each corner of the altar. The horns and the altar itself were one piece of work. He covered the whole altar with bronze." 
Exodus38:3 He made all the tools for the altar with bronze. He made pots to remove the ashes, and also spades and bowls. He made forks for the meat and dishes to carry the fire." 
Exodus38:4 He used bronze to make a square net for the altar. He fixed it inside the altar, in the middle between the top and the ground." 
Exodus38:5 He used bronze to make four rings. He fixed them to the four corners of the net. The poles to carry the altar would go through these rings. 
Exodus38:6 He used acacia wood to make the poles. He covered them with bronze. 
Exodus38:7 He put the poles through the rings on each side of the altar, to carry it. He used boards to make the altar so that it was empty inside." 
Exodus38:8 He used bronze to make a large bowl for water, and a base for it to stand on. He used the bronze from small mirrors to make these things. The mirrors had belonged to women who worked at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Exodus38:9 Bezalel made the yard around the tent. The south side of the yard was 45 metres long. It had curtains that were made from good linen. 
Exodus38:10 He made 20 poles and 20 bronze bases to hold the poles. He used silver to make hooks and sticks to hold the curtains. 
Exodus38:11 The north side of the yard was also 45 metres long. It also had 20 poles and their bronze bases, as well as their silver hooks and sticks." 
Exodus38:12 The west end of the yard was 23 metres long. It had curtains with ten poles and their ten bronze bases. The poles had silver hooks and sticks. 
Exodus38:13 The entrance of the yard was at its east end. That end was also 23 metres long. 
Exodus38:14 There were 7 metres of curtain on each side of the entrance. Three poles with their bases held the curtains on each side. 
Exodus38:15  
Exodus38:16 The workers used good linen to make all the curtains around the yard. 
Exodus38:17 All the poles had bronze bases to hold them. But the workers used silver to make the hooks that were on the poles, and the sticks. They covered the tops of the poles with silver too. All the poles around the yard had silver sticks to join them together." 
Exodus38:18 They used blue, purple and red material, as well as good linen, to make the curtain for the entrance of the yard. A worker used special skills so that it looked beautiful. It was 9 metres wide and 2.2 metres high. All the curtains around the yard were 2.2 metres high." 
Exodus38:19 Four poles in bronze bases held the curtain at the entrance. The poles had silver hooks and silver sticks. Silver also covered the tops of the poles. 
Exodus38:20 The workers used bronze to make the pegs that fixed the tabernacle to the ground. They also made bronze pegs to fix all the curtains around the yard to the ground. 
Exodus38:21 This is a list of the things that the workers used to make the tabernacle, with the Covenant Box inside it. Moses commanded the Levites to write this list. Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest, was the leader of this work." 
Exodus38:22 Bezalel, the son of Uri and grandson of Hur, made all the things that the Lord had commanded Moses. Bezalel belonged to the tribe of Judah." 
Exodus38:23 Ahisamach's son Oholiab helped him. He belonged to the tribe of Dan. He had a special skill to draw things that he wanted to make. He could make beautiful pictures on cloth, with blue, purple and red materials, as well as good linen." 
Exodus38:24 The workers used 1,000 kilograms of gold to do all the work to make the tabernacle. That was its proper weight that they use to weigh things in the holy place. The Israelites had lifted up this gold as an offering to the Lord." 
Exodus38:25 The people had also brought 3,430 kilograms of silver as an offering for the work." 
Exodus38:26 Each person who was 20 years old when Moses counted them had given the right amount, about ten grams. There were 603,550 men that Moses had counted." 
Exodus38:27 The workers used 34 kilograms of silver to make each base to hold the poles. There were 100 bases to hold the tabernacle and the special curtain inside it. So they used 3,400 kilograms of silver to make the bases." 
Exodus38:28 Then they used the 30 kilograms that still remained. With this, they made the hooks and the sticks for the poles, and they covered the tops of the poles." 
Exodus38:29 The people had also lifted up 2,425 kilograms of bronze as an offering to the Lord." 
Exodus38:30 The workers used this bronze to make the bases for the entrance to the tabernacle. They also used it to make the bronze altar, with its net and all its tools." 
Exodus38:31 They used this bronze to make the bases for the curtains around the yard and for the entrance to the yard. They used it to make the pegs to fix the tabernacle and the curtain around the yard to the ground. 
Exodus39:1 The workers used blue, purple and red material to make beautiful clothes for the priests. They would wear them when they served God in the Holy Place. They made these special clothes for Aaron, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:2 The workers used gold, and blue, purple and red material, as well as good linen, to make the ephod." 
Exodus39:3 They used a hammer to hit the gold to make thin pieces. Then they cut these pieces into narrow strings. A worker with special skills used the gold strings to make the blue, purple and red material, and the good linen, look very beautiful." 
Exodus39:4 The workers fixed pieces of cloth on the two edges of the ephod. These pieces went over the priest's shoulders to join the front and the back parts of the ephod. 
Exodus39:5 They made the beautiful belt to tie around the ephod. The belt and the ephod were one piece of work. They used the same materials to make both of them: gold, blue, purple and red material and good linen. They made them in the way that the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:6 The workers fixed the onyx stones into beautiful gold around their edges. A worker used a sharp tool to write on them the names of the sons of Israel. 
Exodus39:7 They fixed the two onyx stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod. They showed the names of the 12 sons of Israel, so that the Lord would remember to bless his people. The Lord had commanded Moses to do this." 
Exodus39:8 Workers used special skills to make the breastpiece, so that it looked beautiful. They made it like the ephod. They used gold, blue, purple and red material as well as good linen to make it." 
Exodus39:9 They bent a piece of cloth over to make a square pocket, 22 centimetres long and 22 centimetres wide." 
Exodus39:10 Then they fixed four rows of jewels on it. These are the names of the jewels: A ruby, a topaz and a beryl in the first row." 
Exodus39:11 A turquoise, a sapphire and an emerald in the second row." 
Exodus39:12 A jacinth, an agate and an amethyst in the third row." 
Exodus39:13 A chrysolite, an onyx and a jasper in the fourth row. They fixed each jewel into beautiful gold around its edges." 
Exodus39:14 There were 12 jewels, one jewel for each of Israel's sons. They used a sharp tool to write one tribe's name on each jewel." 
Exodus39:15 They used pure gold to make two thin chains like strings, to fix the breastpiece to the ephod." 
Exodus39:16 Then they made two gold rings with beautiful gold around the edges. They fixed the rings to the two top corners of the breastpiece. 
Exodus39:17 They fixed the two gold chains to the rings on the corners of the breastpiece. 
Exodus39:18 They fixed the other ends of the two chains to the shoulder pieces of the ephod, in the front. They joined them to the gold pieces that held the onyx stones." 
Exodus39:19 They made two gold rings and they fixed them to the bottom corners of the breastpiece. They were on its inside edge, next to the ephod." 
Exodus39:20 Then they made two more gold rings. They fixed them to the bottom edge of the shoulder pieces, on the front of the ephod. They put the rings just above the ephod's belt." 
Exodus39:21 They used a string of blue material to tie the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod. They fixed them together above the ephod's belt, so that the breastpiece would not become separate. The workers made all these things as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:22 The workers used only blue cloth to make the robe for the ephod. 
Exodus39:23 They made a hole in the top of the robe, in the middle. They made an edge around the hole so that it would not tear. The priest's head would go through this hole." 
Exodus39:24 They used blue, purple and red materials and good linen to make pictures of pomegranates. These were around the bottom edge of the robe." 
Exodus39:25 They used pure gold to make little bells. They fixed the bells between the pomegranates on the bottom edge of the robe. 
Exodus39:26 So there was a bell, then a pomegranate, another bell then a pomegranate, all around the edge of the robe. The priest would wear this robe when he served the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:27 The workers made long shirts for Aaron and his sons, the priests. They used good linen to make cloth for the shirts." 
Exodus39:28 They also used good linen to make Aaron's special hat, as well as hats for the other priests. They used special soft linen to make the trousers that they wore next to their skin." 
Exodus39:29 A worker used special skills to make the long belt, so that it looked beautiful. He used blue, purple and red materials to make it, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:30 The workers used pure gold to make a thin plate as a holy sign. They carefully wrote these words on it with a sharp tool: ‘Holy to the Lord ’. 
Exodus39:31 They fixed a blue string to the plate, to tie it to the front of the special hat. The Lord had commanded Moses to do this." 
Exodus39:32 So the workers finished making everything for the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting. The Israelites made everything exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses. They brought the tent and all the things that would be in it. They brought the hooks, boards, sticks, poles and bases." 
Exodus39:34 They brought the red leather from sheep's skins, and the other kinds of good leather that covered the tent. They also brought the curtain that hid the Most Holy Place." 
Exodus39:35 They brought the Covenant Box with its poles and its special lid. 
Exodus39:36 They brought the special table with all its dishes, and the special bread to put on the table for the Lord." 
Exodus39:37 They brought the gold lampstand with its lamps, and the oil and the tools to make the lamps give light." 
Exodus39:38 They brought the gold altar, the special oil, the sweet incense and the curtain for the entrance of the tabernacle." 
Exodus39:39 They brought the bronze altar and its bronze net, with its poles and all its tools. They brought the large bowl for water and its base." 
Exodus39:40 They brought the curtains for the yard and the curtain for the entrance to the yard, with their poles and their bases. They brought all the ropes and the pegs to fix those things to the ground. They brought everything that the priests needed to serve the Lord in the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting." 
Exodus39:41 They also brought the special clothes for Aaron and his sons to wear when they served the Lord as priests in the Holy Place. 
Exodus39:42 The Israelites did all the work in the way that the Lord had commanded Moses. 
Exodus39:43 Moses carefully checked all the work. He saw that the workers had done it exactly as the Lord had commanded them. When Moses saw this, he blessed the people." 
Exodus40:1 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Exodus40:2 ‘On the first day of the first month, put up the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting." 
Exodus40:3 Put the Covenant Box in the tabernacle and hide it behind the special curtain. 
Exodus40:4 Bring in the special table and put the dishes on it. Then bring in the lampstand. Fix its lamps on it. 
Exodus40:5 Put the gold altar for incense in front of the Covenant Box. Hang the curtain that will be at the entrance to the tabernacle. 
Exodus40:6 Put the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. 
Exodus40:7 Put the large bowl for water between the Tent of Meeting and the altar. Put water in the bowl. 
Exodus40:8 Hang the curtains around the yard of the tabernacle, as well as the curtain at the entrance to the yard." 
Exodus40:9 Take the special oil that makes things holy. Pour some oil on the tabernacle and on everything that is in it. Offer the tabernacle and all its things to God, and then it will be holy." 
Exodus40:10 Then pour some oil on the altar for burnt offerings and all its tools. Offer it all to God, and then it will be completely holy." 
Exodus40:11 Pour some oil on the large bowl for water and on its base to offer them to God. 
Exodus40:12 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Wash them with water. 
Exodus40:13 Then dress Aaron in the special clothes for the priest. Pour some of the special oil on his head. Offer him to me to serve me as my priest. 
Exodus40:14 Also bring his sons and dress them in the special long shirts. 
Exodus40:15 Pour some oil on their heads, as you did to their father. Then they too may serve me as my priests. In this way, they and their descendants will always continue to serve me as priests.’" 
Exodus40:16 Moses did all these things, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:17 So they put up the tabernacle on the first day of the first month. It was in the second year after they had left Egypt. 
Exodus40:18 When Moses put up the tabernacle, he first put the metal bases in their right places. Then he put up the boards, the bars to hold them, and the poles." 
Exodus40:19 Then he covered the tabernacle with the tent and he put the leather covers over the tent. He did this in the way that the Lord had commanded him. 
Exodus40:20 Moses took the two flat stones with God's special commands on them. He put them inside the Covenant Box. He put the poles through the rings on the box. He put the special lid on top of the box. 
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the Covenant Box into the tabernacle. He hung the special curtain to hide it, so that nobody could see it. He did this in the way that the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:22 Moses put the special table inside the Tent of Meeting. He put it outside the curtain, on the north side of the Holy Place." 
Exodus40:23 He put the special bread on the table as an offering to the Lord, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:24 He also put the lampstand in the Tent of Meeting. He put it on the south side of the Holy Place, opposite the table." 
Exodus40:25 Then he put the lamps in their places in front of the Lord, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:26 Moses put the gold altar in the Tent of Meeting, outside the curtain that hid the Covenant Box." 
Exodus40:27 He burned sweet incense on it, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:28 Then he hung the curtain at the entrance of the tent. 
Exodus40:29 He put the altar for burnt offerings outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He offered a burnt offering and a grain offering on it, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Exodus40:30 Moses put the large bowl for water between the Tent of Meeting and the altar. He put water in it for the priests to wash themselves. 
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons used the water to wash their hands and their feet. 
Exodus40:32 They washed themselves every time that they went into the Tent of Meeting. They also washed themselves before they went to offer sacrifices at the altar. They did what the Lord had commanded Moses to do. 
Exodus40:33 Moses also made the yard around the tabernacle and around the altar. He hung the curtain at the entrance of the yard. In that way, Moses finished the work." 
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting. The glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle. 
Exodus40:35 Moses could not go into the Tent of Meeting because of the cloud and the bright glory of the Lord. 
Exodus40:36 The Israelites followed the cloud during all their journeys. Every time that the cloud rose up from the tabernacle, they would continue on their journey." 
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud remained on the tabernacle, the Israelites did not travel. They stayed where they were until the day when the cloud rose up." 
Exodus40:38 So the cloud stayed over the tabernacle during the day, to show that the Lord was with them. During the night, there was fire in the cloud. All the Israelites could see the cloud during all their journeys." 
Leviticus1:1 The Lord spoke to Moses from the Tent of Meeting. 
Leviticus1:2 He said to Moses, ‘Speak to Israel's people. Tell them this. When a person gives an animal to the Lord, it must be a cow, a sheep or a goat." 
Leviticus1:3 A person may want to give a burnt offering to the Lord. That offering may be a bull that he takes from among his animals. It must be perfect. The person must bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, so that the Lord will accept his gift." 
Leviticus1:4 Then he must put his hand on the animal's head. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sins. 
Leviticus1:5 The person who brings the offering must kill the young bull in front of the Lord. Aaron's sons, the priests, will take the blood. They will throw it onto the sides of the altar which is near the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus1:6 The person must remove the skin from the dead animal. Then he must cut the meat into pieces. 
Leviticus1:7 Aaron's sons will light a fire on the altar. They will put pieces of wood on it. 
Leviticus1:8 Then Aaron's sons will put the pieces of meat onto the wood that is burning. They will put the head and the fat on the fire with the other meat. 
Leviticus1:9 The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus1:10 A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a perfect male animal. 
Leviticus1:11 He must kill it in front of the Lord, at the north side of the altar. Aaron's sons, the priests, will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus1:12 The person who brings the offering must cut the animal into pieces. The priests will take the pieces of meat, the head and the fat. They will put them on the wood which is burning on the altar." 
Leviticus1:13 The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus1:14 A person may want to give a bird as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a dove or a young pigeon. 
Leviticus1:15 The priest will take the bird to the altar. He will tear off the head and he will burn it on the altar. He will pour the bird's blood onto the side of the altar. 
Leviticus1:16 Then the priest will remove the part of the bird where it keeps its food, and the food in it. He will throw it on the east side of the altar, where the ashes are." 
Leviticus1:17 He will take hold of the bird's wings and he will tear open the body. But he must not tear it completely into two pieces. He will burn all of it on the wood that is burning on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:1 A person may want to give a grain offering to the Lord. He must choose the best flour for his offering. He must pour olive oil on the flour and he must put incense on it. 
Leviticus2:2 He must take it to Aaron's sons, the priests. The priest will take some of the flour and its olive oil in his hand. He will also take all the incense. He will burn this part of the offering on the altar as a gift to the Lord. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord." 
Leviticus2:3 The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:4 If you bake your grain in an oven before you offer it, this is what you must do. You must use the best flour. Do not use any yeast. You may mix olive oil with the flour to make a loaf. Or you may make biscuits and put olive oil on them." 
Leviticus2:5 You may choose to cook a cake on a flat plate. If so, mix some olive oil with the best flour but do not use any yeast." 
Leviticus2:6 You must break the cake into pieces and then pour olive oil on it. It is a grain offering. 
Leviticus2:7 If you cook the grain in a pot, you must use the best flour. Cook it in olive oil." 
Leviticus2:8 Use these things to make your grain offering for the Lord. Then you must take it to the priest. He will take it to the altar. 
Leviticus2:9 He will take a part of the grain offering and he will burn it in the fire on the altar. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:10 The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:11 You must not use yeast in any grain offering that you give to the Lord. You must not give any yeast or honey to the priests for them to burn as a gift to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:12 You may offer yeast or honey to the Lord as the first part of your harvest. But you must not burn them on the altar to make a sweet smell that gives pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus2:13 You must put salt on all your grain offerings. Salt is a mark of God's covenant with you, his people. So do not forget to put salt on all your grain offerings." 
Leviticus2:14 You may want to use the first crops of your harvest to make a grain offering to the Lord. If so, you must break the new grains into small pieces. Then cook them on a fire." 
Leviticus2:15 It is a grain offering, so you must put olive oil and incense on it." 
Leviticus2:16 The priest will take a part of the grain and olive oil, together with all the incense. He will burn that part as a gift to the Lord." 
Leviticus3:1 A person might want to give a friendship offering to the Lord. He may choose a bull or a cow from among his animals. It must be perfect. 
Leviticus3:2 The person must put his hand on the animal's head. Then he must kill it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons, the priests, will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus3:3 The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal. 
Leviticus3:4 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver. 
Leviticus3:5 Aaron's sons will take these parts of the animal. They will put them on top of the burnt offering that is on the wood that burns on the altar. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus3:6 A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a friendship offering to the Lord. It must be a perfect animal, male or female." 
Leviticus3:7 If it is a young sheep, he must offer it as a gift to the Lord." 
Leviticus3:8 He must put his hand on its head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus3:9 The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must cut the fat part of the tail off the animal. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal. 
Leviticus3:10 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver. 
Leviticus3:11 The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food to the Lord. 
Leviticus3:12 A person may give a goat as his offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus3:13 The person must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus3:14 The person must offer some parts of his offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal. 
Leviticus3:15 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver. 
Leviticus3:16 The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord. All the fat from these offerings belongs to the Lord. 
Leviticus3:17 You must never eat any fat or any blood yourselves. This is a rule that you and your descendants must always obey, in all the places where you live.’" 
Leviticus4:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus4:2 ‘Say to Israel's people, “Someone may do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He may do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so it is a sin. This is what that person must do." 
Leviticus4:3 The person who does a wrong thing might be the leader of the priests. If that is so, his sin will cause the people to be guilty. He must bring a perfect young bull as an offering to the Lord. That will be his sin offering." 
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the young bull to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must put his hand on the bull's head. Then he must kill it in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus4:5 The leader of the priests must take some of the blood into the Tent of Meeting. 
Leviticus4:6 He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place. 
Leviticus4:7 He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place, inside the Tent of Meeting. He must pour all the bull's blood that remains onto the ground in front of the great altar. That is the altar for burnt offerings which is outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus4:8 Then the leader of the priests must remove all the fat from the bull which is his sin offering. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal. 
Leviticus4:9 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver. 
Leviticus4:10 That is the same as the priest does with a bull which is a friendship offering. The priest must burn all the fat on the altar which is for burnt offerings. 
Leviticus4:11 But this is what the priest must do with all the other parts of the bull. He must take its skin, its meat, its head and its legs, as well as its inside parts and the food in its stomach." 
Leviticus4:12 He must take all those parts to a clean place which is outside the camp. He must burn them on a fire of wood on the heap of ashes. 
Leviticus4:13 The whole group of Israel's people might do a wrong thing when they did not want to do it. They might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands. They are guilty even if they did not know about it. 
Leviticus4:14 When they realize that they have done a sin, they must offer a young bull to the Lord. That will be their sin offering. They must bring it to the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus4:15 The leaders of the people must put their hands on the bull's head. Then one of them must kill it in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus4:16 The leader of the priests must take some of the bull's blood into the Tent of Meeting. 
Leviticus4:17 He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place. 
Leviticus4:18 He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place, inside the Tent of Meeting. He must pour all the bull's blood that remains onto the ground in front of the great altar. That is the altar for burnt offerings which is outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus4:19 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the dead animal. He will burn all the fat on the altar for burnt offerings. 
Leviticus4:20 He will do with the other parts of this bull the same things that he did with his own sin offering. This is how the priest will make the people clean from their sins. God will forgive them when the priest does this. 
Leviticus4:21 The priest must take the other parts of the bull outside the camp. He must burn them as he did with the first bull. This is the sin offering for the whole group of Israel's people. 
Leviticus4:22 A leader might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so he is guilty." 
Leviticus4:23 When he realizes that he has done a sin, he must bring a perfect male goat as an offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus4:24 He must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings to the Lord. It is a sin offering for the leader. 
Leviticus4:25 The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar. 
Leviticus4:26 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat. He must burn the fat on the altar, as he does for the sacrifices which are friendship offerings. This is how the priest will make the leader clean from his sin. God will forgive the leader when the priest does this." 
Leviticus4:27 One of the ordinary people might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so he is guilty." 
Leviticus4:28 When he realizes that he has done a sin, he should bring a perfect female goat as an offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus4:29 He must put his hand on the head of the goat which is his sin offering. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings. 
Leviticus4:30 The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar. 
Leviticus4:31 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat, as he did with the sacrifice for the friendship offering. He must burn the fat on the altar so that its smell gives pleasure to the Lord. This is how the priest will make the person clean from his sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this." 
Leviticus4:32 If the person chooses to offer a sheep as his sin offering, it must be a perfect female sheep." 
Leviticus4:33 He must put his hand on the head of the sheep which is his sin offering. He must then kill it at the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings. 
Leviticus4:34 The priest must put his finger in the sheep's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour all the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar. 
Leviticus4:35 He must remove all the fat from the sheep. This is the same as they do with fat from sheep which are sacrifices for friendship offerings. The priest must then burn the fat on the altar. He will burn it on top of the other offerings that people give to the Lord. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. 
Leviticus5:1 Someone may have seen something happen, or they may know about it. A judge may command people to say anything that they know about what happened. If the person refuses to tell what he knows about it, he is guilty of a sin. He deserves punishment." 
Leviticus5:2 Someone may touch something that is unclean. It may be the dead body of a wild animal or a farm animal that is not clean. Or he may touch the dead body of an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. Even if the person does not realize what he has done, he has made himself unclean. He is guilty of a sin." 
Leviticus5:3 Someone may touch something that comes out from another person and it is unclean. The person may not know that he has touched it, but later he realizes what he has done. He is guilty of a sin." 
Leviticus5:4 Someone may not think carefully before he makes a promise. He may promise to do something that is good or something that is bad. Even if he does not know that he has made a careless promise, he is guilty when he realizes what he has done." 
Leviticus5:5 If someone becomes guilty for any of these reasons, he must say clearly that he has done a wrong thing." 
Leviticus5:6 He must bring a female sheep or a female goat for his sin offering. That will be the price to pay for his sin. The priest will offer the animal to the Lord as a sin offering. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sin. 
Leviticus5:7 The person may not have enough money to bring an animal as his sin offering. If so, he must pay for his sin with two doves or two young pigeons. He must bring them to the Lord. One bird will be a sin offering. The second bird will be a burnt offering." 
Leviticus5:8 He must take his birds to the priest. The priest will first offer to the Lord the bird that is for a sin offering. He must break the bird's neck, but he must not remove the head from the body." 
Leviticus5:9 He must shake some of the blood onto the side of the altar. Then he must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar. That bird is a sin offering. 
Leviticus5:10 The priest must then offer the other bird as a burnt offering. He must do this in the way that the rules say he should. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. 
Leviticus5:11 The person may not have enough money to bring two doves or two young pigeons. If so, he must pay for his sin with a tenth of an ephah of the best flour. That will be his sin offering. He must not put any olive oil or incense on the flour, because it is a sin offering." 
Leviticus5:12 He must take it to the priest. The priest must take some of the flour in his hand. He will burn this on the altar as a gift to the Lord, together with the other gifts. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord. It is a sin offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest will make the person clean from any of these sins that he has done. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. The flour that remains will belong to the priest, as the grain offerings do.” ’" 
Leviticus5:14 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus5:15 ‘Someone may not obey the rules about the Lord 's holy things. He may not realize that he has done anything wrong, but he is guilty of a sin. He must bring a perfect male sheep as a guilt offering to the Lord, to pay for his sin. You must decide the value of the sheep in shekels of silver that they use in the holy place." 
Leviticus5:16 The person must also give money to pay for the holy thing which he spoiled. He must add one fifth more to its value. He must give the sheep and the money to the priest. The priest will offer the sheep as a guilt offering to make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. 
Leviticus5:17 Someone may fail to obey one of the Lord 's commands. Even if he does not know that he has done this, he is guilty of a sin. He deserves punishment." 
Leviticus5:18 When he realizes what he has done, he must bring a perfect male sheep to the priest. That will be his guilt offering. You must decide the value of the sheep in shekels of silver that they use in the holy place. The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the person clean from his mistake. He did a wrong thing but he did not realize it at the time. God will forgive the person when the priest does this." 
Leviticus5:19 The sheep is a guilt offering for that person. The person is guilty of a sin against the Lord.’ 
Leviticus6:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:2 ‘Someone may cheat another person, which is a sin against the Lord. He may deceive other people in different ways. He may keep something that belongs to them. It may be something that they have given to him to take care of. He may have robbed them. He may cheat them in other ways." 
Leviticus6:3 He may find something that another person has lost but he keeps it for himself. He may tell lies about it. He may say that he does not have it. He may deceive people in other ways that people do. 
Leviticus6:4 When someone does any of these sins, he is guilty. He must give back everything that he has taken from other people. He may have cheated other people. He may have kept something that belongs to them. He may have found something that they have lost." 
Leviticus6:5 He may have told lies about something so that he keeps it for himself. On the day when he knows that he is guilty, he must give back everything to the proper owner. He must also add one fifth more to its value." 
Leviticus6:6 He must bring a perfect male sheep as his guilt offering to the Lord. You must decide its value in shekels of silver. He must give this guilt offering to the priest. 
Leviticus6:7 The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the person clean from his sin. Whatever sin the person has done to make him guilty, God will forgive him when the priest does this.’" 
Leviticus6:8 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:9 ‘Give this command to Aaron and his sons: “These are the rules about burnt offerings. The burnt offering must stay on the altar fire all night until the morning. The fire on the altar must continue to burn all night. 
Leviticus6:10 When the morning comes, the priest must dress himself in his linen robe. He must also wear linen trousers next to his skin. He must remove the ashes of the burnt offering that has burnt on the fire. He must put the ashes at the side of the altar." 
Leviticus6:11 Then he must dress himself in other clothes. He must carry the ashes outside the camp. He must put them in a special place that God has chosen. 
Leviticus6:12 The fire that is on the altar must always continue to burn. Every morning the priest must put more wood on the fire. He will put the animal for the burnt offering on top of the fire. He will also burn the fat of the friendship offerings on the fire. 
Leviticus6:13 The fire on the altar must always continue to burn. It must never stop burning. 
Leviticus6:14 These are the rules about grain offerings. Aaron's sons must take them in front of the altar to give them to the Lord. 
Leviticus6:15 A priest must take some of the pure flour and the olive oil in his hand. He must also take all the incense that is on the grain offering. He will burn this part of the offering on the altar as a gift to the Lord. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons may eat what remains of the friendship offerings. They must use it to make bread that has no yeast in it. Then they must eat it in a holy place, in the yard outside the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus6:17 The bread must not have any yeast in it. I have given this part of my offerings to the priests. It belongs to them. It is very holy, in the same way that the sin offerings and the guilt offerings are holy." 
Leviticus6:18 Any male descendant of Aaron may eat this part of the Lord 's offerings. This rule will be true for all time. Anyone who touches these offerings will be holy.” ’ 
Leviticus6:19 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:20 ‘On the day when you anoint Aaron and his sons, they must bring an offering to the Lord. It must be a tenth of an ephah of the best flour. It will be a regular grain offering. They must bring half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening." 
Leviticus6:21 They must mix it carefully with olive oil and they must cook it on a flat plate. Then they must break it into pieces and offer it to the Lord. The smell of it will give pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus6:22 The priest who must prepare the offering is Aaron's son that they anoint to take his father's place. This rule will continue for Aaron's descendants for all time. The priest must completely burn the whole offering as a gift to the Lord. 
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering that a priest gives belongs to the Lord completely. Nobody may eat any of it.’ 
Leviticus6:24 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus6:25 ‘Tell Aaron and his sons, “These are the rules about sin offerings. They must kill the animal for a sin offering at the place where they kill the animals for burnt offerings. It is a very holy offering that they give to the Lord." 
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers this sacrifice will eat his part of the animal. He must do that in a holy place, in the yard outside the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus6:27 If anyone touches the meat, they will be holy. If the blood splashes onto the clothes of a person, he must wash them in a holy place." 
Leviticus6:28 If a person uses a clay pot to cook the meat, he must then break the pot into pieces. If a person uses a metal pot to cook the meat, he must rub the pot very well to make it clean. Then he must wash it with clean water." 
Leviticus6:29 The meat is very holy. Only a male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat it. 
Leviticus6:30 But it is different if they take some blood from the sin offering into the Tent of Meeting. If they do that to make people clean from their sin, nobody may eat any meat from the offering. Instead, the priest must burn the animal completely in the fire.”" 
Leviticus7:1 Tell Aaron, “These are the rules about guilt offerings. It is a most holy offering." 
Leviticus7:2 They must kill the animal for a guilt offering at the place where they kill the animals for burnt offerings. The priest must throw the blood onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus7:3 The person who brings the offering must offer all the fat from the animal. That includes the fat part of its tail and the fat that is around its inside parts. 
Leviticus7:4 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is on them. He must also remove the bottom part of the liver. He must offer these to the Lord. 
Leviticus7:5 Then the priest must burn all these parts of the animal on the altar as a gift to the Lord. It is a guilt offering. 
Leviticus7:6 Any male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat the meat that remains. They must eat it in a holy place because it is very holy. 
Leviticus7:7 These rules are the same for sin offerings and for guilt offerings. The meat belongs to the priest who offers the animal to make a person clean from their sin. 
Leviticus7:8 But it is different with burnt offerings. The skin of the animal that the priest burns on the altar belongs to that priest. 
Leviticus7:9 If people bring a grain offering that they have cooked in an oven or on a flat plate, that offering belongs to the priest who offers it to the Lord." 
Leviticus7:10 It is different if the grain offering has dry grain, or the person has mixed the grain with olive oil. The grain from those offerings belongs to all the sons of Aaron, in equal parts." 
Leviticus7:11 These are the rules about sacrifices for friendship offerings which people may offer to the Lord. 
Leviticus7:12 If they do it to thank the Lord, they must bring these things together with the animal for the sacrifice: flat loaves of bread that have olive oil mixed in the flour, flat biscuits that have olive oil on them, and round loaves that have olive oil mixed with the best flour. None of these may have any yeast in them." 
Leviticus7:13 For their offering, they must also bring loaves of bread that have yeast in the flour." 
Leviticus7:14 They must offer one of each kind of these grain offerings to the Lord. These belong to the priest who throws the blood on the side of the altar for the friendship offering. 
Leviticus7:15 The people must eat the meat of the animal for the friendship offering on the same day that they offer it. They must not keep any of the meat until the next day. 
Leviticus7:16 It is different if people bring a friendship offering because of a promise that they have made to the Lord. Or if they do it because they choose to make a gift to the Lord. These people may eat some of the meat on the day that they offer the sacrifice. They may also eat the meat that remains on the next day. 
Leviticus7:17 But if any meat still remains on the third day, they must burn it completely." 
Leviticus7:18 If people eat any of the meat on the third day, the Lord will not accept their offering. The meat has become unclean, so God will not bless them for their offering. Instead, he will punish anyone who eats it on the third day." 
Leviticus7:19 If any of the meat touches something that is unclean, nobody may eat it. Instead, they must burn it completely. But anyone who is clean may eat the meat that remains." 
Leviticus7:20 Anyone who has not made themselves clean must not eat the meat of the sacrifice for a friendship offering. If they do that, they will no longer belong with Israel's people." 
Leviticus7:21 You become unclean if you touch anything that is unclean from a human, or from an animal or any other disgusting thing. If that happens, you must not eat the meat of the sacrifice for the friendship offering which belongs to the Lord. Anyone who eats it will no longer belong with Israel's people.” ’" 
Leviticus7:22 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus7:23 ‘Tell Israel's people, “You must not eat any fat from bulls, sheep or goats." 
Leviticus7:24 You may find the body of an animal that has died by itself, or wild animals have killed it. You must never eat the fat of that animal, but you may use the fat for any other purpose." 
Leviticus7:25 You must not eat the fat of an animal that you have offered to the Lord as a gift. Anyone who does that will no longer belong to Israel's people. 
Leviticus7:26 Wherever you live, you must not eat the blood of any birds or animals." 
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who eats any blood will no longer belong to Israel's people.” ’ 
Leviticus7:28 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus7:29 ‘Tell Israel's people, “When anyone brings a sacrifice for a friendship offering to the Lord, they must bring part of it as a gift to the Lord." 
Leviticus7:30 They must carry it in their own hands to burn on the altar. They must bring the animal's fat and the meat around its ribs. They must lift these up as a special offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus7:31 The priest will then burn the fat on the altar. But the meat around the ribs will belong to Aaron and his sons. 
Leviticus7:32 The top part of the back right leg of the animal is a gift to the priest. 
Leviticus7:33 It will belong to the priest who offers the blood and the fat for the friendship offering. 
Leviticus7:34 I have taken these parts of the animals that the Israelites bring as their friendship offerings. Those are the ribs that are lifted up to me, and the top part of the right leg that is a gift to the priests. I have taken these parts from the Israelite people and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons. That will always be the part that belongs to them.” ’" 
Leviticus7:35 The Lord gave this part of the Israelites' offerings to Aaron and to his descendants. This started on the day that they became priests to serve the Lord. 
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded the Israelites to do this on the day that Moses anointed them as priests. This part of the offerings would always belong to the priests. 
Leviticus7:37 These are the rules for burnt offerings, grain offerings, sin offerings, guilt offerings, friendship offerings and offerings for new priests." 
Leviticus7:38 The Lord gave these rules to Moses on Mount Sinai. On that day, the Lord commanded Israel's people to start making these offerings to him in the Sinai Desert." 
Leviticus8:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus8:2 ‘Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Also bring their special clothes and the olive oil to anoint them as priests. Bring the bull for the sin offering and the two male sheep for sacrifices. Bring the basket of flat bread with no yeast in it. 
Leviticus8:3 Tell all the people to come together in front of the Tent of Meeting.’ 
Leviticus8:4 Moses did what the Lord had commanded him to do. All the Israelite people met at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. 
Leviticus8:5 Moses said to the people, ‘The Lord has commanded us to do this.’" 
Leviticus8:6 Moses told Aaron and his sons to stand in front of the people. He washed them with water. 
Leviticus8:7 Moses put the special shirt on Aaron. He tied the long belt around Aaron. He put the priest's robe on him. Then he put the ephod on him, with its belt. He used the belt to tie the ephod on Aaron." 
Leviticus8:8 He put the breastpiece on Aaron, and he put the Urim and Thummim in its pocket." 
Leviticus8:9 Moses put the linen hat on Aaron's head. He fixed the thin gold plate on the front of the hat, as a holy crown. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do." 
Leviticus8:10 Moses took some of the special olive oil. He splashed it on the tabernacle and on everything in it. He did this to show that they belonged to God. 
Leviticus8:11 He splashed oil on the altar seven times. He put oil on the altar's tools, on the large bowl for water and on its base. He did this to show that they belonged to God." 
Leviticus8:12 Then Moses poured some of the oil on Aaron's head to anoint him. This made Aaron ready to serve God as his priest. 
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put a robe on each of them. He tied long cloth belts around them. He put cloth hats on their heads. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do. 
Leviticus8:14 Then Moses brought the bull for the sin offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the bull's head. 
Leviticus8:15 He killed the bull. Moses took some of the blood on his fingers. He put the blood on the four horns of the altar to make the altar clean. He poured out the blood that remained on the ground in front of the altar. In this way, he made the altar holy. It was now ready to make sacrifices for sin offerings." 
Leviticus8:16 Moses removed the fat that was around the inside parts of the bull. He removed the bottom part of its liver, as well as its two kidneys and the fat around them. He burned all these parts on the altar." 
Leviticus8:17 But Moses took outside the camp the parts of the bull that remained. There he completely burned its skin, its meat and the food in its stomach. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do." 
Leviticus8:18 Moses then brought one of the male sheep to be a burnt offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head. 
Leviticus8:19 He killed the sheep. Moses splashed its blood onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus8:20 He cut the sheep into pieces. He burned its head, the pieces of meat and the fat on the altar." 
Leviticus8:21 He used water to wash its inside parts and its legs. He burned the whole sheep on the altar as a burnt offering to the Lord. The smell of this gift while it was burning gave pleasure to the Lord. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do. 
Leviticus8:22 Moses then brought the other male sheep to be a sacrifice for new priests. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head. 
Leviticus8:23 He killed the sheep. Moses took some of its blood. He put it on Aaron's right ear, on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot." 
Leviticus8:24 Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put blood on their right ears, on the thumbs of their right hands and on the toes of their right feet. Then he splashed the blood that remained onto the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus8:25 Moses took the fat part of the sheep's tail and the fat around its inside parts. He also took the bottom part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat. With all the sheep's fat, he took the top part of its right back leg." 
Leviticus8:26 Moses took one round loaf of bread from the basket of flat bread that belonged to the Lord. He also took one loaf that had olive oil mixed in the flour. And he took one flat biscuit. He put them on top of the fat parts of the sheep and its right leg. 
Leviticus8:27 Then Moses put all of them on the hands of Aaron and his sons. They lifted them up as a special offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus8:28 Moses took them from their hands and he burned them on the altar, on top of the burnt offering. This was an offering for new priests. The smell of this offering while it was burning gave pleasure to the Lord." 
Leviticus8:29 Then Moses took the meat that was around the sheep's ribs. He lifted it up as a special offering to the Lord. This part belonged to Moses, as the Lord had commanded him." 
Leviticus8:30 Moses took some of the oil and some of the blood from the altar. He shook them on Aaron and his clothes. He also shook it on Aaron's sons and on their clothes. In this way he made them holy and ready to serve the Lord. 
Leviticus8:31 Moses said to Aaron and to his sons, ‘Cook the meat that remains at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Eat the meat there, with the bread that is in the basket of bread for the offering. That is what the Lord has commanded you to do." 
Leviticus8:32 Completely burn any meat and bread that remain. 
Leviticus8:33 You must stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days. That is the time to prepare you to serve God as priests. You must not leave until the seven days have finished. 
Leviticus8:34 The things that we have done today are what the Lord has commanded us to do. We have done it to make you clean from your sins. 
Leviticus8:35 Remember to stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days and seven nights. Do everything that the Lord has commanded, so that he does not punish you with death. This is what the Lord has commanded me.’" 
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything that the Lord had told Moses they must do. 
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day, Moses told Aaron and his sons and the leaders of Israel to come." 
Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron, ‘Bring a young bull for a sin offering. And bring a male sheep for a burnt offering. They must be perfect animals. Bring them as an offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus9:3 Then tell Israel's people, “Bring a male goat for a sin offering. Bring a young cow and a lamb for a burnt offering. The cow and the lamb must be one year old and they must be perfect." 
Leviticus9:4 Also bring a bull and a male sheep for a friendship offering that you will offer as a sacrifice to the Lord. And bring a grain offering of flour that is mixed with olive oil. Do this because the Lord will appear to you today.” ’ 
Leviticus9:5 The people brought all the animals and the grain to the front of the Tent of Meeting as Moses had commanded. All the Israelite people stood together there to worship the Lord. 
Leviticus9:6 Moses said to the people, ‘The Lord has commanded you to do this so that his bright glory will appear to you.’" 
Leviticus9:7 Moses said to Aaron, ‘Go to the altar to offer your sin offering and your burnt offering to the Lord. Do that to make yourself clean from your sins and to make the people clean from their sins. Also offer the people's offerings to make them clean from their sins. The Lord has commanded you to do this.’" 
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron went to the altar. He killed the young bull that was a sin offering for himself. 
Leviticus9:9 Aaron's sons brought the blood to him. He put his finger in the blood and he put it on the four horns of the altar. He poured the blood that remained onto the ground in front of the altar. 
Leviticus9:10 Aaron took the bull's fat, its kidneys and the bottom part of its liver. He burnt these parts on the altar, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Leviticus9:11 But Moses took the meat and the skin of the bull outside the camp. There he completely burned it. 
Leviticus9:12 Then Aaron killed the male sheep for the burnt offering. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus9:13 His sons brought the pieces of the sheep to him, including its head. Aaron burned them on the altar." 
Leviticus9:14 He washed the sheep's inside parts and its legs. He burned them on top of the burnt offering on the altar. 
Leviticus9:15 Then Aaron brought to the altar the male goat which was the sin offering for the people. He killed it as a sacrifice to make the people clean, like the first offering." 
Leviticus9:16 Then Aaron brought the animal for the burnt offering. He offered it as a sacrifice in the proper way. 
Leviticus9:17 He also brought the grain offering. He took some of it in his hand. He burned it on the altar together with the burnt offering of that morning. 
Leviticus9:18 Aaron killed the bull and the male sheep which were friendship offerings for the people. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar. 
Leviticus9:19 His sons also took the fat pieces from the bull and from the male sheep. They took the fat part of the tail, the fat around the inside parts, the kidneys and the bottom part of the liver." 
Leviticus9:20 They put those pieces on the meat of the animal's ribs. Aaron burned the fat pieces of the animals on the altar as an offering. 
Leviticus9:21 Then Aaron lifted up the meat of the ribs and the top part of the right leg of each animal as a special offering to the Lord. He did this as Moses had commanded him to do. 
Leviticus9:22 Aaron had now finished making the sin offering, the burnt offering and the friendship offering. He lifted up his hands towards the people and he asked God to bless them. Then he came down from the altar." 
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting. When they came out, they asked God to bless the people. The bright glory of the Lord appeared to all the people." 
Leviticus9:24 Then the Lord sent a fire to completely burn the burnt offering and the fat pieces that were on the altar. When the people saw this, they shouted loudly. They fell down with their faces towards the ground." 
Leviticus10:1 Aaron's sons, Nadab and Abihu, took their pots which held hot coals. They put fire in the pots and they put incense on top. They used it as an offering to the Lord which was wrong. They did it in a way that the Lord had not commanded them to do." 
Leviticus10:2 The Lord suddenly sent fire to completely burn Nadab and Abihu. They died there in front of the Lord 's altar. 
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron, ‘This is what the Lord said: “People who serve me as priests must respect me. They must realize that I am holy. All the people must know that they should give me honour.” ’ Aaron did not say anything." 
Leviticus10:4 Then Moses told Mishael and Elzaphan to come to him. They were sons of Aaron's uncle, Uzziel. Moses said to them, ‘Come here and carry Nadab and Abihu away from the front of the holy tent. Take their bodies to a place outside the camp.’" 
Leviticus10:5 Nadab and Abihu were still wearing their special robes. Mishael and Elzaphan carried them outside the camp, as Moses had told them to do." 
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses spoke to Aaron and to his other sons, Eleazar and Ithamar. He told them, ‘Make your hair look nice. Do not tear your clothes. If you show that you are sad, you will die. The Lord will also be angry with all the people. But your relatives, the Israelite people, should weep. They should be sad because the Lord has killed Nadab and Abihu with fire." 
Leviticus10:7 Do not go away from the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. If you do that, you will die, because the Lord has anointed you as his priests.’ So Aaron and his sons did as Moses had said." 
Leviticus10:8 Then the Lord said to Aaron," 
Leviticus10:9 ‘Do not drink any wine before you go into the Tent of Meeting. Do not drink anything that contains alcohol. If you do, you will die. This rule will continue for ever, for all your descendants." 
Leviticus10:10 You must know the difference between things that are holy and things that are ordinary. You must know what is clean and what is unclean. 
Leviticus10:11 You must teach the Israelites all the laws that I told Moses to give to them.’ 
Leviticus10:12 Moses said to Aaron and to his sons who still lived, Eleazar and Ithamar, ‘Take the part of the grain offering that you did not burn as a gift to the Lord. Use it to make bread that has no yeast in it. Eat it beside the altar, because it is a very holy offering." 
Leviticus10:13 You must eat it in a holy place. It is the part of the grain offering that belongs to you and to your sons. The Lord has given it to you from his special gifts. These are the commands that the Lord has given to me. 
Leviticus10:14 The Lord has also given you parts of the offerings that are lifted up to him. He has given you the meat around the ribs and the top part of the back leg. You may eat these parts. Your sons and your daughters may eat them too. You may only eat these parts in a place that is clean. They belong to you and to your sons. The Lord has given them to you from the friendship offerings that the Israelites give to him. 
Leviticus10:15 Bring the top of the leg and the meat of the ribs, as well as the fat parts of the animal. Lift them up as a special gift to the Lord. These parts will belong to you and to your descendants. This is a rule that will continue for ever, as the Lord has commanded.’" 
Leviticus10:16 After that, Moses looked carefully for the male goat that was the sacrifice for the sin offering. He discovered that they had already burnt it on the fire. So Moses was angry with Aaron's sons, Eleazar and Ithamar." 
Leviticus10:17 Moses asked them, ‘Why did you not stay near the holy place to eat the meat of the sin offering? It is a very holy offering. The Lord gave you this sacrifice so that the people would not be guilty. It is a sacrifice to make the people clean from their sins."
