Leviticus10:18 Look! You did not bring the blood of the goat for the sacrifice into the holy place. So you should have eaten its meat near the holy tent, as I commanded you.’" 
Leviticus10:19 Aaron said to Moses, ‘Today the people brought their sin offering and their burnt offering to the Lord. But these terrible things have happened to me! If I had eaten the meat of the sin offering today, that would not have pleased the Lord.’" 
Leviticus10:20 When Moses heard Aaron's reply, he was not angry any more." 
Leviticus11:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Leviticus11:2 ‘Tell the Israelites, “These are the animals that you may eat from among the animals which live on the land." 
Leviticus11:3 You may eat animals which have feet in two separate parts. They must also be animals which bring their food back into their mouths and eat it again. 
Leviticus11:4 Some animals have feet in two separate parts but they do not eat their food twice. Other animals eat their food twice but their feet do not have two separate parts. You must not eat those animals. Camels eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So camels are not clean for you to eat. 
Leviticus11:5 Rock badgers also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So they are also unclean. 
Leviticus11:6 Rabbits also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. They are unclean too. 
Leviticus11:7 Pigs have feet that are in two completely separate parts. But pigs do not bring their food back to eat it a second time. So pigs are not clean for you to eat. 
Leviticus11:8 You must not eat the meat from these animals. You must not touch their dead bodies. They are unclean animals for you. 
Leviticus11:9 These are the rules about animals that live in rivers or in the sea. If they have both fins and scales on their bodies, you may eat them." 
Leviticus11:10 But you must not eat anything that does not have both fins and scales. That includes fish or any other animals that live in water. They are disgusting for you. 
Leviticus11:11 So do not eat their meat or touch their dead bodies. 
Leviticus11:12 Any fish or other animals that live in water and do not have both fins and scales are unclean. Keep away from them. 
Leviticus11:13 These are the rules about birds. These birds are disgusting so do not eat them: eagles, vultures," 
Leviticus11:14 buzzards, kites, all kinds of crows, different kinds of owls, seagulls, hawks, cormorants, ospreys, herons, hoopoes and bats." 
Leviticus11:15  
Leviticus11:16  
Leviticus11:17  
Leviticus11:18  
Leviticus11:19  
Leviticus11:20 These are the rules about insects that fly in the air and also walk on the ground. They are disgusting and you must not eat them. 
Leviticus11:21 But some of these insects have legs that they can use to jump. You may eat those kinds of insects. 
Leviticus11:22 You may eat any kinds of locusts, crickets and grasshoppers." 
Leviticus11:23 But you must not eat any other insects that fly and they also walk on the ground. 
Leviticus11:24 All these animals will make you unclean if you touch the dead body of one of them. If you do touch one, you will be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:25 If you carry a body of one of these animals, you must wash your clothes. But you will still be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:26 Do not touch an animal that does not have feet in two completely separate parts. Do not touch an animal that does not bring food back into its mouth to eat it again. If you touch the dead body of any of these animals, you become unclean." 
Leviticus11:27 Do not touch the dead body of an animal that has paws to walk on. If you do touch one, you will be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:28 If you carry a body of one of these animals, you must wash your clothes. But you will still be unclean until the evening. These animals are unclean for you." 
Leviticus11:29 These are the rules about animals that move near to the ground. These animals are unclean for you: moles, rats, different kinds of lizards, geckos and chameleons." 
Leviticus11:30  
Leviticus11:31 If you touch the dead body of one of them, you will be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:32 If one of them dies and it falls onto something, that thing becomes unclean too. That includes things made of wood, leather or sackcloth, whatever people use them for. You must put it into water. It will be unclean until the evening. Then it will be clean again." 
Leviticus11:33 If one of these dead animals falls into a clay pot, everything in the pot becomes unclean. You must break the pot." 
Leviticus11:34 If you pour water from the pot onto food, the food becomes unclean. Anything in the pot which you could drink also becomes unclean." 
Leviticus11:35 If one of these dead animals falls onto something, that thing becomes unclean, whatever it is. If it is an oven or a cooking pot, you must break it into pieces. It has become unclean and you must not use it." 
Leviticus11:36 But if one of these dead animals falls into a spring of water, the water will still be clean. The same is true if it falls into a well where people keep clean water. But the person who removes the dead animal will become unclean." 
Leviticus11:37 If one of these dead animals falls onto some dry seeds, they will still be clean. You may plant them in your fields." 
Leviticus11:38 But if the seeds are wet and the dead animal falls onto them, they become unclean. Do not use them." 
Leviticus11:39 An animal that is good for food might die. If anyone touches its dead body, he will be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:40 If anyone eats meat from it, he must wash his clothes. He will still be unclean until the evening. If a person carries the body of the dead animal, he must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until the evening." 
Leviticus11:41 You must not eat small animals that move across the ground. 
Leviticus11:42 This includes animals that move along the ground on their stomachs and animals that walk on four legs or on many legs. All those things that move near to the ground are disgusting. Do not eat them. 
Leviticus11:43 Do not allow those small animals to make you unclean. Do not become disgusting yourselves, as they are." 
Leviticus11:44 I am the Lord your God. So keep yourselves separate from these unclean things. You must be holy, because I am holy. Make yourselves holy. Do not allow any of these small animals that move across the ground to make you unclean." 
Leviticus11:45 I am the Lord. I brought you out of the land of Egypt so that I would be your God. So you must be holy, because I am holy." 
Leviticus11:46 These are the rules about animals which live on the land, about the birds, about all the animals that live in water and about all the animals which move across the ground." 
Leviticus11:47 You must learn the difference between animals that are unclean and animals that are clean. You must know which animals you may eat and which animals you must not eat.” ’ 
Leviticus12:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus12:2 ‘Tell the Israelites, “When a woman gives birth to a boy, she will be unclean for seven days. She is unclean, as she is when she is bleeding each month." 
Leviticus12:3 Eight days after the boy is born, they must circumcise him." 
Leviticus12:4 His mother must wait for 33 days after the birth before she becomes clean again. During this time, she must not touch anything that is holy. She must not go into the holy place. After this time, she will become clean." 
Leviticus12:5 If a woman gives birth to a girl, the mother will be unclean for two weeks. She is unclean, as she is when she is bleeding each month. She must wait for 66 days after the birth until she becomes clean again." 
Leviticus12:6 The mother must wait for the right number of days after she gives birth to a son or a daughter. When those days have finished, she must come to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. She must bring a lamb that is one year old for a burnt offering. She must also bring a pigeon or a dove for a sin offering." 
Leviticus12:7 The priest will offer the gifts to the Lord to make the woman clean again. These are the rules for a mother who gives birth to a boy or a girl. 
Leviticus12:8 If the mother does not have enough money to buy a lamb, she may bring two doves or two pigeons instead. One bird will be for a burnt offering. The other bird will be for a sin offering. The priest will offer these to the Lord to make the woman clean again.” ’" 
Leviticus13:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Leviticus13:2 ‘A person may have a mark on his skin like a sore or a boil. It might become a bad disease that he could give to other people. If he has a mark like that, you must bring him to Aaron or one of his sons, the priests." 
Leviticus13:3 The priest must look at the mark on the person's skin. The hair on the mark may have become white, and the mark may go deep under the skin. If that is so, the person has a bad disease in his skin. If the priest sees that, he must say that the person is unclean." 
Leviticus13:4 But the mark may be a bright place on the person's skin. It may not go deep under the skin. The hair on the mark may not be white. If those things are true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days." 
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the person's skin again. The mark may not have changed. It may not have grown bigger across the skin. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven more days." 
Leviticus13:6 On the seventh day, the priest must again look carefully at the person's skin. The mark may not show as clearly as before and it may not have grown any bigger. If that is true, the priest will say that the person is now clean from the disease. The mark on his skin is not dangerous. The person must wash his clothes and then he will be clean." 
Leviticus13:7 But after the person has shown his mark to the priest, it might grow bigger across his skin. If that happens, the person must go and show his skin to the priest a second time." 
Leviticus13:8 The priest must look carefully at the mark. If it has become bigger, the priest must say that the person is unclean. It is a bad disease of the skin." 
Leviticus13:9 If a person has a bad disease in his skin, you must bring him to the priest." 
Leviticus13:10 The priest must look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may be white and it may grow up above the skin. The hair on it may have become white. It may be a sore that has pus in it. 
Leviticus13:11 If those things are true, it is a disease that continues for a long time. The priest must say that the person is unclean. The priest does not need to keep him separate from other people. They already know that he is unclean." 
Leviticus13:12 It is different if the disease has grown completely across all the person's skin. The priest may see that it covers the whole body, from the person's head to his feet." 
Leviticus13:13 The priest must carefully look at the person's skin. If the skin has become white across the person's whole body, the priest must say that the person is clean." 
Leviticus13:14 But if there is a sore with pus anywhere on the person's skin, the person is unclean." 
Leviticus13:15 The priest must look carefully at the sore and say that the person is unclean. He still has a disease in his skin. 
Leviticus13:16 If the sore changes and it becomes white, the person must go to the priest." 
Leviticus13:17 The priest must look carefully at the skin. If the skin has become white, the priest will say that the person is now clean again. The disease has finished." 
Leviticus13:18 A person might have a boil on his skin. Then it might get better. 
Leviticus13:19 There may be a white mark that grows up above the skin where the boil had been. Or there may be a bright mark on the skin that seems to be red. If this happens, the person must go to the priest." 
Leviticus13:20 The priest will look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin and the hair on it may be white. If this is true, the priest must say that the person is unclean. The person has a bad disease in the place where the boil had been." 
Leviticus13:21 But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days." 
Leviticus13:22 If the mark continues to grow across the skin, the priest must say that the person is unclean. He has a bad disease." 
Leviticus13:23 But the mark may not grow bigger across the person's skin. This shows that it is a mark from the boil and it is not a bad disease. The priest must say that the person is clean. 
Leviticus13:24 A person may have burnt his skin. Because of that, there might be a mark on his skin that seems to be red or bright white." 
Leviticus13:25 The priest must look carefully at the mark. The hair on the bright mark may have become white. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin. This shows that the place where the person burnt himself has become a bad disease in his skin. The priest must say that the person is unclean. He might give the disease to other people. 
Leviticus13:26 But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days." 
Leviticus13:27 On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the mark. If the mark is growing bigger across the skin, the priest must say that the person is unclean. He has a bad disease." 
Leviticus13:28 But the mark may not have grown across the person's skin. It may not show so clearly. This shows that it is a mark from the place where the person burnt his skin. The priest must say that the person is clean. 
Leviticus13:29 A man or a woman may have a sore on their head or on their chin. 
Leviticus13:30 The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore may seem to go deep under the skin. It may have hair in it that is thin and yellow. If this is true, the priest must say that the person is unclean. This kind of sore is a bad disease on the person's head or on their chin." 
Leviticus13:31 But the sore may not go deep below the skin. There may be no hair in it that is black. If the priest sees that, he must keep the person separate from other people for seven days." 
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore may not have grown bigger. There may be no hair in it that is yellow. It may not go deep under the skin." 
Leviticus13:33 If that is true, the person must cut the hair off their head. But they must not cut the hair off where the sore is. Then the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven more days." 
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the sore again. The sore may not have grown bigger and it may not go deep under the person's skin. Then the priest must say that the person is clean. The person must wash their clothes and then they will be clean." 
Leviticus13:35 But after that, the sore may grow bigger again across the person's skin." 
Leviticus13:36 If that happens, the priest must look carefully at the sore. If it has become bigger, the person is unclean. The priest does not need to look for yellow hair." 
Leviticus13:37 But the sore may not have changed. Black hair may have started to grow in it. If that is true, the disease has left the person. The priest must say that the person is clean." 
Leviticus13:38 A man or a woman may have white marks on their skin. 
Leviticus13:39 The priest must look carefully at the marks. If the colour of the marks is not bright, it is not a dangerous disease in the skin. The person is clean." 
Leviticus13:40 The hair may disappear from a man's head until he is bald. That man is clean. 
Leviticus13:41 If the hair disappears from the front of the man's head until that part is bald, the man is clean." 
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a red sore on the bald place at the top or at the back of the man's head, it is a bad disease." 
Leviticus13:43 The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore in the bald place may grow up above the skin and it may seem to be red. If it looks like a skin disease that appears in other places of a person's body," 
Leviticus13:44 the man has a bad disease. He is unclean. The priest must say that the man is unclean because of the sore on his head. 
Leviticus13:45 Any person who has a bad disease in his skin must tear his clothes. He must not make his hair look nice. He must cover the lower part of his face. He must shout, “I am unclean.”" 
Leviticus13:46 All the time that he has the bad skin disease he will continue to be unclean. He must live in a separate place from other people, outside the camp." 
Leviticus13:47 Mould may grow on clothes that are made of wool or linen. 
Leviticus13:48 It may grow on any piece of linen or wool, or on anything made of leather." 
Leviticus13:49 If the mould on any of these things seems to be green or red, it is a bad mould. The person who has the piece of cloth must show it to the priest." 
Leviticus13:50 The priest must look carefully at the mould. He must keep the thing that has the mould on it separate from other things for seven days. 
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day, the priest must look at the mould again. If the place that has the mould is growing bigger, it is a dangerous mould. Whatever the thing is made of, cloth or leather, it is unclean." 
Leviticus13:52 The priest must burn the piece of cloth or the piece of leather which has the mould. Because it has a dangerous mould, the priest must use fire to destroy it." 
Leviticus13:53 When the priest looks at the piece of cloth or leather on the seventh day, the mould might not have grown bigger." 
Leviticus13:54 If that is true, he must tell the person to wash the thing that has the mould. Then he must keep it separate from other things for seven more days." 
Leviticus13:55 The priest must again look carefully at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it. If the mould still looks the same, the piece of cloth is unclean. Even if the mould has not grown bigger, the cloth is unclean. You must use fire to destroy the piece of cloth or leather. You must do this whether the mould is on the inside or the outside of it." 
Leviticus13:56 When the priest looks at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it, the mould may not show so clearly. If that happens, the priest must tear the part that has mould on it out of the piece of cloth or leather." 
Leviticus13:57 After that, if the mould appears again in the piece of cloth or the piece of leather, it is a dangerous mould that may destroy other things. You must use fire to destroy the thing that has the mould on it." 
Leviticus13:58 But if the mould disappears after you have washed it the first time, it is no longer dangerous. You must wash the piece of cloth or leather a second time. Then it will be clean.’" 
Leviticus13:59 These are the rules for mould that grows on cloth that is made of wool or linen. They are also the rules for mould that grows on leather things. They tell the priest how to decide if the things are clean or unclean. 
Leviticus14:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus14:2 ‘These rules are for a person who has had a bad disease in his skin. When the disease has finished, you must bring him to the priest to make him clean." 
Leviticus14:3 The priest must go outside the camp and he must look carefully at the person's skin. If the disease has gone away," 
Leviticus14:4 this is what the priest must do. He must tell someone to bring two birds that are alive. They must be birds that are good for food. They must also bring a piece of wood from a cedar tree, some red wool and a branch of hyssop. The priest will use these to make the person clean." 
Leviticus14:5 The priest will command someone to kill one of the birds. He must do that over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water. 
Leviticus14:6 The priest will take the living bird, the piece of cedar wood, the red wool and the hyssop. He will make them wet with the blood of the dead bird." 
Leviticus14:7 Then the priest will shake the blood over the person who had the disease. He will do this seven times. The priest will then say that the person is clean. He will let the living bird go so that it flies away. 
Leviticus14:8 The person who has become well from the disease must wash his clothes. He must cut off all his hair and wash his body with water. Then he will be clean. After that, he can go into the camp. But he must stay outside his tent for seven days." 
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day, he must completely cut off all his hair. That includes all the hair of his head and his face. He must wash his clothes. He must wash his body with water. Then he will be clean." 
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day, the person must bring two male lambs and one female lamb that is one year old. Both the animals must be perfect. He must also bring three tenths of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. And he must bring a cup of olive oil." 
Leviticus14:11 He must take these to the priest who is making him clean. The priest must tell the person to stand at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must bring his offerings to the Lord there. 
Leviticus14:12 The priest must take one of the male lambs and the cup of olive oil. He must lift them up to the Lord as a special gift. 
Leviticus14:13 He must kill the male lamb in the holy place where they kill the animals for sin offerings and burnt offerings. The guilt offering belongs to the priest, as the sin offering does. It is a very holy offering." 
Leviticus14:14 The priest must put some of the blood from the guilt offering on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some blood on the big toe of the person's right foot. 
Leviticus14:15 The priest must pour some of the olive oil from the cup into his own left hand. 
Leviticus14:16 He must put one finger of his right hand into the oil that is in his left hand. He must shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus14:17 The priest must then take the oil that remains in his left hand. He must put some of it on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some of the oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on top of the blood from the offering. 
Leviticus14:18 The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In this way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus14:19 Then the priest must kill the animal for the sin offering. This makes the person who had the disease clean from his sins. After that, the priest will kill the animal for the burnt offering." 
Leviticus14:20 He will offer this as a sacrifice on the altar, together with the grain offering. The priest will do all these things to make the person who had the disease completely clean." 
Leviticus14:21 If the person is too poor to bring all these offerings, he may bring these things instead. He must bring one male lamb as a guilt offering to lift up in front of the Lord. That will be an offering to make him clean from his disease. He must also bring a tenth of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. And he must bring a cup of olive oil." 
Leviticus14:22 The person must also bring two birds that he has enough money to buy. They must be doves or pigeons. One bird will be for a sin offering and the other one will be for a burnt offering. 
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day, the person must bring his offerings to the Lord. He must bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting to make himself clean." 
Leviticus14:24 The priest will take the male lamb and the cup of oil. He will lift them up as a special gift to the Lord. 
Leviticus14:25 Then he will kill the lamb which is the guilt offering. He will put some of its blood on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will put some of the blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot. 
Leviticus14:26 The priest will pour some of the oil into his own left hand. 
Leviticus14:27 He will put one finger of his right hand into the oil. He will shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus14:28 He will put some oil from his hand on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will also put some oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some oil on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on the same places that he put the blood from the guilt offering. 
Leviticus14:29 The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In that way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus14:30 Then the priest will offer the pigeons or the doves that the person has been able to buy. 
Leviticus14:31 One bird will be for a sin offering. The other will be for a burnt offering. The priest will offer these together with the grain offering. In this way the priest will make the person clean in front of the Lord. 
Leviticus14:32 These are the rules for a poor person who had a bad disease in his skin. These are the offerings he must bring to make himself clean if he is too poor to bring all the usual offerings.’ 
Leviticus14:33 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Leviticus14:34 ‘One day you will go into Canaan, the land that I am giving to you as your home. When you are living there, I may cause mould to come in one of your houses." 
Leviticus14:35 If a person finds mould in his house, he must tell the priest. He must say, “I can see something like a bad mould in my house.”" 
Leviticus14:36 The priest will tell the person to take everything out of the house. If the person does not do that, all the things in the house will become unclean. He must do it before the priest goes to look at the house." 
Leviticus14:37 The priest will look carefully at the walls of the house. He will see if the mould has green or red marks in it. He will see if the mould goes deep into the wall. 
Leviticus14:38 If the priest sees those things, he will go outside the house. He will lock it to stay shut for seven days." 
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day, the priest will return to the house. He will look carefully at the mould again. It may have grown bigger across the walls of the house." 
Leviticus14:40 If that has happened, the priest must say, “You must remove the stones that have mould on them. You must take them outside the town. You must put them in a special place for unclean things.”" 
Leviticus14:41 The person must then remove the clay that covers all the inside walls of the house. He must put the clay outside the town in a place for unclean things. 
Leviticus14:42 The person who lives in the house must get some other stones and some more clay. He will use those instead of the unclean stones and clay that he removed. 
Leviticus14:43 After he has done all those things, the person might see that the mould has come back again." 
Leviticus14:44 The priest will come to the house and look carefully at the mould. If the mould has grown again across the walls, this shows that it is a dangerous mould. The house is unclean." 
Leviticus14:45 The person must completely knock down his house. He must remove all the stones, the pieces of wood and the clay. He must put them all outside the town in a place for unclean things." 
Leviticus14:46 A person might go into the house during the time that the priest has shut it because of the mould. That person will become unclean until evening. 
Leviticus14:47 Anyone who sleeps in the house must wash their clothes. And anyone who eats in the house must also wash their clothes. 
Leviticus14:48 After the person has mended his house, the priest may see that the mould has not grown again. The priest will then say that the house is clean." 
Leviticus14:49 He must take two birds, a piece of wood from a cedar tree, some red wool and a branch of hyssop. He will use these to make the house clean." 
Leviticus14:50 He will kill one of the birds over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water. 
Leviticus14:51 The priest will then take the piece of cedar wood, the hyssop, the red wool and the other bird that is still alive. He will make them wet with the blood of the dead bird and the fresh water. He will shake the blood and water over the house seven times." 
Leviticus14:52 In this way, the priest will make the house clean. He will use the blood of the dead bird, the fresh water, the living bird, the piece of cedar wood, the hyssop and the red wool." 
Leviticus14:53 Then the priest will take the living bird outside the town. He will let the bird fly away over the fields. In this way he will make the house clean from the mould.’ 
Leviticus14:54 These are the rules for all kinds of bad disease. 
Leviticus14:55 They include rules for mould that grows on cloth or on the walls of a house. 
Leviticus14:56 They include rules for sores and boils and marks on someone's skin. 
Leviticus14:57 They show if a person has a dangerous disease in their skin. They show whether the person is clean or he is unclean. 
Leviticus15:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Leviticus15:2 ‘Tell Israel's people, “If any kind of pus is coming out of a man's body, it makes the man unclean." 
Leviticus15:3 It does not matter whether the pus continues to leave the man's body or it stays inside. It causes the man to be unclean. 
Leviticus15:4 If the man lies on a bed, it becomes unclean. Anything that the man sits on also becomes unclean." 
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches the bed must wash his clothes and his whole body. He will continue to be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:6 If anyone sits on anything that the man has sat on, they will also become unclean. So they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:7 Someone might touch the man who has pus coming from his body. If that happens, they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:8 The man who is unclean might spit on another person. Then the other person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:9 The man might sit on something to ride on a horse or a donkey. Whatever he sits on will become unclean. 
Leviticus15:10 Someone might touch or carry any of the things that the man has sat on. Then that person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:11 The unclean man might not wash his hands before he touches another person. The person that he touched then becomes unclean. That person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:12 If the unclean man touches a clay pot, you must break it. If he touches a wooden bowl, you must wash it with clean water." 
Leviticus15:13 The man who has had the pus coming from his body might become well again. He must count seven days after the pus has gone. Then he must wash his clothes. He must wash his whole body in clean water. Then he will be clean. 
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day, he must bring two birds to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must be doves or pigeons. He must give them to the priest to offer to the Lord." 
Leviticus15:15 The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the man clean before the Lord. The pus that came from his body no longer causes him to be unclean. 
Leviticus15:16 When semen comes out of a man by mistake, he must wash his whole body. He will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:17 He must wash any clothes or leather things that has his semen on them. They will be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:18 When a man has sex with a woman, some of his semen might splash out. If that happens, they must both wash their bodies. They will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:19 Blood comes out from a woman's body every month. When this happens, she will be unclean for seven days. If anyone touches her during this time, they will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:20 During the time that the woman is bleeding, if she lies on anything, it becomes unclean. And if she sits on anything, it becomes unclean." 
Leviticus15:21 If anyone touches her bed, they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:22 If anyone touches something that the woman has sat on, they too must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:23 This is true for anything that the woman has sat on, including her bed. If anyone touches any of these things, they will be unclean until evening." 
Leviticus15:24 A man might have sex with a woman during the time that she is bleeding. If her blood touches him, he will be unclean for seven days. Any bed that the man lies on also becomes unclean." 
Leviticus15:25 A woman is unclean all the time that blood is coming out of her body. This is true if it happens at a different time in the month. It is also true if it continues at the usual time for more than seven days. It is all the same. She is unclean. 
Leviticus15:26 The rule is the same as when she is bleeding at the usual time of the month. During any time that she is bleeding, anything that she sits on or she lies on becomes unclean." 
Leviticus15:27 Anyone who touches those things will also be unclean. They must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening. 
Leviticus15:28 When the woman stops bleeding, she must count seven days. Then she will be clean." 
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day, she must bring two birds to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must be doves or pigeons. She must give them to the priest." 
Leviticus15:30 The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the woman who had been bleeding clean before the Lord. 
Leviticus15:31 In this way you will keep Israel's people separate from things that make them unclean. They will not cause my tabernacle to become unclean. That is the place where I live among them. If they make that place unclean, I would punish them with death.” ’" 
Leviticus15:32 These are the rules for anyone who has any kind of pus that comes out of their body. Or it may be semen that comes from a man's sex part. These things cause him to be unclean. 
Leviticus15:33 They are also the rules for a woman during the time when she bleeds each month. They are the rules for any man or woman who becomes unclean because of these things. They are also the rules for a man who has sex with a woman during the time that she is unclean. 
Leviticus16:1 The Lord spoke to Moses after two of Aaron's sons had died. They had burned incense in front of the Lord in a way that was wrong. The Lord punished them with death. 
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Tell your brother Aaron that he must not go behind the curtain into the Most Holy Place of the tabernacle. He must not do that at any time that he wants to. I appear in a cloud over the lid of the Covenant Box that is in there. So Aaron will die if he goes in there when it is not the proper time." 
Leviticus16:3 This is what Aaron must do when he goes into the holy place. He must bring a young bull for a sin offering. He must also bring a male sheep for a burnt offering. 
Leviticus16:4 He must wash his whole body before he puts on his special clothes, because they are holy. He must wear a holy linen robe and linen trousers next to his skin. He must tie a linen belt around his body. He must put a linen hat on his head." 
Leviticus16:5 Israel's people must also give him two male goats for a sin offering and one male sheep for a burnt offering. 
Leviticus16:6 Aaron must offer the bull as a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins. 
Leviticus16:7 Then he must take the two goats to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. There he must offer them to the Lord. 
Leviticus16:8 He must throw special stones to decide what he should do with each goat. One stone will have a mark for the Lord. The other stone will have a mark for Azazel. 
Leviticus16:9 Aaron must take the goat that the stone showed is for the Lord. He must kill it as a sin offering. 
Leviticus16:10 But he must bring the other goat in front of the Lord while it is alive. He will send it away to escape into the desert. In that way, it will take away the sins of the people and it will make them clean." 
Leviticus16:11 Aaron must offer the bull which is for a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins. 
Leviticus16:12 He must take a pot of hot coals that burn on the altar in front of the Lord. He must fill both his hands with incense that has a sweet smell. He must take the hot coals and the incense with him behind the curtain. 
Leviticus16:13 Then he must put the incense onto the hot coals in front of the Lord. The smoke from the incense will rise up over the lid of the Covenant Box. If he does this, he will not die." 
Leviticus16:14 Then he must take the blood from the bull. He must use his finger to splash blood onto the lid of the Covenant Box. He must also splash some blood seven times with his finger in front of the Covenant Box. 
Leviticus16:15 Aaron must then kill the goat which is the sin offering for the people. He must take the goat's blood behind the curtain. He must use it in the same way that he used the bull's blood. He must shake it on the lid of the Covenant Box and in front of the Covenant Box. 
Leviticus16:16 In this way Aaron will make the Most Holy Place clean. He must do this because of the sins of Israel's people that make them unclean. He must do the same thing for the whole Tent of Meeting to make it clean. The tent stands among Israel's people who are unclean. 
Leviticus16:17 While Aaron is doing this, nobody may go into the Tent of Meeting. They must wait while he goes into the Most Holy Place to make everything clean. He must also make himself, his family and all the people of Israel clean from their sins. Everyone must wait outside until Aaron comes out of the Tent of Meeting." 
Leviticus16:18 When he comes out, he must go to the altar that stands in front of the Lord 's tent. He must make the altar clean. He must take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood. He must put some blood on all the four horns of the altar." 
Leviticus16:19 Then he must splash some of the blood on the altar seven times with his finger. In this way he will make it clean for the Lord to use. He must do this because Israel's people are unclean. 
Leviticus16:20 Aaron must then bring the goat that is alive in front of the Lord. He must do this after he has made clean the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting and the altar." 
Leviticus16:21 He must put his hands on the goat's head. He must speak aloud all the sins that Israel's people have done. He must say that they have been wicked and they have not obeyed the Lord. In this way he will put all their sins on the goat's head. Aaron must then tell a man that he has chosen to send the goat away into the desert. 
Leviticus16:22 The goat will carry all the sins of Israel's people far away to an empty place. The man must send it away into the desert. 
Leviticus16:23 After this, Aaron must go into the Tent of Meeting. He must take off his special linen clothes. He had put them on when he went into the holy tent. He must leave the clothes there." 
Leviticus16:24 He must wash his whole body in a holy place. He must put on his own clothes and go out of the tent. Then he will offer the animals for his own burnt offering and the burnt offering for the people. In this way, he will make himself clean and the people clean from their sins." 
Leviticus16:25 Then he must burn on the altar the fat from the sin offering. 
Leviticus16:26 The man who sent the goat away to escape must wash his clothes and his whole body. Then he may return to the camp. 
Leviticus16:27 Someone must carry the dead bodies of the animals for the sin offerings outside the camp. That is the bull and the goat that Aaron had killed. He used their blood to make the Most Holy Place clean. The man who takes them outside the camp must completely burn them. That includes their skin, their meat and their inside parts." 
Leviticus16:28 After that, the man must wash his clothes and his whole body. Then he may return to the camp." 
Leviticus16:29 On the tenth day of the seventh month, you must not eat and you must not do any work. This is a rule that you must obey every year for all time. It is a rule for you who belong to Israel's people and for foreign people who live among you." 
Leviticus16:30 It will be the Day of Atonement. On this day the priest will make you clean. The Lord will forgive you for all your sins. 
Leviticus16:31 It is a very holy day of complete rest for you. You must not eat during that day. This is a rule that you must all obey every year. 
Leviticus16:32 The descendant of Aaron who is chosen as leader of the priests will do all these things on the Day of Atonement. He will put on the linen clothes that are holy. 
Leviticus16:33 He will make clean the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting and the altar. He will make the priests and all the people clean from their sins." 
Leviticus16:34 Once every year, on this day, he will make Israel's people clean from all their sins. This is a rule that you must obey for all time.’ Moses did all these things as the Lord had commanded him." 
Leviticus17:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus17:2 ‘Speak to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people. Tell them, “This is what the Lord has commanded." 
Leviticus17:3 If any Israelite wants to kill a bull, a lamb or a goat as a sacrifice, he must bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must bring it there as an offering to the Lord. If he kills the animal anywhere else in the camp or outside the camp, he is guilty of a sin. He has poured out the animal's blood in a wrong way. He can no longer belong among Israel's people." 
Leviticus17:4  
Leviticus17:5 This rule will stop the people killing animals as sacrifices to the Lord in the fields. Instead, they must bring their offerings to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must kill the animals there as friendship offerings to the Lord." 
Leviticus17:6 The priest must splash the animal's blood on the Lord 's altar at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must also burn the animal's fat. It will make a smell that gives pleasure to the Lord. 
Leviticus17:7 The people must be faithful to the Lord. They must no longer worship the idols of goats and offer sacrifices to them. The people and their descendants must always obey this rule.” 
Leviticus17:8 You must also tell the people, “An Israelite or a foreigner may want to kill an animal as a burnt offering or as another sacrifice." 
Leviticus17:9 If so, he must bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must offer it there to the Lord. If he does not do that, he can no longer belong among Israel's people." 
Leviticus17:10 Any Israelite or a foreigner who lives among you must not eat any blood. If anyone does that, I will turn against that person. I will remove him so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people." 
Leviticus17:11 Everything that lives has life because of its blood. I have told you to offer blood on the altar. That is how you become clean from your sins. The animal's blood is the life that makes you clean. 
Leviticus17:12 For this reason I have told Israel's people that you must not eat blood. And no foreign person who lives among you may eat blood either. 
Leviticus17:13 Israelites or foreigners who live among them may catch a wild animal or a bird. If it is clean to eat, the person who catches it must pour its blood on the ground. He must cover the blood with some soil." 
Leviticus17:14 All living things have life because of their blood. So I have told the Israelites that they must not eat the blood of any living thing. Anyone who eats blood can no longer belong among my people. 
Leviticus17:15 An Israelite or a foreigner may find the body of an animal that has died by itself. Or it may be the body of an animal that wild animals have killed. If he eats the meat of that animal, he must wash his clothes and his whole body. He will be unclean until evening. Then he will become clean." 
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash his clothes and his whole body, he will receive the punishment that he deserves.” ’" 
Leviticus18:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus18:2 ‘Speak to Israel's people. Say to them, “I am the Lord your God." 
Leviticus18:3 You have been living in Egypt, but you must not do the things that the people of Egypt do. I will soon take you to live in the land of Canaan. You must not do the things that the people of Canaan do. You must not live in the way that they think is right." 
Leviticus18:4 Instead, you must live in the way that I say is right. You must obey my laws. I am the Lord your God." 
Leviticus18:5 You must obey my laws and my teaching. Anyone who does that will continue to live. I am the Lord.” 
Leviticus18:6 Tell them this: “You must not have sex with anyone who belongs to your own family. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus18:7 Do not have sex with your mother. That would cause your father to be ashamed. 
Leviticus18:8 Do not have sex with any wife of your father. Your father would be ashamed. 
Leviticus18:9 Do not have sex with your sister. She may be your mother's daughter or your father's daughter. She may have been born in your house or in another house. It does not matter. You must not have sex with any sister. 
Leviticus18:10 Do not have sex with any granddaughter. That would make you ashamed. 
Leviticus18:11 Do not have sex with the daughter of your father and any of his wives. She is your sister so you must not have sex with her. 
Leviticus18:12 Do not have sex with your father's sister. She belongs to your father's family. 
Leviticus18:13 Do not have sex with your mother's sister. She belongs to your mother's family. 
Leviticus18:14 Do not have sex with the wife of your father's brother. That would make your uncle ashamed. His wife is your aunt. You must not try to get near to her. 
Leviticus18:15 Do not have sex with your son's wife. 
Leviticus18:16 Do not have sex with your brother's wife. That would make your brother ashamed. 
Leviticus18:17 Do not have sex with both a woman and her daughter. And do not take the woman's son's daughter or her daughter's daughter so that you can have sex with any of them. They belong to the same family. It would be a very wicked thing to do. 
Leviticus18:18 If your wife is still alive, do not marry her sister as a second wife. While your wife lives, you must not have sex with her sister." 
Leviticus18:19 Do not have sex with a woman at the time when she bleeds each month. She is unclean during that time. 
Leviticus18:20 Do not have sex with the wife of another man. You would become unclean. 
Leviticus18:21 Do not give any of your children as an offering to the god Molech. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord! 
Leviticus18:22 A man must not have sex with another man as he would have sex with a woman. That is a disgusting thing to do. 
Leviticus18:23 Do not have sex with any animal. That would make you unclean. A woman must not offer herself to an animal to have sex with it. She would be doing a very bad thing. 
Leviticus18:24 Do not do any of these things that would make you unclean. The nations who live in Canaan make themselves unclean with all these things. I will chase out those people so that you can live in their land. 
Leviticus18:25 The land itself has become unclean because the people did these things. So I am punishing those people for their sins. The land has thrown out the people who lived on it. 
Leviticus18:26 But you yourselves must obey my laws and my rules. Do not do any of these wicked things. That includes the foreign people who live among you. 
Leviticus18:27 The people who lived in Canaan before you did these disgusting things. Because of that, the land became unclean." 
Leviticus18:28 So do not make the land unclean, as those people have done. Do not cause the land to throw you out, as it did to the nations who lived there before you." 
Leviticus18:29 If anyone does any of these disgusting things, they can no longer belong among Israel's people." 
Leviticus18:30 You must obey what I have commanded you to do. Do not live in the disgusting way that these other people have lived. Do not make yourselves unclean like that. I am the Lord your God.” ’ 
Leviticus19:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus19:2 ‘Speak to all Israel's people. Tell them, “You must be holy because I am the Lord your God and I am holy." 
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must respect his mother and his father. You must keep the Sabbath day as a day of rest. I am the Lord your God. 
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn away from me to worship idols. Do not use metal to make images of gods for yourselves. I am the Lord your God. 
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a friendship offering as a sacrifice to me, do it in the right way. Then I will accept your gift." 
Leviticus19:6 When you kill an animal for the sacrifice, eat its meat on the same day. You may eat what remains on the second day but you must completely burn any meat that remains on the third day." 
Leviticus19:7 If anyone eats the meat on the third day, it will not please me. I will not accept their offering." 
Leviticus19:8 The person who has eaten the meat on the third day deserves punishment. He has caused a gift that is holy to the Lord to become unclean. That person can no longer belong to Israel's people. 
Leviticus19:9 At harvest time, when you cut the crops on your land, do not cut to the edge of your field. Do not pick up the grain that falls onto the ground." 
Leviticus19:10 Do not pick all the grapes off your vines. Do not pick up the grapes that have fallen onto the ground. Leave some of these for the poor people and for the foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God. 
Leviticus19:11 You must not take things that belong to other people. You must not deceive other people or tell them lies. 
Leviticus19:12 Do not use my name to promise something that you will not do. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus19:13 Do not cheat anyone. Do not rob anyone. If you are paying someone to work for you, pay them the same day." 
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse a deaf person. Do not put something in front of a blind person, to make them fall down. Respect and obey me as your God. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus19:15 When you judge other people, do this in a way that is fair. Do not be more kind to a rich person than to a poor person. You must be fair to everyone." 
Leviticus19:16 Do not say bad things about anyone for other people to hear. If somebody is in danger of death, speak the truth to help them if you can. Do not keep quiet. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus19:17 You must not think of ways to hurt another person. If someone has done a wrong thing, warn them about it. Then you will not be guilty of a sin yourself." 
Leviticus19:18 Do not try to punish another Israelite who has done something wrong against you. Do not try to hurt them. Instead, you must love other people as much as you love yourself. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus19:19 You must obey my rules. You must not allow two different kinds of animals to have babies together. Do not put two different kinds of seeds in one field. Do not wear clothes that are made from two different kinds of material. 
Leviticus19:20 If a man has chosen a slave girl to marry, no other man may have sex with her. She may not have become a free woman. If so, any other man who has sex with her must pay money to the first man. But you must not punish them with death, because the girl was still a slave." 
Leviticus19:21 The man must bring a male sheep to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. That will be his guilt offering. 
Leviticus19:22 The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the man clean from his sin. Then the Lord will forgive the man for the sin that he has done. 
Leviticus19:23 When you go into Canaan, you will plant trees that have fruit to eat. But the fruit will not be clean for you to eat. You must not eat the fruit for three years." 
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year, all the fruit will be holy. Use it as an offering to praise the Lord." 
Leviticus19:25 In the fifth year, you may eat the fruit from your trees. If you do this, you will have more food to eat at harvest time. I am the Lord your God." 
Leviticus19:26 You must not eat any meat that still has blood in it. Do not use magic to learn about future times or other things. 
Leviticus19:27 Do not cut the hair from the sides of your head or from the edges of your beard. 
Leviticus19:28 Do not cut marks in your body to show that you are sad about a dead person. Do not cut pictures into your skin. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus19:29 Do not cause your daughter to be a prostitute. That would bring shame on her. It would also cause many people to turn away from me and do wicked things. 
Leviticus19:30 You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. Respect the holy place where you meet with me. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus19:31 Do not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask other people to do this for you. That will make you unclean. I am the Lord your God. 
Leviticus19:32 You must stand up to give honour to old people. Respect and obey me, your God. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus19:33 Do not be cruel to foreign people who live among you. 
Leviticus19:34 Be kind to them, as you are to your own people. Love foreigners as much as you love yourselves. Remember that you yourselves lived in Egypt as foreigners. I am the Lord your God." 
Leviticus19:35 Always be fair when you measure things. When you measure how long, how heavy or how big something is," 
Leviticus19:36 always use correct weights and measures. Use the correct size of bowl to measure grain or oil. I am the Lord your God. I led you out of the land of Egypt. 
Leviticus19:37 You must always obey all my laws and my rules. I am the Lord.” ’ 
Leviticus20:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus20:2 ‘Speak to Israel's people. Say to them, “Nobody among you must ever give any of his children as an offering to the god Molech. That includes you Israelites and the foreign people who live in Israel. If anyone does that, you must punish him with death. Israel's people must throw stones at him to kill him." 
Leviticus20:3 I myself will turn against that person so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people. He deserves that punishment because he has offered some of his children to Molech. He has caused my holy place to become unclean. He has brought shame to my holy name. 
Leviticus20:4 The people of that man's town may decide not to punish him. He has offered some of his children to Molech, but they might refuse to punish him with death." 
Leviticus20:5 If that happens, I myself will turn against that man and his family. I will cause him to become separate from his own people. I will do that to everyone who turns away from me to worship Molech as he has done." 
Leviticus20:6 You must not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask anyone to do that for you. Anyone who does that is no longer faithful to me. I will turn against that person so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people. 
Leviticus20:7 I am the Lord your God, so you must be faithful to me. You must be holy." 
Leviticus20:8 You must always be careful to obey my laws. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy. 
Leviticus20:9 If anyone curses his mother or his father, you must punish him with death. He deserves to die for his sin." 
Leviticus20:10 If a man has sex with another man's wife you must punish both of them with death. 
Leviticus20:11 If a man has sex with his father's wife, he has caused his father to be ashamed. You must punish both of them with death. They deserve to die for their sin." 
Leviticus20:12 If a man has sex with his son's wife, you must punish both of them with death. They have done a very bad thing. They deserve to die for their sin." 
Leviticus20:13 If a man has sex with another man, you must punish both of them with death. They have done a disgusting thing together. They deserve to die for their sin." 
Leviticus20:14 If a man marries a woman and her mother, that is a wicked thing. You must burn the man and both the women in a fire until they die. That will stop wicked things like that from happening among you." 
Leviticus20:15 If a man has sex with an animal, you must punish him with death. You must also kill the animal." 
Leviticus20:16 If a woman offers herself to an animal to have sex with it, you must punish her with death. You must also kill the animal. They both deserve to die for this sin." 
Leviticus20:17 If a man takes his sister to have sex with her, it is a terrible sin. It does not matter whether she is the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother. You must remove them from among their people. The man has brought shame on his sister. He must receive the punishment that he deserves." 
Leviticus20:18 A man must not have sex with a woman during the time when she is bleeding. If he does that, they have had sex while she is unclean They can no longer belong among Israel's people." 
Leviticus20:19 A man must not have sex with his mother's sister or with his father's sister. If he does that, he has brought shame on someone from his own family. They must receive the punishment that they deserve." 
Leviticus20:20 If a man has sex with his aunt, he brings shame on his uncle. They must receive the punishment that they deserve for their sin. Neither of them will ever have any children." 
Leviticus20:21 If a man marries his brother's wife, that makes him unclean. Neither of them will have any children. The man has brought shame on his brother." 
Leviticus20:22 You must always obey all my laws and my rules. If you do that, the land where I am taking you to live will not throw you out." 
Leviticus20:23 I will chase out the nations who live there now, so that you can live there. You must not live in the way that those people live. I hate them because of all the disgusting things that they have done." 
Leviticus20:24 I have told you that the land will belong to you as your home. It is a land where there is plenty of food and drink, enough for everyone. I am the Lord your God. I have chosen you to be separate from other people." 
Leviticus20:25 So you must know the difference between animals that are clean and animals that are unclean. You must know the difference between birds that are clean and birds that are unclean. You must not eat anything that I have told you is unclean. That includes any small thing that moves across the ground. 
Leviticus20:26 I am the Lord and I am holy. So you must be holy as my people. I have chosen you to be separate from other people to belong to me. 
Leviticus20:27 Any man or woman who talks with the spirits of dead people must die. Anyone who uses magic to learn about future times must also die. You must throw stones at that person until they die. They deserve to die for their sin.” ’ 
Leviticus21:1 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Speak to Aaron's sons, the priests. Tell them, “A priest must not touch a dead body. That would make him unclean among his people." 
Leviticus21:2 But if it is someone from his family who has died, he may touch their body. It may be his mother, his father, his son, his daughter or his brother." 
Leviticus21:3 He may touch the dead body of his sister if she has no husband to take care of her. The priest may make himself unclean for her. 
Leviticus21:4 But a priest must not touch the dead body of someone who belongs to his family because of marriage. He must not make himself unclean for that person. 
Leviticus21:5 A priest must not cut the hair off his head. He must not cut the edges of his beard. He must not cut marks in his body. 
Leviticus21:6 Priests must be holy to serve me. They must not bring shame to the name of their God. They are the people who offer gifts to the Lord. They bring food to their God. So they must be holy people. 
Leviticus21:7 A priest must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. He must not marry a woman who has become separate from her husband. A priest must be holy to serve his God. 
Leviticus21:8 You must respect priests as holy people, because they offer food to your God. I am the Lord and I am holy. I am the one who causes you to be holy." 
Leviticus21:9 If a priest's daughter becomes a prostitute, she is unclean. She has brought shame on her father. You must burn her in a fire until she dies." 
Leviticus21:10 They have poured special oil on the head of the leader of the priests. He is able to wear the special clothes for priests. This shows that he is greater than his brothers. So he must always make his hair look nice. He must not tear his clothes. 
Leviticus21:11 He must not go anywhere that is near a dead body. Even if it is his father or his mother who has died, he must not make himself unclean for them." 
Leviticus21:12 He must not leave the holy place to go to a funeral. That would cause the holy place to be unclean. The oil that they poured on his head shows that I have chosen him as my servant. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus21:13 The leader of the priests must marry a woman who has not had sex before. 
Leviticus21:14 He must not marry a widow or a woman who has become separate from her husband. He must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. Instead, he must choose a Levite woman who has not had sex before." 
Leviticus21:15 I, the Lord, have caused priests to be holy. So he must not marry a woman who would cause his children to be unclean.” ’" 
Leviticus21:16 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus21:17 ‘Tell Aaron, “Your descendants may only serve me as priests if they have a perfect body. If not, they must not bring offerings of food to their God. This rule will continue for all time." 
Leviticus21:18 No man may serve me if he has anything wrong with his body. He must not be blind. He must not have a weak leg or one leg that is shorter than the other leg. He must not have any part of his face that is the wrong shape. 
Leviticus21:19 He must not have an arm or a leg that is broken. 
Leviticus21:20 He must not have a back that is not straight. He must not be very small and thin. He must not have an eye disease or a skin disease. He must not have testicles that are spoiled. 
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron who has something wrong with his body may serve me as a priest. He may not offer gifts to me, the Lord. His body is not perfect, so he may not bring offerings of food to his God." 
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the holy food offerings and the most holy food offerings. 
Leviticus21:23 But he must not go near the curtain in the Holy Place. He must not go up to the altar. This is because he has something wrong with his body. He must not make the holy places unclean. I am the Lord. I cause the priests to be holy.” ’ 
Leviticus21:24 Moses told all these rules to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people." 
Leviticus22:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus22:2 ‘Tell Aaron and his sons to be careful with the holy offerings from Israel's people. Those are gifts that the people offer to me. The priests must not spoil the honour of my holy name. I am the Lord. Tell them this, as a rule that will continue for ever:" 
Leviticus22:3 No priest may come near to the holy offerings when he is unclean. Those are gifts that Israel's people have offered to me, the Lord. If any unclean priest goes near them, he can never serve me again. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus22:4 If any descendant of Aaron is unclean, he must not eat the holy offerings until he becomes clean again. He may be unclean because of a skin disease, or some kind of pus is coming out from his body. He may have touched something that has touched a dead body and is unclean. He may have touched a man whose semen has come out from his sex part." 
Leviticus22:5 He may have touched an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. He may have touched a person who is unclean for any reason. All these things will cause the priest himself to become unclean. 
Leviticus22:6 Any priest who touches any of these unclean things will remain unclean until evening. He must not eat any of the holy offerings until he has washed his whole body. 
Leviticus22:7 When the sun goes down, he will be clean. Then he can eat the holy offerings which are his food." 
Leviticus22:8 A priest may find the body of an animal that has died by itself, or wild animals have killed it. He must not eat the meat of that animal. It will make him unclean. I am the Lord." 
Leviticus22:9 Priests must obey my commands. If they do not obey them, they will be guilty of sin. They will die because they have not respected my commands. I am the Lord. I cause the priests to be holy." 
Leviticus22:10 Only people who belong to a priest's family may eat any of the holy offerings. No visitor who is living in the priest's house may do that. No servant that the priest pays to work for him may do that. 
Leviticus22:11 But a slave that the priest has bought for himself may eat the holy offerings. And a slave that was born in the priest's house may eat them. 
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter marries a man who is not a priest, she may not eat the holy offerings." 
Leviticus22:13 But if her husband dies or she becomes separate from him, it is different. She may have no children to take care of her. Then she may return to live in her father's home where she lived when she was young. If so, she is allowed to eat some of the food that her father receives as a priest. Only someone who belongs to a priest's family may eat that food." 
Leviticus22:14 Someone might eat a holy offering by mistake. If that happens, he must pay the priest for that food. He must also add one fifth of its value." 
Leviticus22:15 The priests must always respect the holy offerings as special food. They are gifts which Israel's people offer to the Lord. 
Leviticus22:16 If the priests allow other people to eat the holy offerings, those people are guilty of sin. They will receive punishment. I am the Lord and I cause the offerings to be holy.’" 
Leviticus22:17 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus22:18 ‘Speak to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people. Tell them, “This is the rule about burnt offerings from the people of Israel or from the foreign people who live among them. It may be an offering that they have promised to give to the Lord. Or it may be an offering that they choose to give." 
Leviticus22:19 I will only accept your gift if it is a perfect male animal. It can be a bull, a sheep or a goat." 
Leviticus22:20 You must not offer to me any animal that has something wrong with it. If it is not perfect, I will not accept it as a gift from you." 
Leviticus22:21 This is also the rule if someone brings an animal as a sacrifice for a friendship offering. He may do that because of a special promise. Or he may do it because he chooses to give it. It may be a bull, a sheep or a goat. I will only accept it if the animal is perfect. It must not have anything wrong with it." 
Leviticus22:22 You must not offer to the Lord an animal that is blind. It must not have a broken bone or a body that has become spoiled. It must not have sores or any other disease on its skin. You must not offer on the altar any animal like that as a gift to the Lord. 
Leviticus22:23 You may bring a bull or a sheep that has a leg which is longer or shorter than the other legs. I will accept that animal as a gift that you choose to bring to me. I will not accept that animal as an offering that you make because of a promise. 
Leviticus22:24 You must not offer to the Lord an animal that has something wrong with its testicles. I will not accept the animal if anyone has hurt its testicles or removed them. You must never hurt the testicles of any animal in your land. 
Leviticus22:25 You must not offer to me animals like that which you have bought from a foreign person. I will not accept them as a gift of food from you. Those animals have bodies which are spoiled. They are not perfect.” ’ 
Leviticus22:26 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus22:27 ‘When a calf, a lamb or a goat is born, it must stay with its mother for seven days. From the eighth day, you may offer it as a gift to the Lord. I will accept it." 
Leviticus22:28 You must not kill a cow or a sheep on the same day that you kill its baby. 
Leviticus22:29 When you bring an animal as a sacrifice to thank the Lord for something, offer it in the proper way. Then I will accept it as your gift." 
Leviticus22:30 You must eat the animal's meat on the same day that you offer it. None of the meat may remain until the next morning. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus22:31 You must be careful to obey my commands. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus22:32 You must not bring shame to my holy name. You people of Israel must know that I am holy as I live among you. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy. 
Leviticus22:33 I am the one who brought you out of Egypt, so that I would be your God. I am the Lord.’" 
Leviticus23:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus23:2 ‘Tell this to Israel's people: These are the special days and festivals that I have chosen. At these times you must meet together to worship me. 
Leviticus23:3 In each week, there are six days when you can do your work. The seventh day is the Sabbath day. On that day you must rest. You must not do any work. It is a holy day when you meet together to worship the Lord. This is the rule in all the places where you live." 
Leviticus23:4 There are also times that I have chosen for festivals. They are holy days when you must meet together to worship me. 
Leviticus23:5 The Passover festival will be on the 14th day of the first month of each year. It will begin when the sun goes down on that day. It is a time to give honour to the Lord. 
Leviticus23:6 On the next day, the 15th day of the first month, the festival of Flat Bread will begin, to give honour to the Lord. For seven days you must eat bread that has no yeast in it." 
Leviticus23:7 On the first of those seven days, you must all meet together to worship the Lord. You must not do your usual work." 
Leviticus23:8 For seven days you must offer sacrifices as a gift to the Lord. On the seventh day you must meet together again to worship the Lord. You must not do your usual work on that day.’ 
Leviticus23:9 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus23:10 ‘Tell Israel's people, “When you go into the land that I will give you, crops will grow there. When you bring home the crops at harvest time, you must take some of the first grain to the priest." 
Leviticus23:11 He will lift up the grain as a special offering to the Lord. The Lord will accept your gift. The priest will do this on the day after the Sabbath day. 
Leviticus23:12 On the same day, you must also bring a perfect lamb that is one year old. That will be a burnt offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus23:13 You must also bring two tenths of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil. That will be your gift to the Lord. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord. You must also offer 1 litre of wine as a drink offering. 
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat any of the new grain until you have done this. You must not use the grain to make bread. You must not cook it. Wait until the day that you bring the offering to your God. Then you may eat the grain. This is a rule that will continue for ever, in all the places where you live." 
Leviticus23:15 Count seven weeks from the day when the priest has made this offering of grain. 
Leviticus23:16 You must count until the day after the seventh Sabbath day. That will be 50 days. Then you must bring another grain offering to the Lord. 
Leviticus23:17 Each family must bring from their homes two loaves of bread as a special gift to the Lord. You must use two tenths of an ephah of good flour and yeast to make the bread. This is a gift to the Lord from the first crops of your harvest. 
Leviticus23:18 As well as the bread, the whole group of people must bring seven perfect lambs that are one year old. They must bring a young bull and two male sheep. These animals will be a burnt offering to the Lord, together with the grain offering and the drink offerings. The smell of these offerings while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord." 
Leviticus23:19 You must also offer one male goat as a sin offering. And you must offer two lambs that are one year old as a friendship offering. 
Leviticus23:20 The priest will lift up the two lambs and the bread as a special gift to the Lord. They are a holy offering to the Lord that belongs to the priests. 
Leviticus23:21 On that day, you must not do any of your usual work. It is a day when you must meet together to worship the Lord. This is a rule that will continue for ever, in all the places where you live." 
Leviticus23:22 At harvest time, when you cut the crops on your land, do not cut to the edge of your field. Do not pick up the grain that falls onto the ground. Leave some of the grain for the poor people and for the foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.” ’" 
Leviticus23:23 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus23:24 ‘Tell Israel's people, “Each year, on the first day of the seventh month, you must have a complete rest. When you hear the sound of trumpets you must meet together to worship the Lord." 
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any of your usual work on that day. Instead, you must bring special offerings to the Lord.” ’" 
Leviticus23:26 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus23:27 ‘The tenth day of the seventh month is the Day of Atonement. On that day, you must come together to worship the Lord. You must not eat any food. Instead, you must make a special offering to the Lord." 
Leviticus23:28 You must not do any work on that day. It is a day of atonement. The priests will offer sacrifices to the Lord your God to make you clean from your sins. 
Leviticus23:29 You must punish anyone who does not obey the rules for this day. That person can no longer belong among God's people. 
Leviticus23:30 I will destroy any person from among my people who does any work on that day. 
Leviticus23:31 You must not do any kind of work. This rule will continue for ever in all the places where you live. 
Leviticus23:32 It is a Sabbath day of complete rest for all of you. It is a day when you must not eat any food. You must rest from the evening of the ninth day until the evening of the tenth day.’ 
Leviticus23:33 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus23:34 ‘Tell Israel's people, “Each year, on the 15th day of the seventh month, the Festival of Huts will start. The festival will continue for seven days, to give honour to the Lord." 
Leviticus23:35 On the first day of the festival, you must meet together to worship the Lord. You must not do any of your usual work." 
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you must offer special gifts to the Lord. On the eighth day you must meet together to worship the Lord. You must offer a special gift to him. It is the last day of the festival and you must all meet together. You must not do any of your usual work. 
Leviticus23:37 These are the rules for the special days that the Lord has chosen. They are times when you must meet together to worship the Lord. You will also offer special gifts to him. You will bring burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings and sacrifices each day, as the rules teach." 
Leviticus23:38 You must meet to worship the Lord on these festival days, as well as on each Sabbath day. You must make these special offerings at festivals, as well as your usual gifts. You must give them to the Lord, as well as the gifts that you have promised to give and the gifts that you choose to give." 
Leviticus23:39 On the 15th day of the seventh month, you must begin the Festival of Huts. That is the time when you bring your crops home from the fields. This festival will continue for seven days, to give honour to the Lord. The first day and the eighth day of the festival are days of complete rest from your usual work." 
Leviticus23:40 On the first day, you must pick fruit from your best trees. Cut off branches of palm trees, willow trees and other trees with many leaves. Then for seven days you will be happy as you give honour to the Lord." 
Leviticus23:41 You must worship the Lord in this way for seven days. Do this in the seventh month of each year. This is a rule that will continue for all time. 
Leviticus23:42 All of you who were born as Israelites must live in huts for seven days. 
Leviticus23:43 Then your descendants in the future will know that Israel's people lived in tents for many years. I caused them to do that after I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.” ’ 
Leviticus23:44 Moses told the Israelites these rules about the festivals that the Lord had chosen. They were times for them to worship him. 
Leviticus24:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus24:2 ‘ Command Israel's people to bring you pure oil from olives that they have beaten. Use this oil to burn in the lamps in the Tent of Meeting. This will cause the light to shine all the time. 
Leviticus24:3 Aaron must take care of the lamps that are in front of the curtain in the tent. The Covenant Box stands behind the curtain. Aaron must make sure that the lamps burn all night in front of the Lord, from evening until morning. This rule will continue for ever." 
Leviticus24:4 Aaron must take care of the lamps on the lampstand that is made of pure gold. He must make sure that the lamps always burn there, in front of the Lord." 
Leviticus24:5 Use the best flour to bake 12 round loaves of bread. Use two tenths of an ephah of flour for each loaf. 
Leviticus24:6 Put the loaves on the gold table in front of the Lord. Put them in two rows, with six loaves in each row." 
Leviticus24:7 Put pure frankincense along each row. This incense will burn as a special gift to the Lord instead of the bread. 
Leviticus24:8 Aaron must put new bread on the table every Sabbath day. It shows the covenant that continues for ever between Israel's people and the Lord. 
Leviticus24:9 The bread will belong to Aaron and his sons. They must eat it in a holy place because it is a very holy offering to the Lord. It will always be a part of the offerings that belongs to the priests.’ 
Leviticus24:10 There was a man whose mother's name was Shelomith. She was an Israelite woman, the daughter of Dibri who belonged to the tribe of Dan. The man's father was an Egyptian. The man started to fight against an Israelite man inside the camp. While they were fighting, the man insulted the Lord 's name with a curse." 
Leviticus24:11
