Leviticus24:12 So the Israelites took hold of the man and they put guards to watch him. They wanted the Lord to show them what they should do. 
Leviticus24:13 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus24:14 ‘Take the man who spoke the curse outside the camp. All the people who heard what he said must put their hands on his head. Then all the people must throw stones at him to kill him. 
Leviticus24:15 Warn Israel's people, “If anyone curses me, their God, they deserve their punishment." 
Leviticus24:16 So if someone insults the Lord 's name, his punishment must be death. All the people must throw stones at him until he is dead. It does not matter whether he is a foreign person or an Israelite. You must kill anyone who insults the Lord 's name." 
Leviticus24:17 If anyone kills another person, you must punish him with death." 
Leviticus24:18 If someone kills an animal that belongs to someone else, he must pay for it with a living animal. He took away a life, so he must give a life." 
Leviticus24:19 If a man hurts another person, you must punish him as he deserves. Hurt him in the same way that he has hurt the other person." 
Leviticus24:20 If he has broken one of the person's bones, break one of his bones. If he hurt the person's eye, hurt his eye. If he has broken a person's tooth, break his tooth." 
Leviticus24:21 If someone kills an animal that belongs to someone else, he must pay for it with a living animal. But if someone kills another person, you must punish him with death." 
Leviticus24:22 The same rule is for everyone, both Israelites and foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.” ’" 
Leviticus24:23 Moses told Israel's people what the Lord had said. They took the man who spoke the curse outside the camp. They threw stones at him until he died. Israel's people did what the Lord had commanded Moses to do. 
Leviticus25:1 When Moses was on Mount Sinai, the Lord said to him," 
Leviticus25:2 ‘Tell Israel's people this: You will go into the land that I will give to you. Then the land must have a rest every seven years. That will give me honour. 
Leviticus25:3 For six years, you may plant seeds in your fields. You may take care of your vines. You may bring home your crops at harvest time." 
Leviticus25:4 But in the seventh year, the land must have a complete rest. It is a Sabbath year to give honour to the Lord. You must not plant seeds in your fields. You must not take care of your vines." 
Leviticus25:5 You must not cut your crops or your grapes at harvest time. The land must have a year of complete rest. 
Leviticus25:6 But some things will still grow on the land during that year. You may eat anything that grows by itself in the Sabbath year. That will be food for you and for your servants. It will be food for the people that you pay to work for you. And it will be food for the foreign people who are living with you. 
Leviticus25:7 The land will also provide food for your farm animals and for the wild animals. You may eat any food that the land provides during that year. 
Leviticus25:8 You must also count seven Sabbath years. That is seven times seven years, or 49 years." 
Leviticus25:9 In the 50th year you must make a loud noise with trumpets everywhere in your land. Do that on the tenth day of the seventh month, the Day of Atonement." 
Leviticus25:10 You must make sure that the 50th year is a special holy year. It is a happy Jubilee year. It is a time for everyone in the land to become free from their debts. Anyone who has sold his family's land will receive it back again. Slaves will return to live with their own people. 
Leviticus25:11 The 50th year is a Jubilee year for you. You must not plant any seeds in your fields. You must not cut your crops at harvest time. You must not pick any grapes from your vines. 
Leviticus25:12 It is a Jubilee year for you. You must keep it as a holy year. You may only eat food that grows by itself in your fields. 
Leviticus25:13 In the Jubilee year you will all return to the land that belongs to your family. 
Leviticus25:14 At any time, if you agree with someone to buy or sell land, you must be fair." 
Leviticus25:15 If you buy some land, you must count the number of years until the next Jubilee year. That will decide the fair price for you to pay. You are paying for the number of years that you can grow crops on the land." 
Leviticus25:16 If there are many years until the Jubilee year, the price of the land will be larger. If only a few years remain, the price will be smaller. The other man is selling you a certain number of years when you will get crops from the land." 
Leviticus25:17 Do not cheat each other. Respect and obey your God. I am the Lord your God. 
Leviticus25:18 You must obey my rules and my laws. If you are careful to do that, you will live safely in the land." 
Leviticus25:19 The land will provide plenty of food for you to eat. You will live there safely. 
Leviticus25:20 You may think, “If we do not plant any seeds in the seventh year, what food will we eat?”" 
Leviticus25:21 This is what will happen. I will bless the land in the sixth year so that it grows a lot of food for you. It will provide enough crops to give you food for three years! 
Leviticus25:22 When you plant seeds in the eighth year, you will still be eating your old crops. You will be eating food that the land provided in the sixth year. You will continue to eat those old crops until you bring home new crops in the ninth year." 
Leviticus25:23 You must never sell your land so that it completely belongs to someone else. The land always belongs to me, the Lord. I allow you to live on it for a time, like foreigners." 
Leviticus25:24 Anybody who sells land must always have the chance to buy it back again. 
Leviticus25:25 If an Israelite becomes poor, he may need to sell some of his land. If so, the person who has authority in his family must buy the land back from the new owner." 
Leviticus25:26 There might not be anyone in the man's family who can do that. Later, the man himself may have enough money to buy the land back again." 
Leviticus25:27 He must count how many years remain until the next Jubilee year. He must pay the value of those years to the man that he sold the land to. Then it will become his own land again. 
Leviticus25:28 But the man may never have enough money to pay the value of the land. If so, the land that he sold will belong to the other person until the next Jubilee year. When the Jubilee year comes, the new owner must return the land to the poor man who sold it. Then the man can return to live on his land again." 
Leviticus25:29 It is different for a house inside a city that has walls. If someone sells his house in a city, he may buy it back in the first year. For one complete year after he has sold it, he has the chance to buy it back." 
Leviticus25:30 If the man does not buy back the house in that year, it will completely belong to the new owner. It will continue to belong to him and to his descendants for ever. They will not have to return it in the Jubilee year." 
Leviticus25:31 But a house may be in a village that has no walls around it. If someone sells the house, it is the same as a piece of land like a field. The person who sells it must always have the chance to buy it back again. If not, when the Jubilee year comes, the house will belong again to the first owner." 
Leviticus25:32 Some cities belong to the Levites for them to live in. If a Levite sells his house, he must always have the chance to buy it back again." 
Leviticus25:33 When the Jubilee year comes, a house that a Levite has sold will belong to him again. This is because the Levites need to live in the houses that belong to them in their cities. The other Israelite tribes have given those places to the Levites." 
Leviticus25:34 But the Levites must never sell the fields that are around their cities. That land belongs to them for ever. 
Leviticus25:35 If an Israelite becomes poor, he may not be able to take care of himself. You must help him with the things that he needs. Help him to live with you, as you would help a foreigner or a stranger." 
Leviticus25:36 If you lend money to him, do not take back more than you gave him. Respect and obey your God. Allow the poor man to live with you as part of your family." 
Leviticus25:37 If you lend money to him, or you sell food to him, do not take more than its true value." 
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God who rescued you from the land of Egypt. Now I am giving you the land of Canaan. I have done this to be your God. 
Leviticus25:39 If a poor Israelite is in debt to you, he might sell himself to you. But you must not make him work for you as a slave." 
Leviticus25:40 Instead, you must pay him to work as your servant, or like a foreigner who is living with you. He must work for you until the next Jubilee year." 
Leviticus25:41 In the Jubilee year, he will be free to leave. He and his children can go back to his own family on the land that belongs to his ancestors." 
Leviticus25:42 Israel's people are my servants. I brought them out of Egypt. So nobody may buy them as slaves. 
Leviticus25:43 You must not be cruel to poor people. Instead, respect and obey your God." 
Leviticus25:44 You are allowed to buy male and female slaves, if they are people from other nations." 
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy people from the families of foreign people who live among you. The children that they give birth to in your land may also become your slaves. 
Leviticus25:46 Foreign slaves may continue to belong to you for your whole life. After you die, they can belong to your children. But you must not make other Israelites your slaves. You must not be cruel to them." 
Leviticus25:47 A foreign person who lives with you might become rich. A poor Israelite might be in debt to him. So the Israelite might sell himself to the foreigner or to someone from the foreigner's family. 
Leviticus25:48 Someone from the family of the poor Israelite always has the chance to buy him back. 
Leviticus25:49 It may be his brother, his uncle or his cousin. Anyone from his own family may buy him back so that he is free again. And if the poor man himself has success, he can pay to make himself free." 
Leviticus25:50 The poor man and the foreign man who bought him must decide together the fair price. They must count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee year. They must decide how much pay a worker would receive during that time. That is the right price for the poor Israelite man to become free. 
Leviticus25:51 Many years may remain until the Jubilee year. If so, the man must pay most of the sale money to be free." 
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years remain, the man will pay a smaller price to become free." 
Leviticus25:53 The foreign person who has bought the Israelite man must pay him each year, as he would pay a worker. Do not allow the foreign owner to be cruel to the Israelite." 
Leviticus25:54 When the Jubilee year comes, the poor Israelite man must go free, if he has not already become free. His children will go free with him." 
Leviticus25:55 Israel's people are my own servants. They belong to me because I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.’ 
Leviticus26:1 The Lord said to Moses, ‘You must not make idols for yourselves. Do not use wood or stone to make images or statues of false gods. Do not worship any stone shape that you have put on your land. I am the Lord your God." 
Leviticus26:2 You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. You must respect my holy tabernacle. I am the Lord. 
Leviticus26:3 You must carefully obey my laws and my commands. 
Leviticus26:4 If you do, I will send rain at the proper time each year. The land will provide you with food. Plenty of fruit will grow on your trees." 
Leviticus26:5 You will continue to bring in grain from your fields until it is time to pick your grapes. And you will continue to pick your grapes until it is time to plant seeds for the next year. You will have all the food to eat that you need. You will live safely in your land. 
Leviticus26:6 I will cause there to be peace in your land. You will not be afraid of trouble when you lie down to sleep. I will remove dangerous animals from your land. No enemies will come to attack you. 
Leviticus26:7 You will be able to chase after your enemies and kill them. 
Leviticus26:8 Five of your men will chase away 100 of your enemies. 100 of your men will chase away 10,000 enemies. You will attack them and kill them." 
Leviticus26:9 I will help you. I will cause you to have many children so that the number of your people grows. I will do what I have promised to do in my covenant with you. 
Leviticus26:10 When harvest time comes each year, you will still be eating food that you have stored. You will need to throw food out of your store rooms to make space for the new crops." 
Leviticus26:11 I will have my home among you. I will never turn against you. 
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you. I will be your God and you will be my people. 
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God. I brought you out from Egypt so that you would no longer be slaves of the Egyptians. I destroyed the power that caused you to suffer. Then you could live as free people. 
Leviticus26:14 But it will be different if you do not respect me and obey my commands. 
Leviticus26:15 I will punish you if you do not accept my laws and you turn against my rules. I will punish you if you do not obey all my commands and you spoil the covenant that I made with you. 
Leviticus26:16 This is how I will punish you: I will cause terrible things to happen to you. You will have diseases that make you weak and diseases that make your bodies very hot. You will have diseases that spoil your eyes and diseases that take away your strength. You will plant seeds in your fields but your enemies will eat the crops. 
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you. Your enemies will attack you and they will win against you. People who hate you will rule over you. You will run away to escape even if nobody is chasing you! 
Leviticus26:18 Even after I have punished you like that, you might still choose not to obey me. If so, I will continue to punish you seven times more for your sins." 
Leviticus26:19 I will stop you being proud of your strength. I will not send any rain from the sky. Your land will become hard and dry. 
Leviticus26:20 You will use all your strength to work very hard, but it will be useless. Your land will not provide any food for you. No fruit will grow on your trees." 
Leviticus26:21 If you continue to turn against me, I will cause you to suffer even more. If you refuse to obey me, I will punish you seven times more again for your sins." 
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals to attack you. They will kill your children and your animals. Very few of your people will remain and nobody will travel on your roads. 
Leviticus26:23 After all that punishment, you may still not turn back to me. If you continue to turn against me," 
Leviticus26:24 I myself will turn against you. I will punish you another seven times more strongly for your sins. 
Leviticus26:25 I will send armies to attack you. They will punish you because you have not obeyed my covenant with you. If you escape into your cities, I will send bad diseases among you. Then your enemies will have power over you." 
Leviticus26:26 I will stop you getting any food. Ten women will bake all the bread for their families in only one oven. They will weigh the bread for each person but nobody will have enough to eat. 
Leviticus26:27 After this, you may still not obey me. If you continue to turn against me," 
Leviticus26:28 I will be angry and I will turn against you. I myself will punish you seven times more strongly because of your sins. 
Leviticus26:29 Then you will be so hungry that you will eat your own children. 
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your altars for sacrifices to false gods and your altars for incense. I will put your dead bodies in a heap on top of your dead idols. I will hate you. 
Leviticus26:31 I will destroy your cities and the places where you worship. The sweet smell of your offerings will not give me pleasure. 
Leviticus26:32 I myself will destroy the land where you live. Your enemies who come to live there will be very upset when they see it. 
Leviticus26:33 I will chase you out to go and live among other nations. I will cause enemies to attack you. I will destroy your land. Your cities will become heaps of stones. 
Leviticus26:34 While you are living in the countries of your enemies, your own land will have a rest. It will have the Sabbath rest that it should have had every seven years." 
Leviticus26:35 When you lived on the land, you did not allow it to have its Sabbath rest. Now it will have complete rest while it is an empty place." 
Leviticus26:36 Some of you will remain alive in the countries of your enemies. But you will live in great fear. The sound of a leaf that moves in the wind will cause you to run away. You will think that someone is chasing you to kill you. Nobody is chasing you, but you will still fall down as you try to escape." 
Leviticus26:37 You will fall over each other, like people who are running away from their enemies. Nobody is chasing you, but you are still afraid. None of you will be strong enough to fight against your enemies." 
Leviticus26:38 You will die among the nations where you are living. That is where you will come to an end. 
Leviticus26:39 Those of you who still remain will slowly die in the countries of your enemies. You will become weak and you will die because of your sins and because of the sins of your ancestors. 
Leviticus26:40 But one day, your descendants may realize that they and their ancestors have been wrong. They may clearly agree that they have turned against me. They may agree that they have not been faithful to me." 
Leviticus26:41 That was why I myself turned against them and I took them to the countries of their enemies. When they know that, they will no longer be proud. They will accept the punishment that they deserve for their sins." 
Leviticus26:42 When that happens, I will do what I have promised to do. I made those promises in my covenant with your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. I will remember to bless the land that I gave to them." 
Leviticus26:43 It was necessary for my people to go away from the land for a time. The land needed to be empty so that it could have the Sabbath rest that it should have had. My people refused to obey my rules. They hated my laws. So they needed to receive the punishment that they deserved for their sins. 
Leviticus26:44 But I am the Lord their God. So I will not forget them while they are living in the countries of their enemies. I will not turn away from them. I will not destroy them completely. I will not forget the covenant that I made with them. 
Leviticus26:45 Yes, I will help them because of the covenant that I made with their ancestors. While other nations watched, I brought my people out of Egypt to be their God. I am the Lord.’" 
Leviticus26:46 These are the laws, rules and commands that the Lord told Moses on Mount Sinai. They are his covenant with Israel's people." 
Leviticus27:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Leviticus27:2 ‘Speak to Israel's people. Tell them, “Someone might promise to offer another person as a gift to the Lord. He may pay money as his offering instead of the person." 
Leviticus27:3 He must pay the value of the offering that he has promised. For a man between 20 years and 60 years old, the value is 50 shekels of silver that they use in the holy place." 
Leviticus27:4 For a woman, the value is 30 shekels of silver." 
Leviticus27:5 For a male between 5 years and 20 years old, the value is 20 shekels. For a female of that age, the value is 10 shekels." 
Leviticus27:6 For a boy between one month and five years old, the value is 5 shekels. For a girl of the same age, the value is 3 shekels." 
Leviticus27:7 For a man who is older than 60 years, the value is 15 shekels. For a woman of that age, the value is 10 shekels." 
Leviticus27:8 The person who makes the promise might be too poor to pay the value of his offering. If so, he must bring the person that he has offered to God to the priest. The priest will decide the value of the poor person's offering. He will decide how much money the poor person is able to give." 
Leviticus27:9 Someone might promise to offer an animal as a gift to the Lord. If it is the kind of animal that the Lord would accept as an offering, the animal becomes a holy offering." 
Leviticus27:10 The person must not then change his offering in any way. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If the person does change one animal for another, both the animals become holy." 
Leviticus27:11 The animal that the person promises to give might be an unclean animal. The Lord does not accept that kind of animal as an offering. So the person must bring the animal to the priest. 
Leviticus27:12 The priest will look at the animal to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering. The priest is the person who decides the value. 
Leviticus27:13 The person who promised his animal as an offering might want to buy the animal back. If so, he must pay the value of the animal and add one fifth more." 
Leviticus27:14 Someone might offer his house as a holy gift to the Lord. The priest will look at the house to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering. 
Leviticus27:15 If the person who offered the house wants to buy it back, he must pay its value and add one fifth more. Then the house will belong to him again." 
Leviticus27:16 Someone might offer part of his family's land as a holy gift to the Lord. He must decide how much seed the land needs to grow crops. The value of the land will be 50 shekels of silver for each homer of seed that the land needs. 
Leviticus27:17 If a person gives his land to the Lord in a Jubilee year, the land will have its complete value." 
Leviticus27:18 But the land's value will be less if the person gives it after the Jubilee year. The priest will count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee. Then he will decide the value of the land. 
Leviticus27:19 If the person wants to buy the land back, he must pay its value and add one fifth more. Then the land will belong to him again." 
Leviticus27:20 It is different if he does not buy the land back and he sells it to someone else. If he does that, he is not allowed to buy the land back for himself." 
Leviticus27:21 When the next Jubilee year comes, the land will become the Lord 's holy land for ever. It will belong to the priests." 
Leviticus27:22 Someone might offer to the Lord a field that is not part of his family's land. It might be a field that he has bought from someone. 
Leviticus27:23 If so, the priest will count how many years remain until the Jubilee year. Then he will decide the value of the field. The person who offered the field to the Lord must pay the money on that day. The money belongs to the Lord as a holy gift." 
Leviticus27:24 When the Jubilee year comes, the field will belong again to the first owner. It is part of his family's land." 
Leviticus27:25 The priest is the person who must decide the value of all these offerings. He must use the shekel that they use in the holy place to decide the value. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel. 
Leviticus27:26 The first baby that an animal gives birth to is holy. It belongs to the Lord. So nobody can offer it as a special gift to the Lord. If it is an ox or a sheep, it already belongs to the Lord." 
Leviticus27:27 But if it is an unclean animal, the owner may pay to buy it. He must pay the value and add one fifth more. If the owner does not buy it, the priest must sell it to someone else for its value." 
Leviticus27:28 Someone might give to the Lord as a complete gift something that belongs to him. It might be a person, an animal or part of his family's land. Every gift like that belongs to the Lord for ever as a most holy gift. The priest must not sell it. The owner cannot ever buy it back." 
Leviticus27:29 If you give a person to the Lord as a complete gift, nobody may buy him back. His punishment must be death." 
Leviticus27:30 One tenth of all the food that the land provides belongs to the Lord as a holy gift. It may be grain that grows in the fields, or fruit that grows on the trees." 
Leviticus27:31 If a person chooses to buy back any of his tithe, he must pay its value and add one fifth more." 
Leviticus27:32 Out of every ten farm animals, one animal is holy. It belongs to the Lord." 
Leviticus27:33 The owner must not pick what animal he will give to the Lord. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If anyone does that, both animals will belong to the Lord as holy animals. The owner cannot buy the animal back for himself.” ’" 
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands that the Lord gave to Moses on Mount Sinai. He told Moses to teach them to Israel's people. 
Numbers1:1 The Israelites were in the Sinai Desert. It was the first day of the second month of the second year after they left Egypt. Moses was in the Tent of Meeting. The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers1:2 ‘Put the people into clans and families and count them. Make a list. Write the name of each man on the list. 
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron must record the names of all the men in each group who can fight in the army. Record the names of men who are 20 years old or older. 
Numbers1:4 A man from each tribe must help you. Each of them will be the leader of his family. 
Numbers1:5 These are the men from each tribe who must help you: Reuben: Elizur, the son of Shedeur." 
Numbers1:6 Simeon: Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai." 
Numbers1:7 Judah: Nahshon, the son of Amminadab." 
Numbers1:8 Issachar: Nethanel, the son of Zuar." 
Numbers1:9 Zebulun: Eliab, the son of Helon." 
Numbers1:10 Ephraim (Joseph's son): Elishama, the son of Ammihud. Manasseh (Joseph's son): Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur." 
Numbers1:11 Benjamin: Abidan, the son of Gideoni." 
Numbers1:12 Dan: Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai." 
Numbers1:13 Asher: Pagiel, the son of Ocran." 
Numbers1:14 Gad: Eliasaph, the son of Deuel." 
Numbers1:15 Naphtali: Ahira, the son of Enan.’" 
Numbers1:16 These were the men that they chose from among the people. They were leaders of their clans. Each of them worked on behalf of the tribe of their ancestor. 
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron told these leaders to come and help. 
Numbers1:18 On the first day of the second month, they said that all the Israelite people must meet together. The people were in clans and in families. They made a list of all the men who were 20 years old or older." 
Numbers1:19 They did the things that the Lord had told Moses to do. So Moses recorded the names there in the Sinai Desert. 
Numbers2:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers2:2 ‘The Israelites must put up their tents on each side of the Tent of Meeting. They must not be very near to it but they must be all around it. They must live together in their tribes. Each person must be with his own tribe. Each tribe must have its own flag. 
Numbers2:3 The people in Judah's tribe must be on the east side of the Tent. They must have their flag with them, and they must put up their tents in groups. The leader of the tribe of Judah is Nahshon, the son of Amminadab." 
Numbers2:4 There will be 74,600 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:5 The tribe of Issachar will put up their tents on one side of Judah's camp. The leader of the tribe of Issachar is Nethanel, the son of Zuar." 
Numbers2:6 There will be 54,400 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:7 The tribe of Zebulun will be on the other side of Judah's camp. The leader of the tribe of Zebulun is Eliab, the son of Helon." 
Numbers2:8 There will be 57,400 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:9 So there will be 186,400 soldiers in the three groups on Judah's side of the camp. When the people travel, these tribes will march first." 
Numbers2:10 The tribe of Reuben must be on the south side of the tent. They must have their flag with them, and they must put up their tents in groups. The leader of the tribe of Reuben is Elizur, the son of Shedeur." 
Numbers2:11 There will be 46,500 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:12 The tribe of Simeon will put up their tents on one side of Reuben's camp. The leader of the tribe of Simeon is Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai." 
Numbers2:13 There will be 59,300 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:14 The tribe of Gad will be on the other side of Reuben's camp. The leader of the tribe of Gad is Eliasaph, the son of Deuel." 
Numbers2:15 There will be 45,650 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:16 So there will be 151,450 soldiers in the three groups on Reuben's side of the camp. When the people travel, they will march second." 
Numbers2:17 The Levites must march next. The Levites must carry the Tent of Meeting. They must march between the first two groups of tribes and the last two groups of tribes. Each man must be in his own place. They must march with their flag. 
Numbers2:18 The tribe of Ephraim must be on the west side of the tent. They must have their flag with them and they must live in groups. The leader of the tribe of Ephraim is Elishama, the son of Ammihud." 
Numbers2:19 There will be 40,500 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:20 The tribe of Manasseh will be on one side of Ephraim's camp. The leader of the tribe of Manasseh is Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur." 
Numbers2:21 There will be 32,200 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:22 The tribe of Benjamin will be on the other side of Ephraim's camp. The leader of the tribe of Benjamin is Abidan, the son of Gideoni." 
Numbers2:23 There will be 35,400 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:24 So there will be 108,100 soldiers in the three groups on Ephraim's side of the camp. When the people travel, they will march third." 
Numbers2:25 The tribe of Dan must be on the north side of the tent. They must have their flag with them and they must live in groups. The leader of the tribe of Dan is Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai." 
Numbers2:26 There will be 62,700 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:27 The tribe of Asher will put up their tents on one side of Dan's camp. The leader of the tribe of Asher is Pagiel, the son of Ocran." 
Numbers2:28 There will be 41,500 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:29 The tribe of Naphtali will be on the other side of Dan's camp. The leader of the tribe of Naphtali is Ahira, the son of Enan." 
Numbers2:30 There will be 53,400 soldiers in his army." 
Numbers2:31 So there will be 157,600 soldiers in the three groups on Dan's side of the camp. They will march last with their flag.’" 
Numbers2:32 In that way, Moses and his helpers counted all the Israelite men who were able to fight. They counted them in groups, clan by clan. There were 603,550 soldiers." 
Numbers2:33 They did not count the Levites with the other Israelites. That was what the Lord had told Moses. 
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did everything that the Lord had told Moses. They put up their tents in groups, as the Lord wanted. Each tribe put up its tents under its own flag. When they started to travel, each man walked with his own clan and his own family." 
Numbers3:1 This is the list of the family of Aaron and Moses. This was at the time when the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai. 
Numbers3:2 The names of the sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar. Nadab was the firstborn son." 
Numbers3:3 Aaron anointed them, and he gave authority to them to work as priests." 
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu fell down and died in front of the Lord. That happened when they offered strange fire to the Lord in the Sinai Desert. They had no children. So only Eleazar and Ithamar worked as priests while their father Aaron was alive. 
Numbers3:5 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers3:6 ‘Bring the tribe of Levi to Aaron the priest. They must help him. 
Numbers3:7 They must serve him and all the Israelites. They must work at the Tent of Meeting and take care of it. 
Numbers3:8 They must work on behalf of the Israelites. They must take care of the things that are in the Tent of Meeting. That is how they will serve God in the tabernacle. 
Numbers3:9 Give the Levites to Aaron and to his descendants. They will be the Israelites who help him. They do not have to do anything else. 
Numbers3:10 But you must make a list of Aaron and his descendants. They will be the priests. If any other person comes near to the special tent, they must die.’" 
Numbers3:11 The Lord also said to Moses," 
Numbers3:12 ‘I will not take the firstborn son of every woman in Israel to belong to me. I will take the Levites instead of them. 
Numbers3:13 I killed all the firstborn sons of the Egyptians. But I kept the firstborn sons of every person and animal among the Israelites for myself. They belong to me. I am the Lord!’ 
Numbers3:14 The Lord said to Moses in the Sinai Desert," 
Numbers3:15 ‘Put the Levites into clans and families and count them. Make a list of every male who is a month old or older.’ 
Numbers3:16 Moses obeyed the Lord. So Moses recorded them as the Lord said. 
Numbers3:17 The names of the sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath and Merari." 
Numbers3:18 Gershon had two sons. Their names were Libni and Shimei. 
Numbers3:19 Kohath's sons were called Amram, Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel." 
Numbers3:20 Merari's sons were called Mahli and Mushi. Those were the sons and grandsons of Levi. They became the leaders of the clans of Levi's tribe. 
Numbers3:21 There were two families in Gershon's clan. They were the families of Libni and Shimei. 
Numbers3:22 There were 7,500 males who were one month or older in their list." 
Numbers3:23 Gershon's clan had to put up their tents on the west side of the special tent. 
Numbers3:24 The leader of the clan of Gershon was Eliasaph the son of Lael. 
Numbers3:25 Gershon's clan had to take care of the Tent of Meeting, with the cloth that covered it, and the curtain at its entrance." 
Numbers3:26 There was a yard round the outside of the tabernacle and the altar. Gershon's family took care of the curtains that hung around the yard, with their ropes. They also took care of the curtain at the entrance to the yard. They took care of all those kinds of things." 
Numbers3:27 There were four families in Kohath's clan. They were the families of Amram, Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel." 
Numbers3:28 There were 8,600 males who were one month old or older in their list. Kohath's clan had to take care of the things inside the special tent." 
Numbers3:29 Kohath's clan had to put up their tents on the south side of the tabernacle. 
Numbers3:30 The leader of Kohath's descendants was Elizaphan, the son of Uzziel." 
Numbers3:31 Kohath's family had to take care of the Covenant Box, the table and the lampstands. They had to take care of the altars and the tools that the priests used in the special tent. They also took care of the curtain in front of the Most Holy Place in the tent. They did all the work with those things." 
Numbers3:32 Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, was the most important leader of the Levites. He ruled over those people who took care of God's special tent." 
Numbers3:33 There were two families in Merari's clan. They were the families of Mahli and Mushi. 
Numbers3:34 There were 6,200 males in that list. They were one month old or older when Moses counted them." 
Numbers3:35 The leader of Merari's clan was Zuriel, the son of Abihail. They had to put up their tents on the north side of the tabernacle." 
Numbers3:36 Merari's family had to take care of all the wood parts of the tabernacle. They took care of the heavy things that stood on the ground to hold the poles. 
Numbers3:37 They also took care of the poles that held the cloth around the yard, their ropes and other things that held the tent in its place." 
Numbers3:38 Moses, Aaron and Aaron's sons had to put up their tents in front of the tabernacle, on the east side. They were the only people who could go into the Holy Place in the tent. Any other person who went near it had to die." 
Numbers3:39 So Moses and Aaron obeyed the Lord. They counted the Levites by their clans. There were 22,000 males who were one month old or older." 
Numbers3:40 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Count all the firstborn sons of the Israelites. Record every male who is one month old or older. Make a list of their names." 
Numbers3:41 The Israelites' firstborn sons belong to me. I am the Lord. But I will accept all the males among the Levites instead. The Israelites' firstborn animals also belong to me. But I will accept the animals of the Levites instead.’ 
Numbers3:42 So Moses counted all the firstborn males among the Israelites as the Lord had said. 
Numbers3:43 He counted 22,273 firstborn males who were one month old or older. He made a list of their names." 
Numbers3:44 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers3:45 ‘Do not take the firstborn males among the people to belong to me. Accept the Levites instead. Do not use the firstborn animals of the people. Accept the animals of the Levites instead. The Levites will belong to me. I am the Lord. 
Numbers3:46 There are 273 more firstborn males of the people than there are Levites. So the people must pay for the extra firstborn sons. 
Numbers3:47 You must accept five shekels for each extra firstborn male. The shekel must have the same weight as the shekel that the Levites use in the holy place. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel. 
Numbers3:48 Give that money to Aaron and his sons.’ 
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the money to buy back the extra 273 firstborn sons. That was because the number of firstborn sons was more than the number of Levites. 
Numbers3:50 He took 1,365 shekels from them. The shekels had the same weight as the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place." 
Numbers3:51 Moses did what the Lord had told him to do. He gave the money to Aaron and his sons. 
Numbers4:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers4:2 ‘Write a list of the people in Kohath's clan in their families. That list must be separate from the list of the other Levites. 
Numbers4:3 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:4 These men must take care of everything that is most holy in the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers4:5 When the people are ready to move, Aaron and his sons must go into the Tent of Meeting. They must take down the curtain that is in front of the Covenant Box. They must cover the Covenant Box with the curtain." 
Numbers4:6 Then they must cover the curtain with good leather. On the top of that, they must put a blue cloth. Then, they must put the poles through the rings, so that they can carry the Covenant Box." 
Numbers4:7 They must put a blue cloth over the table for the special bread. The bread must remain on the table. They must put on the cloth the plates, bowls and dishes. They must also put on it the jars for the wine that they offer to me." 
Numbers4:8 Then they must cover all those things with a red cloth. On the top of the red cloth, they must put good leather. Then they must fix the poles to the table, so that they can carry it." 
Numbers4:9 They must cover the lampstand and its tools with a blue cloth. They must also cover the jars for the oil with the blue cloth. 
Numbers4:10 Then they must cover all this with good leather. They must carry it on a board. 
Numbers4:11 They must cover the gold altar with a blue cloth. They must cover the blue cloth with good leather. Then they must fix the poles to the altar, so that they can carry it." 
Numbers4:12 They must cover all the tools from God's holy tent with a blue cloth. They must cover the blue cloth with good leather. They must carry all this on a board. 
Numbers4:13 They must remove the ashes from the bronze altar. They must cover the bronze altar with a purple cloth. 
Numbers4:14 They must put all the tools on top of it. These include the plates for the fire, the forks for the meat, the spades and the bowls. They must cover all these things with good leather. Then they must put the poles through the rings on the altar, so that they can carry it." 
Numbers4:15 When the people are ready to leave, the men from Kohath's clan must be ready to carry all those things. But they must wait until Aaron and his sons have covered all the holy things of the tabernacle. The men from Kohath's clan must not touch the holy things. They will die if they touch them. Those are the jobs of Kohath's clan. They must carry the things from the Tent of Meeting when the Israelites travel." 
Numbers4:16 Eleazar, who is the son of Aaron the priest, must keep the oil for the light. He must keep the incense and the grain offering. He must keep the special oil. The special oil is a mark on everything that God chooses. Eleazar must take care of the whole Tent of Meeting. He must work with God's holy tent and with everything in it.’" 
Numbers4:17 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers4:18 ‘You must not allow Kohath's clan to come to an end among the other Levites. 
Numbers4:19 Do not let the men in that clan die. That will happen if they come near to the most holy things. You must do this: Aaron and his sons must go into the Tent of Meeting. They will tell each man the work that he must do. And they must tell each man the things that he must carry. 
Numbers4:20 But the men from Kohath's clan must not look at any of the holy things, even for a moment. If they do look at those things, they will die.’" 
Numbers4:21 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers4:22 ‘Also write a list of the people in Gershon's clan in their families. 
Numbers4:23 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:24 The work of the men in Gershon's clan is to carry these things: 
Numbers4:25 They must carry the curtains of God's tent. They must carry the Tent of Meeting with the things that cover it. They must also carry the good leather skin that covers everything. And they must carry the curtain for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers4:26 They must carry the curtains of the yard that is around the tabernacle. Also, they must carry the curtain and the ropes for the entrance to the yard. That is the yard that is around the tent and the altar. The men from Gershon's clan must carry all the tools that belong with those curtains. They must do everything that they need to do with those things." 
Numbers4:27 The men in Gershon's clan must do everything that Aaron and his sons ask them to do. Aaron and his sons must tell them the things that they should carry. And they must tell them everything that they should do. 
Numbers4:28 That is the work for the Tent of Meeting that the descendants of Gershon must do. Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest, will tell them the things that they must do.’" 
Numbers4:29 Also God said to Moses, ‘Write a list of the people in Merari's clan in their families." 
Numbers4:30 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:31 This is the work of the men in Merari's clan: They must carry the wood parts that hold the Tent of Meeting, and its poles. They must carry its pillars and its bases." 
Numbers4:32 And they must carry the pillars, bases, pegs and ropes for the yard that is around the tent. And the men in Merari's clan must keep all the tools that belong with those things safe. Each man in Merari's clan must take care of something. Tell each man which things he must carry." 
Numbers4:33 That is the work of the descendants of Merari in the Tent of Meeting. Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest, will tell them the things that they must do.’" 
Numbers4:34 So Moses, Aaron and the people's leaders recorded the names of Kohath's descendants in their clans and in their families." 
Numbers4:35 They recorded the men who could work in the Tent of Meeting. They recorded them if they were between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:36 They recorded them in clans. There were 2,750 men." 
Numbers4:37 Moses and Aaron recorded the descendants of Kohath who worked at the Tent of Meeting. The Lord had told Moses that they must do that. 
Numbers4:38 Moses and Aaron also recorded the names of Gershon's descendants in their clans and in their families. 
Numbers4:39 They recorded the men who could work in the Tent of Meeting. They recorded them if they were between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:40 They recorded them in clans and families. There were 2,630 men." 
Numbers4:41 Moses and Aaron recorded the descendants of Gershon who worked at the Tent of Meeting. The Lord had told Moses that they must do that. 
Numbers4:42 Moses and Aaron also recorded the names of Merari's descendants in their clans and in their families. 
Numbers4:43 They counted all the men who worked in the Tent of Meeting. They counted them if they were between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:44 They recorded them in clans. There were 3,200 men." 
Numbers4:45 Moses and Aaron recorded the descendants of Merari who worked at the Tent of Meeting. The Lord had told Moses that they must do that. 
Numbers4:46 Moses, Aaron and the people's leaders recorded the names of the Levites in their clans and in their families." 
Numbers4:47 They counted all the men who worked in the Tent of Meeting. They also counted all the men who carried the tent and all its things. They counted them if they were between 30 and 50 years old. 
Numbers4:48 There were 8,580 Levites in the list." 
Numbers4:49 They told each man the things that he must do and what he must carry. Moses recorded all their names. The Lord had told him to do that. 
Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers5:2 ‘Speak to the Israelites. They must send any person with a disease of the skin away from the camp. Or somebody may have a disease that causes pus to come out of his body. Any person like that is unclean. Anyone who has touched a dead person is also unclean. Send those people out of the camp too. 
Numbers5:3 Do this with both men and women. Send them out of the camp. They must not make the camp unclean, because that is where I live among the people.’" 
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did what the Lord had said to Moses. They sent those people outside the camp. 
Numbers5:5 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers5:6 ‘Tell these rules to the Israelites. A person might do a wrong thing against another person. If they do, then they have turned against the Lord." 
Numbers5:7 That person must agree that they have done this sin. They must pay the right amount to the other person. They must also pay them one fifth extra. 
Numbers5:8 But the other person may have died. If they have no relative who can accept the money, the money will belong to the Lord. The person who did a wrong thing must give the money and a male sheep to the priest. The sheep is a sacrifice to make the person clean from their sin. Then I will forgive the guilty person." 
Numbers5:9 All the special gifts from the Israelites to God are for the priest. 
Numbers5:10 The priest will keep all these gifts that he receives from people.’ 
Numbers5:11 The Lord said to Moses: 
Numbers5:12 ‘Speak to the Israelites. Tell them to follow these rules. A woman may do wrong things against her husband. 
Numbers5:13 She might have had sex with another man. The husband might not know about this. His wife might have kept it secret. Nobody saw what was happening. So there is nobody to say that it happened. 
Numbers5:14 But the man may think that it happened. He will be jealous. But his wife may not really have made herself unclean in that way. 
Numbers5:15 The man must take his wife to the priest. He must bring one kilogram of barley flour with him. This is a grain offering to discover if his wife did do those things. So he must not put oil or incense on the grain offering. It is an offering because the man does not trust his wife. 
Numbers5:16 Then the priest will bring the woman to stand in front of the Lord. 
Numbers5:17 He must pour some holy water into a bowl that is made from clay. He must take some dust from the floor of the tabernacle. And he must put the dust into the water. 
Numbers5:18 Then the priest must bring the woman in front of the Lord. He must make the woman's hair free. He must put into her hands the grain offering. That is the offering because the man does not trust his wife. The priest must hold the bowl that contains bitter water. The bitter water brings a curse on guilty people. 
Numbers5:19 Then the woman must agree to the strong promise that the priest tells her to say. The priest must say to her, “This water brings a curse on guilty people. If no other man has had sex with you, it will not hurt you." 
Numbers5:20 But if another man has had sex with you," 
Numbers5:21 may the Lord bring the curse on you. The bitter water will cause your female parts to become smaller. Your stomach will become very big. 
Numbers5:22 That will happen when you drink the bitter water.” The woman must say, “That should happen. I agree!”" 
Numbers5:23 The priest must write the curses on a scroll of paper. Then he must wash the words off the paper into the bitter water. 
Numbers5:24 He must tell the woman that she must drink the bitter water. This water that brings a curse will go into her body. It may cause her to be very ill. 
Numbers5:25 But first, the priest must take the grain offering from her hands. That is the offering because the man does not trust his wife. The priest must lift it up in front of the Lord. Then he must bring it to the altar." 
Numbers5:26 He must burn a small part of it as a sacrifice. After that, the woman must drink the bitter water." 
Numbers5:27 If the woman has had sex with another man, the water will cause her to have very bad pain. Her female parts will become smaller. Her stomach will become very big. People who know her will curse her." 
Numbers5:28 But, if she did not do those wrong things, the water will not hurt her. She will be able to have babies." 
Numbers5:29 Perhaps a man is jealous because his wife has had sex with another man. Or he may only think that his wife has had sex with another man. This is what the Law says that the priest must do: The man must bring her in front of the Lord. 
Numbers5:30  
Numbers5:31 If the woman did not do those wrong things, you must not punish her husband. But if the woman did do those wrong things, you must punish her.’" 
Numbers6:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers6:2 ‘Tell the Israelites this: “A man or a woman may want to make a special promise to the Lord. That promise will make the person separate from other people, as a Nazirite." 
Numbers6:3 They must not drink wine. They must not drink any alcohol or vinegar. They must not drink the juice of grapes, or eat grapes or raisins." 
Numbers6:4 They must not eat the seeds of grapes for all the time that they are Nazirites. They must not even eat the skins of grapes. 
Numbers6:5 A person must not cut his hair during the time that he is a Nazirite. He must stay like this all the time that he is separate for the Lord. He must let the hair on his head grow long. 
Numbers6:6 He must not go near a dead body while he belongs to the Lord as a Nazirite. 
Numbers6:7 His hair shows that he has become separate for God. So he must not go near a dead body. He must not go near it even if the dead person is his father, mother, brother or sister." 
Numbers6:8 During the time that the person is a Nazirite, they are holy because they belong to the Lord." 
Numbers6:9 The Nazirite may be near to a person when that person dies suddenly. Then his head becomes unclean. He must remove all the hair from his head seven days after the person dies. Then he will be clean. 
Numbers6:10 On day 8, he must bring two doves or two young pigeons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must give them to the priest." 
Numbers6:11 The priest must sacrifice one bird as a sin offering and the other bird as a burnt offering. The priest must do that because the Nazirite has done a wrong thing. He went too near to a dead body. So the priest must make him clean from his sin. The hair on the Nazirite's head will be clean again on that same day. 
Numbers6:12 He must begin his time as a Nazirite again, with a new promise to the Lord. He cannot include the days before he became unclean. He must bring a male lamb that is one year old. That is a guilt offering." 
Numbers6:13 He is a Nazirite for a certain time. When that time finishes, the people must bring him to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Numbers6:14 He must offer to the Lord three animals that have nothing wrong with them. He must offer a young male sheep that is one year old. That is a burnt offering. He must offer a young female sheep that is one year old. That is a sin offering. He must offer a male sheep. That is a friendship offering. 
Numbers6:15 And he must bring a basket of bread that people have made without yeast. He must bring cakes that people have made with the best flour and oil. Also, he must bring thin biscuits with oil on them. But they must not have yeast in them either. He must bring the proper grain offerings and drink offerings with them." 
Numbers6:16 The priest must offer those things to the Lord. And he must sacrifice the sin offering and the burnt offering. 
Numbers6:17 Then the priest must sacrifice the male sheep as a friendship offering to the Lord. He must offer the basket of bread. He must give the grain offering and the drink offering with it. 
Numbers6:18 Then the Nazirite must stand at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. He must remove all the hair from his head. He must put his hair into the same fire where the priest has cooked the friendship offering. 
Numbers6:19 When the male sheep is cooked, the priest must take its shoulder. Also, he must take one cake and one thin biscuit from the basket. The cake and the biscuit must not have yeast in them. The priest must put these things into the hands of the Nazirite who has removed the hair from his head." 
Numbers6:20 The Nazirite must give those things back to the priest. The priest must lift them up to the Lord as a special gift. They are a special gift for the priest. Also, the priest can eat the front part of the sheep and its back leg. After that, the Nazirite can drink wine.”" 
Numbers6:21 Those are the rules about Nazirites. Also, the Nazirite may give anything else that he can give. And he must bring any other gift that he promised to the Lord.’" 
Numbers6:22 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers6:23 ‘Tell Aaron and his sons to use these words to bless the Israelites. 
Numbers6:24 They must say: “The Lord will bless you and he will keep you safe. 
Numbers6:25 The Lord will smile at you and he will be very kind to you. 
Numbers6:26 The Lord will be pleased with you and he will give you peace in your minds.” 
Numbers6:27 Aaron and his sons must use my name to bless the Israelites. If they do that, I will certainly bless them.’" 
Numbers7:1 When Moses had put up the tabernacle, he poured special oil on it. He also poured the oil on the things in the tent, the altar and all the tools. This showed that all these things were special for God to use." 
Numbers7:2 Then the 12 leaders of Israel's tribes brought their offerings. These men were the leaders who had helped to count the people. 
Numbers7:3 They brought their gifts to the Lord. They brought six carts, one cart for every two leaders. They brought 12 oxen. That was one ox for each leader. They brought all their offerings to the tabernacle." 
Numbers7:4 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers7:5 ‘Accept these gifts from the leaders. Give them to the Levites. All the Levites can use them for their work at the Tent of Meeting.’ 
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the carts and the oxen and he gave them to the Levites. 
Numbers7:7 He gave two carts and four oxen to the descendants of Gershon. 
Numbers7:8 He gave four carts and eight oxen to the descendants of Merari for their work. Ithamar was the son of Aaron. He had to tell the descendants of Merari the work that they should do. 
Numbers7:9 But the descendants of Kohath had to carry all the holy things on their shoulders. So Moses did not give any carts or oxen to the descendants of Kohath. 
Numbers7:10 When Moses had poured special oil on the altar, the leaders brought their gifts. They offered their gifts to God in front of the altar." 
Numbers7:11 The Lord said to Moses, ‘One leader must bring a gift to the altar every day. That is how they will make the altar special for me.’" 
Numbers7:12 On the first day, Nahshon, the son of Amminadab from the tribe of Judah brought his gift." 
Numbers7:13 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels, the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering." 
Numbers7:14 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:15 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:16 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:17 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab." 
Numbers7:18 On day 2, Nethanel, the son of Zuar, from the tribe of Issachar brought his gift." 
Numbers7:19 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels, the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering." 
Numbers7:20 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:21 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:22 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:23 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Nethanel, the son of Zuar." 
Numbers7:24 On day 3, Eliab, the son of Helon, from the tribe of Zebulun brought his gift." 
Numbers7:25 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels, the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering." 
Numbers7:26 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:27 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:28 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:29 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Eliab, the son of Helon." 
Numbers7:30 On day 4, Elizur, the son of Shedeur, from the tribe of Reuben brought his gift." 
Numbers7:31 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels, the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering." 
Numbers7:32 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:33 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:34 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:35 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Elizur, the son of Shedeur." 
Numbers7:36 On day 5, Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai, from the tribe of Simeon brought his gift." 
Numbers7:37 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:38 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:39 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:40 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:41 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai." 
Numbers7:42 On day 6, Eliasaph, the son of Deuel, from the tribe of Gad brought his gift." 
Numbers7:43 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:44 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:45 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:46 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:47 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Eliasaph, the son of Deuel." 
Numbers7:48 On day 7, Elishama, the son of Ammihud, from the tribe of Ephraim brought his gift." 
Numbers7:49 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:50 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:51 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:52 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:53 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Elishama, the son of Ammihud." 
Numbers7:54 On day 8, Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur, from the tribe of Manasseh brought his gift." 
Numbers7:55 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:56 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:57 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:58 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:59 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur." 
Numbers7:60 On day 9, Abidan, the son of Gideoni, from the tribe of Benjamin brought his gift." 
Numbers7:61 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:62 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:63 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:64 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:65 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Abidan, the son of Gideoni." 
Numbers7:66 On day 10, Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai, from the tribe of Dan brought his gift." 
Numbers7:67 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:68 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:69 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:70 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:71 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai." 
Numbers7:72 On day 11, Pagiel, the son of Ocran, from the tribe of Asher brought his gift." 
Numbers7:73 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:74 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:75 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:76 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:77 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Pagiel, the son of Ocran." 
Numbers7:78 On day 12, Ahira, the son of Enan, from the tribe of Naphtali brought his gift." 
Numbers7:79 He brought a plate and a bowl that someone had made from silver. The plate weighed 130 shekels. The bowl weighed 70 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. Both of those were full of good flour that he had mixed with oil. They were for a grain offering. 
Numbers7:80 He brought one small dish that someone had made from gold. It weighed ten shekels and it was full of incense. 
Numbers7:81 He brought one young bull, one male sheep and one male lamb that was less than one year old. Those animals were for a burnt offering." 
Numbers7:82 He brought one male goat for a sin offering. 
Numbers7:83 He also brought two oxen, five male sheep, five male goats, and five male lambs that were less than one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offerings. That was the gift of Ahira, the son of Enan." 
Numbers7:84 The leaders of Israel brought these gifts to the altar when Moses poured special oil on it. They brought all the gifts in the list below. They brought 12 plates and 12 bowls that people had made from silver. 
Numbers7:85 Each plate weighed 130 shekels and each bowl weighed 70 shekels. All the silver weighed 2,400 shekels. That was the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place." 
Numbers7:86 They brought 12 small dishes that people had made from gold. Each small dish weighed ten shekels, the shekel that the Levites used in the holy place. They were each full of incense. All the gold weighed 120 shekels." 
Numbers7:87 All the animals for the burnt offering were 12 young bulls, 12 male sheep and 12 male lambs that were one year old. They also brought the grain offerings with those animals. And they brought 12 male goats for a sin offering." 
Numbers7:88 There were also another 24 oxen, 60 male sheep, 60 male goats and 60 male lambs. The male lambs were one year old. Those animals were for the friendship offering. That was the offering when they made the altar special for God. That was after Moses had poured special oil on it." 
Numbers7:89 When Moses went into the Tent of Meeting, to speak with the Lord, the Lord did speak to him. And Moses heard the Lord. The sound came from above the lid which was on the top of the Covenant Box. On the lid, there were images of two cherubs. The sound came from between the cherubs. That was how the Lord spoke to Moses." 
Numbers8:1 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers8:2 ‘Say to Aaron, “When you put the lamps on the lampstand, the seven lamps must give light in front of the lampstand.” ’" 
Numbers8:3 So Aaron did that. He put its lamps at the front of the lampstand, as the Lord had said to Moses." 
Numbers8:4 A worker had made the lampstand from one piece of gold that he had made flat with a hammer. From its base to the flowers at the top, he had made it in that way. It was completely like the lampstand that the Lord had shown to Moses." 
Numbers8:5 Again the Lord spoke to Moses. He said," 
Numbers8:6 ‘Take the Levites from among the Israelites and make them clean. 
Numbers8:7 This is how you will do it: Splash on them the water that makes people clean. They must remove all the hair from their whole body, and they must wash their clothes. Then they will be clean." 
Numbers8:8 Then they must take a bull with the proper grain offering and oil. And you must take another bull for a sin offering. 
Numbers8:9 Then you must bring the Levites in front of the Tent of Meeting. You must also bring all the Israelites together there. 
Numbers8:10 You must bring the Levites in front of the Lord. Then the Israelites must put their hands on the Levites' heads. 
Numbers8:11 Aaron must give the Levites to the Lord as a special offering from the Israelites. Then they will be ready to do the Lord 's work. 
Numbers8:12 Then the Levites must put their hands on the heads of the bulls. Then offer one bull for a sin offering and the other one for a burnt offering to the Lord. That will make the Levites clean from their sins. 
Numbers8:13 And you must cause the Levites to stand in front of Aaron and his sons. So they will be a special gift to the Lord. 
Numbers8:14 That is how you will make the Levites separate from the other Israelites. And then the Levites will belong to me. 
Numbers8:15 After that, the Levites will go in to work in the Tent of Meeting. But you must make them clean and you must bring them to me as a special gift." 
Numbers8:16 That is because all the Levites among the Israelites are mine. All the Israelites' firstborn sons are mine. But I have taken the Levites for myself instead. 
Numbers8:17 Every firstborn son among the Israelites is mine. And all the firstborn male animals are mine too. On the day that I killed all the firstborn sons in Egypt, I kept the Israelite firstborn sons safe. So they belong to me in a special way." 
Numbers8:18 But I have not taken all the firstborn sons among the Israelites. Instead, I have taken the Levites." 
Numbers8:19 I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the Israelites. They will work at the Tent of Meeting on behalf of the Israelites. They will make the Israelites clean from their sins. So there will not be a bad disease among the Israelites when they go near to God's holy tent.’ 
Numbers8:20 So Moses and Aaron and all the Israelites did to the Levites everything that the Lord had commanded Moses to do. 
Numbers8:21 The Levites made themselves clean and they washed their clothes. Then Aaron brought them in front of Lord as a special gift. Aaron also burnt sacrifices to make them clean. 
Numbers8:22 After that, the Levites went in to the Tent of Meeting to do their work. They helped Aaron and his sons. The Israelites did everything with the Levites that the Lord had commanded Moses about them." 
Numbers8:23 Then the Lord spoke to Moses again. He said," 
Numbers8:24 ‘These are the rules about the Levites: The men who are 25 years old or older must go in to work in the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers8:25 But they must stop that work when they are 50 years old. They must not do this work any more. 
Numbers8:26 But they can help the other Levites in the Tent of Meeting. They can help them to do their jobs. But they must not have any of their own jobs. Those are the rules about the Levites and their jobs.’ 
Numbers9:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in the Sinai Desert. The Israelites had left Egypt. It was the second year after the Israelites had left that country. It was the first month during that year. The Lord said," 
Numbers9:2 ‘The Israelites must eat the Passover meal at the proper time. 
Numbers9:3 The proper time is during the evening of the 14th day of this month, when it starts to become dark. That is when you must eat it. You must obey all its rules and its laws.’" 
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the Israelites that they should eat the Passover feast. 
Numbers9:5 They ate it in the first month, on the 14th day when it started to become dark. They were in the Sinai Desert. The Israelites did everything that the Lord had commanded Moses about it." 
Numbers9:6 But there were some people who were not clean. That was because they had touched a dead body. So they could not eat the Passover on that day. They came to Moses and Aaron on that day. 
Numbers9:7 They said to Moses, ‘We are not clean because we touched a dead body. But we would like to offer our gift to the Lord at the proper time with the other Israelites.’" 
Numbers9:8 So Moses said to them, ‘Wait here. I will ask the Lord about this.’" 
Numbers9:9 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers9:10 ‘Say to the Israelites, “Anyone among you or your descendants may become unclean because of a dead body. Or they may be away because they are travelling. But they can still eat the Lord 's Passover meal." 
Numbers9:11 They must eat it during the evening of the 14th day of the second month, when it starts to become dark. They must eat the Passover lamb with bread and with bitter herbs. The bread must not have yeast in it." 
Numbers9:12 They must not keep any of the food until the next morning. They must not break any of the bones of the lamb. And they must obey all the rules about the Passover. 
Numbers9:13 But a person might be clean and he might not have travelled away from home. If that person chooses not to eat the Passover meal, you must remove him from his people. That is because he did not offer the proper sacrifices to the Lord at the right time. He must receive the punishment for his sin." 
Numbers9:14 Foreign people may also eat the Lord 's Passover meal, if they are living among you. But they must obey all the rules about the Passover festival. The rules are the same for a foreign person as they are for an Israelite.” ’" 
Numbers9:15 On the day that Moses put up the tabernacle, a cloud covered it. In the evening the cloud looked like a fire over the tent, until morning." 
Numbers9:16 It continued to be like this. The cloud covered it during the day. But at night, the cloud seemed to have fire in it." 
Numbers9:17 When the cloud went up from above the tent, the Israelites started to travel. When the cloud came down again, the Israelites made their camp in that place." 
Numbers9:18 In this way, the Lord decided when the Israelites should start to travel. He also decided where they should stop and make their camp. During all the time that the cloud was over the tabernacle, they stayed in their camp." 
Numbers9:19 Even when the cloud stayed over the tabernacle for many days, the Israelites obeyed the Lord. They did not start to travel." 
Numbers9:20 Sometimes the cloud stayed over the tabernacle for a few days. Then they remained in their camp. When the Lord showed them that they should travel, they started to travel." 
Numbers9:21 Sometimes the cloud stayed there from evening until morning. When the cloud went up in the morning, they started to travel. Whenever the cloud went up, in the day or in the night, they started to travel again." 
Numbers9:22 Sometimes the cloud continued to be above the tabernacle for two days or for a month or for a year. It stayed above it. Then, the Israelites stayed in their camp. They did not start to travel. But when it went up, they did start to travel." 
Numbers9:23 The Lord decided when they should make their camp. And the Lord decided when they should start to travel. So they obeyed the Lord about everything that the Lord said to Moses. 
Numbers10:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers10:2 ‘Make two trumpets from one piece of silver. Use a hammer to make the silver the right shape. Make a noise with the trumpets to tell the people that they must meet together. Also use them to tell the people that they must move the camp. 
Numbers10:3 When the people hear the sound from both trumpets, they must all come to you. They must meet you at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Numbers10:4 But you may want only the leaders of Israel's tribes to come to you. If you want that, a priest should make the sound from only one trumpet." 
Numbers10:5 When the trumpets make a loud short noise, the tribes on the east side of the camp must start to travel." 
Numbers10:6 When they make a second short noise, the tribes on the south side of the camp must start to travel. Short sounds from the trumpets will tell the people that they must start to travel." 
Numbers10:7 But when you want everyone to meet together, the trumpets must make a different sound." 
Numbers10:8 Only Aaron's sons, the priests, may use the trumpets to make a noise. This is a rule for you and for all your descendants." 
Numbers10:9 Use the trumpets to tell everyone when an enemy is attacking you in your own land. Warn the people so that they are ready to fight the enemy. Then the Lord your God will hear you and he will save you from your enemies. 
Numbers10:10 You must also make a noise with the trumpets on days when you are happy. This may happen at your regular festivals or on the first day of a month. You must make a noise with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over your friendship offerings. I will hear you and I will help you. Remember that I am the Lord your God.’" 
Numbers10:11 In the second year, in the second month, on the 20th day of the month, the cloud went up. It went away from God's Tent of Meeting." 
Numbers10:12 So the Israelites started to travel on their journey away from the Sinai Desert. Then the cloud stopped in the Paran Desert. 
Numbers10:13 This was how the Israelites started to travel for the first time, in the way that the Lord had commanded Moses to lead them." 
Numbers10:14 The flag of the camp of Judah's tribe started to travel first, with all their armies. Nahshon the son of Amminadab was the leader of all their armies." 
Numbers10:15 Nethanel, the son of Zuar, was the leader of the army of the tribe of Issachar."
