Numbers10:16 Eliab, the son of Helon, was the leader of the army of the tribe of Zebulun." 
Numbers10:17 Then the Levites packed the tabernacle. Then Gershon's clan and Merari's clan, who were carrying tabernacle, started to travel." 
Numbers10:18 Next the flag of the camp of Reuben started to travel, with all their armies. Elizur, the son of Shedeur, was the leader of all their armies." 
Numbers10:19 Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai, was the leader of the army of the tribe of Simeon." 
Numbers10:20 Eliasaph, the son of Deuel, was the leader of the army of the tribe of Gad." 
Numbers10:21 Then Kohath's clan started to travel. They were carrying the holy things. Before they arrived at the new camp, the other Levites would put up the tabernacle." 
Numbers10:22 Next the flag of the camp of the tribe of Ephraim started to travel, with all their armies. Elishama the son of Ammihud was the leader of all their armies." 
Numbers10:23 And Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur was the leader of the army of the tribe of Manasseh. 
Numbers10:24 Abidan the son of Gideoni was the leader of the army of the tribe of Benjamin. 
Numbers10:25 Then the flag of the camp of the tribe of Dan started to travel last, with all their armies. They could keep the Israelites safe if an enemy attacked them from behind them. Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai was the leader of all their armies." 
Numbers10:26 Pagiel the son of Ocran was the leader of the army of the tribe of Asher. 
Numbers10:27 And Ahira the son of Enan was the leader of the army of the tribe of Naphtali. 
Numbers10:28 The Israelites marched out like that every time that they started to travel. 
Numbers10:29 Moses spoke to Reuel's son, Hobab. He was the brother of Moses' wife. He was a Midianite. Moses said to Hobab, ‘We are leaving here now to go to another place. The Lord has said to us, “I will give that place to you.” The Lord has promised to help Israel's people with good things. So come with us and we will take care of you.’" 
Numbers10:30 But Hobab said to him, ‘I will not come. Instead, I will return to my own country and to my family there.’" 
Numbers10:31 Moses said, ‘Please do not leave us. You know good places for us to put up our tents in the desert. So you can be our guide." 
Numbers10:32 And if you come with us, we will share with you the good things that the Lord gives to us.’" 
Numbers10:33 So the Israelites left the Lord 's mountain. They travelled for three days. During that time, the Levites carried the Lord 's Covenant Box in front of them. This was so that the Lord could show them where to make their camp." 
Numbers10:34 And the Lord 's cloud was over them during the day, whenever they started to travel from the camp." 
Numbers10:35 Each time that the Covenant Box started to travel Moses said, ‘Be ready, Lord! Cause your enemies to run away in every direction!’" 
Numbers10:36 When they stopped travelling, Moses said, ‘Return, Lord, to be near to the thousands of Israel's people.’" 
Numbers11:1 Then the people started to complain about their problems. The Lord heard them and he became very angry. The Lord sent his fire to burn among them. It destroyed some parts at the edge of the camp. 
Numbers11:2 So the people asked Moses to help them. Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire stopped." 
Numbers11:3 So that place was called Taberah, because the Lord 's fire burned among them there." 
Numbers11:4 Some foreign people were travelling with the Israelites. These people wanted to eat better food. So the Israelites wept again. They said, ‘We want meat to eat!" 
Numbers11:5 We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt. We had as much as we wanted. And we remember all the nice vegetables that we ate, like cucumbers, leeks, onions and garlic. And we remember the melons." 
Numbers11:6 But now our mouths are dry and we do not want to eat this food. There is not even anything to look at, except this manna.’" 
Numbers11:7 The manna was like small coriander seeds. Its colour was yellow and white. 
Numbers11:8 The people walked about and they picked it up from the ground. They used heavy stones to make it into flour. Or they beat it with a stick. They cooked it in pots of water, and they made cakes with it. The taste of the cakes was like olive oil." 
Numbers11:9 At night, the manna fell with the dew on the ground in the camp." 
Numbers11:10 People in every family were crying at the doors of their tents. And Moses heard them. The Lord became very angry with the people. Moses was also not happy. 
Numbers11:11 So Moses said to the Lord, ‘I am your servant. But you have given this problem to me! It seems that you are not pleased with me. You have given me the hard job to be leader of all these people." 
Numbers11:12 They are not my children! But you have said to me, “Take care of them like parents take care of their babies. Carry them to the land that I have promised to their ancestors.”" 
Numbers11:13 They are complaining to me. They are saying, “Give us meat that we can eat!” But I cannot get meat to feed all these people!" 
Numbers11:14 I cannot be the leader of all these people unless I receive help. This job is too difficult for me. 
Numbers11:15 So if you do not want to help me, please kill me now. I do not want to continue to be so upset. So please be kind to me.’" 
Numbers11:16 So the Lord said to Moses, ‘Choose 70 leaders of the Israelites and bring them to the Tent of Meeting. You must know that they are leaders and officers that the people respect. Bring them to stand there with you." 
Numbers11:17 Then I will come down and I will speak with you there. I will take some of the authority of my Spirit that I gave to you. I will share it with them. Then they will help you to lead the people. So you will not be the only man who is doing that job. 
Numbers11:18 Then say to the people, “The Lord heard you when you were weeping. You said, ‘We want someone to give us meat to eat! Life was better when we were in Egypt.’ So the Lord will give you meat and you will eat it. So make yourselves clean for tomorrow, and then you will eat meat." 
Numbers11:19 You will not eat it only for one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or 20 days." 
Numbers11:20 You will eat it every day for a whole month! You will hate the smell of it and it will seem disgusting to you. This is because you have turned against the Lord. He is near to you and you have wept in front of him. You have said, ‘We should never have left Egypt.’ ” ’" 
Numbers11:21 Moses said to the Lord, ‘There are 600,000 men here. But you have said, “I will give them meat to eat for a whole month.”" 
Numbers11:22 Even if we killed all our sheep and cows, we would not have enough meat for them. If we caught every fish in the sea, we still would not have enough!’" 
Numbers11:23 Then the Lord said to Moses, ‘Do you think that I, the Lord, do not have enough power to do this? I have promised you to do it. So now you will see me do it!’" 
Numbers11:24 So Moses went outside and he told the people the Lord 's message. He chose 70 leaders of the Israelites and he put them around the tabernacle. 
Numbers11:25 Then the Lord came down in the cloud to speak to them. He took some of the authority of his Spirit that he had given to Moses. And he shared it with the 70 leaders. While God's Spirit stayed on them, they prophesied. But they did not do it again after that." 
Numbers11:26 But two men had stayed in the camp. Their names were Eldad and Medad. They were among the men that Moses had chosen. But they had not gone to meet with God at the tabernacle. God's Spirit also came on them. So they prophesied in the camp. 
Numbers11:27 A young man ran to Moses and he said, ‘Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.’" 
Numbers11:28 Nun's son Joshua was there. Since he was a boy, he had been Moses' servant. Joshua said to Moses, ‘Moses, my master, stop them.’" 
Numbers11:28 Nun's son Joshua was there. Since he was a boy, he had been Moses' servant. Joshua said to Moses, ‘Moses, my master, stop them.’" 
Numbers11:29 But Moses said to him, ‘Perhaps you think that I might be jealous of them. But I would be happy if the Lord would give his Spirit to all his people. I would like them all to be prophets!’" 
Numbers11:30 Then Moses and the leaders returned to the camp. 
Numbers11:31 Then the Lord sent a wind. It blew in birds called quails from the sea. They fell to the ground everywhere around the camp. There were birds everywhere as far as a person could walk in one day in any direction. They were about 1 metre deep all over the ground. 
Numbers11:32 The people were picking up the birds all day and all night and all the next day. Each person picked up at least 1,000 kilograms of birds! They put their dead bodies on the ground everywhere around the camp." 
Numbers11:33 But the Lord became very angry with the people. They had not even started to eat the meat when the Lord sent a very bad disease among them. 
Numbers11:34 They buried there the bodies of people who had wanted better food. So the people called that place Kibroth-Hattaavah. 
Numbers11:35 From Kibroth-Hattaavah the people travelled to Hazeroth. They stayed at Hazeroth. 
Numbers12:1 Moses had married a woman who was a descendant of Cush. Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because he had married that woman. 
Numbers12:2 They said, ‘We do not believe that the Lord has spoken only by Moses. We believe that he has spoken by us too.’ The Lord heard what they said." 
Numbers12:3 Moses was a very humble man. He was the most humble person in the whole world. 
Numbers12:4 Immediately, the Lord said to Moses, Aaron and Miriam, ‘You three people come out to the Tent of Meeting.’ So the three people went there." 
Numbers12:5 Then the Lord came down in a pillar of cloud. He stood at the entrance of the tent. He said that Aaron and Miriam should come nearer. They both came nearer. 
Numbers12:6 Then the Lord said, ‘Listen to my words! If there is a prophet among you, I, the Lord, will show myself to him in a vision. Or I will speak to him in a dream." 
Numbers12:7 But it is different when I speak to Moses. He is the one that I trust to lead all my people. 
Numbers12:8 I speak clearly to him, as I come near to him. I do not say things to him that are hard to understand. He even sees the shape of the Lord. So you should have been afraid to speak against my servant Moses!’" 
Numbers12:9 The Lord was very angry with Aaron and Miriam and he left them. 
Numbers12:10 When the cloud had gone away from above the tent, Miriam's skin became white, like snow. She now had the bad disease called leprosy in her skin. Aaron looked at her and he saw that she had leprosy." 
Numbers12:11 Then Aaron said to Moses, ‘My master, please do not punish us because we have done this very silly thing. We know it was a sin." 
Numbers12:12 But do not let Miriam be like a baby that is born dead, with a body that is only half there.’" 
Numbers12:13 So Moses called loudly to the Lord, ‘Please God, make her well again!’" 
Numbers12:14 But the Lord said to Moses, ‘Even if her father had only spat on her face, she would have to be ashamed for seven days. So you must keep her outside the camp for seven days. After that you can let her come back inside the camp.’" 
Numbers12:15 So Moses kept Miriam outside the camp for seven days. The people did not start to travel again until they had brought Miriam back into the camp again. 
Numbers12:16 After that, the people left Hazeroth. They went to the Paran Desert and they put up their tents there." 
Numbers13:1 Then the Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers13:2 ‘I will give the land of Canaan to the Israelites. Now you must send men to explore that land. Choose one man from each tribe of your ancestors. Each man must be a leader among his tribe.’ 
Numbers13:3 So Moses obeyed the Lord 's command. He sent the men from the Paran Desert into Canaan. Each man was a leader among the Israelites. 
Numbers13:4 This is a list of their names: From Reuben's tribe: Shammua, the son of Zaccur." 
Numbers13:5 From Simeon's tribe: Shaphat, the son of Hori." 
Numbers13:6 From Judah's tribe: Caleb, the son of Jephunneh." 
Numbers13:7 From Issachar's tribe: Igal, the son of Joseph." 
Numbers13:8 From Ephraim's tribe: Hoshea, the son of Nun." 
Numbers13:9 From Benjamin's tribe: Palti, the son of Raphu." 
Numbers13:10 From Zebulun's tribe: Gaddiel, the son of Sodi." 
Numbers13:11 From Manasseh's tribe: Gaddi, the son of Susi." 
Numbers13:12 From Dan's tribe: Ammiel, the son of Gemalli." 
Numbers13:13 From Asher's tribe: Sethur, the son of Michael." 
Numbers13:14 From Naphtali's tribe: Nahbi, the son of Vophsi." 
Numbers13:15 From Gad's tribe: Geuel, the son of Maki." 
Numbers13:16 Those are the names of the men that Moses sent to explore the land. Moses changed the name of Hoshea, the son of Nun, to Joshua." 
Numbers13:16 Those are the names of the men that Moses sent to explore the land. Moses changed the name of Hoshea, the son of Nun, to Joshua." 
Numbers13:17 When Moses sent them to explore the land, he said to them, ‘Go north from here, through the Negev Desert. Then continue to go north, into the place where there are hills." 
Numbers13:18 Look at the land to see what it is like. See whether the people who live there are strong or weak. See whether few people or many people live there. 
Numbers13:19 See whether their land is good or bad. Look at the towns where the people live. See if they have strong walls or if they are in the open. 
Numbers13:20 See whether the land is able to make good food grow there, or not. See whether there are forests that grow there. Then try to get some fruit from the land and bring it back here.’ (It was the time of year that the first grapes become ready to eat.)" 
Numbers13:21 So the men went and they explored the land of Canaan. They started in the Zin Desert, and they continued to Rehob, near Lebo-Hamath." 
Numbers13:22 When they had gone through the Negev Desert in the south of the land, they arrived at Hebron. That was where Ahiman, Sheshai and Talmai, the descendants of Anak, were living. They had built Hebron seven years before the Egyptians built Zoan." 
Numbers13:23 Then the men came to the valley called Eshcol. They cut a branch which had one group of grapes on it. It was so large that two men carried it between them on a pole. They also brought some other fruits called pomegranates and figs. 
Numbers13:24 People called that valley ‘Eshcol’ because of the large group of grapes that the Israelites cut down there. 
Numbers13:25 The men returned after they had explored the land for 40 days. 
Numbers13:26 The men returned to Kadesh in the Paran Desert. They brought their message to Moses and Aaron and to all the Israelites there. They told them all about the land of Canaan and they showed its fruit to them. 
Numbers13:27 They said to Moses, ‘We went to look at the land where you sent us. It is certainly a land where there is plenty of food and drink, enough for everyone. This is some of its fruit." 
Numbers13:28 But the people who live in the land are strong. Their towns are very big, and strong walls keep them safe. We also saw some descendants of Anak there." 
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites are living in the Negev, the land in the south of the country. The Hittites, Jebusites and Amorites live in the hill country. The Canaanites live by the sea and beside the edge of the Jordan River.’" 
Numbers13:30 As the people stood in front of Moses, Caleb told them to be quiet. He said, ‘We should go into the land and take it for ourselves. We will certainly be able to do it!’" 
Numbers13:31 But the men who had gone into Canaan with Caleb said, ‘These people are stronger than we are. So we cannot go and fight against them.’" 
Numbers13:32 Those men gave a bad report to the Israelites about the land that they had explored. They said, ‘We went through the whole land and we explored it. The land seems to eat the people who live there! All the people that we saw there were very big and strong." 
Numbers13:33 We also saw descendants of the Nephilites there. And we felt like very small insects when we looked at them. And we seemed like very small insects to them too.’ (The descendants of Anak are also descendants of the Nephilites.) 
Numbers14:1 Then all the people started to weep loudly. And they cried all night. 
Numbers14:2 All the Israelites spoke strongly against Moses and Aaron. All the people said to them, ‘We would rather have died in Egypt, or even in this desert!" 
Numbers14:3 Why is the Lord taking us into this land? Its people will kill us all with swords. They will take our wives and our children for themselves. We think that it would be better for us to return to Egypt.’ 
Numbers14:4 So they said to each other, ‘We should choose a new leader instead of Moses. Then we should return to Egypt.’" 
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron threw themselves on the ground in front of the whole group of the Israelites. 
Numbers14:6 Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, were two of the men who had explored the land. They were very upset and they tore their clothes." 
Numbers14:6 Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, were two of the men who had explored the land. They were very upset and they tore their clothes." 
Numbers14:7 Joshua and Caleb said to all the Israelites, ‘The land that we travelled through to explore is a very good land." 
Numbers14:7 Joshua and Caleb said to all the Israelites, ‘The land that we travelled through to explore is a very good land." 
Numbers14:8 If the Lord is happy with us, he will bring us into this land. And he will give it to us. In that land there is plenty of food and drink, enough for everyone." 
Numbers14:9 But do not refuse to obey the Lord. Do not be afraid of the people in that land. They are like food that is ready for us to eat! They have no place to hide. The Lord is with us to help us. So do not be afraid of them.’ 
Numbers14:10 Then all the people wanted to throw stones at Joshua and Caleb to kill them. But the glory of the Lord appeared over the Tent of Meeting and all the Israelites could see it. 
Numbers14:10 Then all the people wanted to throw stones at Joshua and Caleb to kill them. But the glory of the Lord appeared over the Tent of Meeting and all the Israelites could see it. 
Numbers14:11 The Lord said to Moses, ‘These people continue to turn away from me. I did great miracles to show them my power. But still they will not trust me." 
Numbers14:12 I will send a disease to kill them. They will no longer be my people. But I will make you, Moses, the ancestor of a nation that is greater and more powerful than they are.’" 
Numbers14:13 Moses said to the Lord, ‘Please do not do that! You used your great power to bring these people out of Egypt. If you destroy them, the Egyptians will hear about it." 
Numbers14:14 Then they will tell the people who live in this land about it. They know that you, Lord, are among your people. They know that you meet with your people. They know that your cloud stays over them. They know that you lead them as a pillar of cloud during the day and as a pillar of fire during the night." 
Numbers14:15 The people in other nations have heard of your power. So now, if you kill all your people at one time, the people in those nations will say," 
Numbers14:16 “The Lord could not bring those people into the land that he had promised to give to them. That is why he killed all of them in the desert.” 
Numbers14:17 But now, I pray that you will show how powerful you are. You yourself have said," 
Numbers14:18 “The Lord does not quickly become angry. His faithful love for his people continues for ever. He forgives people when they turn against him and they do bad things. But if someone is guilty, he surely punishes them. And he punishes their children, their grandchildren and their grandchildren's children.”" 
Numbers14:19 Because your faithful love is so great, please forgive the sins of these people. You have forgiven them since they left Egypt until now, so please continue to do that now.’" 
Numbers14:20 Then the Lord said, ‘I have forgiven them as you asked me to do." 
Numbers14:21 As surely as I live and all the earth will be full of my glory, I tell you this:" 
Numbers14:22 All these people have seen my glory. They have seen the miracles that I did in Egypt and in the desert. But they have tested me many times now to see if I would punish them. They still do not obey me. 
Numbers14:23 So I tell you, none of them will ever see the land that I promised to give to their ancestors. None of those men who did not trust me will see that place." 
Numbers14:24 But my servant Caleb has thought differently, and he has obeyed me completely. So I will bring him into the land that he explored. It will belong to his descendants." 
Numbers14:25 The Amalekites and the Canaanites live in the valleys now. So tomorrow you must turn back. You must travel through the desert towards the Red Sea.’ 
Numbers14:26 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers14:27 ‘These evil people have complained against me too many times! I have heard the bad things that the Israelites have said against me. 
Numbers14:28 Tell them this: “I, the Lord, have heard your words. I promise you, as surely as I live, I will certainly do to you as you have said." 
Numbers14:29 Your dead bodies will fall to the ground in this desert. All the men that Moses counted will die. Those were the men who were 20 years old or older when he counted them. They complained against me and now they will die in this desert. 
Numbers14:30 None of them will go into the land that I promised to give to you to live in. The only men who will go in are Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, the son of Nun." 
Numbers14:30 None of them will go into the land that I promised to give to you to live in. The only men who will go in are Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, the son of Nun." 
Numbers14:31 You said that the people in Canaan would take your children to be their slaves. But I will bring those children into the land. You did not want that land for yourselves, so now it will belong to your children." 
Numbers14:32 But as for you, your dead bodies will fall to the ground in this desert." 
Numbers14:33 Your children will be shepherds in the desert for 40 years. Because you did not trust me, your children will suffer This will continue until all your dead bodies are lying in the desert." 
Numbers14:34 You explored the land of Canaan for 40 days. Now you will suffer for 40 years, because of your sins. Each day becomes one year! You will know that I have become your enemy." 
Numbers14:35 I, the Lord, have spoken! I will certainly punish these wicked people. They have all joined together to speak against me. In this desert they will die. Yes, that will be the end of them!” ’" 
Numbers14:36 The men who had given a bad report to the people about the land of Canaan died immediately. Moses had sent them to explore the land, but they had caused all the people to turn against the Lord. So the Lord sent a bad disease to kill those men." 
Numbers14:37  
Numbers14:38 Only two men who had gone to explore the land did not die. They were Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh." 
Numbers14:38 Only two men who had gone to explore the land did not die. They were Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh." 
Numbers14:39 When Moses gave God's message to all the Israelites, the people were very sad." 
Numbers14:40 The next morning, they got up early. They went up to the top of the hills near there. They said, ‘We have certainly done a bad thing. But now we are ready to obey the Lord! We will go to the place that he has promised to give us.’" 
Numbers14:41 But Moses said, ‘You are still not obeying the Lord 's command. Why is that? You will not have any success." 
Numbers14:42 Do not go into that land. The Lord is not with you. So your enemies will win against you. 
Numbers14:43 The Amalekites and the Canaanites will be there in front of you. They will kill you with their swords. Because you have turned away from the Lord, he will not be with you to help you.’" 
Numbers14:44 But the people did not listen to Moses. Moses and the Lord 's Covenant Box stayed in the camp. But the men marched up to the top of the hills. 
Numbers14:45 Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in the hill country ran down. They attacked the Israelite men and they chased them to Hormah. 
Numbers15:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers15:2 ‘Say to the Israelites, “You will go into the land that I am giving to you." 
Numbers15:3 When you are living there, you may offer bulls, sheep or goats as a sacrifice to the Lord. You will burn them on the altar as a burnt offering. You may offer special sacrifices to the Lord when you have promised to do it. Or you may offer sacrifices because you choose to do it. Also, you will give sacrifices during your regular festivals. The smell of these sacrifices as they burn will please the Lord." 
Numbers15:4 The person who brings the gift to the Lord must also bring a gift of flour. He must mix the flour with oil. It should be 1 kilogram of your best flour. Mix this flour with 1 litre of olive oil. 
Numbers15:5 You must also give 1 litre of wine for each lamb. You must give that as a drink offering with each burnt offering or sacrifice. 
Numbers15:6 If the animal is a male sheep, you must also offer 2 kilograms of flour. Mix this flour with 1.5 litres of olive oil." 
Numbers15:7 Also, you must pour 1.5 litres of wine on the altar as a drink offering. The smell of this sacrifice will please the Lord." 
Numbers15:8 A person can give a young bull as a burnt offering. This may be a special sacrifice that he has promised to give to God. Or it may be a friendship offering to the Lord. 
Numbers15:9 With the bull, you must also offer 3 kilograms of flour. Mix this flour with 2 litres of olive oil." 
Numbers15:10 Also pour 2 litres of wine on the altar as a drink offering. The smell of this sacrifice will please the Lord. 
Numbers15:11 You must do the same thing for each bull or for each male sheep. And you must do the same thing for each male lamb or for each young goat. 
Numbers15:12 You must do this for every animal that you offer as a gift to God. 
Numbers15:13 All the Israelites must do this when they burn sacrifices. The smell of the sacrifices as they burn will please the Lord. 
Numbers15:14 A stranger who is living in your land may want to give a gift by fire to the Lord. He must do the same as you do now, and for all future time." 
Numbers15:15 These rules will be the same for the Israelites and for all foreign people who live among you. They are for now and for all future time. Both you and the foreign person must obey the Lord 's rules in the same way. 
Numbers15:16 There is the same law for you and for the foreign person who is living among you. There is only one set of rules.” ’ 
Numbers15:17 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers15:18 ‘Tell the Israelites this: “I am bringing you into the land of Canaan. After you have arrived there, you must obey my rules." 
Numbers15:19 When you first eat the food from that land, you must give a special offering to the Lord." 
Numbers15:20 You must give a cake as a special offering from the first part of your flour. Do this as you give your special gift from your threshing floor. 
Numbers15:21 From the first part of your flour, you must give to the Lord a special offering. Your descendants must also continue to do this." 
Numbers15:22 Someone among you might not obey a command that the Lord gave to Moses. But you might not realize your mistake. 
Numbers15:23 It might be any command that the Lord gave to Moses for you and your descendants to obey. 
Numbers15:24 If it was a mistake that the whole group did not know about it, this is what you must do. The whole group of Israelites must prepare a young bull as a burnt offering. The smell of this sacrifice will please the Lord. You must also bring a grain offering, a drink offering and a male goat as a sin offering." 
Numbers15:25 The priest will use these offerings to make the all the Israelites clean from their sin. The Lord will forgive them. Their sin was a mistake and they have brought the proper offerings to the Lord. They have brought to him their burnt offering and their sin offering. So the Lord will forgive them for their mistake. 
Numbers15:26 He will forgive all the Israelites and the foreign people who live among you. The sin was a mistake that the whole group should not have made. 
Numbers15:27 If one person makes a mistake like that, they must offer a female goat as a sin offering. The goat must be one year old." 
Numbers15:28 The priest will use this sacrifice to make the person clean from their sin. Then the Lord will forgive that person for their mistake. The person did not realize that it was a sin against the Lord. 
Numbers15:29 The same rule is for all people who make a mistake like that. It is for the Israelites and for all the foreign people who live among you. 
Numbers15:30 But someone might do something that he knows is a sin. He does it because he wants to do it, and so he insults the Lord. You must remove that person from among the Israelite people. Do that if he is an Israelite or if he is a foreign person who lives among you." 
Numbers15:31 He has not respected the Lord 's teaching. He has not obeyed the Lord 's commands. So you must remove that person completely from among the people. He deserves his punishment because he is guilty.” ’ 
Numbers15:32 When the Israelites were in the desert, a man was picking up sticks of wood on the day of rest." 
Numbers15:33 The people who saw him took him to Moses, Aaron and the whole group of people." 
Numbers15:34 They were not sure how to punish the man. So they put him by himself, with a guard to watch him." 
Numbers15:35 The Lord said to Moses, ‘You must punish this man with death. All the people must take him outside the camp. They must throw stones at him to kill him.’" 
Numbers15:36 So the people took the man outside the camp. They threw stones at him until he died. They did what the Lord had told Moses to do. 
Numbers15:37 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers15:38 ‘Tell the Israelites to put tassels on the corners of their clothes. They must use blue strings to fix the tassels. This is a rule for you and for your descendants 
Numbers15:39 When you look at the tassels, it will help you to remember all the Lord 's commands. You will remember to obey them, and not obey your own ideas. You will not turn away from me and do the things that you yourselves want to do." 
Numbers15:40 In this way, you will remember all my commands and you will obey them. You will belong to me, your God, as my holy people." 
Numbers15:41 I am the Lord your God. I brought you out safely out from Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord your God.’ 
Numbers16:1 One day, Korah the son of Izhar and grandson of Kohath, the son of Levi, decided to speak against Moses. With him were Eliab's sons, Dathan and Abiram, and Peleth's son, On. These men belonged to Reuben's tribe." 
Numbers16:2 Together with 250 leaders of the Israelites, they all turned against Moses. These were famous men that the people had chosen as their leaders." 
Numbers16:3 They all came to speak against Moses and Aaron. They said, ‘You are taking more authority for yourselves than you should. All of us Israelites belong to God as his holy people. The Lord lives among us all. So you should not say that you are more important than the rest of the Lord 's people.’" 
Numbers16:4 When Moses heard that, he threw himself down with his face on the ground." 
Numbers16:5 He said to Korah and to the other leaders who were with him, ‘Tomorrow morning, the Lord will show us who belongs to him as his holy people. He will allow the person who is holy to come near to him." 
Numbers16:6 Now, Korah, do this: You and your friends must get some metal dishes for yourselves." 
Numbers16:7 Tomorrow, put fire and incense in the dishes, to burn in front of the Lord. Then the Lord will choose the man who is holy. It is you Levites who have made yourselves too important!’" 
Numbers16:8 Then Moses said to Korah, ‘Listen to me, you Levites!" 
Numbers16:9 The God of Israel has caused you to be separate from all the other tribes of Israel. He has brought you near to himself, to work in the Lord 's tabernacle. He has chosen you to serve the other Israelite people as they worship the Lord. But it seems that this is not enough for you." 
Numbers16:10 The Lord has brought you, Korah, and the other Levites near to himself. Now it seems that you also want to become priests." 
Numbers16:11 You and you friends have turned against the Lord. It is not only Aaron that you are complaining about!’ 
Numbers16:12 Then Moses called Eliab's sons, Dathan and Abiram, to come to him. But they said, ‘No, we refuse to come!" 
Numbers16:13 You have brought us out of a country where there is plenty of food and drink, enough for everyone. You have brought us here, where we will die in the desert. But it seems that this is not enough for you. You also want to rule us as our master!" 
Numbers16:14 You have certainly not brought us into a land where there is plenty of food and drink. You have not given us fields and vineyards, for us and our descendants to enjoy. You cannot deceive all these men. We will not come up to meet with you!’" 
Numbers16:15 Moses was very angry. He said to the Lord, ‘Do not accept what these men offer to you. I have never taken anything from them, not even one donkey! I have never done a wrong thing to any of them.’" 
Numbers16:16 Moses said to Korah, ‘Tomorrow, you and all your friends must come to the tabernacle. You must come there to stand in front of the Lord. Aaron will be there too." 
Numbers16:17 Each of you must bring a dish and you must put incense in it. You must burn the incense as an offering to the Lord. There will be 250 dishes with incense, one for each of you as well as one for Aaron.’" 
Numbers16:18 So each man brought a dish. Each man put fire on the incense to burn it. They all stood with Moses and Aaron at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers16:19 Korah brought the whole group of Israelites to stand against Moses and Aaron at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Then the bright glory of the Lord appeared to all the people. 
Numbers16:20 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers16:21 ‘Stand away from all these people so that I can destroy them immediately!’ 
Numbers16:22 But Moses and Aaron threw themselves down with their faces on the ground. They said, ‘You, our God, are the God who gives life to all people. When only one man has done a bad thing, will you be angry with all the people? Is that right?’" 
Numbers16:23 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers16:24 ‘Tell all the people to move away from the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram.’" 
Numbers16:25 Moses got up from the ground. He went to Dathan and Abiram. The leaders of the Israelites went with him. 
Numbers16:26 Moses said to all the people, ‘Move away from the tents of these wicked men. Do not touch anything that belongs to them. If you do not move away, God may destroy you together with them, because of all their sins.’" 
Numbers16:27 So the people moved away from the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram came and stood at the doors of their tents, together with their wives, their babies and their children." 
Numbers16:28 Then Moses said, ‘It is the Lord who has sent me to do all these things for you. It was not my own idea. This is how you will know." 
Numbers16:29 If these men die of old age, as all men will die one day, then it shows that the Lord has not sent me." 
Numbers16:30 But if the Lord causes something completely new to happen to them, you will know that they are guilty people. They are the ones who have turned against the Lord. You will know this if the ground opens its mouth and eats them! You will know it if these men and everything that belongs to them fall down into the deep hole of death while they are still alive. Then you will know that these men have insulted the Lord.’" 
Numbers16:31 When Moses finished speaking, a great hole opened in the ground." 
Numbers16:32 It was like a big mouth that ate those men, their families and everything that belonged to them. The earth ate Korah, all his group and everything that belonged to them." 
Numbers16:33 They all went down into the deep hole of death while they were still alive. The ground closed over them and they disappeared. They no longer belonged among God's people. 
Numbers16:34 The other Israelites who were standing near those men heard them shout. So they ran away quickly. They said, ‘Help! The ground might eat us too!’" 
Numbers16:35 Then the Lord sent fire down on the other 250 men who were offering incense to God. The fire destroyed them completely. 
Numbers16:36 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers16:37 ‘Tell Aaron's son, Eleazar, to take the dishes with incense out of the burning fire. That is because those dishes are holy. Then take the coal that was burning in the dishes and throw it a long way away." 
Numbers16:38 Those men died as punishment for their sin. But they were offering incense to the Lord, so the metal dishes that they were holding have become holy. Workers must use hammers to make the dishes into thin metal. Then they must use the metal to cover the altar. That will be a sign to warn the Israelites.’" 
Numbers16:39 So Eleazar took the 250 dishes that the men had used to burn incense. Workers used the bronze from the dishes to cover the altar. 
Numbers16:40 This would help the Israelites to remember that only descendants of Aaron could burn incense in front of the Lord. They knew that God would punish anyone else, as he punished Korah and his friends. Eleazar obeyed the command that the Lord had given to Moses." 
Numbers16:41 But on the next day, the whole group of Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron. They said, ‘You have killed the Lord 's people!’" 
Numbers16:42 When the people met together to complain against Moses and Aaron, they turned to look at the Tent of Meeting. They saw that the cloud now covered the tent. The Lord 's bright glory appeared to them." 
Numbers16:43 Moses and Aaron went to stand at the front of the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers16:44 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers16:45 ‘Move away from the people so that I can destroy them immediately!’ Moses and Aaron threw themselves down with their faces on the ground. 
Numbers16:46 Moses said to Aaron, ‘Take your incense dish. Take coals with fire from the altar. Put them on the dish and add incense on top. Then take it quickly among all the people. This will make them clean from their sin. Be quick! The Lord is very angry with the people. He has already started to punish them with a bad disease!’" 
Numbers16:47 So Aaron did what Moses had told him to do. He ran among the people where the disease was already killing some of them. He burned incense on the coals and he made the people clean from their sin. 
Numbers16:48 He stood between the people who had died and those who were still living. In that way, he caused the disease to stop." 
Numbers16:49 But 14,700 people died because of the disease, as well as the people who had died with Korah." 
Numbers16:50 After the disease stopped, Aaron went back to Moses at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting." 
Numbers17:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers17:2 ‘Tell the Israelites to give you 12 sticks, one for each tribe. Write the name of the leader of each tribe on his stick." 
Numbers17:3 Write Aaron's name on the stick from the tribe of Levi. 
Numbers17:4 Take the sticks to the Tent of Meeting and put them in front of the Covenant Box. 
Numbers17:5 Leaves will start to grow on the stick of the man that I have chosen as my priest. In that way, I will cause the Israelites to stop complaining against you.’" 
Numbers17:6 Moses told the Israelites to do this. The leader of each tribe gave him a stick. So there were 12 sticks, one for each tribe. Aaron's stick was among them." 
Numbers17:7 Moses put the sticks in front of the Lord 's Covenant Box in the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers17:8 The next day, Moses went into the tent where the Covenant Box was. He saw that Aaron's stick had started to make leaves. There were even flowers and almonds growing on it! That was the stick from Levi's tribe." 
Numbers17:9 So Moses brought all the sticks out of the tent and he showed them to the people. The leader of each tribe took his own stick. 
Numbers17:10 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Put Aaron's stick in front of the Covenant Box again. It will stay there as a sign to warn people who complain against me. They will know that, if they continue to complain, they will die.’" 
Numbers17:11 Moses did what the Lord commanded him to do. 
Numbers17:12 Then the Israelites said to Moses, ‘Look! We will all die! We cannot escape!" 
Numbers17:13 Anyone who comes near to the Lord 's tabernacle will surely die! Must we all die?’ 
Numbers18:1 The Lord said to Aaron, ‘You and your sons and the other Levites serve me in my holy tent. So if any wrong thing happens there, you are the people who will be guilty. You will also be guilty for any wrong thing that happens among the priests." 
Numbers18:2 Bring the other people of your tribe, the Levites, to help you in the tabernacle. They will join with you while you and your sons serve me there." 
Numbers18:3 They must take care of the tabernacle on your behalf. But they must never go near anything that is inside the Holy Place. And they must not go near the altar. If they do, they will die, and you will die too." 
Numbers18:4 The Levites must join with you as you serve me in the Tent of Meeting. It is their job to do all the work to take care of the Tent. No other people have authority to join you as you work there. 
Numbers18:5 You and your sons must take care of the Holy Place and the altar. Then I will not have to punish the Israelites again. 
Numbers18:6 I have chosen your family, the Levites, from among the other Israelite tribes. I, the Lord, have given them to you. They will help you to take care of the Tent of Meeting." 
Numbers18:7 But only you, Aaron, and your sons will serve me as priests. You alone will serve me at the altar and in the Most Holy Place behind the curtain. I have chosen you to serve me as my priests. That is my gift to you. If any other person tries to do that work, they must die.’" 
Numbers18:8 The Lord said to Aaron, ‘I have given you authority over the offerings that people give to me. I give to you all the holy gifts that the Israelite people bring to me. They will belong to you and to your sons, the priests. That will be your share for all time." 
Numbers18:9 The share for the priests will be the parts of the holy offerings that do not burn on the altar. A part of all these gifts will belong to you: grain offerings, sin offerings and guilt offerings. These are holy gifts that will belong to you and to your sons." 
Numbers18:10 Any man in your family may eat these gifts. But remember to eat them in a holy place, because they are holy gifts." 
Numbers18:11 You will also receive part of the special gifts that the Israelites lift up as an offering to me. I give these parts to you and to your sons and daughters, for all time. Any person in your family who is clean may eat it." 
Numbers18:12 You will also receive part of the first crops that people give to the Lord each year. I give you the best olive oil, the best wine and the best grain from these gifts." 
Numbers18:13 All of the first food that people give to the Lord from their harvest belongs to you. Any person in your family who is clean may eat it. 
Numbers18:14 Everything in Israel that people have chosen to give to me will belong to you. 
Numbers18:15 I give you every firstborn animal and male child that people offer to the Lord. But people must pay a price to buy back their firstborn sons. They must also buy back any firstborn male animal that is unclean. 
Numbers18:16 They must do this when they are one month old. The price for each one will be five shekels of silver. The shekel must have the same weight as the shekel that the Levites use in God's holy place. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel. 
Numbers18:17 But people cannot buy back the firstborn of a cow, a sheep or a goat. These animals are holy. Splash their blood on the altar and burn the fat from them. The smell of these offerings will please the Lord." 
Numbers18:18 The meat from these animals will belong to you. That will be the same as the meat from the ribs and the right back leg of the special gifts that you lift up to me. These all belong to you. 
Numbers18:19 I give you all the special gifts that the Israelites offer to the Lord. They will belong to you, to your sons and to your daughters. That is a rule for all time. This is the Lord 's promise to you and to your descendants for all time. It will never change.’" 
Numbers18:20 Then the Lord said to Aaron, ‘When Israel's tribes receive land to be their home, you priests will not have your own land. You will not have a share in the things that they receive. Instead, I myself am the share that belongs to you." 
Numbers18:21 I give to the Levites all the tithes that the Israelites give to me. These tithes will belong to the Levites because they serve me in the Tent of Meeting. The tithes will pay them for their work. 
Numbers18:22 The other Israelites must not go near the Tent of Meeting again. If they do go near it, they will be guilty of a sin. They will die as their punishment." 
Numbers18:23 Only the Levites must do the work in the Tent of Meeting. And if they do anything wrong there, they will be guilty. This will be a rule for you and for all your descendants. The Levites will not receive their own land among the other Israelite tribes." 
Numbers18:24 Instead, I give to the Levites the tithes that the Israelites bring to the Lord as their offerings. That is why I have said to the Levites, “You will not receive your own land, as the other Israelites do.” ’" 
Numbers18:25 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers18:26 ‘You must tell the Levites, “When you receive the tithes from the Israelites, you must offer one tenth of those tithes as a special gift to the Lord. That will be your tithe from the tithes that I have given to you." 
Numbers18:27 The Lord will accept this special gift like the tithe that a farmer gives from his grain or his wine. 
Numbers18:28 That is how you will make a special offering to the Lord from the tithes that you receive from the Israelites. You must give this special offering to Aaron the priest. 
Numbers18:29 From all the gifts that you receive, you must give the best part to the Lord. That is the part that belongs to the Lord.”" 
Numbers18:30 You must also tell the Levites, “When you give the best parts as an offering to the Lord, he will accept them. It will be as if they came from your own fields and vineyards." 
Numbers18:31 You Levites and your families may eat the part that remains from the gifts that you receive. You may eat this part anywhere that you choose. It belongs to you because of your work in the Tent of Meeting. 
Numbers18:32 If you have given the best part to the priests, you will not be guilty when you eat what remains. But remember that these gifts from the people of Israel are holy. You must obey these rules about them. If you eat them in a careless way, your punishment will be death.” ’" 
Numbers19:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron," 
Numbers19:2 ‘This is another rule that the Lord has commanded in his Law. Tell the Israelites to bring you a young red cow that is perfect. It must not have anything wrong with it. Nobody must have put a yoke on it. 
Numbers19:3 You must give the cow to Eleazar the priest. He must take it outside the camp. People must kill it there while Eleazar watches. 
Numbers19:4 Eleazar must use his finger to take some of the blood from the cow. He must turn towards the Tent of Meeting and splash the blood seven times in that direction. 
Numbers19:5 Then people must burn the whole cow while Eleazar is watching. They must burn its body completely, with the skin, the meat, the blood and everything that is inside its body." 
Numbers19:6 The priest must then take some wood, some hyssop leaves and some red wool. He must throw these things on the fire where the cow is burning." 
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and wash his whole body with water. After that, he can come into the camp, but he will continue to be unclean until the evening." 
Numbers19:8 The man who burns the cow must also wash his clothes in water, as well as his whole body. He will continue to be unclean until the evening." 
Numbers19:9 Then a man who is clean must pick up all the ashes of the red cow. He must put them in a clean place outside the camp. Keep them safe there for the Israelites to use. Mix the ashes with water. That water will cause unclean people to become clean again. It is water to make people clean from their sin. 
Numbers19:10 The man who picks up the cow's ashes must also wash his clothes. He will continue to be unclean until evening. This rule will be for all time, for the Israelites and for foreign people who are living among them." 
Numbers19:11 Any person who touches a dead body will be unclean for seven days. 
Numbers19:12 He must use the water to wash himself on the third day and on the seventh day. Then he will be clean again. If he does not wash himself on the third day and on the seventh day, he will not be clean." 
Numbers19:13 If anyone touches the body of a dead person and he does not make himself clean again, he causes the Lord 's tabernacle to become unclean. You must remove that person from among the Israelites. He continues to be unclean because he did not splash the special water on himself." 
Numbers19:14 This is another rule: When someone dies in a tent, anyone who goes into the tent will be unclean for seven days. Everyone who is in the tent will become unclean." 
Numbers19:15 Any pot or bowl in the tent that does not have a lid will also become unclean. 
Numbers19:16 These people also become unclean: anyone who touches the dead body of a person out in the country. Somebody may have killed the dead person or they may have died by themselves. Also, anyone who touches a human bone or the grave of a dead person. All these people will be unclean for seven days." 
Numbers19:17 When a person has become unclean, this is how to make them clean again: Take some of the ashes from the red cow that was burned. Put the ashes in a jar and pour some fresh water over them." 
Numbers19:18 Then someone who is clean must take a branch of hyssop. He must put it into the water. He must use the hyssop to splash the water on the tent and on everything that is in it. He must also splash the water on all the people who have been in the tent. He must also splash it on anyone who has touched a human bone or a dead body or someone's grave. 
Numbers19:19 The clean person must splash the water on the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day. On the seventh day he will cause the person to be clean again. Then the unclean person must use water to wash his clothes and his whole body. In the evening, that person will be clean again." 
Numbers19:20 But if an unclean person does not make himself clean in this way, you must remove that person from among the Israelites. He has caused the Lord 's holy tent to become unclean. He did not use the special water that makes people clean. So he continues to be unclean." 
Numbers19:21 This is a rule for all time for the people. The man who splashes the special water on unclean people must wash his clothes. Anyone who touches the special water will be unclean until evening. 
Numbers19:22 If an unclean person touches anything, that thing also becomes unclean. If anyone else touches the unclean thing, he also will be unclean until evening.’" 
Numbers20:1 In the first month, the Israelites came to the Zin Desert. They put up their tents at Kadesh. Miriam died there and the people buried her." 
Numbers20:2 The people did not have any water to drink. They complained to Moses and Aaron. 
Numbers20:3 They said to Moses, ‘We would rather be dead! When the Lord killed our brothers, he should have killed us too." 
Numbers20:4 We are the Lord 's people. Have you brought us and our animals here to die? 
Numbers20:5 You should not have brought us to this terrible place. When we were in Egypt, we had grain, figs, grapes and pomegranates. There is no food like that here! And there is no water to drink!’" 
Numbers20:6 Moses and Aaron went away from the people to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They threw themselves down with their faces on the ground. The glory of the Lord appeared to them. 
Numbers20:7 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers20:8 ‘Get the stick from in front of the Covenant Box. Take your brother Aaron with you and go to the whole group of the Israelites. Then speak to that rock that you can all see. Water will pour out from it. You will give water to the people and their animals for them to drink.’ 
Numbers20:9 So Moses did what the Lord commanded him to do. He took the stick from its place in the tabernacle. 
Numbers20:10 Then Moses and Aaron brought all the people to meet together in front of the rock. Moses said to them, ‘Listen now, you people who refuse to obey God. It seems that we must bring water out of this rock for you!’" 
Numbers20:11 Moses lifted up his arm. He hit the rock twice with his stick. Water poured out from the rock. The people and their animals drank the water. 
Numbers20:12 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron, ‘You did not trust me enough to show the Israelites that I am holy. So you will not lead them into the land that I have promised to give to them.’" 
Numbers20:13 The place where this happened was called ‘Meribah’, because the Israelites quarrelled with the Lord there. But he showed them there that he was their holy God." 
Numbers20:14 From Kadesh, Moses sent men to the king of Edom with this message: ‘We are Israelites, your relatives. You know about all the troubles that have happened to us." 
Numbers20:15 Our ancestors went to Egypt. We lived there for many years. The people in Egypt were cruel to us. 
Numbers20:16 But when we asked the Lord to help us, he heard us. He sent an angel and he brought us out from Egypt. Now we are here at Kadesh, which is a town at the border of your land." 
Numbers20:17 Please let us travel through your land. We will not go through any fields or through any vineyards. We will not even drink water from your wells. We will go along the main road. We will not leave that road on either side until we have gone through all your land.’ 
Numbers20:18 The king of Edom said, ‘You must not travel through my country. If you do, we will bring our army to attack you.’" 
Numbers20:19 The Israelites replied, ‘We will stay on the main road. If our animals drink your water, we will pay for it. We only want to travel through your country. We do not want anything else.’" 
Numbers20:20 The king of Edom said, ‘No, you must not travel through our country.’ He sent many strong soldiers to attack the Israelites." 
Numbers20:21 In that way, the Edomites stopped the Israelites from travelling through Edom. So the Israelites turned away from there." 
Numbers20:22 All the Israelites travelled from Kadesh to Mount Hor. 
Numbers20:23 When they were there, at the border of Edom's land, the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron." 
Numbers20:24 He said, ‘Aaron will soon die. He will not go into the land that I am giving to the Israelites. That is because you both refused to obey me at Meribah." 
Numbers20:25 Moses, you must take Aaron and his son Eleazar to climb up Mount Hor." 
Numbers20:26 Take the priest's clothes from Aaron and put them on Eleazar. Aaron will die there.’ 
Numbers20:27 Moses did what the Lord told him to do. Moses, Aaron and Eleazar climbed up Mount Hor, while the Israelites watched." 
Numbers20:28 Moses took the priest's clothes from Aaron and he put them on Eleazar. Aaron died there, at the top of the mountain. Moses and Eleazar walked back down the mountain." 
Numbers20:29 The Israelites realized that Aaron had died. They wept for 30 days because of his death. 
Numbers21:1 The king of Arad lived in the Negev Desert, in the south part of Canaan. People told him that the Israelites were coming on the road to Atharim. So he attacked the Israelites and he caught some of them." 
Numbers21:2 The Israelites made this promise to the Lord: ‘If you put these people under our power, we will destroy their towns for you.’" 
Numbers21:3 The Lord accepted the Israelites' promise. He put the Canaanites under their power. The Israelites destroyed them and all their towns. After that, the place was called Hormah." 
Numbers21:4 The Israelites left Mount Hor. They travelled round the edge of Edom to the Red Sea. As they travelled, the people were upset." 
Numbers21:5 They complained to God and to Moses. They said, ‘You should not have brought us from Egypt to die here in the desert. There is nothing to eat. There is no water to drink. We do not like this terrible food, manna.’" 
Numbers21:6 Then the Lord sent snakes among the people. They bit the people and many of the Israelites died. 
Numbers21:7 So the people came to Moses and they said, ‘We did a bad thing when we spoke against the Lord and against you. Please pray to the Lord on our behalf. Ask him to remove the snakes from us.’ So Moses prayed for the people." 
Numbers21:8 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Make a model of a snake and put it up high on a pole. When a snake bites anyone, they must look at the bronze snake. Then they will not die.’" 
Numbers21:9 So Moses made a bronze snake and he put it on a pole. When a snake bit anyone and they looked at the bronze snake, they did not die." 
Numbers21:10 The Israelites travelled to Oboth. They put up their tents there. 
Numbers21:11 Then they travelled from Oboth, and they put up their tents at Iye-Abarim, in the desert. That place is on the east border of Moab." 
Numbers21:12 Then they travelled again and they put up their tents in the Zered Valley. 
Numbers21:13 They left there and they put up their tents in the desert on the north side of the Arnon River. That river is the border between the Moabites and the Amorites. 
Numbers21:14 The Book of the Lord 's Wars tells about this. It talks about Waheb in Suphah and about the valleys of the Arnon River. 
Numbers21:15 It also tells about the sides of the valleys on the border of Moab that lead to the town of Ar. 
Numbers21:16 From there, the Israelites continued to travel until they arrived at Beer. That is the well where the Lord had said to Moses, ‘Bring the people together. Then I will give water to them.’" 
Numbers21:17 The Israelites sang this song at that well: ‘Sing to this well. Ask it to give water to us. 
Numbers21:18 This was the well that our rulers dug. Our leaders dug it with their sceptres and their ruler's sticks.’ After that, the Israelites went from the desert to Mattanah." 
Numbers21:19 Then they went from Mattanah to Nahaliel. They went from Nahaliel to Bamoth. 
Numbers21:20 They went from Bamoth to the valley in the region of Moab. They put up their tents near the top of Mount Pisgah that is high above the desert. 
Numbers21:21 The Israelites sent men with a message to Sihon, the king of the Amorites." 
Numbers21:22 They said, ‘Please let us travel through your country. We will not go through any fields or through any vineyards. We will not even drink water from your wells. We will go along the main road. We will not leave that road on either side until we have gone through all your land.’" 
Numbers21:23 But Sihon would not let the Israelites travel through his land. Sihon and his army marched to Jahaz, in the desert. They attacked the Israelites there." 
Numbers21:24 The Israelites fought against the army of Sihon and they won. They got power over the land from the Arnon River to the River Jabbok. But the border of the Ammonites' land had strong towns, so they could not go beyond them." 
Numbers21:25 The Israelites took all the cities of the Amorites for themselves. Those cities included Heshbon and the towns around it. 
Numbers21:26 Heshbon was the city of Sihon, the king of the Amorites. He had fought against the king of Moab. He had taken from the king of Moab the land as far as the Arnon River." 
Numbers21:27 Because of that, the Amorite singers wrote this song: ‘Come to Heshbon and build it up again. Build the city of Sihon again." 
Numbers21:28 An army that was like a fire went out from Heshbon. It was like a flame that went out from Sihon's city. It destroyed Ar in Moab, and everything on the hills near the Arnon River." 
Numbers21:29 It will be very bad for you, people of Moab. King Sihon has destroyed you people who worship the god Chemosh. Your men have run away and your women have become prisoners." 
Numbers21:30 We have completely won against the Moabites! We have destroyed Heshbon, and as far as Dibon. We have killed them as far as Nophah, and all the way to Medeba.’" 
Numbers21:31 So the Israelites lived in the land of the Amorites. 
Numbers21:32 Moses sent men to find the best way to attack Jazer. Then the Israelites attacked Jazer and the towns around it. They caused the Amorites who lived there to run away. 
Numbers21:33 After that, the Israelites travelled towards Bashan. Og, the king of Bashan, marched out with his army to fight against them at Edrei." 
Numbers21:34 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Do not be afraid of Og. I will put him under your power, as well as his army and his land. Attack him in the same way that you attacked Sihon, the king of the Amorites who ruled in Heshbon.’" 
Numbers21:35 So the Israelites killed Og and his sons and all his army. Nobody was left alive. Their land became the Israelites' land. 
Numbers22:1 The Israelites travelled to Moab. They lived in tents at the side of the Jordan River. Jericho city was on the other side of the river. 
Numbers22:2 The king of Moab at that time was Balak, the son of Zippor. He saw what the Israelites had done to the Amorites." 
Numbers22:3 The people in Moab were afraid because there were so many Israelites. All the people in Moab became very afraid of the Israelites. 
Numbers22:4 So the Moabites said to the leaders of the Midianites, ‘This large group of people will destroy everything around us. They will be like a bull that eats all the grass in a field! Nothing will remain!’ So Balak, the king of Moab," 
Numbers22:5 sent men to get Balaam, the son of Beor. Balaam was living near the Euphrates River at Pethor, where his people lived. Balak told the men to say to Balaam, ‘A large group of people has come here from Egypt. They cover all the ground, everywhere. They have put up their tents near to our land." 
Numbers22:6 They are more powerful than I am. Come here and put a curse on them. Then maybe I will win against them and I can chase them away from our land. I know that you have the authority to bless people or to curse them.’ 
Numbers22:7 The leaders of Moab and Midian went to find Balaam. They took the money to pay for Balaam's work. When they found him, they told him what Balak had said." 
Numbers22:8 Balaam said to them, ‘Stay here tonight. I will talk to the Lord. Tomorrow, I will tell you his answer.’ So the Moabite officers stayed with Balaam." 
Numbers22:9 Then God came to Balaam. He asked Balaam, ‘Who are these men who are staying with you?’" 
Numbers22:10 Balaam said to God, ‘Zippor's son Balak, the king of Moab, has sent a message to me." 
Numbers22:11 He said, “A big group of people has come from Egypt and they cover all the ground. So please come and curse them for me. Then perhaps I will be able to fight them and send them away.” ’" 
Numbers22:12 God said to Balaam, ‘Do not agree to go with them. You must not curse those people who have come from Egypt. I have blessed them.’" 
Numbers22:13 The next morning, Balaam said to the officers from Moab, ‘Go back to Moab. The Lord has told me that I should not go with you.’" 
Numbers22:14 So the officers went back to Moab. They told King Balak that Balaam refused to come with them. 
Numbers22:15 Then Balak sent other officers to speak to Balaam. These officers were even more important than the first group, and there were more of them." 
Numbers22:16 They said to Balaam, ‘King Balak says, “Do not let anything stop you from coming to me." 
Numbers22:17 I will certainly make you rich and famous. I will do for you anything that you want. So please come and curse these people for me.” ’ 
Numbers22:18 Balaam said to them, ‘No, I will only do what the Lord tells me to do. I can do no more than that, and no less. Even if Balak gives me his house, all his gold and all his silver, I will only do what the Lord says." 
Numbers22:19 Stay here tonight, as the other men did. I will find out if the Lord has anything more to tell me.’" 
Numbers22:20 That night, God said to Balaam, ‘These men have asked you to go with them, so go with them. But only do what I tell you.’" 
Numbers22:21 In the morning, Balaam prepared his donkey to travel. He went with the officers to Moab." 
Numbers22:22 God was angry because Balaam went with the men. The angel of the stood on the road to stop Balaam. Balaam was riding on his donkey and his two servants were with him. 
Numbers22:23 The angel of the Lord was holding a sword. When the donkey saw the angel, it tried to leave the road and go into a field. Balaam hit the donkey to make it go back to the road." 
Numbers22:24 Then the angel of the Lord stood on the road between two vineyards. The path was narrow there, with a wall on each side." 
Numbers22:25 When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord, it pushed against the wall. Balaam's foot became fixed between the donkey and the wall. So Balaam hit the donkey again." 
Numbers22:26 Then the angel of the Lord moved on. He stood in a very narrow place where there was no space to pass on either side. 
Numbers22:27 When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord it lay down on the ground. Balaam was still sitting on the donkey and he became very angry. He hit the donkey with his stick. 
Numbers22:28 Then the Lord caused the donkey to speak. It said, ‘What have I done to hurt you? Why have you hit me three times like that?’" 
Numbers22:29 Balaam said to the donkey, ‘You have made me look silly. If I had a sword in my hand now, I would kill you.’" 
Numbers22:30 The donkey said to Balaam, ‘I am your own donkey that you ride every day. Do I usually do things like this to you?’ Balaam said, ‘No.’" 
Numbers22:31 Then the Lord let Balaam see the angel of the Lord. He could see that the angel stood there with a sword in his hand. Balaam lay down with his face down towards the ground. 
Numbers22:32 The angel of the Lord said to him, ‘You should not have hit your donkey like that! I have come here to stop you because you refuse to obey me." 
Numbers22:33 Three times the donkey saw me and turned back. If it had not done that, I would have killed you. But I would not have killed your donkey.’" 
Numbers22:34 Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord, ‘I have done a wrong thing. I did not realize that you were standing in the road to stop me. If you do not want me to continue, I will return to my home.’" 
Numbers22:35 But the angel of the Lord said to Balaam, ‘Go with the men. But you must say only the words that I will tell you.’ So Balaam went with Balak's officers." 
Numbers22:36 Balak heard the news that Balaam was coming. He went out to meet Balaam at the border of Moab's land. The town was beside the Arnon River. 
Numbers22:37 Balak said to Balaam, ‘You should have come the first time that I sent men to get you. Why did you not come? Did you think that I could not pay you with enough good things?’" 
Numbers22:38 Balaam said to Balak, ‘Look, I have come to you now! But I cannot choose to say anything. I can only say the words that God tells me to speak.’" 
Numbers22:39 Balaam went with Balak to Kiriath-Huzoth. 
Numbers22:40 When they arrived there, Balak killed bulls and sheep as a sacrifice. He sent some of the meat to Balaam and the officers who were with him." 
Numbers22:41 The next day, Balak took Balaam to Bamoth-Baal. From there, Balaam could see some of the big group of the Israelites." 
Numbers23:1 Balaam said to King Balak, ‘Build seven altars here for me. Prepare seven bulls and seven male sheep.’" 
Numbers23:2 Balak did this. Balaam and Balak then offered a bull and a male sheep on each altar as a sacrifice. 
Numbers23:3 Then Balaam said to Balak, ‘Stand next to your burnt offering. I will go to see if the Lord will come to meet me. If he shows something to me, I will tell you about it.’ Then Balaam went to the top of a hill where there were no trees." 
Numbers23:4 God met Balaam there. Balaam said to him, ‘I have made seven altars. On each altar, I have offered a bull and a male sheep.’" 
Numbers23:5 The Lord told Balaam the message that he should speak to Balak. 
Numbers23:6 So Balaam returned to Balak. Balak was still standing beside his burnt offering with all the Moabite officers. 
Numbers23:7 Then Balaam spoke his message from God. He said, ‘Balak, the king of Moab, has brought me from Syria. He brought me from the mountains in the east. He said “Come and curse Jacob's descendants for me. Say bad things against the Israelites!”" 
Numbers23:8 But I cannot curse these people, because God has not cursed them. I cannot speak against people that the Lord does not speak against." 
Numbers23:9 When I stand on the rocks, I can see the Israelites. I can watch them from the hills. They are people who are not the same as the other nations." 
Numbers23:10 Nobody can count Jacob's descendants. They are as many as the dust on the ground! It would be too difficult to count even a quarter of the Israelites. I would like to die in peace like those righteous people!’ 
Numbers23:11 Balak said to Balaam, ‘You have not done what I wanted you to do! I brought you here to curse my enemies. But instead, you have blessed them!’" 
Numbers23:12 Balaam answered, ‘I can only speak the message that the Lord has told me.’" 
Numbers23:13 Then Balak said to Balaam, ‘Please come with me to another place where you can see the Israelites. You will not see all of them from there. Then curse those people for me.’" 
Numbers23:14 Balak took Balaam to the field of Zophim. It was at the top of Mount Pisgah. Balak built seven altars there. He offered a bull and a male sheep on each altar as sacrifices. 
Numbers23:15 Balaam said to Balak, ‘Stay here beside your burnt offering. I will go to meet with the Lord.’" 
Numbers23:16 The Lord met Balaam and told him the message that he should speak to Balak. He told Balaam to go and tell it to Balak. 
Numbers23:17 So Balaam went back to Balak. Balak and the Moabite officers were standing beside the burnt offerings. Balak asked Balaam, ‘What did the Lord say?’" 
Numbers23:18 Balaam spoke his message from God. He said, ‘Balak, son of Zippor, be ready! Listen carefully to me." 
Numbers23:19 God is not human. He does not tell lies. He does not change his thoughts. If he promises to do something, it happens." 
Numbers23:20 He told me that I must bless the Israelites. He has blessed them, so I must do it too." 
Numbers23:21 The Lord their God is with them, to help them. They give him honour as their king. He has no plans to punish the Israelites. No trouble will come to Jacob's descendants." 
Numbers23:22 He brought them out of Egypt. He fights on their behalf, as strong as a wild bull." 
Numbers23:23 Nobody can curse the Israelites. No magic has any power to hurt them. Now people will say about them, “See the great things that God has done for them!”" 
Numbers23:24 The Israelites are like a strong lion. It eats the animals that it has killed. It does not rest until it has eaten as much as it wants. It also drinks their blood.’ 
Numbers23:25 Balak said to Balaam, ‘You do not have to curse the Israelites, but please do not bless them!’" 
Numbers23:26 Balaam replied, ‘I have already told you, “I must do everything that the Lord tells me.” ’" 
Numbers23:27 Balak said to Balaam, ‘Let me take you to a different place. Perhaps God will let you curse the Israelites from that place.’" 
Numbers23:28 So Balak took Balaam to the top of Mount Peor. From there they could look over the desert. 
Numbers23:29 Balaam said, ‘Build seven altars here. Prepare seven bulls and seven male sheep as sacrifices.’" 
Numbers23:30 So Balak offered a bull and a male sheep on each altar as sacrifices. 
Numbers24:1 Balaam realized that the Lord wanted to bless the Israelites. So he did not use magic again to find out what the Lord wanted him to do. Instead, he looked towards the desert." 
Numbers24:2 When he looked, he saw the Israelite people. They had put up their tents in groups for each tribe. Then the Spirit of God came on Balaam." 
Numbers24:3 He spoke this message: ‘This is God's message that he has given to Balaam, the son of Beor. It is the words of a man who understands what God is saying." 
Numbers24:4 It is the message of a man who hears God's words. I am a man who sees a vision that God Almighty has shown to him. I fall with my face towards the ground, but my eyes are open! I see this:" 
Numbers24:5 The tents where the Israelites live are beautiful! 
Numbers24:6 They are like green gardens in a valley beside a river. They are like tall aloe trees that the Lord has put there. They are like cedar trees that grow beside water. 
Numbers24:7 God will give them plenty of rain. Their descendants will always have plenty of water. Their king will be more powerful than Agag. Their kingdom will be famous. 
Numbers24:8 God brought them out of Egypt. God fights for them like a wild bull. He will destroy the nations who are their enemies. He will break their enemies' bones into pieces. He will shoot his arrows at them. 
Numbers24:9 The Israelites are like a lion that is lying down. It is ready to attack. When it is asleep, nobody is brave enough to wake it. Those who bless you, Israel, are truly blessed. And those who curse you are truly cursed.’" 
Numbers24:10 When Balaam said that, Balak became very angry with him. He hit his hands together and he said to Balaam, ‘I asked you to come here and to curse my enemies, the Israelites. But you have blessed them three times!" 
Numbers24:11 So go home now. I said that I would pay you very well. But the Lord has stopped me from giving you anything.’ 
Numbers24:12 Balaam said to Balak, ‘I already said this to the men that you sent to bring me." 
Numbers24:13 I told them, “I can only speak the message that the Lord tells me. I cannot do good things or bad things just because I want to do them. Even if Balak gives his palace full of silver and gold to me, I cannot do anything different. I have to obey the Lord 's command.”" 
Numbers24:14 So I will now return to my own people. But listen! Before I go, I will tell you what these Israelites will do to your people in the future.’" 
Numbers24:15 Then Balaam spoke this message from God. He said, ‘This is the message of Balaam, the son of Beor. It is the message of a man who sees clearly." 
Numbers24:16 It is the message of a man who hears God's words. The Most High God has shown me things that only he knows. I am a man who sees a vision that God Almighty has shown to him. I fall with my face towards the ground, but my eyes are open! I see this:" 
Numbers24:17 I see a king who will come from among the Israelites. He will not come now, but at a future time. He will appear as king, like a bright star. He will knock down the leaders of Moab. He will destroy the descendants of Sheth." 
Numbers24:18 He will win against his enemies in Edom and Seir. The Israelites will become stronger. 
Numbers24:19 A descendant of Jacob will become their ruler. He will destroy all the people who are still alive in the city.’ 
Numbers24:20 Then, in his vision, Balaam saw the Amalekites. He said, ‘Amalek was the most powerful of all the nations. God will destroy it now and for ever.’" 
Numbers24:21 Then Balaam saw the Kenites. He spoke this message: ‘The place where you live seems strong and safe, high up among the rocks." 
Numbers24:22 But Assyria's army will attack you. They will take you away as prisoners.’ 
Numbers24:23 Then Balaam spoke this message: ‘It is very bad for you! Nobody can live when God does these things! 
Numbers24:24 Ships will come from the coast of Cyprus. Their soldiers will attack Assyria and Eber. Then God will destroy them too.’ 
Numbers24:25 After he spoke those messages, Balaam returned to his home. King Balak also left that place." 
Numbers25:1 The Israelites were staying in Shittim. The men began to have sex with the women from Moab. 
Numbers25:2 The women from Moab took Israelite men to watch them as they offered sacrifices to the gods of Moab. The Israelite men joined with the Moabite women to eat at their feasts. They also worshipped the gods of Moab. 
Numbers25:3 The Israelites joined the Moabite women to worship their god, Baal of Peor. So the Lord became very angry with them." 
Numbers25:4 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Take hold of all the leaders of these people. Hang them up during the day so that they die and people see them. Do what I tell you, so that I will no longer be angry against the Israelites.’" 
Numbers25:5 Moses told the officers of the Israelites, ‘Each of you, kill the men who have worshipped the god, Baal of Peor.’" 
Numbers25:6 Moses and all the people were at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They were weeping. While they were there, an Israelite man brought a Midianite woman into his tent. Moses and all the people could see them." 
Numbers25:7 Phinehas, the son of Eleazar and grandson of Aaron the priest, saw what happened. So he stood up among all the people and he took a spear in his hand." 
Numbers25:8 He went into the man's tent. He pushed the spear through the body of the man and the Midianite woman. When he did that, the bad disease that was killing the Israelites stopped." 
Numbers25:9 But 24,000 people had already died as a result of the disease." 
Numbers25:10 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers25:11 ‘I am no longer angry with the Israelites. That is because of what Phinehas, the son of Eleazar and grandson of Aaron the priest, did. He did not want the people to worship any god except me. Because of him, I did not kill all the Israelites." 
Numbers25:12 So you must tell him that I have promised that my peace will be with him. 
Numbers25:13 That promise will be for him and for his descendants after him. They will always be priests. That is because he did not want the people to worship any god except me. He has made the Israelites clean from their sin.’ 
Numbers25:14 The name of the man that Phinehas killed with the Midianite woman was Zimri. He was the son of Salu. Salu was the leader of a clan from the tribe of Simeon. 
Numbers25:15 The name of the Midianite woman was Cozbi. She was the daughter of Zur, a leader of a Midianite clan." 
Numbers25:16 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers25:17 ‘Attack the Midianites and destroy them. 
Numbers25:18 They have deceived you and they have become your enemies. They took some of you to worship their god, Baal of Peor. They used Cozbi to make you do a wrong thing, and she was the daughter of a Midianite leader. Phinehas killed her on the day when the bad disease killed many Israelites at Mount Peor.’" 
Numbers26:1 When the bad disease stopped killing people, the Lord spoke to Moses and to Eleazar, the son of Aaron." 
Numbers26:2 He said, ‘Count all the Israelites, clan by clan. Count all the men who are 20 years old or older. Count only those men who can fight in the army.’"
