Numbers26:3 So Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke to the Israelites. Their camp was on the flat ground of Moab, beside the Jordan River. Jericho was on the other side of the river. They said," 
Numbers26:4 ‘Count all the men who are 20 years old or older. The Lord has told Moses that we must do that.’ These are the names of the Israelites who came out of Egypt: 
Numbers26:5 The tribe of Reuben, Jacob's firstborn son, had these clans: Hanoch's family, Pallu's family," 
Numbers26:6 Hezron's family and Carmi's family. 
Numbers26:7 There were 43,730 men in the clans of the tribe of Reuben." 
Numbers26:8 Pallu's descendant was Eliab. 
Numbers26:9 Eliab's descendants were Nemuel, Dathan and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram were the men who turned against Moses and Aaron. Korah was their leader and they all turned against the Lord." 
Numbers26:10 A hole opened in the ground. Korah, Dathan, Abiram and their families fell into the hole and they died. And fire killed 250 other men. So they became a sign to warn the other Israelites." 
Numbers26:11 But Korah's clan did not all die at that time. 
Numbers26:12 The tribe of Simeon had these clans: Nemuel's family, Jamin's family, Jakin's family," 
Numbers26:13 Zerah's family and Shaul's family. 
Numbers26:14 There were 22,200 men in the clans of the tribe of Simeon." 
Numbers26:15 The tribe of Gad had these clans: Zephon's family, Haggi's family, Shuni's family," 
Numbers26:16 Ozni's family, Eri's family," 
Numbers26:17 Arod's family and Areli's family. 
Numbers26:18 There were 40,500 men in the clans of the tribe of Gad." 
Numbers26:19 The sons of Judah were Er and Onan, but Er and Onan died in Canaan." 
Numbers26:20 The tribe of Judah had these clans: Shelah's family, Perez's family, Zerah's family," 
Numbers26:21 Hezron's family and Hamul's family. 
Numbers26:22 There were 76,500 men in the clans of the tribe of Judah." 
Numbers26:23 The tribe of Issachar had these clans: Tola's family, Puah's family," 
Numbers26:24 Jashub's family and Shimron's family. 
Numbers26:25 There were 64,300 men in the clans of the tribe of Issachar." 
Numbers26:26 The tribe of Zebulun had these clans: Sered's family, Elon's family and Jahleel's family." 
Numbers26:27 There were 60,500 men in the clans of the tribe of Zebulun." 
Numbers26:28 The descendants of Joseph became two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim." 
Numbers26:29 The tribe of Manasseh had these clans: Makir's family and Gilead's family. Makir was Gilead's father. 
Numbers26:30 These clans came from Gilead's descendants: Iezer's family, Helek's family," 
Numbers26:31 Asriel's family, Shechem's family," 
Numbers26:32 Shemida's family and Hepher's family. 
Numbers26:33 Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, had no sons, but only daughters. The names of Zelophehad's daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah." 
Numbers26:34 There were 52,700 men in the clans of the tribe of Manasseh." 
Numbers26:35 The tribe of Ephraim had these clans: Shuthelah's family, Becher's family, and Tahan's family." 
Numbers26:36 Eran's family was a clan from the descendants of Shuthelah. 
Numbers26:37 There were 32,500 men in the clans of the tribe of Ephraim. The clans of the tribes of Manasseh and Ephraim were all descendants of Joseph." 
Numbers26:38 The tribe of Benjamin had these clans: Bela's family, Ashbel's family, Ahiram's family," 
Numbers26:39 Shupham's family and Hupham's family. 
Numbers26:40 Ard's family and Naaman's family were clans from Bela's descendants. 
Numbers26:41 There were 45,600 men in the clans of the tribe of Benjamin." 
Numbers26:42 The tribe of Dan had this clan: Shuham's family. 
Numbers26:43 There were 64,400 men in Shuham's clan." 
Numbers26:44 The tribe of Asher had these clans: Imnah's family, Ishvi's family and Beriah's family." 
Numbers26:45 Heber's family and Malkiel's family were clans from Beriah's descendants. 
Numbers26:46 There was also a daughter of Asher who was called Serah. 
Numbers26:47 There were 53,400 men in the clans of the tribe of Asher." 
Numbers26:48 The tribe of Naphtali had these clans: Jahzeel's family, Guni's family," 
Numbers26:49 Jezer's family and Shillem's family. 
Numbers26:50 There were 45,400 men in the clans of the tribe of Naphtali." 
Numbers26:51 There were 601,730 men on the list of all the Israelites." 
Numbers26:52 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers26:53 ‘I will give part of the land to each tribe. You need to know the number of names that you have on your list for each tribe. 
Numbers26:54 The big tribes will receive a big piece of land. The small tribes will receive a smaller piece of land. 
Numbers26:55 You must use lots to decide the land that each tribe receives. 
Numbers26:56 The larger clans must have more land than the smaller clans.’ 
Numbers26:57 The Levites included these clans: Gershon's family, Kohath's family and Merari's family." 
Numbers26:58 It also included these clans: Libni's family, Hebron's family, Mahli's family, Mushi's family and Korah's family. Kohath was the father of Amram." 
Numbers26:59 The name of Amram's wife was Jochebed. She was a descendant of Levi, and she was born in Egypt. Amram and Jochebed were the parents of Moses and Aaron, and their sister Miriam." 
Numbers26:60 Aaron was the father of Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar." 
Numbers26:61 Nadab and Abihu died when they offered strange fire to the Lord. 
Numbers26:62 There were 23,000 male Levites who were more than one month old. Moses did not include them on the list of the other Israelite tribes. They did not receive any land as the other tribes did." 
Numbers26:63 Those are the men that Moses and Eleazar the priest counted. They counted them on the flat ground in Moab beside the Jordan River. Jericho was on the other side of the river. 
Numbers26:64 None of the men that Moses and Aaron had counted in the Sinai Desert were still alive. 
Numbers26:65 The Lord had said about those men, ‘They will certainly die in the desert.’ Among all those men, only Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun were still alive." 
Numbers26:65 The Lord had said about those men, ‘They will certainly die in the desert.’ Among all those men, only Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun were still alive." 
Numbers27:1 Then the daughters of Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, came to speak to Moses. Hepher was the son of Gilead, the son of Makir, the son of Manasseh, the son of Joseph. So Zelophehad belonged to the tribe of Manasseh. His daughters were called Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah." 
Numbers27:2 Moses, Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the people were standing at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Zelophehad's daughters said to them," 
Numbers27:3 ‘Our father died in the desert. But he was not in Korah's group of men who came together to speak against the Lord. He died in the desert because of his own sin, and he did not have any sons." 
Numbers27:4 We are his daughters, so please give us some land along with our father's male relatives. Then people will not forget our father's family name.’" 
Numbers27:5 Moses asked the Lord about this. 
Numbers27:6 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers27:7 ‘What the daughters of Zelophehad said is right. You must give them some of the land that their father's family will receive. Give them the land that their father would have received. 
Numbers27:8 Tell the Israelites this: “Sometimes a man only has daughters. When he dies, his daughters must receive what belongs to their family." 
Numbers27:9 If he does not have any sons or daughters, you must give his family's land to his brothers." 
Numbers27:10 If he does not have any brothers, you must give his land to his father's brothers." 
Numbers27:11 Sometimes the man's father may have no brothers. If that happens, you must give his land to another person in his family. This is a rule for all the people. The Lord gave this command to Moses.” ’" 
Numbers27:12 The Lord said to Moses, ‘Go up to the top of the Abiram mountains. From there, look at the land that I am giving to the Israelites." 
Numbers27:13 After that, you will die, just as your brother Aaron also died." 
Numbers27:14 In the Zin Desert, when the people turned against me, you did not obey my command. That happened when you hit the rock to get water at Meribah Kadesh. You did not show the people that I was holy, so now you will not go into the land of Canaan.’" 
Numbers27:15 Moses said to the Lord," 
Numbers27:16 ‘You are the Lord, the God of the spirits of all people. Please choose another man to be leader over all the people." 
Numbers27:17 He will lead them when they go out to fight, and he will bring them back safely. Then the Lord 's people will not be like sheep who have no shepherd.’" 
Numbers27:18 The Lord replied to Moses, ‘Choose Joshua, the son of Nun. My Spirit is in him to help him lead well. Put your hand on him to take your place as leader." 
Numbers27:18 The Lord replied to Moses, ‘Choose Joshua, the son of Nun. My Spirit is in him to help him lead well. Put your hand on him to take your place as leader." 
Numbers27:19 He must stand in front of Eleazar the priest and in front of all the Israelites. Let all the people see that he is the new leader that I have chosen. 
Numbers27:20 Tell the people that they must obey him, because he now has your authority." 
Numbers27:21 Eleazar the priest will use the Urim and Thummim to find out from me what Joshua should do. Then Joshua can tell the people when to go out to fight, and he can lead them safely back again. He will tell all the Israelite people what they must do.’" 
Numbers27:21 Eleazar the priest will use the Urim and Thummim to find out from me what Joshua should do. Then Joshua can tell the people when to go out to fight, and he can lead them safely back again. He will tell all the Israelite people what they must do.’" 
Numbers27:22 Moses did as the Lord told him. He brought Joshua to stand in front of Eleazar the priest and all the people. 
Numbers27:22 Moses did as the Lord told him. He brought Joshua to stand in front of Eleazar the priest and all the people. 
Numbers27:23 He put his hands on Joshua to show that he would be the new leader. Moses gave his authority to Joshua, as the Lord had told him to do." 
Numbers27:23 He put his hands on Joshua to show that he would be the new leader. Moses gave his authority to Joshua, as the Lord had told him to do." 
Numbers28:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers28:2 ‘Tell the Israelites that they must give their gifts to God at the proper time. The smell of the gifts when they burn will make him happy. 
Numbers28:3 You must say to them, “This is the offering by fire that you must offer to the Lord: It must be two male lambs that are one year old. They must not have anything wrong with them. That is the burnt offering for every day." 
Numbers28:4 You must give one lamb in the morning and one lamb in the evening. 
Numbers28:5 With the lambs, you must also offer to God one kilogram of flour that you have mixed with one litre of good olive oil." 
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering that God told you about. He told you about it on Mount Sinai. It is an offering by fire to the Lord. The smell as it burns makes the Lord happy. 
Numbers28:7 The drink offering with it will be one litre of wine for each lamb. In the holy place, you must pour out a drink offering of the best wine to the Lord." 
Numbers28:8 You must offer the other lamb when it begins to get dark. Offer it with a grain offering and a drink offering like the offerings in the morning. You must give it as an offering by fire. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will make the Lord happy. 
Numbers28:9 Then on the Sabbath day, you must offer two male lambs that are one year old. They must not have anything wrong with them. And you must offer 2 kilograms of good flour. Mix that with oil as a grain offering. And you must give the proper drink offering with it." 
Numbers28:10 This is the burnt offering for every Sabbath. But you must also give the regular burnt offering with its proper drink offering. 
Numbers28:11 Also, on the first day of every month, you must give a burnt offering to the Lord. It must be two young bulls, one male sheep and seven male lambs. The lambs must be one year old and they must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers28:12 With each bull and with the male sheep, you must give a grain offering. Mix together 3 kilograms of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with each bull. Mix together 2 kilograms of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with the male sheep." 
Numbers28:13 And with each lamb you must give a grain offering. Mix together 1 kilogram of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with each male lamb as a burnt offering to the Lord. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will make the Lord happy. 
Numbers28:14 There must also be drink offerings with these sacrifices. The offering must be 2 litres of wine for a bull. It must be 1½ litres for the male sheep and 1 litre for a lamb. That is the burnt offering of each month for every month of the year. 
Numbers28:15 Also, you must offer one male goat as a sin offering to the Lord. You must offer it with the proper drink offering. But you must still offer the regular burnt offering." 
Numbers28:16 On the 14th day of the first month, you must eat the Lord 's Passover meal." 
Numbers28:17 Day 15 is the start of a week when you must eat the flat bread. You must not put any yeast in the bread. You must do that for seven days. 
Numbers28:18 The first day will be a holy day and all the people must meet together. You must not work at your jobs on that day. 
Numbers28:19 And you must give an offering by fire, a burnt offering to the Lord. The offering must be two young bulls, one male sheep and seven male lambs that are one year old. But these animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers28:20 Offer the proper grain offering with each animal. Mix together 3 kilograms of the best flour and olive oil. Offer it to me with each bull. With the male sheep, offer 2 kilograms of the best flour with oil." 
Numbers28:21 With each of the seven lambs, you must offer one kilogram of flour that you have mixed with oil." 
Numbers28:22 You must also give one male goat as a sin offering. That is to make things right with God for you. 
Numbers28:23 But you must still give the regular burnt offering in the morning. 
Numbers28:24 You must offer those gifts every day for seven days. They are the food of an offering by fire. The smell of them as they burn will make the Lord happy. But you must still give the regular burnt offering with the proper drink offering. 
Numbers28:25 And the seventh day must be a holy day and all the people must meet together. You must not work at your jobs on that day. 
Numbers28:26 The day when you offer to the Lord the first part of the harvest must also be a holy day. That is also the day when you give to the Lord an offering of new grain. That is the Festival of Weeks. All the people must meet together. You must not work at your jobs on that day. 
Numbers28:27 And you must give a burnt offering to the Lord. The offering must be two young bulls, one male sheep and seven male lambs that are one year old. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will make the Lord happy." 
Numbers28:28 Mix together 3 kilograms of the best flour and olive oil. Offer that with each bull. With the male sheep, offer 2 kilograms of flour with oil." 
Numbers28:29 With each of the seven lambs, you must offer one kilogram of flour that you have mixed with oil." 
Numbers28:30 You must also offer one male goat to remove your sins. 
Numbers28:31 You must still give the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering. You must give them with the proper drink offerings. The animals must not have anything wrong with them. 
Numbers29:1 On the first day of the seventh month, all the people must come together for a holy meeting. You must not work at your jobs. It is a day for you to make music with your trumpets." 
Numbers29:2 And you must offer a burnt offering to the Lord. You must give one young bull and one male sheep. Also offer seven lambs that are one year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will cause the Lord to be happy. 
Numbers29:3 Offer the proper grain offering to me with each animal. Mix together three kilograms of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with the young bull. Offer two kilograms of flour with oil with the male sheep. 
Numbers29:4 Offer one kilogram of flour with each lamb. 
Numbers29:5 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering, so that I will forgive the people's sins." 
Numbers29:6 Offer these sacrifices as well as the burnt offering, grain offerings and drink offerings that you give each month and each day. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will cause the Lord to be happy." 
Numbers29:7 On the tenth day of the seventh month, you must come together for a holy meeting. During that day, you must make yourselves humble. And you must not do any work." 
Numbers29:8 You must offer a burnt offering to the Lord. The smell of the smoke will make the Lord happy. Offer one young bull, one male sheep and seven male lambs that are one year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:9 Offer to me the proper grain offering with each animal. Mix together three kilograms of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with the young bull. Offer two kilograms of flour with oil with the male sheep. 
Numbers29:10 Offer one kilogram of flour with oil with each lamb. 
Numbers29:11 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. Give these gifts as well as the burnt offering, the grain offerings and the drink offering s that you give each day and each month." 
Numbers29:12 On the 15th day of the seventh month, you must come together for a holy meeting. You must not work at your jobs. You must have a feast to the Lord for seven days." 
Numbers29:13 Give an offering by fire as a smell to make the Lord happy. Give a burnt offering of 13 young bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 lambs that are one year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:14 With each of the 13 bulls give a grain offering. Mix together three kilograms of the best flour with olive oil. Offer it to me with each bull. With each male sheep, offer two kilograms of flour with oil." 
Numbers29:15 Offer one kilogram of flour that you have mixed with oil with each male lamb. 
Numbers29:16 Also offer one male goat as a sin offering. Offer it with the regular burnt offering, with the proper grain offering and the drink offering." 
Numbers29:17 On the second day, give 12 young bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 lambs that are one year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:18 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:19 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and their drink offerings. 
Numbers29:20 On the third day, give 11 bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:21 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:22 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:23 On the fourth day give 10 bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:24 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:25 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:26 On the fifth day give 9 bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:27 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:28 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:29 On the sixth day give 8 bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:30 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:31 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:32 On the seventh day give 7 bulls, 2 male sheep and 14 male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:33 With the bulls, male sheep and lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:34 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:35 On the eighth day, all the people must come together for a holy meeting. You must not work at your jobs." 
Numbers29:36 Give an offering by fire as a smell to make the Lord happy. Give a burnt offering of one bull, one male sheep and seven male lambs that are a year old. The animals must not have anything wrong with them." 
Numbers29:37 With the bull, the male sheep and the lambs, give the proper grain offerings and drink offerings for each animal." 
Numbers29:38 Include one male goat as a sin offering. Sacrifice it with the regular burnt offering with the proper grain offering and the drink offering. 
Numbers29:39 You must offer those gifts to the Lord at the times of the feasts. You must do that even if you have given your usual burnt offering, grain offerings, drink offerings or friendship offerings. And you must do it even if you have given other offerings. You might have given those other offerings because you wanted to give them. Or you might have given them because you had promised to give them.” ’" 
Numbers29:40 Moses told the Israelites everything that the Lord had said to him. 
Numbers30:1 Moses said to the leaders of the Israelite tribes: ‘The Lord commands this: 
Numbers30:2 If a man promises to the Lord that he will do something, he must do everything that he has promised." 
Numbers30:3 A young woman who still lives in her father's house might make a promise to the Lord. 
Numbers30:4 If her father hears about her promise and he does not say anything, she must do everything that she has promised." 
Numbers30:5 But her father may refuse to let her do it. Then she does not have to do what she promised to do. The Lord will understand why she does not do it. 
Numbers30:6 But a young woman like that might make a promise to God and then marry a husband. She might have promised something when she had not thought carefully about it. 
Numbers30:7 After she marries, if her husband hears about it but he does not stop her, she must do what she promised." 
Numbers30:8 But her husband may say that he refuses to let her do it. He must say that on the day when he first hears about it. Then the woman does not have to do what she promised to do. The Lord will forgive her. 
Numbers30:9 If a woman no longer has a husband, she must do whatever she has promised to the Lord. Maybe she is a widow, or she has become separate from her husband." 
Numbers30:10 A woman who is living with her husband may make a promise to the Lord. 
Numbers30:11 If her husband hears about it but he does not say anything, she must do everything that she has promised." 
Numbers30:12 But her husband may say clearly that she must not do it. Then she must obey him. The Lord will understand why she does not do it. 
Numbers30:13 The husband may let his wife do what she promised to do, or he may refuse." 
Numbers30:14 If the husband does not say anything for many days about the promise, the woman must do what she promised." 
Numbers30:15 The husband may wait for many days after he hears about his wife's promise. If he then refuses to let her do what she promised, he will be guilty. God will punish him instead of his wife.’" 
Numbers30:16 Those are the rules that the Lord gave to Moses. They say what must happen when husbands, wives or young women who still live at home make a promise to the Lord." 
Numbers31:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers31:2 ‘You must punish the Midianites because they caused the Israelites to worship idols. After you have done that, you will die.’" 
Numbers31:3 Moses said to the people, ‘Choose some men to go and fight against the Midianites. Give them weapons to punish the Midianites as the Lord wants." 
Numbers31:4 Choose 1,000 men from each Israelite tribe to go and fight.’" 
Numbers31:5 They did that. There were 12,000 men ready with weapons to fight the battle." 
Numbers31:6 Moses sent those men out to fight. There were 1,000 men from each tribe. Phinehas, the son of Eleazar the priest, went with them. Phinehas took with him the holy things from the tabernacle. He also took the trumpets to make sounds for the battle." 
Numbers31:7 They attacked the Midianites as the Lord had told them. They killed all the men. 
Numbers31:8 That included the five kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur and Reba. They also killed Balaam, the son of Beor, with a sword." 
Numbers31:9 Israel's men took hold of the women and children of Midian. They took their animals and everything that was valuable. 
Numbers31:10 They burned all their towns and their camps. 
Numbers31:11 They took for themselves the people, the animals and everything that was valuable." 
Numbers31:12 They took all the prisoners and the things that they had taken to Moses, and to Eleazar the priest. They brought them to the camp of the Israelites. That was on the flat ground in Moab beside the Jordan River. Jericho was on the other side of the river." 
Numbers31:13 Moses, Eleazar and the leaders of the Israelites went to meet them outside the camp." 
Numbers31:14 Moses was angry with the leaders and the officers of the army who had come back from the battle against the Midianites. 
Numbers31:15 Moses said to them, ‘Why have you let all the women live?" 
Numbers31:16 You should not have done that. These women caused the Israelites to turn against the Lord. They did what Balaam said to them at Peor. As a result, a bad disease killed many of the Lord 's people." 
Numbers31:17 Because of that, kill all the boys, and kill every woman who has had sex with a man." 
Numbers31:18 But as for the girls who have not had sex with a man, you may keep them for yourselves.’" 
Numbers31:19 Moses said, ‘Any soldier who has killed someone, or who has touched a dead body is unclean. He must stay outside the camp for seven days. On the third day and on the seventh day, you must wash yourselves to become clean. You must do the same thing to your Midianite prisoners." 
Numbers31:20 You must also make these things clean: any clothes or anything made from leather, wool or wood.’" 
Numbers31:21 Eleazar the priest said to the soldiers, ‘This is the rule that the Lord told Moses:" 
Numbers31:22 You must put all the gold, the silver, the bronze, the iron and other metals in a fire." 
Numbers31:23 Put in the fire anything that the fire will not destroy. The fire will make those things clean. But you must also wash them with water. As for anything that the fire would destroy, wash them with water to make them clean." 
Numbers31:24 On the seventh day, you must wash your clothes. After that you will be clean again. Then you can come into the camp.’" 
Numbers31:25 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers31:26 ‘You and Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the family clans must count all the people and animals that you took in the battle." 
Numbers31:27 Give one half of all those things to the soldiers who went to fight the war against the Midianites. Give the other half to the rest of the people. 
Numbers31:28 From the half which belongs to the soldiers, keep one part out of every 500. Do that with the Midianite prisoners, the cows, the donkeys and the sheep. Those parts will be for the Lord." 
Numbers31:29 Give those to Eleazar the priest. He will offer them to the Lord. 
Numbers31:30 From the half that belongs to the people, keep one part out of every 50. Do that with the Midianite prisoners, the cows, the donkeys and the sheep. Those parts will be for the Levites who take care of the Lord 's special tent.’" 
Numbers31:31 Moses and Eleazar did as the Lord said. 
Numbers31:32 The soldiers had brought from the battle: 675,000 sheep," 
Numbers31:33 72,000 cows," 
Numbers31:34 61,000 donkeys" 
Numbers31:35 and 32,000 young women who had not had sex with a man." 
Numbers31:36 The soldiers' half included 337,500 sheep." 
Numbers31:37 So there were 675 sheep for the Lord. 
Numbers31:38 The soldiers' half also included 36,000 cows. So there were 72 cows for the Lord." 
Numbers31:39 The soldiers' half also included 30,500 donkeys. So there were 61 donkeys for the Lord." 
Numbers31:40 The soldiers' half also included 16,000 people. So there were 32 people for the Lord." 
Numbers31:41 Moses gave the part that was for the Lord to Eleazar, the priest. He would offer these to the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses." 
Numbers31:42 Moses made the half for the Israelite people separate from the half that was for the soldiers. 
Numbers31:43 The people's half included 337,500 sheep," 
Numbers31:44 36,000 cows," 
Numbers31:45 30,500 donkeys" 
Numbers31:46 and 16,000 people." 
Numbers31:47 From the Israelites' half, Moses took one part out of every 50. He gave those to the Levites who took care of the Lord 's special tent. That was what the Lord had commanded Moses to do." 
Numbers31:48 Then the leaders and the officers of the army went to Moses. 
Numbers31:49 They said to him: ‘We have counted the soldiers who went to fight against the Midianites. They have all come back safely. 
Numbers31:50 So we have brought a gift to offer to the Lord. We have brought gold rings, ear-rings, necklaces and other gold things that people wear. They are gifts to remove our sins so that the Lord will forgive us.’" 
Numbers31:51 Moses and Eleazar took all the gold things that the soldiers' officers and leaders brought to them. 
Numbers31:52 They weighed all the gold that was offered to the Lord. The weight was 16,750 shekels." 
Numbers31:53 The soldiers had also taken gold things from the enemy for themselves. 
Numbers31:54 Moses and Eleazar took the gold gifts from the soldiers' officers and leaders into the Tent of Meeting. It would help the Israelites to remember how the Lord had helped them. 
Numbers32:1 The people in the tribes of Reuben and Gad had very many animals. They saw that the land in Jazer and Gilead was good for their animals. 
Numbers32:2 They went to speak to Moses, Eleazar and the other leaders of the Israelites." 
Numbers32:3 They said, ‘The land at Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo and Beon" 
Numbers32:4 is good for animals. The Lord has given all this land to us Israelites. We have very many animals," 
Numbers32:5 so please let us have this land for our animals. Let us have this land to live on, instead of land on the other side of the Jordan River.’" 
Numbers32:6 Moses said to the men of Reuben and Gad, ‘The other Israelite tribes will go across the river to fight against the people who live there. It is not fair that you should stay here!" 
Numbers32:7 If you stay here, the rest of the Israelites may not want to go across the river. Do not stop them going into the land that the Lord has given to them!" 
Numbers32:8 Your fathers did the same thing when I sent them from Kadesh-Barnea to look at the land of Canaan. 
Numbers32:9 They went to Eshcol Valley and they saw the land. But they told the Israelites that it was not a good idea to go into Canaan. So the people did not go into the land that the Lord had given to them. 
Numbers32:10 As a result, the Lord was very angry with them." 
Numbers32:11 He promised, “None of the men that I brought out from Egypt will go into the land. It is the land that I promised to give to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. But they have not trusted me completely. So any man who is 20 years old or older will not go into this land." 
Numbers32:12 Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun are the only men who will go into the land. They are the only men who trusted me completely.”" 
Numbers32:12 Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun are the only men who will go into the land. They are the only men who trusted me completely.”" 
Numbers32:13 At that time, the Lord was angry with all the Israelites. He caused them to travel in the desert for 40 years. After that, all the men who had turned against him were dead.’" 
Numbers32:14 Moses continued to say, ‘Now you are turning against God, as your fathers did. You are as bad as them! You will cause the Lord to be even more angry with the Israelites." 
Numbers32:15 If you turn against him, he will leave them here in the desert. If all the Israelite people die, it will be because of you!’" 
Numbers32:16 The men of Reuben and Gad said to Moses, ‘Let us build strong houses here for our women and our children. We will also build places to keep our animals safe." 
Numbers32:17 Then we will be ready to join the other tribes and we will lead them to the battle. While we are fighting, our families will be safe here. The people who live in this land will not be able to hurt them." 
Numbers32:18 We will not return to our homes here until all the Israelite tribes receive their own part of the land. 
Numbers32:19 We will not receive any of the land on the other side of the Jordan River. Our part of the land will be here, on the east side of the river.’" 
Numbers32:20 Moses said to the men, ‘You must really do as you have said. You must take your weapons and go to fight for the Lord." 
Numbers32:21 Your men must go across the Jordan River with the other tribes. You must continue to fight until we have won against all our enemies. 
Numbers32:22 When the Lord has power over the whole land, then you can return here. You will have done everything that the Lord wants you to do. You will have helped the whole Israelite nation in the way that you should. The Lord will give to you this land on the east side of the Jordan River." 
Numbers32:23 But if you do not do what you have promised, that will be a sin against the Lord. And be sure that God will punish you for your sin." 
Numbers32:24 So build towns for your families, and build safe places for your animals. But then do everything that you have promised to do.’" 
Numbers32:25 The men in the tribes of Gad and Reuben said to Moses, ‘Yes sir, we will do what you have said." 
Numbers32:26 Our women, children and our animals will stay here in Gilead's towns." 
Numbers32:27 But all our men who are able to fight will go across the river. We will fight on behalf of the Lord, as you have said that we should do.’" 
Numbers32:28 Moses gave this message to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua the son of Nun and to the leaders of the family clans." 
Numbers32:28 Moses gave this message to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua the son of Nun and to the leaders of the family clans." 
Numbers32:29 He said to them, ‘The men in Reuben's tribe and in Gad's tribe must go across the Jordan River to fight with you on behalf of the Lord. If they do that, you must give them the land here in Gilead. When you have won power over the whole land, then this land will belong to them." 
Numbers32:30 If they refuse to fight together with the other tribes, they will have no land here. They must have part of the land in Canaan, like the other tribes.’" 
Numbers32:31 The men in the tribes of Reuben and Gad said, ‘We will do what the Lord has told us to do." 
Numbers32:32 We will take our weapons and we will go across the Jordan River to fight on behalf of the Lord in Canaan. But then we will live in our land here on this side of the river.’ 
Numbers32:33 So Moses gave them all the kingdom of Sihon, king of the Amorites and Og, the king of Bashan. He gave them the land with its towns and all the land round the towns. He gave it to the descendants of Gad and of Reuben and half of the tribe of Joseph's son Manasseh." 
Numbers32:34 The men in the tribe of Gad built up again Dibon, Ataroth and Aroer." 
Numbers32:35 They also built up again Atroth-Shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah," 
Numbers32:36 Beth-Nimrah and Beth-Haran. They built houses for their families and their animals. 
Numbers32:37 The men in the tribe of Reuben built up again Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim," 
Numbers32:38 Nebo, Baal-Meon and Sibmah. They gave new names to the cities that they built up again." 
Numbers32:39 The descendants of Makir, the son of Manasseh, attacked Gilead. They sent away the people who lived there." 
Numbers32:40 So Moses gave Gilead to the descendants of Makir who was the son of Manasseh. And they lived there. 
Numbers32:41 Jair, who was a descendant of Manasseh, attacked some villages. He got power over them and he called them Havvoth-Jair." 
Numbers32:42 Nobah attacked Kenath and the villages round it. He called it Nobah, like his own name." 
Numbers33:1 This is the way that the Israelites travelled, after Moses and Aaron led them out of Egypt. They travelled in their family groups." 
Numbers33:2 The Lord told Moses that he should write about the journey. These are the places where they made their camps on the way: 
Numbers33:3 They left Rameses in Egypt on the 15th day of the first month. That was the day after the Passover meal. The Israelites marched out bravely, while all the Egyptian people watched them." 
Numbers33:4 As they left, the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn sons, because the Lord had killed them. The Lord had shown his power over the Egyptians' gods." 
Numbers33:5 They left Rameses and they travelled to Succoth. They made their camp there. 
Numbers33:6 They travelled from Succoth to Etham. They made their camp there, on the edge of the desert." 
Numbers33:7 They travelled from Etham and they turned round to go to Pi-Hahiroth, which is east of Baal-Zephon. They made their camp near Migdol." 
Numbers33:8 They travelled from Pi-Hahiroth. They went through the middle of the sea and then went into the desert. They travelled for three days and then they made their camp at Marah, in the Etham Desert." 
Numbers33:9 They travelled from Marah to Elim. At Elim, there were 12 wells of water and 70 palm trees. They made their camp there." 
Numbers33:10 They travelled from Elim and they made their camp at the side of the Red Sea. 
Numbers33:11 They travelled from the Red Sea. And they made their camp in the Sin Desert. 
Numbers33:12 They travelled from the Sin Desert and they made their camp at Dophkah. 
Numbers33:13 They travelled from Dophkah and they made their camp at Alush. 
Numbers33:14 They travelled from Alush and they made their camp at Rephidim. There was no water there for the people to drink. 
Numbers33:15 They travelled from Rephidim and they made their camp in the Sinai Desert. 
Numbers33:16 They travelled from the Sinai Desert and they made their camp at Kibroth-Hattaavah. 
Numbers33:17 They travelled from Kibroth-Hattaavah and they made their camp at Hazeroth. 
Numbers33:18 They travelled from Hazeroth and they made their camp at Rithmah. 
Numbers33:19 They travelled from Rithmah and they made their camp at Rimmon-Perez. 
Numbers33:20 They travelled from Rimmon-Perez and they made their camp at Libnah. 
Numbers33:21 They travelled from Libnah and they made their camp at Rissah. 
Numbers33:22 They travelled from Rissah and they made their camp at Kehelathah. 
Numbers33:23 They travelled from Kehelathah and they made their camp at Mount Shepher. 
Numbers33:24 They travelled from Mount Shepher and they made their camp at Haradah. 
Numbers33:25 They travelled from Haradah and they made their camp at Makheloth. 
Numbers33:26 They travelled from Makheloth and they made their camp at Tahath. 
Numbers33:27 They travelled from Tahath and they made their camp at Terah. 
Numbers33:28 They travelled from Terah and they made their camp at Mithcah. 
Numbers33:29 They travelled from Mithcah and they made their camp at Hashmonah. 
Numbers33:30 They travelled from Hashmonah and they made their camp at Moseroth. 
Numbers33:31 They travelled from Moseroth and they made their camp at Bene-Jaakan. 
Numbers33:32 They travelled from Bene-Jaakan and they made their camp at Hor-Haggidgad. 
Numbers33:33 They travelled from Hor-Haggidgad and they made their camp at Jotbathah. 
Numbers33:34 They travelled from Jotbathah and they made their camp at Abronah. 
Numbers33:35 They travelled from Abronah and they made their camp at Ezion-Geber. 
Numbers33:36 They travelled from Ezion-Geber and they made their camp at Kadesh in the Zin Desert. 
Numbers33:37 They travelled from Kadesh and they made their camp at Mount Hor. It is on the border of Edom land. 
Numbers33:38 The Lord told Aaron that he must go up Mount Hor. Aaron went up and he died there. That was in the 40th year after the Israelites had left Egypt. He died on the first day of the fifth month. 
Numbers33:39 Aaron was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor. 
Numbers33:40 The king of Arad lived in the south part of the land of Canaan. People told him that the Israelites were coming. 
Numbers33:41 The Israelites travelled from Mount Hor and they made their camp at Zalmonah. 
Numbers33:42 They travelled from Zalmonah and they made their camp at Punon. 
Numbers33:43 They travelled from Punon and they made their camp at Oboth. 
Numbers33:44 They travelled from Oboth and they made their camp at Iye-Abarim, on the border of Moab land." 
Numbers33:45 They travelled from Iye-Abarim and they made their camp at Dibon-Gad. 
Numbers33:46 They travelled from Dibon-Gad and they made their camp at Almon-Diblathaim. 
Numbers33:47 They travelled from Almon-Diblathaim and they made their camp in the mountains of Abarim, near Nebo." 
Numbers33:48 They travelled from the mountains of Abarim and they made their camp in Moab beside the Jordan River. They were on the other side of the river from Jericho city. 
Numbers33:49 They made their camp on the flat ground beside the river. Their tents covered the ground from Beth-Jeshimoth to Abel-Shittim. 
Numbers33:50 Then the Lord spoke to Moses there in Moab beside the Jordan River. Jericho was on the other side of the river. The Lord said," 
Numbers33:51 ‘Say to the Israelites, “You will go across the Jordan River and you will go into the land of Canaan." 
Numbers33:52 Then you must chase out all the people who live in that land. You must destroy all their idols that are made from wood or stone. Also destroy the altars where they worship their false gods. 
Numbers33:53 I have given this land to you to live in. So you must win power over the people who live there now. 
Numbers33:54 You must use lots to choose how to give part of the land to each family. You must give more land to the larger clans and you must give less land to the smaller clans. The lots will decide which piece of land belongs to each family. Each of your ancestors' tribes will get its own part of the land. 
Numbers33:55 If any Canaanites remain in the land, they will cause the Israelites to suffer. So you must chase them all away." 
Numbers33:56 If you do not do that, I will punish you in the way that I wanted to punish them.” ’" 
Numbers34:1 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers34:2 ‘Tell the Israelites this: “These are the borders of Canaan, the land that will be yours to live in." 
Numbers34:3 The south edge will start at the Zin Desert and it will go along the border of Edom's land. It will continue to the east, and it will reach the south end of the Salt Sea." 
Numbers34:4 It will go south of the place called Scorpion Hill, and it will continue to Zin. Then it will go south of Kadesh-Barnea. It will go to Hazar-Addar and then across to Azmon." 
Numbers34:5 There the border will turn and it will go along the Stream of Egypt. It will continue as far as the Mediterranean Sea. 
Numbers34:6 The west border of your land will be the coast of the Mediterranean Sea. 
Numbers34:7 This will be the north border: From the Mediterranean Sea draw a line to Mount Hor. 
Numbers34:8 From Mount Hor the border will go to Lebo-Hamath. Then it will go to Zedad. 
Numbers34:9 It will continue to Ziphron and it will finish at Hazar-Enan. 
Numbers34:10 This will be the east border: Draw a line from Hazar-Enan to Shepham. 
Numbers34:11 Then the border will go from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain. It will go along the east shore of Lake Galilee." 
Numbers34:12 Then it will go along the Jordan River and it will finish at the Salt Sea. That will be your land, with its borders.” ’" 
Numbers34:13 Moses said to the Israelites, ‘That is the land for the Israelites to live in. You must use lots to give part of the land to each tribe. The Lord has told you to give it to the nine and a half tribes." 
Numbers34:14 That is because the tribes of Reuben and Gad, and half of Manasseh's tribe have their land already." 
Numbers34:15 Their land is on the east side of the Jordan River. It is on the other side of the river from Jericho city.’ 
Numbers34:16 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers34:17 ‘Joshua, the son of Nun, and Eleazar the priest will decide which parts of the land will belong to each family." 
Numbers34:17 ‘Joshua, the son of Nun, and Eleazar the priest will decide which parts of the land will belong to each family." 
Numbers34:18 One leader from each tribe must help them. 
Numbers34:19 These are the names of those leaders: Caleb the son of Jephunneh from the tribe of Judah. 
Numbers34:20 Shemuel the son of Ammihud from the tribe of Simeon. 
Numbers34:21 Elidad the son of Kislon from the tribe of Benjamin. 
Numbers34:22 Bukki the son of Jogli from the tribe of Dan. 
Numbers34:23 These will be the two leaders from the descendants of Joseph: Hanniel the son of Ephod from the tribe of Manasseh. 
Numbers34:24 Kemuel the son of Shiphtan from the tribe of Ephraim. 
Numbers34:25 Then there will be Elizaphan the son of Parnach from the tribe of Zebulun. 
Numbers34:26 Paltiel the son of Azzan from the tribe of Issachar. 
Numbers34:27 Ahihud the son of Shelomi from the tribe of Asher. 
Numbers34:28 Pedahel the son of Ammihud from the tribe of Naphtali.’ 
Numbers34:29 Those were the men that the Lord chose to decide about the land for the Israelite families. 
Numbers35:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in Moab, beside the Jordan River. Jericho was on the other side of the river." 
Numbers35:2 The Lord said, ‘Tell the people this. They must give some of their land to the Levites. Then the Levites can build towns to live in. They will also have the land round the towns for their animals." 
Numbers35:3 The Levites must have towns to live in and land for their cows and all their animals. 
Numbers35:4 The fields that you give to them for their animals must reach 500 metres from the walls of each town. 
Numbers35:5 You must also measure 1,000 metres outside the town on each side, the east side, the south side, the west side and the north side. So the town will be in the centre. Those fields of grass will belong to them for each of their towns." 
Numbers35:6 Six of the Levites towns must be safe places. If anyone kills another person, he can run there to be safe. The Levites must also have another 42 towns." 
Numbers35:7 In that way, the Levites will have 48 towns and land for their animals." 
Numbers35:8 Each of the Israelite tribes must give to the Levites towns from their own land. The larger tribes will give more towns. The smaller tribes will give fewer towns. That is because the larger tribes have received more land than the smaller tribes.’ 
Numbers35:9 The Lord said to Moses," 
Numbers35:10 ‘Tell the Israelites to do this when they go across the Jordan River and they go into Canaan. 
Numbers35:11 They must choose some towns to be safe places. If anyone kills another person but he did not mean to, he can run there to be safe." 
Numbers35:12 Those towns will be places where the killer can be safe from the dead person's relative. The killer will not die before the people in the town have judged him. 
Numbers35:13 You must choose six towns to be safe places. 
Numbers35:14 Three towns must be in Canaan and three towns must be on the other side of the Jordan River. 
Numbers35:15 Those six towns will be safe places for Israelites and for foreign people who live among them. Anyone who has killed another person may run to one of these towns and be safe there. But they will only be safe if they did not mean to kill the other person. 
Numbers35:16 Murderers are different. You must kill a murderer. Maybe someone hits another person with a heavy iron tool. If the person dies, the murderer must also die." 
Numbers35:17 Someone might hit another person with a big stone. If the person dies, the man is a murderer. He must die." 
Numbers35:18 A man might hit a person with a dangerous weapon made from wood. If the person dies, the man is a murderer. He must die." 
Numbers35:19 The dead person's relative himself must kill the murderer when he meets him. 
Numbers35:20 These are other examples of murder: A man might push another person hard because he hates him. A man might throw something at another person to hurt him. 
Numbers35:21 Or a man might hit someone with his hand because he hates him. If the person dies as a result, you must kill the murderer. The dead person's relative must kill the murderer when he meets him." 
Numbers35:22 But when a man kills another person, it might be a mistake. For example, he might push someone very hard, or he might throw something at another person. If he did not hate the other person, then it might be a mistake." 
Numbers35:23 Or he might drop a heavy stone on someone when he had not seen them. If he did not hate the other person, perhaps he did not mean to hurt him." 
Numbers35:24 If that happens, the people must judge who is right. They must choose between the killer and the dead person's relative. They must use those rules to decide if the man is a murderer." 
Numbers35:25 If the man is a killer but not a murderer, the people must keep him safe from the dead person's relative. They must send him back to the safe city that he ran to. He will live there safely until the death of the leader of God's priests." 
Numbers35:26 But one day, the killer might go outside the border of the safe city that he ran to." 
Numbers35:27 If he does that, the dead person's relative might find him outside the safe city. Then, if the dead person's relative kills him, the relative will not be guilty of murder." 
Numbers35:28 That is because the killer should have stayed in his safe city until the death of the leader of the priests. But after the leader of the priests dies, the killer may return to his own home." 
Numbers35:29 Wherever you and your descendants live, these are the rules that you must obey for all future time." 
Numbers35:30 Before you kill a murderer, people must agree that they saw him kill someone. But if only one person saw what happened, that is not enough for you to kill him." 
Numbers35:31 You must not accept money to pay for the life of a murderer. If he is guilty of murder, you must kill him." 
Numbers35:32 Also, you must not accept money from a killer who has run to a safe city. You must not let him return to his own home until the leader of the priests has died." 
Numbers35:33 Murder causes the land where you live to become unclean. You have to make it clean again. You must kill anyone who murders another person. That is the only way to make the land clean again. 
Numbers35:34 You must not make the land where you live become unclean. It is the land where I also live. That is because I, the Lord, live among the Israelites.’" 
Numbers36:1 The leaders of the families in Gilead's clan went to Moses and to the other leaders of the Israelites' clans. (Gilead was the descendant of Makir, who was the descendant of Manasseh. Manasseh was Joseph's son.)" 
Numbers36:2 They said, ‘The Lord told you, sir, that you should decide which parts of the land to give to each Israelite family. You used special stones to do that. The Lord told you to give Zelophehad's land to his daughters. Zelophehad was in our clan." 
Numbers36:3 But if his daughters marry, the land will then belong to their husbands. If they marry men from other tribes, the land will no longer belong to us, the tribe of Manasseh." 
Numbers36:4 Even when the Jubilee year comes, the land will not return to our tribe. It will belong to the tribes of the men that Zelophehad's daughters have married.’" 
Numbers36:5 The Lord gave Moses his answer. So Moses gave this command to the Israelites: ‘What Joseph's descendants are saying is right. 
Numbers36:6 This is what the Lord has commanded about the daughters of Zelophehad: Let them marry men that they want to marry. But they must marry men from the clans of their father's tribe. 
Numbers36:7 If they do that, each tribe will keep the land that has been given to their ancestors. None of their land will go to another tribe." 
Numbers36:8 Any young woman who receives land when her father dies must marry a man from her father's tribe. All the Israelite tribes must obey that rule. Then every Israelite person will keep the land that belonged to his ancestors. 
Numbers36:9 No land that belongs to any tribe will become the land of another tribe. So each tribe will keep the land that their ancestors had.’ 
Numbers36:10 The daughters of Zelophehad did what the Lord had commanded Moses. 
Numbers36:11 Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, married sons of their father's brothers." 
Numbers36:12 They married men from the clans of the descendants of Joseph's son, Manasseh. Because of that, their land remained with the tribe of their father's clan." 
Numbers36:13 Those are the rules that the Lord gave to the Israelites at that time. The Lord told Moses what he should say to the people. That happened when they were beside the Jordan River in Moab, on the flat ground on the other side of the river from Jericho city." 
Deuteronomy1:1 These are the words that Moses spoke to all the Israelites. They were together in the desert on the east side of the Jordan River. It was in the Jordan Valley near Suph, the towns called Paran, Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth and Dizahab." 
Deuteronomy1:2 It is a journey of 11 days from Sinai to Kadesh-Barnea, along the road past Mount Seir." 
Deuteronomy1:3 Moses spoke to the Israelites on the first day of the 11th month in the 40th year of their journey. He told the Israelites the message that the Lord had commanded him to speak. 
Deuteronomy1:4 This happened after Moses and the Israelites had won against Sihon, king of the Amorites. Sihon ruled in Heshbon. They had also won against Og, the king of Bashan, who ruled in Ashtaroth. The Israelites fought against him at Edrei." 
Deuteronomy1:5 Moses began to speak the Lord 's message on the east side of the Jordan River, in Moab's country. This is what he said:" 
Deuteronomy1:6 The Lord our God said to us at Sinai, ‘You have stayed for a long time near this mountain." 
Deuteronomy1:7 Now you must continue your journey. Go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all the regions near there. Go to the Jordan Valley and the mountains and the low hills in the west. Go to the Negev in the south, and to the Mediterranean coast. Go to the land of Canaan and to Lebanon, even as far as the great Euphrates River." 
Deuteronomy1:8 Look! I have given you all this land. Go there and take it for yourselves. I, the Lord, promised to give this land to your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, and to their descendants.’" 
Deuteronomy1:9 At that time I, Moses, said this to you: ‘I cannot lead you and help you by myself. It is too difficult for me to do alone." 
Deuteronomy1:10 The Lord your God has caused you to become very many people. You are as many as the stars in the sky! 
Deuteronomy1:11 I pray that the Lord, your ancestors' God, will cause you to become a thousand times as many people. That is how he has promised to bless you." 
Deuteronomy1:12 But I cannot help you with your troubles and arguments all by myself. 
Deuteronomy1:13 You must choose some wise men who understand what is right. Choose people that you know, from each of your tribes. Then I will give them authority as your leaders.’" 
Deuteronomy1:14 You answered me, ‘That is a good idea!’" 
Deuteronomy1:15 So I chose men that you knew were wise to be the leaders in your tribes. I gave them authority to rule over you. Some were leaders of groups of 1,000 men. Some led groups of 100 men, or 50 men, or ten men. I also chose other officers for each tribe." 
Deuteronomy1:16 At that time, I said to your leaders, ‘Listen to the arguments between the Israelites that you lead. Decide what is right. Do that when the argument is between two Israelites. Do the same thing when the argument is between an Israelite and a foreign person who lives among you." 
Deuteronomy1:17 When you judge them, do not make any difference between important people and poor people. Listen to all of them in a careful way. Do not be afraid of anyone. God is the one who decides what is right. If it is too difficult for you to decide who is right, bring it to me. I will listen and then I will decide what is right.’" 
Deuteronomy1:18 So, at that time, I told you about everything that you should do." 
Deuteronomy1:19 Then we left Sinai, as the Lord our God had commanded us to do. We travelled through the desert towards the hill country of the Amorites. You saw that the desert was very big and it made you very afraid. Finally, we arrived at Kadesh-Barnea." 
Deuteronomy1:20 Then I said to you, ‘You have reached the hill country of the Amorites. The Lord our God is giving this land to us." 
Deuteronomy1:21 Look at the land! It is now in front of you! The Lord your God has given it to you. Go in and take it for yourselves. The Lord, your ancestors' God, has told you to do this. So do not be afraid. Be brave!’" 
Deuteronomy1:22 Then all of you came to me and you said, ‘Let us send some men into the land first, before we all go in. They can explore the land. Then they can bring a report to us. They can tell us the best way for us to go in there. They can tell us about the towns that we will come to.’" 
Deuteronomy1:23 I thought that this was a good idea. I chose 12 men, one from each of your tribes." 
Deuteronomy1:24 They left and went up into the hill country. They came to Eshcol Valley and they explored it. 
Deuteronomy1:25 They picked some of the fruit that they found there and they brought it back to us. They told us, ‘The land that the Lord our God is giving to us is a very good land.’" 
Deuteronomy1:26 But you refused to go into the land. You turned against the Lord your God. 
Deuteronomy1:27 You complained to each other in your tents. You said, ‘The Lord hates us. That is why he brought us out of Egypt. He wanted to give us to the Amorites, so that they would kill us." 
Deuteronomy1:28 We do not know what will happen to us now. Our brothers who explored the land have made us afraid. They say, “Those people in Canaan are taller and stronger than we are. Their cities are big and strong. They have walls around them that reach up to the sky. We even saw some men there who are descendants of Anak.” ’" 
Deuteronomy1:29 Then I said to you, ‘Do not be so frightened of those people." 
Deuteronomy1:30 The Lord your God will go in front of you. He will fight to help you, as he fought for you in Egypt. You saw him do it." 
Deuteronomy1:31 He was also with you in the desert. He helped you when you were weak, as a father carries his son. You saw how he helped you all the way on your journey. He brought you safely here to this place.’" 
Deuteronomy1:32 But you did not trust the Lord your God to help you. 
Deuteronomy1:33 He was the one who went in front of you on your journey. He was there in a fire during the night and in a cloud during the day. He found good places for you to put up your tents. He showed you the way that you should go. 
Deuteronomy1:34 When the Lord heard that you were complaining, he was angry. He made this serious promise:" 
Deuteronomy1:35 ‘You people who are now alive are wicked. None of you will see the good land that I promised to give to your ancestors. 
Deuteronomy1:36 Only Jephunneh's son Caleb will see it. He has obeyed me faithfully. So I will give to him and to his descendants the land that he walked on.’ 
Deuteronomy1:37 As for me, the Lord became angry with me also, because of you. He said to me, ‘You also will not go into the land." 
Deuteronomy1:38 But your servant Joshua, the son of Nun, will go in there. You must tell him to be brave. He will lead the Israelites into the land. It will become their land." 
Deuteronomy1:38 But your servant Joshua, the son of Nun, will go in there. You must tell him to be brave. He will lead the Israelites into the land. It will become their land." 
Deuteronomy1:39 You thought that enemies would catch your small children. But they are the people who will go into the land. Your children who are still too young to know the difference between good and bad will also go in there. I will give the land to them and it will become their land. 
Deuteronomy1:40 But now you must all turn back towards the desert. Travel along the road to the Red Sea.’ 
Deuteronomy1:41 Then you answered me. You said, ‘Yes, we have done wrong things against the Lord. We will now go and fight those people. We will do what the Lord our God has told us to do.’ So then each one of you picked up his weapons. You thought that it would be easy to go up into the hill country." 
Deuteronomy1:42 But the Lord told me, ‘Do not let them go up there to fight. I will not be with you to help you. Your enemies will win against you.’" 
Deuteronomy1:43 So I told you what the Lord had said, but you would not agree. Instead, you turned against the Lord. You were so proud that you marched up into the hill country." 
Deuteronomy1:44 The Amorite people who lived in those hills came out to attack you. You ran away from them, as if they were a crowd of bees. They attacked you as you ran down into the country of Edom, all the way to Hormah." 
Deuteronomy1:45 Then you came back to Kadesh-Barnea. You wept to show the Lord that you were very sorry. But he would not listen to your prayers. 
Deuteronomy1:46 So you stayed in Kadesh for a long time, until it was time to leave." 
Deuteronomy2:1 Then we turned back to travel towards the desert. We went along the road to the Red Sea, as the Lord had told me. For many days, we walked around the region of Mount Seir." 
Deuteronomy2:2 Then the Lord said to me," 
Deuteronomy2:3 ‘You have walked round this mountain for a long time. Now turn to the north. 
Deuteronomy2:4 Give this message to the Israelites: “You will soon go through the country that belongs to Esau's descendants. They have the same ancestors that you have. They live in Seir. They will be afraid of you, so be very careful." 
Deuteronomy2:5 Do not attack them at all. I will not give any of their land to you, not even enough ground to put your foot on. I have given the hills around Mount Seir to Esau, for his descendants to live there." 
Deuteronomy2:6 When you eat their food and you drink their water, you must pay them for it." 
Deuteronomy2:7 I, the Lord your God, have blessed you in everything that you have done. I have taken care of you as you travelled through this great desert. I have been with you for 40 years. You have received everything that you needed.” ’" 
Deuteronomy2:8 So we went on. We passed the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. They have the same ancestors that we have. We left the road through the Jordan Valley that goes from Elath and Ezion-Geber. Instead, we travelled on the road through the Moab Desert." 
Deuteronomy2:9 Then the Lord said to me, ‘Do not make the people of Moab angry. Do not start a fight against them. I will not give any part of their land to you. I have given Ar to Lot's descendants to be their city.’" 
Deuteronomy2:10 (Some people called the Emites once lived there. There were many of them and they were as strong and tall as the Anakites. 
Deuteronomy2:11 Some people called the Emites and the Anakites, ‘Rephaites’. But the people from Moab called them ‘Emites’." 
Deuteronomy2:12 Before this time, the Horites lived in Seir, but the descendants of Esau chased them away. They destroyed the Horites and they lived in their country. The Israelites did the same thing to the people who had lived in the land that the Lord gave to them.)" 
Deuteronomy2:13 Then the Lord said, ‘Get up now and go across the Zered Valley.’ So we went across the stream." 
Deuteronomy2:14 It had been 38 years since we left Kadesh-Barnea. By the time we went across the Zered Valley, all the men who had been old enough to fight had now died. The Lord had said that this would happen." 
Deuteronomy2:15 The Lord himself removed them so that they all died, one by one." 
Deuteronomy2:16 Finally, the last one of these men died and no longer lived among the people." 
Deuteronomy2:17 Then the Lord said to me," 
Deuteronomy2:18 ‘Today you will go across the border of Moab at Ar. 
Deuteronomy2:19 You will go into the land of the Ammonites. Do not make them angry so that they fight against you. I will not give any of their land to you. I have already given it to Lot's descendants so that they can live there.’ 
Deuteronomy2:20 (People also call this region the land of the Rephaites, who had once lived there. The Ammonites called them Zamzummites." 
Deuteronomy2:21 There were many Zamzummites and they were as tall and strong as the Anakites. The Lord destroyed the Rephaites when the Ammonites moved in. Then the Ammonites lived there instead of the Rephaites. 
Deuteronomy2:22 The Lord had done the same thing for Esau's descendants who lived in Seir. The Lord destroyed the Horites when Esau's descendants moved in and chased them out. Esau's descendants still live in their country today. 
Deuteronomy2:23 The same thing happened to the Avvites. They had lived in villages as far west as Gaza. But Philistines came from Crete island and they killed the Avvites. The Philistines lived there instead.) 
Deuteronomy2:24 Then God said, ‘Now go across Arnon Valley. Look, I have given you the land of King Sihon, the Amorite. He is the king of Heshbon. You must go and fight against him. Attack him and take his land for yourselves." 
Deuteronomy2:25 Today I will begin to make all the people in the world afraid of you. They will hear reports about you. That will cause them to be very frightened. They will shake with fear!’ 
Deuteronomy2:26 When we were in Kedemoth Desert, I sent a message to Sihon, king of Heshbon. We wanted to agree that we would not fight against each other." 
Deuteronomy2:27 I said to him, ‘Please let us go through your country. We will only travel along the road. We will not turn away from the road, to the right side or to the left side." 
Deuteronomy2:28 Please sell us food to eat and water to drink. We will pay you with silver coins. We only want to walk through your country. 
Deuteronomy2:29 The descendants of Esau who live in Seir let us do this. So did the descendants of Moab who live in Ar. We will go through your country and then we will go across the Jordan River. We will go into the land that the Lord our God is giving to us.’ 
Deuteronomy2:30 But Sihon, the king of Heshbon, refused to let us travel through his country. He was too proud to agree with us. The Lord our God had caused him to be like that. He wanted to put King Sihon under your power and give his land to you. You still live in his land today." 
Deuteronomy2:31 The Lord said to me, ‘Look! I have begun to give King Sihon and his country to you. Now you must begin to fight against him and take his land for yourselves.’" 
Deuteronomy2:32 Then Sihon and all his army came out to fight a battle against us at Jahaz. 
Deuteronomy2:33 The Lord our God put him under our power. We won the battle against him. We killed King Sihon and his sons and all his soldiers. 
Deuteronomy2:34 At that time we took all his towns and we gave everything as a gift to the Lord. We killed the men, women and children. We left no one alive." 
Deuteronomy2:35 But we kept the farm animals and other valuable things in the cities for ourselves. 
Deuteronomy2:36 The Lord our God gave us all their towns. That included Aroer, on the edge of the Arnon Valley, the city in the valley and other towns all the way to Gilead. No town was too strong for us to take." 
Deuteronomy2:37 But we did not go into the Ammonites' country. We did not go into the Jabbok Valley. We did not go near the towns in the hill country. The Lord our God had commanded us not to attack those places. 
Deuteronomy3:1 Then we turned and we went along the road towards Bashan. Og, king of Bashan, marched out with his whole army to attack us at Edrei." 
Deuteronomy3:2 But the Lord said to me, ‘Do not be afraid of him. I have given him to you with his whole army and his land. You must do the same thing to Og as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who ruled in Heshbon.’" 
Deuteronomy3:3 So the Lord our God put Og, king of Bashan, under our power, as well as his whole army. We killed them all so that we left no one alive." 
Deuteronomy3:4 Then we took all of his cities. We took power over all the cities in the region of Argob where King Og of Bashan ruled. There were 60 cities. 
Deuteronomy3:5 All the cities had high walls around them, with gates that the people locked. There were also very many villages which did not have walls around them." 
Deuteronomy3:6 We destroyed them completely as a gift to the Lord. We had done the same thing to the cities of Sihon, king of Heshbon. We destroyed every city and we killed all the men, women and children." 
Deuteronomy3:7 But we kept all the animals and the valuable things from the cities for ourselves. 
Deuteronomy3:8 So, at that time, we took power over all the land that the two Amorite kings, Og and Bashan, had ruled. Their land was east of the Jordan River, from the Arnon Valley to Mount Hermon." 
Deuteronomy3:9 (The people from Sidon call Mount Hermon ‘Sirion’. The Amorites call it ‘Senir’.) 
Deuteronomy3:10 We took all their cities in the high, flat land and cities in the whole of Gilead and in Bashan. We went as far as Salecah and Edrei, cities that belonged to the kingdom of Og in Bashan." 
Deuteronomy3:11 Og, king of Bashan, was the only descendant of the Rephaites who had still been alive. He had an iron bed that is still in the Ammonites' town, Rabbah. It was 4 metres long and 2 metres wide." 
Deuteronomy3:12 When we had taken power over all that land, I gave some of it to the tribes of Reuben and Gad. I gave them the land north of Aroer near the Arnon Valley. I also gave them half of the hill country of Gilead and the cities there." 
Deuteronomy3:13 I gave the other part of Gilead to one half of the tribe of Manasseh. I also gave Og's kingdom, Bashan, to them. People called all the region of Argob in Bashan the land of the Rephaites." 
Deuteronomy3:14 Jair belonged to the tribe of Manasseh. He took power over all the region of Argob. He went as far as the borders of Geshur and Maakah. Then he gave his own name to that land. To this day, Bashan has the name ‘Jair's towns’." 
Deuteronomy3:15 I gave Gilead to Makir, who also belonged to the tribe of Manasseh." 
Deuteronomy3:16 As for the tribes of Reuben and Gad, I gave them land from Gilead to the Arnon Valley. The middle of the valley was the border of their land. Their land went as far as the Jabbok Valley, which is the border of the Ammonites' land." 
Deuteronomy3:17 The valley of the Jordan River was also a border of their land. It went from Lake Galilee as far as the Salt Sea, with Mount Pisgah to the east." 
Deuteronomy3:18 At that time I commanded you, ‘The Lord your God has given this land to you, for you to live in it. But all your soldiers must take their weapons and they must go across the Jordan River. You must go in front of the other Israelite tribes across the river." 
Deuteronomy3:19 But your wives, your children and your animals may stay here in the towns that I have given to you. I know that you have many animals." 
Deuteronomy3:20 Your men must fight to help the other Israelites until the Lord gives them a safe place to live. He has already given you a safe place to live. Now you must help the other Israelites to take the land which the Lord your God is giving to them. That land is on the other side of the Jordan River. When they take the land for themselves, you may return here to the land that I have given to you.’" 
Deuteronomy3:21 At that time, I commanded Joshua, ‘You have seen everything that the Lord your God has done to those two kings, Sihon and Og. He will do the same thing to the kingdoms where you are going." 
Deuteronomy3:21 At that time, I commanded Joshua, ‘You have seen everything that the Lord your God has done to those two kings, Sihon and Og. He will do the same thing to the kingdoms where you are going." 
Deuteronomy3:22 So do not be afraid of them. The Lord your God himself will fight to help you.’ 
Deuteronomy3:23 At that time, I asked the Lord many times," 
Deuteronomy3:24 ‘ Almighty Lord, I am your servant. You are showing me how great and how strong you are. There is no other god in heaven or on earth that can do the great things that you do." 
Deuteronomy3:25 Please let me go across the Jordan River! I want to see the good land over there. I want to see the beautiful hills and the mountains in Lebanon.’ 
Deuteronomy3:26 But the Lord was angry with me, because of you. He would not answer my prayer. He said, ‘You have said enough! Do not speak to me about that any more." 
Deuteronomy3:27 Go up to the top of Mount Pisgah. Stand there and look all around, to the west, north, south and east. Look carefully at the land, because you will not go across the Jordan River." 
Deuteronomy3:28 Now you must tell Joshua what to do. Tell him to be brave and strong. He will lead my people across the river into the land that you see. He will help them to take the land for themselves.’ 
Deuteronomy3:28 Now you must tell Joshua what to do. Tell him to be brave and strong. He will lead my people across the river into the land that you see. He will help them to take the land for themselves.’ 
Deuteronomy3:29 After that, we stayed in the valley near Beth-Peor." 
Deuteronomy4:1 Israel's people, listen to the rules and laws that I will now teach you. If you obey them, you will live. You will go into the land that the Lord, the God of your ancestors, is giving to you. The land will belong to you, for you to live in." 
Deuteronomy4:2 Do not add anything to these rules. Do not remove anything from them. You must obey the commands of the Lord your God that I am giving to you. 
Deuteronomy4:3 You saw for yourselves what the Lord your God did at Baal-Peor. He killed every person among you who worshipped Baal there. 
Deuteronomy4:4 But each of you who continued to trust the Lord your God is still alive today. 
Deuteronomy4:5 Look! I have taught you rules and laws, as the Lord my God told me to do. Now you must obey them when you go into the land where you will live." 
Deuteronomy4:6 Obey them carefully. That will show people of other nations that you are wise. They will hear about all your laws. Then they will say, ‘The people of this great nation are very wise and clever!’" 
Deuteronomy4:7 No other nation has a god who is near to them like the Lord our God. Even great nations have no god like that. But when we pray to the Lord our God, he listens!" 
Deuteronomy4:8 And no other great nation has rules and laws that are as fair as ours. That is God's Law that I am giving to you today. 
Deuteronomy4:9 But be very careful! Never forget the things that you yourselves have seen. Remember these things for as long as you live. Tell your children and your grandchildren about all the things that God has done. 
Deuteronomy4:10 Remember when you stood in front of the Lord your God at Sinai. He said to me, ‘Bring all the people here to me so that I can tell them my commands. Then they will learn to respect me and obey me. They will do this for as long as they live and they will teach my commands to their children.’" 
Deuteronomy4:11 At that time, you came and you stood at the edge of the mountain. You saw that the fire on the mountain reached up to the sky. But the sky was full of black clouds and it was dark." 
Deuteronomy4:12 Then the Lord spoke to you from the middle of the fire. You heard him as he spoke, but you did not see him. You only heard a voice." 
Deuteronomy4:13 He told you about his covenant and the ten commands that he wrote on two flat pieces of stone. He commanded you to obey them. 
Deuteronomy4:14 At that time, the Lord commanded me to teach you his rules and his laws. You must obey them in the land that you will now go to live in as your home." 
Deuteronomy4:15 Remember when the Lord spoke to you at Mount Sinai from the middle of the fire. You did not see him in any shape. So be very careful about everything that you do. 
Deuteronomy4:16 Do not make any image to be your god. That would be a sin. Do not make an image in any shape as your idol. Do not copy the shape of a man or a woman," 
Deuteronomy4:17 or the shape of any animal or bird. 
Deuteronomy4:18 Do not copy the shape of anything that moves along the ground, or any fish that swims in the deep water." 
Deuteronomy4:19 When you look up at the sky, you see the sun, the moon and the stars. They all shine up in the sky. But do not bend down low to them or worship them. They are there because the Lord your God has made them. He has given them to people everywhere in the world." 
Deuteronomy4:20 But you belong to the Lord. He brought you out of Egypt, which was like a hot oven where you had much pain. He rescued you, to be his own special people, as you are today." 
Deuteronomy4:21 The Lord your God became angry with me, because of you. He told me very strongly that I would not go across the Jordan River. He said that I would not go into the good land that he is now giving to you." 
Deuteronomy4:22 So I will die here. I will not go across the Jordan River. But you will soon go across. That good land will belong to you as your home. 
Deuteronomy4:23 Be careful! Do not forget the covenant that the Lord your God has made with you. Do not make for yourselves any kind of image as your idol. That is the command that he has given to you. 
Deuteronomy4:24 That is because the Lord your God wants you to belong to him alone. He is like a fire that destroys all bad things. 
Deuteronomy4:25 One day, you will have lived in the land for a long time. You will have children and grandchildren. Remember that you still must not make any kind of image as your idol. That would be a sin. Do not do any evil things that will cause the Lord your God to become angry." 
Deuteronomy4:26 I am warning you today, as everyone on earth and everyone in heaven hears what I say. If you do not obey this command, you will soon die. You will not live long in the land that is your home on the other side of the Jordan River. You will die very soon." 
Deuteronomy4:27 The Lord will send you out of that land to live among other nations. Only a few of you will continue to be alive.
