Job28:5 We grow our food on the earth above. But deep under the ground, people use fire to break the rocks." 
Job28:6 Sapphires come from the rocks in those places. The dust there contains gold. 
Job28:7 Even eagles or other birds with good eyes do not see those places. 
Job28:8 Lions and other wild animals do not walk there. 
Job28:9 People break into the hard rocks. They turn over rocks under the mountains. 
Job28:10 They cut holes through the rocks. They discover stones that are very valuable. 
Job28:11 They look carefully in the springs of water where rivers start. They bring into the light things that were hidden away. 
Job28:12 But as for wisdom, people do not know where to find it. They cannot learn how to understand things properly." 
Job28:13 Humans do not understand the value of wisdom. They cannot find it in this world where people live. 
Job28:14 Wisdom is not in the sea. Even in the deepest sea, no one will find it." 
Job28:15 Nobody can buy wisdom. You may have lots of gold and silver, but you can never buy wisdom." 
Job28:16 All the pure gold from Ophir will not be enough to buy it. It is worth more than valuable onyx or sapphires. 
Job28:17 Neither gold nor beautiful glass is as valuable as wisdom. A whole pot full of gold could not buy it. 
Job28:18 As for coral and jasper, they are not nearly as valuable as wisdom. Its price is more than the price of rubies." 
Job28:19 Topaz from Ethiopia is not as valuable as wisdom. No one can buy it, not even with pure gold." 
Job28:20 So where can we find wisdom? Where can we learn to understand things properly? 
Job28:21 We cannot see where it is. No person or animal can see it. No bird that flies in the sky can see it. 
Job28:22 In the place of death, they say, “We have heard only a little about wisdom, and where to find it.”" 
Job28:23 Only God knows the way to find wisdom. Only he knows where it is. 
Job28:24 He can see to the ends of the earth. He knows what happens everywhere in the world. 
Job28:25 He decided how strong the wind must be. He decided the size of the seas. 
Job28:26 He made a command for the rain to decide where it should fall. He chose where storms should go and make thunder. 
Job28:27 Then he thought about wisdom and its value. He prepared it and he tested it. 
Job28:28 God said this to us humans: “If you want to be wise, learn to respect the Lord with fear. If you want to understand things properly, turn away from evil acts.” ’" 
Job29:1 Job continued to speak. This is what he said: 
Job29:2 ‘I would like my life to be as it was before, many months ago. At that time, God took care of me." 
Job29:3 He was my guide, as if he had a lamp to show me the way. His light kept me safe as I walked in dark places." 
Job29:4 I was strong and well. God was my friend and he protected my home. 
Job29:5 At that time, Almighty God was still with me. My children were near me." 
Job29:6 I got plenty of milk from my cows, so that my life was easy. My olive trees gave me lots of oil." 
Job29:7 I would go to the gate of the city, and sit there as one of the leaders." 
Job29:8 When young men saw me, they would move to the side. Even the old men would stand up to respect me." 
Job29:9 The city's rulers would stop speaking. They would cover their mouths with their hands. 
Job29:10 The important leaders would be quiet. They would not want to say anything. 
Job29:11 Everyone was very pleased to listen to the things that I said. They respected me when they saw the things that I did. 
Job29:12 When poor people cried for help, I rescued them. I helped children who had no family that could help them." 
Job29:13 People who were near to death praised me for my help. Widows were happy when I helped them. 
Job29:14 I always did what was right, like good clothes that I wore. I was fair and honest, like my coat and my hat." 
Job29:15 If people were blind, I would be their guide. If people could not walk, I would get things for them." 
Job29:16 If poor people needed help, I helped them like their father would do. I spoke on behalf of strangers to help them get justice." 
Job29:17 I destroyed the power of wicked people. I rescued the people that they had attacked. 
Job29:18 At that time, I thought, “I will live to be an old man. After a long life, I will die in my own home." 
Job29:19 My body is strong and well, like a tree that always has plenty of water." 
Job29:20 People will continue to give me honour. Each day I will have new strength.” 
Job29:21 People waited patiently to hear my advice. They listened carefully to what I said. 
Job29:22 After I had spoken, they did not say any more. They listened to every word and they thought about it." 
Job29:23 They would wait for me to speak to them, like people who wait for rain to fall. My words would make them happy, like rain in spring that makes a farmer happy." 
Job29:24 They were surprised when I smiled at them. My smile made them happy. 
Job29:25 As their leader, I told them what they should do. I was like a king who leads his soldiers. When they were sad, I would comfort them.’" 
Job30:1 ‘But now it is different. Men who are younger than I am laugh at me. I would not even trust their fathers to take care of my sheep. 
Job30:2 The work that those old men could do was no help to me. They no longer were strong. 
Job30:3 They were weak and thin because they were hungry. They had to look for food in the desert at night. They found roots to eat in dry places. 
Job30:4 They pulled up plants in the desert as their food. They burned the roots of bushes to keep themselves warm. 
Job30:5 People chased them away from their towns, as if they were shouting at robbers." 
Job30:6 They had to live in dry valleys, among the rocks, or in caves." 
Job30:7 They cried aloud like animals among the bushes. They hid together under the thorn bushes. 
Job30:8 They were foolish people that nobody respected. People chased them away from their land. 
Job30:9 But now their sons sing songs that insult me. They tell jokes about me. 
Job30:10 They hate me and they do not come near me. When they see me, they spit at my face." 
Job30:11 God has made me weak and helpless. People do to me anything that they want to do. 
Job30:12 From one side, the crowd attacks me. They make me run away. They are like an army that builds a road to come and attack me." 
Job30:13 They stop me from escaping. They are ready to destroy me. They do not need anyone to help them. 
Job30:14 I cannot stop them when they attack me. They rush forward to knock me down. 
Job30:15 So I am always afraid. My honour has disappeared as if a strong wind has blown it away. My riches have also gone, like a cloud that passes across the sky." 
Job30:16 Now I know that my life will soon end. Every day I continue to suffer. 
Job30:17 At night, my bones are painful. The pain is always there in my body." 
Job30:18 God has used his power to take hold of me. He has held my shirt around my neck. 
Job30:19 He has thrown me down into the mud. I am no better than dust and ashes on the ground. 
Job30:20 I call aloud to you, God, but you do not answer me. When I stand to pray, you only look at me." 
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me. You have used your strength to attack me. 
Job30:22 You take hold of me and you carry me away, like a strong wind that blows. You use a storm to destroy me." 
Job30:23 I know that you are already taking me to my death. Everyone who lives has to go to that place. 
Job30:24 When someone is weak and in trouble, people do not usually try to hurt him. When someone calls aloud for help, they do not refuse to help him." 
Job30:25 When other people were in trouble, I wept for them. When I saw poor people it made me very upset." 
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good things to happen, trouble came instead. When I looked for light to shine on me, everything became dark." 
Job30:27 I hurt inside me all the time. Every day pain causes me to suffer. 
Job30:28 Illness has caused my skin to become black. It is not the sun that has made that happen. I stand up in the public meeting place and I call aloud for help. 
Job30:29 I have become like the jackals and the ostriches. My sad voice sounds like them. 
Job30:30 My skin has become black, and bits of skin fall off. My body is hot with fever." 
Job30:31 Now, when I make music with my harp, I only sing funeral songs. When I make music with my flute, I do it for people who are weeping.’" 
Job31:1 ‘I have made a promise with myself about how I look at young women. I will never think that I want to have sex with them. 
Job31:2 Anyone who does that should expect God to punish them. Almighty God in heaven above punishes people who do wrong things. 
Job31:3 He sends trouble to people who do wicked things. He punishes those people who do evil things. 
Job31:4 God sees all the things that I do. He carefully watches how I live my life. 
Job31:5 He must know that I am an honest man. I have never tried to deceive people. 
Job31:6 So he should judge me in a fair way. He should realize that I am not guilty. 
Job31:7 God seems to think that I have not lived in the right way. He may think that I have wanted bad things when they looked nice to me. Or perhaps I have done something else that was wrong. 
Job31:8 If I am really guilty of these things, someone else should eat the crops that I have planted. They should destroy all the crops in my fields." 
Job31:9 I have not wanted to love any woman except my wife. I have not hidden outside my friend's house to wait for his wife to be alone. 
Job31:10 If I had done those things, my wife should prepare food for another man. It would be right for other men to have sex with her." 
Job31:11 I would not have been faithful to my wife. I would have done a wicked thing and I would deserve punishment. 
Job31:12 If I loved another woman, it would be like a dangerous fire. It would destroy everything that I have worked hard to get." 
Job31:13 When my servants complain against me, I have always been fair to them." 
Job31:14 If I had been cruel to them, God would be right to punish me. When God judges me, I would have no honest answer to give to him." 
Job31:15 God made me and he made my servants too. God brought us all to birth. 
Job31:16 I have never refused to help people who are poor. I have always helped widows to hope for good things. 
Job31:17 I have never kept all my food for myself. I have always shared it with children who have no family. 
Job31:18 Since I was a young man, I have helped those children like a father. All my life I have helped widows too." 
Job31:19 I have seen people without enough clothes to keep them warm, or without a coat to wear." 
Job31:20 Then I would give them clothes. The wool from my sheep would keep them warm. And they all thanked me. 
Job31:21 I have not turned against children who have no family, even when the city judges agreed with me." 
Job31:22 God should punish me if these things are not true. I would accept the punishment that I deserve. 
Job31:23 I was afraid of the trouble that God might bring to me. So I could not do these bad things, because God is so great and powerful." 
Job31:24 I have not trusted in money or in gold. I have never thought that they would make me strong. 
Job31:25 When I was very rich, I was not proud about it. I did not think that I had worked hard to deserve it." 
Job31:26 I have looked at the sun when it shines brightly. And I have seen the moon when it is very beautiful. 
Job31:27 But I have never wanted to worship them or to give them honour. 
Job31:28 If I had done that, I would not have been faithful to God in heaven. I would have deserved punishment for that sin." 
Job31:29 I do not laugh at my enemies when they suffer. If my enemy has troubles, it does not make me happy." 
Job31:30 I have not cursed my enemies, to ask God to kill them. That would have caused a sin to come from my mouth!" 
Job31:31 None of the people who work in my house have ever said, “Job has not welcomed strangers into his house. He has not shared his food with them.”" 
Job31:32 Travellers may come to sleep in my house. They do not have to sleep outside in the street. 
Job31:33 I do not try to hide my sins, as some people do." 
Job31:34 I am not afraid of what people think about me. Even if a great crowd of people speak against me, I do not stay inside my house and keep quiet." 
Job31:35 I want someone to listen to me! All the things that I say are true. Almighty God should give me an answer! I need to know what I have done that is wrong. He should write down the reason why I am guilty! 
Job31:36 Then I would wear that paper clearly on my shoulders. I would be proud to wear it like a crown on my head. 
Job31:37 I would explain to God everything that I have done. I would stand in front of him without fear, like a ruler." 
Job31:38 I have not grown crops on land that does not belong to me. The land itself would accuse me if that were true. 
Job31:39 I have always paid for the food that other people have worked to grow. I have never cheated the farmers so that they suffered. 
Job31:40 If I have ever done any of those things, then may thorn bushes grow in my fields instead of wheat. May weeds grow in my fields instead of barley.’ That is the end of the words that Job spoke." 
Job32:1 Job would not agree that he had done anything that was wrong. So his three friends refused to argue with him any more. 
Job32:2 Another man was also there. His name was Elihu, Barakel's son. Barakel was a descendant of Buz. He belonged to Ram's clan. Elihu became very angry with Job. He was angry because Job continued to say that he was not guilty. Job said that God was wrong to punish him." 
Job32:3 Elihu was also angry with Job's three friends. They had not been able to find an answer to Job's problem. But they still said that Job was guilty. 
Job32:4 Elihu had waited for Job's three friends to speak before he spoke to Job himself. He waited because he was younger than them. 
Job32:5 But he became angry when Job's three friends had nothing more to say. So he finally decided to speak. 
Job32:6 Barakel's son, Elihu, said this: ‘I am young and you are old. So I was afraid to tell you what I was thinking." 
Job32:7 I thought, “Older men should speak. They have lived for many years and they should share their wisdom.”" 
Job32:8 But it is a person's spirit that helps them to understand things. Almighty God has to put his breath into them. 
Job32:9 Not all old people are wise. Old people do not always understand what is right. 
Job32:10 So I say, “Listen to me. I too will explain what I know.”" 
Job32:11 Yes, I waited for you all to speak. I listened to your wise thoughts, as you tried to find the right words to say." 
Job32:12 I listened carefully to you, as you argued with Job. But none of you could show Job that he was wrong. None of you was able to answer his problems." 
Job32:13 So do not say, “We are wise.” Do not say, “God must show Job that he is wrong. People cannot do that.”" 
Job32:14 Job was not speaking to me about his problems. So I will not answer him in the way that you did. 
Job32:15 Now they are confused! They have no more answers to give. There is nothing more that they can say. 
Job32:16 I have waited for them, and now they say nothing. They stand there and they have no more answers." 
Job32:17 So now I will say what I think. I, too, will tell you what I know." 
Job32:18 There are many things that I want to say. The spirit inside me causes me to speak. 
Job32:19 I cannot keep quiet any longer! I am like a bag that is full of new wine. I am ready to break so that everything pours out! 
Job32:20 So I have to speak or I will become ill. I must open my mouth and reply to you. 
Job32:21 I will be fair in what I say. I will not give honour to anyone to get their help. 
Job32:22 I am too honest to do that. If I am not fair, God, my Maker, would quickly remove me.’" 
Job33:1 ‘But now, Job, listen to the things that I will tell you. Listen carefully to everything that I will say." 
Job33:2 I will speak to you now. I am ready to tell you what I think. 
Job33:3 I am an honest man, so you can believe what I say. I will tell you what I know is true." 
Job33:4 The Spirit of Almighty God has made me. His breath gives me life. 
Job33:5 Answer me now, Job, if you can. Prepare yourself. Be ready to explain your problem to me." 
Job33:6 Listen! When God looks at us, we are the same as each other. God used clay to make both of us." 
Job33:7 So you should not be afraid of me. I will not do anything to hurt you. 
Job33:8 I have heard the things that you have said, Job. This is what I heard you say:" 
Job33:9 “I am not guilty. I have not done anything that is wrong. I am pure and clean. 
Job33:10 But God has decided to punish me! He attacks me as if I am his enemy. 
Job33:11 He has tied my feet with chains. He always watches me to see where I go.” 
Job33:12 But now I tell you this, Job: You are not right! God is greater than any human." 
Job33:13 So you should not argue against him. You should not say, “God does not answer any of my questions.”" 
Job33:14 God does speak to people. He speaks to them in many different ways. But often people do not recognize his voice. 
Job33:15 Sometimes God speaks to people in dreams and visions. He speaks to them at night, while they sleep in bed." 
Job33:16 He tells them about things that will happen. He warns them about terrible things, so that they are afraid." 
Job33:17 He tells them to turn away from their sins. He tells them not to be proud. 
Job33:18 He keeps people safe from death, so that they do not go to their graves." 
Job33:19 Or God may warn people with a painful illness. He may make their bodies hurt all the time. 
Job33:20 Then they do not want to eat anything. They cannot eat even the best food. 
Job33:21 Their bodies become very thin. People can even see their bones. 
Job33:22 They will soon die and go to their graves. Death is waiting to take them away. 
Job33:23 But perhaps an angel may come to help one of those people. He may speak on behalf of God, as a special angel among the many thousands of God's angels. The angel may tell that person how to live in a way that is right." 
Job33:24 Then God will be kind to that person. God will say, “Save that person, so that he does not go to his grave. I have found someone to pay the price for his life. So keep him alive.”" 
Job33:25 Then that person's body will become healthy again. He will be strong, like a young man." 
Job33:26 When he prays to God, God will be happy to answer him. He will again worship God with joy. And God will give him a good way of life again." 
Job33:27 That person will say clearly to everyone, “I did things that were not right. I did wrong things, but God did not punish me as I deserved." 
Job33:28 God rescued me from death, so that I did not go to my grave. Now I will enjoy my life in the light.”" 
Job33:29 God does all these things for people. He does them many times. 
Job33:30 He saves a person from death, so that he does not go to his grave. He gives light to that person, so that he may enjoy his life." 
Job33:31 Job, listen carefully to me. Be quiet, and I will speak to you." 
Job33:32 After that, you may answer me, if you have something to say. If I could say that you have not done anything wrong, that would make me happy." 
Job33:33 But if you have nothing to say, then listen to me. Be quiet and I will teach you how to be wise.’" 
Job34:1 Elihu continued speaking and he said this: 
Job34:2 ‘You men who are so wise and who know so much, listen to me. Hear what I have to say." 
Job34:3 You recognize nice food when you eat it. So you should recognize the value of my words. 
Job34:4 We should decide among ourselves what is right. We should agree together what is good. 
Job34:5 Job has said, “I am not guilty, but God refuses to judge me in a fair way." 
Job34:6 I am right, but nobody believes me. I have not done anything wrong, but I am suffering and I am near to death.”" 
Job34:7 There has never been a man like Job! He laughs at God as easily as he drinks water. 
Job34:8 He is a friend of wicked people, and people who do evil things." 
Job34:9 Job says, “If anyone tries to please God, it does not help them at all.”" 
Job34:10 So listen to me, you men who understand so much. Almighty God can never do anything that is wrong or wicked!" 
Job34:11 He judges people fairly as he sees the things that they do. He gives people what they deserve. 
Job34:12 It is true! Almighty God does not do wicked things. He never refuses to judge people in a fair way. 
Job34:13 God's great power did not come from anyone else! He himself has authority over the whole world. 
Job34:14 God could decide to remove life from the world. He could take back his spirit and his breath. 
Job34:15 If he did that, everyone that is alive would die. As humans, we would become dirt again." 
Job34:16 If you really understand things, Job, listen to me. Hear what I have to say." 
Job34:17 If God refused to do what is right, he could not rule the world. He is the powerful Judge who knows what is right. But you say that he is guilty!" 
Job34:18 It is God who may say to a king, “You are useless!” He may say to rulers, “You are wicked!”" 
Job34:19 God created everyone. He is not more kind to important people than he is to other people. He does not help rich people more than he helps poor people. 
Job34:20 Anyone may die suddenly, in the middle of the night. God may shake them, so that they die. He easily removes even the most powerful people." 
Job34:21 He watches the way that each person lives. He sees what they do every day. 
Job34:22 People who do wicked things cannot hide themselves. There is no place dark enough for them to hide from God. 
Job34:23 God does not have to tell people when they must come to him, so that he can judge them." 
Job34:24 He destroys powerful people, and he chooses other people to take their place. He does not have to ask them anything." 
Job34:25 He already knows the things that they have done. In the night, he removes their power, and he destroys them." 
Job34:26 He punishes them because they have done wicked things. He does that where everyone can see. 
Job34:27 Those powerful people have turned away from God. They have not respected his commands. 
Job34:28 They were cruel to poor people, so that God saw what was happening. God heard those poor people when they called to him for help." 
Job34:29 But if God decides to do nothing, no one can say that he is wrong. If he hides himself, no one can see him. But he still rules over people and over nations." 
Job34:30 God does not let men rule, if they do not respect his ways. He stops them from causing trouble to the people." 
Job34:31 Someone could say to God, “I am guilty, and I have received my punishment. But I will not do any more wrong things." 
Job34:32 Show me the sins that I have done when I did not know about them. If I have done wrong things, I will not do them again.”" 
Job34:33 But you, Job, refuse to accept God's justice. Do you expect God to do what you think is right? But you must choose what you will say. I cannot decide for you. So tell us what you are thinking now." 
Job34:34 Wise people will agree with me. When they hear my words, they will say," 
Job34:35 “Job speaks like a fool who knows nothing. He only says useless things.” 
Job34:36 Yes Job, you speak to us like a wicked person. So you should continue to suffer for ever!" 
Job34:37 You have not only done wrong things, you have also turned against God. You have shown that you do not respect us. You have said many things that insult God.’" 
Job35:1 Elihu continued to speak to Job. This is what he said: 
Job35:2 ‘The things that you say cannot be true. You say, “I am more right than God is.”" 
Job35:3 But you also say, “If I do nothing wrong, God does not bless me. No good thing comes to me.”" 
Job35:4 Now I will give you an answer. I will also speak to the friends who are with you. 
Job35:5 Look up at the sky to see what is there. Look at the clouds that are so high above you. 
Job35:6 If you do something that is wrong, it does not hurt God. Even if you do many wrong things, it does nothing to him." 
Job35:7 If you are righteous, that does nothing to help God. He does not receive anything that he needs from you." 
Job35:8 If you do wicked things, it is other people who suffer, not God. And if you do good things, you are only helping other people." 
Job35:9 When people suffer, they call aloud for help. They cry for help when powerful people attack them." 
Job35:10 But none of them says, “I want God to help me. He made me. He gives me songs to sing in dark nights." 
Job35:11 God teaches us more things than he teaches to the animals. He makes us wiser than the birds.” 
Job35:12 Finally, they call for help. But God does not answer them, because they are proud and wicked people." 
Job35:13 Their prayers mean nothing. Almighty God does not even listen to them. 
Job35:14 So God will certainly not listen to you, Job, when you say that you cannot see him. You say that you have explained your problem to him. You are waiting for him to give you an answer." 
Job35:15 You also say that God does not become angry with people who do wrong things. You say that he does not punish bad people. 
Job35:16 So, Job, you do not understand what you are saying. You speak a lot of words but they do not mean anything.’" 
Job36:1 Elihu continued to speak to Job. This is what he said: 
Job36:2 ‘Be patient a little bit longer, so that I can teach you some more. I still have more to say to show you that God is right." 
Job36:3 I will tell you the many different things that I know. I will show you that God, my Maker, is completely fair." 
Job36:4 Everything that I say to you is true. I am speaking to you as someone who knows everything. 
Job36:5 God is very powerful but he does not turn anyone away. When he decides to do something, he surely does it." 
Job36:6 He does not allow wicked people to continue to live. But he is always fair to those who are poor and weak. 
Job36:7 He takes care of righteous people. He gives them authority to rule like kings. He gives them honour for ever. 
Job36:8 People who do wrong things may suffer in prison. Chains may hold their legs so that they cannot move. 
Job36:9 When that happens, God shows them what they have done. He shows them their sins. They have done wrong things because they are proud." 
Job36:10 He tells them this to warn them. They must turn away from wicked things. 
Job36:11 They must obey him and agree to serve him. If they do that, they will have everything that they need. They will enjoy all the days that they live." 
Job36:12 But if they refuse to listen to God, they will go to their graves. They will die and they still will not understand what is right." 
Job36:13 People who turn away from God are always angry. Even when God punishes them, they do not pray for help." 
Job36:14 They die while they are still young. Their lives finish in shame, among the male prostitutes who serve false gods." 
Job36:15 But God rescues people who suffer. He uses their troubles to teach them about himself. 
Job36:16 God has called you out of your troubles, Job. He wants you to live in a safe place where you have no problems. He wants you to have a comfortable life with plenty of good food to eat." 
Job36:17 But now you have a lot of trouble, because God is punishing you as wicked people deserve. God is right and fair to judge you like that." 
Job36:18 Be very careful! Do not let anyone use their riches to deceive you. Do not let them pay you a lot of money to do something that is wrong. 
Job36:19 Even if you become rich, that would not keep you safe. Even if you were strong and powerful, that would not keep trouble away from you." 
Job36:20 Do not wait for the night to hide what you want to do. That is the time when people disappear from their homes. 
Job36:21 Be careful! Do not try to do wicked things. God has sent these troubles to test you. He wants to stop you doing evil things. 
Job36:22 Yes, God is very powerful. There is no teacher as good as he is." 
Job36:23 Nobody can tell him what he should do. No one can say to him, “You have done things that are wrong.”" 
Job36:24 People sing songs to praise God for the things that he has done. You also should remember to praise him. 
Job36:25 People everywhere have seen the things that God has done. But we look at them from far away. 
Job36:26 Yes, God is very great! We cannot know him completely. Nobody can count the number of his years!" 
Job36:27 It is God who pulls up water from the earth to make the clouds. Then he causes it to become rain. 
Job36:28 The rain pours down from the clouds. There is plenty for the people on the earth. 
Job36:29 Who understands how the clouds move across the sky? How does the noise of thunder come from God's home? 
Job36:30 Look at the lightning that God sends all over the sky! But he causes the bottom of the sea to remain dark. 
Job36:31 This is the way that God rules the people of the world. He sends the rain to give them plenty of food. 
Job36:32 He sends his lightning like an arrow. He commands it to hit the right place. 
Job36:33 He sends thunder to show that a storm is coming. Even the cows know what is happening.’ 
Job37:1 ‘When I hear the noise of a storm like that, I shake with fear." 
Job37:2 Listen carefully to the sound of God's voice, when he speaks like thunder." 
Job37:3 He sends lightning across the whole sky. Its light reaches everywhere on the earth. 
Job37:4 After the lightning comes the sound of his voice. God roars like the great sound of thunder. When he speaks like that, the storm never stops." 
Job37:5 When God's voice speaks, he causes wonderful things to happen. He does great things that we cannot understand." 
Job37:6 He tells the snow to fall to the earth. He tells the rain to pour down and make the ground wet. 
Job37:7 He does that to show people what he can do. He causes everyone to stop their own work. 
Job37:8 The wild animals have to hide in a safe place. They have to stay in their homes. 
Job37:9 The strong wind of a storm comes from the place where it starts. Cold winds blow strongly from the north. 
Job37:10 The cold wind is like God's breath. He causes ice to cover the rivers and the lakes. 
Job37:11 He fills the clouds with lots of water. He sends the lightning through the clouds. 
Job37:12 The clouds move around across the whole earth. They go wherever God sends them. He tells them what they must do. 
Job37:13 He may send a storm as punishment for his land. Or he may send it to show his love for his people. 
Job37:14 Listen carefully to what I am telling you, Job. Stop and think about the wonderful things that God does." 
Job37:15 Do you know how God commands the storms? Do you know how he sends the bright lightning from the clouds? 
Job37:16 Can you explain how the clouds hang in the sky? God knows everything perfectly and he does these wonderful things. 
Job37:17 When the hot wind blows from the south, nothing moves on the earth. Your body is too hot in the clothes that you wear." 
Job37:18 God puts the clouds across the whole sky, like a metal mirror that shines brightly. Can you also do that? No!" 
Job37:19 Tell us, Job, what things we should say to God. We cannot argue with him, because our minds are in a dark place." 
Job37:20 Should I tell God that I want to speak to him? That would be the end of me! 
Job37:21 Now the wind has blown all the clouds away. The sun shines brightly in the sky. It is too bright for us to look at. 
Job37:22 God comes with his bright glory from the north! His glory is all around him. It makes us afraid. 
Job37:23 Almighty God is so great that we cannot come near to him. He is very powerful. But he always does what is right and fair. He is not cruel to people. 
Job37:24 So people respect God with fear. He does not listen to those who think that they are wise.’ 
Job38:1 The Lord spoke to Job from the middle of the storm. This is what he said: 
Job38:2 ‘Who do you think you are? Why do you ask questions about my wisdom? You speak many words, but you do not understand anything." 
Job38:3 Prepare yourself for a difficult job! I will ask you some questions and you must answer them. 
Job38:4 Were you with me when I made the world? If you think you are so clever, tell me all about it!" 
Job38:5 It was me who decided the size of the world. You surely must know that! I measured it as I made it. 
Job38:6 Tell me how I fixed the foundations of the earth. Who made it stay in its right place? I did! 
Job38:7 At the dawn of that day, the stars sang together. They sang for joy with all the angels." 
Job38:8 When the sea poured out of the ground, I decided where its borders should be. I kept the water in its right place." 
Job38:9 I caused the clouds to come over the sea. I covered it with complete darkness. 
Job38:10 I put a border around the sea. I locked it in its place. 
Job38:11 I said to the sea, “This is as far as you can come. You may come no further. Your powerful waves must stop there!”" 
Job38:12 But you, Job, did you ever command the morning to begin? No! You have never in your whole life told the sun when it should rise." 
Job38:13 You have not told the light of day to cover the earth. You have not told it to chase away the wicked things that happen in the night. 
Job38:14 When the light of day comes, people see the land clearly. Its shape and its colours show like bright clothes." 
Job38:15 The light of day is too bright for wicked people. It destroys their power to do cruel things. 
Job38:16 You have not gone to the springs that fill the seas with water. You have not walked on the bottom of the deep seas. 
Job38:17 Nobody has shown you the gates of death. People go through them to that very dark place. 
Job38:18 You do not understand the great size of the earth. If you know all about that, tell me!" 
Job38:19 You do not know where light comes from. You do not know where darkness has its home. 
Job38:20 Can you take them to the places where they belong? Do you know the roads that go there? 
Job38:21 Surely you must know all this, because you have lived for so long! It seems that you were already born when I created these things!" 
Job38:22 Have you visited the place where I store all the snow? Or the place where I keep the hail? 
Job38:23 I keep the snow and the hail ready to use. I use them in times of trouble and wars. 
Job38:24 You do not know where lightning comes from. Or where the east wind starts when it blows across the earth. 
Job38:25 I make a path for the storms to travel along. I tell the thunder and the strong rain which way to go. 
Job38:26 I cause rain to fall in places where no person lives. It makes even a desert become a wet place. 
Job38:27 The rain falls on dry ground where nothing grows. It causes grass to grow there again. 
Job38:28 Do you know if the rain has a father? Who is a father to the dew? 
Job38:29 Who is a mother to the ice? Who gives birth to the frost that makes the air so cold? 
Job38:30 It causes the water to become as hard as stone. The water of the sea also becomes ice. 
Job38:31 You cannot make the stars of the Pleiades stay in their places. You cannot change the shape of Orion. 
Job38:32 You cannot bring out the groups of stars for people to see at different times of the year. You cannot put the stars of the Great Bear and the Little Bear in their right places in the sky. 
Job38:33 You do not know the rules that the stars above obey. You cannot make the earth obey those rules. 
Job38:34 You cannot shout commands to the clouds. You cannot tell them to pour down their rain, so that a flood of water covers you." 
Job38:35 You cannot send the lightning to hit the place where you want it to go. The lightning does not say to you, “We are ready to go wherever you want.”" 
Job38:36 Who has given people wisdom, so that they understand things with their minds?" 
Job38:37 Is anyone wise enough to count the clouds? Can anyone cause the clouds to pour down rain from above? 
Job38:38 The rain causes the dust of the dry ground to become hard. The dry soil becomes pieces of mud. 
Job38:39 When the lions become hungry, can you find food for the young lions to eat?" 
Job38:40 You cannot do that for them when they sit in their caves. You cannot help them as they hide in the bushes, ready to attack other animals." 
Job38:41 You cannot find food for the ravens to eat. You cannot help them when their babies cry to me because they are weak and hungry.’ 
Job39:1 ‘Job, do you know that time when the goats on the mountains give birth? Do you watch the wild deer when their babies are born?" 
Job39:2 Can you count the number of months that these animals are pregnant? How long must they wait until they give birth? 
Job39:3 They bend down low to the ground. They give birth to the babies that they have carried inside them. 
Job39:4 The young animals grow and they become strong in the fields. Then they leave their parents and they do not return to them. 
Job39:5 Did you send out the wild donkeys to go wherever they want? 
Job39:6 No! It was me who gave them the desert as their home. I let them live in places where the ground has salt. 
Job39:7 They stay far away from the busy cities. They do not allow anyone to make them work. 
Job39:8 Instead, they live on the hills, where they find fresh plants to eat." 
Job39:9 Can you tell a wild ox to work for you? No! At night, it will not stay to feed at your farm." 
Job39:10 It will not let you tie it to a plough. It will not agree to prepare your fields in the valleys. 
Job39:11 A wild ox is very strong. But you cannot trust it to help you with your difficult work. 
Job39:12 It will not help you to bring in your harvest of grain. It will not take the grain to your threshing floor. 
Job39:13 When an ostrich is happy, it waves its wings. But it cannot use its wings to fly, as a stork can do." 
Job39:14 A mother ostrich leaves her eggs on the ground. She lets the sand cause them to be warm. 
Job39:15 She does not realize that people or wild animals might break the eggs with their feet. 
Job39:16 She does not take care of her babies, as if they did not belong to her. She does not worry that all her work might be useless." 
Job39:17 This is because I did not give wisdom to ostriches. I did not give them minds that understand things. 
Job39:18 But when an ostrich begins to run, it can run very fast. It can run faster than a horse and someone who rides on it." 
Job39:19 Did you, Job, give horses their strength? Did you give them the long hair that they have on their necks?" 
Job39:20 You did not make horses able to jump like locusts. They frighten people when they blow air out through their noses. 
Job39:21 They stamp their feet on the ground, as they prepare to go to a battle. They are ready to go and attack the enemy." 
Job39:22 A horse is brave and it is not afraid of anything. It does not run away from the enemy's weapons. 
Job39:23 The soldier who is riding it has his arrows ready at the horse's side. Swords and spears shine brightly in the sun. 
Job39:24 The horse shakes with joy as it runs to the battle. When the battle trumpet makes its noise, the horse wants to run even faster!" 
Job39:25 When it hears the sound of the trumpet, it makes a happy noise. From far away, it recognizes the smell of the battle. It hears the army officers as they shout their commands." 
Job39:26 Was it your wisdom, Job, that taught hawks how to fly? No! You could not teach them to fly towards the south in winter." 
Job39:27 Do eagles wait for your command to fly high into the sky? No! You could not teach them how to build their nests high up in the mountains. 
Job39:28 They live among the highest rocks. That is where they stay at night. They are safe on the sharp rocks. 
Job39:29 From the high rocks, eagles look for their food. They see small animals far away, that they can catch and eat." 
Job39:30 They come together around the bodies of dead animals. The young eagles drink the blood.’ 
Job40:1 The Lord said to Job," 
Job40:2 ‘Do you still want to argue with me, the Almighty God? You say that I have not been fair. Now you must explain what you mean.’" 
Job40:3 Then Job answered the Lord. This is what he said: 
Job40:4 ‘I am only a foolish man. I cannot answer your questions. I cover my mouth with my hand so that I say nothing. 
Job40:5 I have already spoken too many times. I will say no more.’ 
Job40:6 Then the Lord spoke to Job from inside the storm. This is what he said: 
Job40:7 ‘Prepare yourself for a difficult job! I will ask you some more questions and you must answer them. 
Job40:8 Do you say that I am not a fair judge? Do you want to show that I am guilty, not you? Then you could say that you are right." 
Job40:9 Do you have the same power to do great things as I have? Can you speak with a voice as loud as thunder? I can! 
Job40:10 If you think that you can do all this, show how great you are! Show everyone your power and your glory!" 
Job40:11 Show everyone your great anger! Look at all the people who like to boast and bring them down low! 
Job40:12 Yes, cause all the proud people to be humble. Knock down wicked people to the ground." 
Job40:13 Bury them all together in the dust of the ground. Cover their faces and put them in their graves. 
Job40:14 If you can do these things, Job, I will praise you. I will agree that you have the power to save yourself." 
Job40:15 Listen! I created the monsters called Behemoth. I made you and I made them too. They eat grass, as cows do." 
Job40:16 But look at the strength of their legs and their body! 
Job40:17 Their tails are like the branches of a cedar tree. Their legs are thick and very strong. 
Job40:18 Their bones are like sticks made of bronze. Their legs are as strong as iron. 
Job40:19 These monsters are some of the greatest animals that I have made. But I who made them can take my sword and go near to them. 
Job40:20 They eat the grass that grows on the hills. That is where the wild animals play. 
Job40:21 They lie down under the thorn trees. They hide among the reeds at the edge of the water. 
Job40:22 They rest in the shadow of the thorn trees, and among the willow trees beside the water." 
Job40:23 Even when the river is deep and dangerous, these monsters are not afraid. The water of the Jordan River may cover their mouths, but they still feel safe." 
Job40:24 There is no way that anyone can catch one of them. No one can make its eyes blind. No one can put a ring in its nose and lead it away.’ 
Job41:1 ‘It is the same with the sea monsters called Leviathan. Can you catch them with a hook? Can you use a rope to keep their mouths shut? 
Job41:2 Can you tie a rope through their noses? Can you put a hook into their mouths? 
Job41:3 No! They will not ask you nicely to be kind and let them go free. 
Job41:4 They will not make any agreement with you. You cannot make them your slaves to work for you as long as they live. 
Job41:5 Leviathan is not something to enjoy at home, like a bird. You cannot tie it with a rope, so that your girls can play with it." 
Job41:6 Traders do not buy it at the market. They do not cut it up to sell it. 
Job41:7 Job, can you throw spears into Leviathan's body to kill it? Can you shoot arrows into its head?" 
Job41:8 If you try to take hold of it, you will never forget the fight! You will never try to do that again!" 
Job41:9 If anyone hopes to catch it, he is deceiving himself. As soon as he sees it, he no longer feels so brave!" 
Job41:10 No one is brave enough to wake it. So is anyone brave enough to argue against me? 
Job41:11 Nobody has ever won against me to make me pay them anything. Everything in the whole world belongs to me. 
Job41:12 I must tell you how strong Leviathan's legs are. Their bodies are very strong and they have a wonderful shape. 
Job41:13 Nobody is able to remove their thick skin. It is like armour that nobody can cut through. 
Job41:14 Nobody is strong enough to open its mouth. Its teeth make everyone afraid. 
Job41:15 Its back is like rows of shields. They are as hard as stone with no spaces between them. 
Job41:16 Each shield is very near the next one. Not even air can pass between them. 
Job41:17 They are strongly joined together, and nothing can pull them apart." 
Job41:18 When Leviathan breathes out loudly, bright light shines from its nose. Its eyes shine like the red sun at dawn." 
Job41:19 Fire comes out from its mouth. Bright flames shoot out! 
Job41:20 Smoke pours out from its nose, like reeds that burn under a pot." 
Job41:21 Its breath is hot enough to light a fire! Flames pour out of its mouth. 
Job41:22 Its neck is very strong. Wherever it goes, people shake with fear." 
Job41:23 There are no weak places in its skin. Its skin is as hard as iron. 
Job41:24 It is cruel and it has no fear. Its heart is as hard as stone. 
Job41:25 Even the bravest people are afraid when Leviathan appears. They run away when it starts to move. 
Job41:26 If you hit it with a sword, you will not hurt it. Spears, arrows and knives will be useless." 
Job41:27 It can easily break iron or bronze weapons. It breaks them as easily as grass or soft wood. 
Job41:28 If you shoot arrows at it, it will not run away. If you use a sling to throw stones at it, the stones will hurt it no more than bits of chaff." 
Job41:29 If you hit it with a heavy stick, that hurts it no more than a piece of straw. If you throw a spear at it, it laughs!" 
Job41:30 Its stomach has sharp pieces, like bits of a pot that someone has broken. When it moves along, it ploughs the soil under it." 
Job41:31 When it swims in the deep water of the sea, the water seems to boil. It makes big waves, like oil that is boiling in a pot." 
Job41:32 As it swims, the water behind it shines. It makes waves that look like white hair on the sea." 
Job41:33 There is no other animal like it in the whole world. It is not afraid of anything. 
Job41:34 It does not respect any other great animal. It rules as king over all the proud animals.’ 
Job42:1 Then Job replied to the Lord. He said this: 
Job42:2 ‘I know that you can do all things. You can do anything that you want to do. Nobody can stop you. 
Job42:3 You asked me, “Why do you ask questions about my wisdom, when you do not understand anything?” It is true. I was speaking about things that I do not understand. They are things that are too wonderful for me to know." 
Job42:4 You said to me, “Listen carefully to what I will say. I will ask you questions and you must answer them.”" 
Job42:5 In the past, I had heard about you from other people. Now I have seen you for myself." 
Job42:6 So I am ashamed of the things that I said. I sit here in dirt and in ashes to show you that I am very sorry.’ 
Job42:7 When the Lord had spoken to Job, he said this to Eliphaz, the man from Teman: ‘I am very angry with you and with your two friends. The things that my servant Job has said about me are true. But the things that you said were not right." 
Job42:8 You three men must now go to Job. Take seven bulls and seven male sheep with you. Burn them as sacrifices for yourselves. After you have done that, my servant Job will pray for you. I will answer his prayer. Then I will not punish you as you deserve because of your foolish words. My servant Job has spoken the truth about me, but you have not.’" 
Job42:9 So Eliphaz, the man from Teman, Bildad, the man from Shuah, and Zophar, the man from Naamah, did what the Lord had told them to do. And the Lord answered Job's prayer." 
Job42:10 After Job prayed for his three friends, the Lord made him a rich man again. He gave Job twice as many things as he had before." 
Job42:11 Then Job's brothers and sisters came to eat a big meal with him in his house. People who had been Job's friends came also. They all told him that they were sad about his troubles. They were upset because the Lord had caused him to suffer. Each of them gave Job a piece of silver and a gold ring. 
Job42:12 After this, the Lord blessed Job more than in the beginning of his life. All these animals belonged to Job: 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 pairs of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys." 
Job42:13 He had seven sons and three daughters. 
Job42:14 The daughters' names were Jemimah, Keziah, and Keren-Happuch." 
Job42:15 They were the most beautiful women in the whole land of Uz. Job said that they should receive some of his things after he died, along with their brothers." 
Job42:16 Job lived for 140 years after this. He was still alive when his grandchildren and their grandchildren were born. 
Job42:17 So when Job died, he had lived for a long time." 
Psalms1:1 If someone does not do what wicked people tell him to do, if he does not join with sinners, if he does not meet with those who laugh at God, God has blessed that person!" 
Psalms1:2 He is happy when he obeys the Law of the Lord. He thinks about God's Law during the day and at night. 
Psalms1:3 He will become like a tree that grows beside streams of water. It gives its fruit at the right season. Its leaves do not fall off. Everything that person does will have a good result. 
Psalms1:4 But wicked people are as empty as chaff. The wind blows them away. 
Psalms1:5 So they will not go free when God judges them. Sinners cannot join with God's people, the people who are right with God." 
Psalms1:6 The Lord takes care of people who respect him. But wicked people are living in a way that will take them to a bad end. 
Psalms2:1 Why are nations making plans to turn against the Lord? All their plans will fail! 
Psalms2:2 Kings and rulers decide to work together, to fight against the Lord and the king that he has chosen." 
Psalms2:3 They say, ‘We will not accept their authority over us! We will get free from their power!’" 
Psalms2:4 The Lord who sits on his throne in heaven laughs at them. He says that their plans are useless. 
Psalms2:5 Then he becomes very angry and he frightens them. He warns them with strong words and he says," 
Psalms2:6 ‘I have put my king on his throne in Zion. I have put him on my holy mountain.’ 
Psalms2:7 I will tell you what the Lord has promised to me, his king. He said to me, ‘You are my son. Yes, today I have become your father!" 
Psalms2:8 Ask me and see what I will do! I will give you all the nations on the earth. They will all belong to you. 
Psalms2:9 You will break them with an iron sceptre, as if they are clay pots.’" 
Psalms2:10 So, you kings, think carefully! You who rule the nations of the world, learn this lesson:" 
Psalms2:11 Respect the Lord and serve him. Enjoy his rule with fear! 
Psalms2:12 Bend down low in front of his son! If not, the Lord will be angry. He will quickly become very angry. You will suddenly die! But as for those who serve the Lord as their king, he blesses them and he takes care of them all." 
Psalms3:1 Lord, every day I have more enemies. Many people fight against me." 
Psalms3:2 They say about me, ‘God will not save him.’" 
Psalms3:3 But Lord, you are like a shield for me. You are the one who gives me honour. You lift my head up high." 
Psalms3:4 I called aloud to the Lord. He answered me from his holy mountain. 
Psalms3:5 I lie down and I sleep. Then I wake up, because the Lord takes care of me." 
Psalms3:6 Even if 10,000 enemies are all around me to attack me, I will not be afraid of them." 
Psalms3:7 Lord, stand up! My God, save me from my enemies! Hit all my enemies in the face. Break the teeth of those bad people." 
Psalms3:8 Lord, you are the one who saves us. Please bless your people." 
Psalms4:1 God, please answer me when I call aloud for help. You are the one who shows that I am right. Help me in my trouble. Show me that you are kind. Listen to me when I pray." 
Psalms4:2 Tell me this, you people: How long will you refuse to respect me? How long will you love stupid ideas? How long will you agree with lies?" 
Psalms4:3 The Lord is very kind to those who serve him. He has chosen them to belong to himself. So he listens when I pray to him. 
Psalms4:4 Shake with fear and stop doing wrong things! Lie on your bed and think quietly about your life. 
Psalms4:5 Offer the right sacrifices to the Lord. Continue to trust in him. 
Psalms4:6 Many people say, ‘Will anyone do a good thing for us?’ Lord, let your face smile on us." 
Psalms4:7 You have made me very happy. I am happier than anyone who has lots of food and wine. 
Psalms4:8 I will lie down and I will sleep in peace. You, Lord, are the one who takes care of me, and you keep me safe." 
Psalms5:1 Lord, listen to what I say to you. I am upset, so think about what I am saying." 
Psalms5:2 My King and my God, listen to my voice. I am asking you to help me. I am praying to you!" 
Psalms5:3 Lord, every morning you listen to my voice. At sunrise I will turn to you for help, and I will wait for you to answer." 
Psalms5:4 My God, you do not like anything that is bad. Evil people cannot live near you." 
Psalms5:5 Proud people cannot stand in front of you. You hate everyone who does evil things. 
Psalms5:6 You destroy those who tell lies. The Lord hates murderers, and those who deceive others." 
Psalms5:7 But I will come into your house, because of your faithful love. I will bend low in your holy temple, and I will worship you." 
Psalms5:8 Lord, help me to follow your right way. My enemies are waiting to attack me. Show the right way clearly to me, so that I do not go the wrong way." 
Psalms5:9 My enemies never speak what is true. They only want to destroy people. Their mouth has the smell of an open grave. Their tongues say nice things, but they are all lies!" 
Psalms5:10 God, say that they are guilty! Let their own wicked ideas bring punishment to them. Send them away because they do so many bad things. Yes, they have turned against you." 
Psalms5:11 May all people who turn to you be happy! Make them always sing with joy, because you keep them safe. Take care of those who are faithful to you. Then they will be really happy." 
Psalms5:12 Lord, you bless those who live in a right way. Your love is like a shield that keeps them safe." 
Psalms6:1 Lord, when you are angry, please do not punish me." 
Psalms6:2 I do not feel strong, Lord, so please be kind to me. Lord, please make me well again. Even my bones are shaking." 
Psalms6:3 I am very frightened. Lord, when will you do something to help me?" 
Psalms6:4 Lord, turn back to me. Give me back my life. Make me safe because of your faithful love." 
Psalms6:5 If I die, I will no longer remember you. Nobody can praise you in their grave!" 
Psalms6:6 I am crying so much that I have no more strength. My bed becomes wet because I weep all night. My tears run into my pillow. 
Psalms6:7 I am so sad that my eyes do not see well any more. They are becoming weak because of all my enemies. 
Psalms6:8 Go away from me, you people who do wicked things! Now the Lord has heard me when I weep." 
Psalms6:9 I asked the Lord to be kind and to help me. Now he has answered me! 
Psalms6:10 All my enemies will become ashamed! They will go away from me in fear! Shame will suddenly come to them! 
Psalms7:1 Lord, my God, I come to you to keep me safe. Please save me from all those people who want to catch me." 
Psalms7:2 If you do not keep me safe, my enemies will tear me into pieces. They will attack me like lions do, and nobody will be able to save me." 
Psalms7:3 Lord, my God, if I have done a wrong thing, then let my enemies chase me and catch me. If I have done a bad thing against a friend, let my enemies catch me. If I have helped a friend's enemy to attack him, let my enemies catch me! If I am guilty of any of these things, let my enemies knock me down to the ground. They should leave me there, so that I lie as a dead person in the dirt." 
Psalms7:4  
Psalms7:5  
Psalms7:6 But Lord, now do something! Wake up and help me! Show how angry you are against my enemies. Punish them in the way that you know is right!" 
Psalms7:7 Bring together the people of all nations, to stand in front of you. Rule over them from high above." 
Psalms7:8 You, Lord, judge the people of all nations. Lord, Most High God, say that I am not guilty. Truly, I am righteous and honest." 
Psalms7:9 Stop wicked people from doing evil things. Keep your own people safe. You always do what is right, and you know how we think and feel." 
Psalms7:10 The Most High God is like a shield to keep me safe. He takes care of good, honest people." 
Psalms7:11 God is a judge who is fair. Every day he is angry against wicked people. 
Psalms7:12 If they do not change the way that they live, God will prepare to punish them. He will make his sword sharp, and he will put an arrow in his bow." 
Psalms7:13 He will prepare his dangerous weapons. He is ready to shoot his arrows that burn with fire. 
Psalms7:14 Look! Wicked people prepare to do evil things! They want to cause trouble. Like a pregnant woman, what starts inside them comes out for everyone to see! They give birth to lies that deceive people." 
Psalms7:15 Wicked people want to hurt people. They are digging a deep hole for people to fall into. But they fall into the hole themselves! 
Psalms7:16 The ideas that they have to hurt other people bring trouble on themselves! They want to be cruel to other people, but they themselves receive the pain!" 
Psalms7:17 So I will thank the Lord because he does what is right. The Lord is the Most High God, so I will sing to praise his name!" 
Psalms8:1 Lord, our ruler, your name is famous all over the world. The sky above shows how great you are." 
Psalms8:2 You have taught children and babies to praise you. You do that to show your enemies how strong you are. Anyone who turns against you has to be quiet. Your cruel enemies can do nothing! 
Psalms8:3 You made the skies with your own hands. When I look up, I see the moon and the stars. You have put them all in their right place." 
Psalms8:4 So why do you even think about men and women? Why do you take care of humans? 
Psalms8:5 You have made them a little lower than God himself. You have given them honour, like kings." 
Psalms8:6 You have chosen them to rule everything that you have made. You have put everything under their authority. 
Psalms8:7 Yes! Humans rule them all: Farm animals and wild animals, birds, fish and all the things that swim in the sea." 
Psalms8:8  
Psalms8:9 Lord, our ruler, your name is famous all over the world." 
Psalms9:1 I will thank you, Lord, with all that I am. I will tell people about all the great things that you have done." 
Psalms9:2 I will be very happy because of who you are. I will sing to praise your name, Most High God." 
Psalms9:3 When my enemies see your power, they run away. They fall down and that is the end of them." 
Psalms9:4 You have shown that I am right. You have sat on your throne as a judge. You decide in a fair and honest way. 
Psalms9:5 You have spoken against the nations, to make them afraid. You have destroyed the wicked people. Nobody will remember them ever again." 
Psalms9:6 The cities of our enemies have become a heap of stones! You destroyed their cities for ever. Nobody remembers our enemies at all. 
Psalms9:7 The Lord rules as king for ever. He sits on his throne as a righteous judge. 
Psalms9:8 He is fair when he judges all the people in the world. He brings justice to the nations. 
Psalms9:9 The Lord is a strong, safe place for people who are suffering. When trouble comes to them, he keeps them safe." 
Psalms9:10 Everyone who knows you, Lord, trusts in you. You never turn away from anyone who comes to you for help." 
Psalms9:11 Sing to the Lord to praise him! He rules as king in Zion. Tell the nations about the great things that he has done. 
Psalms9:12 God punishes murderers. He takes care of people who are suffering. When they call to him, he does not refuse to help them." 
Psalms9:13 Please be kind to me, Lord. See how my enemies cause me to suffer! You are the one who can save me from death." 
Psalms9:14 Then I will praise you for everyone to hear! I will tell the people of Zion that you have saved me, and I will be very happy!" 
Psalms9:15 People of other nations dug a deep hole to catch me. Now they have fallen into it themselves! The trap that they hid to catch me has caught their own feet! 
Psalms9:16 The Lord has shown us who he is. We know that he brings justice. The evil things that wicked people do catch them in their own traps. 
Psalms9:17 Wicked people will go away into the deep hole of death. This will happen to all the nations that turn away from God. 
Psalms9:18 But as for those who are poor and weak, God will never forget them. People who suffer should continue to hope." 
Psalms9:19 Lord, stand up! Do not let people turn against you! Bring the nations to stand in front of you. Judge them!" 
Psalms9:20 Lord, make them afraid! Make the nations know that they are only weak and human." 
Psalms10:1 Lord, why do you stand so far away from us? Why do you hide when we are in trouble?" 
Psalms10:2 Wicked people are cruel to poor people. They use their evil ideas to take hold of weak people. 
Psalms10:3 Wicked people boast because they get the things that they want. They praise greedy people, and they curse the Lord." 
Psalms10:4 Wicked people are too proud to turn to God. They think, ‘God will not give me any trouble.’" 
Psalms10:5 Wicked people always seem to have success. They do not respect your commands, God. They laugh at all their enemies." 
Psalms10:6 They think, ‘No trouble will cause us to fail. Nothing bad will ever happen to us.’" 
Psalms10:7 Many lies come from their mouths, and they are always cursing people. They say evil things to be cruel and to hurt people." 
Psalms10:8 They hide near the villages. They wait there to catch people and they kill them. They look for any weak person who passes that way. 
Psalms10:9 They hide like lions among bushes. They wait to catch a poor, weak person. Like hunters who catch animals in their nets, they carry the people away." 
Psalms10:10 They stamp on these helpless people. They knock them down to the ground. The weak people fall down, because the wicked people are too strong." 
Psalms10:11 Wicked people think, ‘God will give us no trouble! He does not even see what we do.’" 
Psalms10:12 Rise up, Lord! Knock down wicked people! Do not forget to help weak people." 
Psalms10:13 Why do wicked people insult God? They think, ‘God will not give me any trouble.’" 
Psalms10:14 But you, God, see what is happening. You see how wicked people bring pain and trouble. You decide how to punish them. Helpless people trust that you will help them. You take care of children who have no father." 
Psalms10:15 So destroy the power of wicked, evil men! Punish them for the wicked things that they have done. Cause them to stop." 
Psalms10:16 The Lord rules as king for ever! The nations who do not serve him will disappear from his land. 
Psalms10:17 Lord, you listen to the prayers of people who are suffering. When they call to you for help, you make them feel brave and strong." 
Psalms10:18 You stand beside children who have no father, and all those who are poor and weak. As a result, no human on the earth can frighten them again." 
Psalms11:1 I have trusted the Lord to keep me safe. So do not say this to me: ‘Fly away like a bird to the hills! 
Psalms11:2 See what the wicked people are doing! They are preparing their bows and arrows. They are hiding in dark places, to shoot their arrows at good, honest people." 
Psalms11:3 When the law no longer has authority, righteous people can do nothing!’" 
Psalms11:4 The Lord is in his holy temple. He rules from his throne in heaven. He carefully watches what people are doing. He knows about each person. 
Psalms11:5 The Lord watches both good people and wicked people. Good people please him, but he hates wicked people and cruel people." 
Psalms11:6 He will send hot coal and sulphur to fall like rain on wicked people. A dangerous wind will be the punishment that they deserve. 
Psalms11:7 The Lord always does what is right. He loves people to do good things. Those who do what is right will know that God loves them. 
Psalms12:1 Lord, please help me! All the good people have gone away! No kind or faithful people remain." 
Psalms12:2 Everybody tells lies to one another. When they say nice things, they do it to deceive their friends." 
Psalms12:3 Lord, please shut their mouths! When they deceive people or they boast, stop them doing that!" 
Psalms12:4 They say: ‘Nobody can tell us what to do! We will say what we like to get what we want!’ 
Psalms12:5 But the Lord says: ‘Now I will do something! I know the cruel things that wicked people do to poor people. When weak people cry with pain, I hear them. So I will make them safe, as they want me to do for them.’" 
Psalms12:6 The Lord 's promises are true. We can trust them, because they are pure. They are as pure as silver that people have put in a hot fire seven times." 
Psalms12:7 Yes Lord, you will take care of the poor people. You will always keep each one of them safe from all those evil people." 
Psalms12:8 But if people praise evil things, then wicked people will boast more and more." 
Psalms13:1 Lord, will you forget about me for ever? When will you remember to come and help me?" 
Psalms13:2 My thoughts make me worry all the time. How long must I continue to be sad? How long will my enemies continue to win against me? 
Psalms13:3 Lord, please listen to me! I feel that I am nearly dead. Please give me the strength to live!" 
Psalms13:4 If I die, my enemies will say, ‘We have won against him!’ My enemies will be very happy if I fall." 
Psalms13:5 But I will trust you because of your faithful love. Please make me safe, so that I can be happy and thank you." 
Psalms13:6 Yes, I will sing to praise the Lord, because he will surely help me." 
Psalms14:1 Only fools say to themselves, ‘There is no God.’ People like that do bad and evil things. None of them do things that are good and right." 
Psalms14:2 The Lord is looking down from heaven. He looks at the people of the world, to see if there are any wise people among them. He looks to see if anyone wants to know God." 
Psalms14:3 But everyone has turned away from God. They have all become evil. Not even one person does things that are right! 
Psalms14:4 Will those evil people never learn? They destroy my people, as if they are eating a meal. They never pray to the Lord." 
Psalms14:5 Look at them! Now they are very afraid! They see that God is with the people who obey him. 
Psalms14:6 You wicked people tried to bring shame to poor people. But the Lord keeps poor people safe. 
Psalms14:7 The Lord is the one who can save Israel's people. Please Lord, come now from your home in Zion! When the Lord makes his people strong again, Jacob's descendants will be happy! Yes, Israel's people will shout with joy!" 
Psalms15:1 Lord, who may stay with you, in your own home? Who may live there, on your holy hill?" 
Psalms15:2 It is the person who does only things that are right. It is the person who lives in a completely good way, and who speaks only what he knows is true." 
Psalms15:3 He says nothing bad against other people. He does nothing that will hurt his friends, nor does he insult them." 
Psalms15:4 He does not respect wicked people, but he does respect people who serve the Lord. When he promises to do something, he always does it." 
Psalms15:5 He lends his money to help other people, and he does not ask for more money in return. He does not accept a bribe, to speak against people who have done nothing wrong. People who live in this way will always be safe." 
Psalms16:1 Please keep me safe, God, because I come to you for help." 
Psalms16:2 I have said to the Lord, ‘You are my Lord. All the good things that I have in my life are gifts from you.’" 
Psalms16:3 When I think about your own people who live in this land, it makes me happy! They are truly great people!" 
Psalms16:4 But many troubles will come to people who choose to worship other gods. I will not join with them to offer gifts of blood to their gods. I will never use the names of their gods to make promises. 
Psalms16:5 You, Lord, are all that I need. What you give me is enough, for me and for my descendants." 
Psalms16:6 Yes, you have given me a good way of life. Your gifts are better than good fields or valuable land." 
Psalms16:7 I will praise the Lord, who is my guide through life. In the dark nights, you help me to learn what is right." 
Psalms16:8 I will always trust the Lord to be with me. He is very near to me, so I will not be upset." 
Psalms16:9 I am truly happy and I thank God. I know that my life is safe with God. 
Psalms16:10 You, Lord, will not leave me in the deep hole of death. As someone who belongs to you, you will not leave my body to spoil in my grave." 
Psalms16:11 You will lead me along the path of life. Because you are with me, I am very happy. I know that I will be with you for ever, and that makes me very happy!" 
Psalms17:1 Lord, please listen to me. I am asking you to help me. I am speaking what is true, so please do what I ask you to do." 
Psalms17:2 Say clearly that I am not guilty. You can see for yourself that I am right. 
Psalms17:3 You look deep into my mind. You check my thoughts at night. When you test me, you find nothing that is wrong. I have decided that I will never tell any lies." 
Psalms17:4 I have watched people do bad things. But I have obeyed your commands. I have not joined with cruel people to do what they do. 
Psalms17:5 No, I carefully follow your ways. I do not turn away from them." 
Psalms17:6 I pray to you, God, because you will answer me. Please listen to me when I pray to you." 
Psalms17:7 Do great things that show your faithful love! You have the power to keep people safe from their enemies. You help them when they trust you for help. 
Psalms17:8 Please keep me safe, in the same way that people take care of their eyes. Keep me near to you, in the way that birds protect their babies under their wings." 
Psalms17:9 Keep me safe from the wicked men who attack me. My enemies are all around me, and they want to kill me!" 
Psalms17:10 They refuse to be kind to anyone. They speak proud words. 
Psalms17:11 Now they have found me. They are ready to attack! They look for their chance to knock me down to the ground. 
Psalms17:12 They are like hungry lions. They want to tear me into pieces. They are hiding like young lions. They are ready to jump out and catch me! 
Psalms17:13 Lord, please do something! Fight against them! Knock them down! Take your sword and save me from those wicked men." 
Psalms17:14 Lord, use your power to save me from them. They only think about this world, and they enjoy the good things that it gives them. So give them all that you have ready for them. Give them more than they want! May their children also have plenty, and enough left to give to their children too." 
Psalms17:15 As for me, Lord, I am not guilty of sin. So I will see your face. When I wake up, I will see you clearly, and I will be really happy!" 
Psalms18:1 I love you, Lord. You make me strong." 
Psalms18:2 The Lord keeps me safe. He is my great rock and my strong place. God is my high rock. I run to him to hide and be safe. He keeps me safe like a soldier's shield. He is the strong place where I can hide safely. 
Psalms18:3 I praise the Lord because he deserves it! When I called to him for help, he saved me from my enemies." 
Psalms18:4 Death was near enough to catch me! Danger was like a river that poured over me. 
Psalms18:5 Death was tying me up with ropes, to pull me into the deep hole of the grave." 
Psalms18:6 In my trouble, I prayed to the Lord. I called to my God to help me. He heard my voice from his home in heaven. My prayer reached his ears." 
Psalms18:7 Then the earth moved and it shook. Even the strong mountains shook, because God was angry." 
Psalms18:8 He breathed out smoke from his nose. Fire and hot coals came out from his mouth, to destroy his enemies." 
Psalms18:9 God caused the sky to open as he came down. Dark clouds were under his feet. 
Psalms18:10 He sat on a cherub and he flew. The wind carried him as he travelled. 
Psalms18:11 God covered himself with darkness. He hid himself in dark clouds that held rain. 
Psalms18:12 Bright light went in front of him. Hail and lightning fell from the clouds. 
Psalms18:13 The Lord sent thunder from the sky. People everywhere heard the voice of the Most High God. 
Psalms18:14 The Lord shot his arrows of lightning. He caused his enemies to run away. 
Psalms18:15 When you shouted against your enemies, Lord, even the bottom of the deep sea appeared. People could see the foundations of the earth, when you breathed out in anger." 
Psalms18:16 The Lord reached down to me from above. He took hold of me and he pulled me up out of the deep water. 
Psalms18:17 Yes, he saved me from my powerful enemies. The people who hated me were too strong for me, but the Lord saved me from their power." 
Psalms18:18 They attacked me when I was in trouble, but the Lord helped me." 
Psalms18:19 He led me out to a place where I would be safe. He saved me because I made him happy. 
Psalms18:20 The Lord was kind to me because I do things that please him. He blesses me because I am not guilty of bad things. 
Psalms18:21 I have obeyed the Lord 's commands. I have not turned against my God. 
Psalms18:22 I understand all his rules, and I do not refuse to obey them." 
Psalms18:23 He knows that I have done nothing that is wrong. I kept away from any sin. 
Psalms18:24 The Lord has been kind to me, because I did good things. He sees that I am not guilty." 
Psalms18:25 Lord, if people are faithful to you, then you are faithful to them. If people do nothing that is bad, they can trust you to do nothing bad to them." 
Psalms18:26 If people always do what is right, they can trust you to be kind to them. But if people are wicked, you turn against them." 
Psalms18:27 You save people who are humble. But if people are proud, you bring them down very low." 
Psalms18:28 Lord, you make my lamp burn brightly. My God is my light in the dark." 
Psalms18:29 You make me strong, so that I can attack a whole army! With the help of my God, I can climb over any high wall." 
Psalms18:30 God's way is perfect. Yes, the Lord 's promises are always true. He keeps safe everyone who goes to him for help, like a shield that keeps a soldier safe." 
Psalms18:31 We know that the Lord is God, and no one else. Only our God can keep us safe, like a strong rock!" 
Psalms18:32 It is God who makes me strong. He shows me the safe way through my life. 
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet stand strongly on the ground. Like a deer, I can stand on high mountains and not fall." 
Psalms18:34 He teaches me how to fight in war. He makes my arms strong to shoot arrows from a metal bow. 
Psalms18:35 Lord, your power has helped me to be strong. You have kept me safe, like a shield. Your kind help has made me great." 
Psalms18:36 You have helped me to move safely, so that my feet do not slip." 
Psalms18:37 I ran after my enemies and I caught them. I did not turn back until I had killed them. 
Psalms18:38 I knocked them down to the ground. They could not get up again. They fell under my feet. 
Psalms18:39 You have made me strong to fight battles, so that I win against my enemies." 
Psalms18:40 You cause my enemies to turn around, and they run away from me. I can destroy those who hate me." 
Psalms18:41 They call out for help, but nobody is there to help them. They call to the Lord, but he does not answer them." 
Psalms18:42 I beat them into dust that the wind blows away. I walk on them, like dirt that lies on the streets." 
Psalms18:43 You have saved me from people who attacked me. You made me a ruler of other nations. People that I did not know before now serve me as their ruler. 
Psalms18:44 When foreign people hear about me, they choose to obey me. They cannot stand against me because they are afraid." 
Psalms18:45 They are not brave enough to fight me. They shake with fear, as they come out of their strong cities." 
Psalms18:46 The Lord is alive! He is my strong Rock! Everyone should praise him! He rules as king! He is the God who saves me. 
Psalms18:47 He is the true God who punishes my enemies. He makes the nations obey me. 
Psalms18:48 He is the one who saves me from my enemies. Yes, God, you give me power to win against those who attack me. You keep me safe from angry and cruel men." 
Psalms18:49 Lord, I will thank you, so that all the nations know about you. I will sing to praise your name." 
Psalms18:50 Yes, the Lord gives power to me, David, his king. He gives me great strength to win against my enemies. I am the king that he has chosen to rule his people. He will continue to be kind to me and to my descendants for ever." 
Psalms19:1 The heavens show us how great God is. The sky above shows his good work. 
Psalms19:2 Each day speaks about God, until the next day continues the story. Every night tells us how great God is." 
Psalms19:3 They do not use words to speak with. Nobody hears their voice. 
Psalms19:4 But their message goes round all the earth. All the world can understand what they say. God has made a home for the sun in the sky. 
Psalms19:5 Each morning, the sun comes out, like a happy man who has just married. It comes out, like a strong man who wants to run a race." 
Psalms19:6 The sun rises at one end of the heavens. It travels in a big circle to the other end. Nothing can hide from its heat. 
Psalms19:7 The Law of the Lord is perfect. It makes us strong again. We can trust what the Lord teaches us. He helps ordinary people to be wise. 
Psalms19:8 The Lord 's rules are always right. If we obey them, they make us really happy. The Lord 's commands are completely good. They show us how to live in a good way." 
Psalms19:9 It is right for us to respect the Lord. That will always help us. The laws of the Lord are true. They are right and fair. 
Psalms19:10 They are more valuable than gold, even the best gold. They are sweeter than the best honey." 
Psalms19:11 Your laws warn us to do what is right. If we obey them, they are a great help to us." 
Psalms19:12 Nobody realizes every time when they do something wrong. Please forgive me for the sins that I do not know about. 
Psalms19:13 Lord, stop me doing things that I know are wrong. Do not let those sins rule my life. Then I will not be guilty. I will not have turned against you in an evil way." 
Psalms19:14 Lord, I want to make you happy. I want my words and my thoughts to please you. You are my strong Rock and you are my Redeemer." 
Psalms20:1 May the Lord answer you when you are in trouble and you call to him for help. May Israel's great God keep you safe. 
Psalms20:2 He will send help to you from his holy place, from his temple in Zion." 
Psalms20:3 He will remember the gifts that you have offered to him. He will accept your burnt offerings. 
Psalms20:4 May God give to you all the things that you really want. He will help you to do everything that you want to do. 
Psalms20:5 Then we will be happy and we will shout aloud when you win against your enemies. We will wave our flags to praise our God. Yes, I pray that the Lord will do for you everything that you ask him to do." 
Psalms20:6 Now I know that the Lord will rescue the king that he has chosen. God will agree to help him from his holy place in heaven. God will use his great power to make his king safe. 
Psalms20:7 Some people hope that their chariots will keep them safe. Other people trust their horses to save them. But we will trust in the name of the Lord our God. 
Psalms20:8 Those people will fall down, and they will not get up again. But we will get up and stand again, and we will be strong." 
Psalms20:9 Lord, help the king to win against his enemies! Please answer us when we ask you for help."
