Psalms21:1 Lord, the king is happy, because you help him with your great strength. You have helped him to win against his enemies, so he is very happy." 
Psalms21:2 You have given to him all the things that he really wanted. When he asked for them, you have not refused to give them to him." 
Psalms21:3 You came to him, and you blessed him with many good things: You put a crown on his head. It was made from the best gold." 
Psalms21:4 He asked you to keep his life safe, and you gave him a long life. His descendants will continue for ever." 
Psalms21:5 People praise him, because you have helped him to win against his enemies. You have caused him to rule as a great king." 
Psalms21:6 You will continue to bless him for ever. Because you are very near to him, he is very happy." 
Psalms21:7 The king trusts in the Lord. Because of the Most High God's faithful love, the king will never fail." 
Psalms21:8 You will use your great power to take hold of your enemies. You will catch everybody that hates you. 
Psalms21:9 When you come out to fight against them, you will destroy them like a hot fire. The Lord destroys them, because he is angry with them." 
Psalms21:10 You will remove your enemies' children from the earth. They will no longer have any descendants. 
Psalms21:11 They decided to hurt you. They thought how they would do that. But they could not do anything bad against you. 
Psalms21:12 No! You shoot your arrows at them, so that they turn round and they run away!" 
Psalms21:13 Get up, Lord, and show your strength! We will sing to praise you because you have great power." 
Psalms22:1 My God! My God, why have you left me alone? Why is my help far away? I am crying out in great pain!" 
Psalms22:2 My God! I call aloud to you for help every day, all day and all night! But you do not answer my prayers, so I cannot sleep." 
Psalms22:3 I know that you are the Holy God. You sit on your throne as king, and Israel's people praise you." 
Psalms22:4 Our ancestors trusted in you. When they trusted you, you saved them." 
Psalms22:5 They called aloud to you and you saved them. They trusted in you, and they did not become disappointed." 
Psalms22:6 People insult me. They think that I am worth nothing. It seems that I am only a worm, and I am not really a man." 
Psalms22:7 Everyone that sees me laughs at me. They laugh and they shake their heads. 
Psalms22:8 They say, ‘He should trust in the Lord! The Lord should save him. If the Lord is really happy with him, the Lord should rescue him.’" 
Psalms22:9 Lord, you brought me safely to birth. You took care of me at my mother's breasts." 
Psalms22:10 From the day that I was born, I have always been in your care. You have been my God since my mother gave birth to me." 
Psalms22:11 So do not stay far from me now. Trouble is near to me, and there is nobody to help me." 
Psalms22:12 There is danger all around me, like the strong bulls of Bashan. I cannot move because my enemies are very near, all around me." 
Psalms22:13 They open their mouths like lions! They are ready to eat me! They are like lions that tear their food into pieces. 
Psalms22:14 My strength has gone, like water that is poured away. My bones do not join together properly. I feel weak inside and hope has disappeared." 
Psalms22:15 My mouth has become dry like a piece of a broken pot. My tongue sticks to my mouth. You have left me down in the dirt, as good as dead!" 
Psalms22:16 Wicked people are everywhere around me. They are ready to attack me, like a group of wild dogs. They tear apart my hands and my feet." 
Psalms22:17 My body is so weak I can count all my bones. My enemies look at me and they laugh. They are happy because I have pain. 
Psalms22:18 They have taken my clothes, and they choose what they want for themselves. They throw dice to decide who receives each piece." 
Psalms22:19 But Lord, please do not stay far away from me! You can make me strong again. Please hurry to help me!" 
Psalms22:20 Keep me safe! My enemies want to kill me with their swords. Save my life from those wild dogs! 
Psalms22:21 Do not let those lions eat me! Keep me safe from the horns of those wild bulls! I know that you have answered my prayer! 
Psalms22:22 I will tell my people how great you are. I will praise you when we meet together. 
Psalms22:23 Everyone who serves the Lord must praise him! All Jacob's descendants must respect him! Yes, all Israel's people must worship him!" 
Psalms22:24 God did not forget to help the man who was in pain. He did not turn away from the one who suffered. When he called to God for help, God answered his prayer." 
Psalms22:25 Yes, I will praise you, God, in the great meeting of your people. I will give you what I have promised to give. Everyone who serves you, Lord, will see that." 
Psalms22:26 Poor people will have plenty of food to eat. Those who come to the Lord will praise him. May God help you to live well for ever! 
Psalms22:27 People everywhere in the world will respect the Lord. They will turn to him for help. Families from all nations will worship you. 
Psalms22:28 The Lord rules as king over all the nations. 
Psalms22:29 All the rich people on earth will bend down, and they will worship God. Everyone will die one day. They must all bend down in front of God. Nobody can keep himself alive." 
Psalms22:30 Our children will also serve God. And they will tell their children about our Lord God. 
Psalms22:31 They will speak about all the great things that God has done to save his people. People who have not yet been born will learn about the things that God has done. 
Psalms23:1 The Lord takes care of me, like a shepherd with his sheep. I have everything that I need." 
Psalms23:2 He takes me to green fields where I can rest. He leads me to streams of water where I can drink. 
Psalms23:3 He gives me new strength in my life. He is my guide to the right paths. He does this to give honour to his name. 
Psalms23:4 I may walk through a valley that is as dark as death, but I will not be afraid of any danger. This is because you are with me, Lord. Your stick and your shepherd's pole protect me from danger." 
Psalms23:5 You prepare a feast for me while my enemies watch. You put olive oil on my head. You bless me so much that my cup is completely full. 
Psalms23:6 I am sure that you will always be good to me. You will love me all the days of my life. That will never change. I will always live in the Lord 's house. 
Psalms24:1 The whole earth belongs to the Lord. Everybody who lives in the world and all the things in it belong to him. 
Psalms24:2 The Lord built the foundation of the earth. He built it on the seas and the rivers. 
Psalms24:3 Who may climb up the hill of the Lord? Who can stand in his holy place? 
Psalms24:4 Only the person who does what is right, and who has pure thoughts. He does not worship idols and he does not make false promises." 
Psalms24:5 The Lord will bless people who are like that. The Lord God will save them. He will say that they are not guilty of wrong things. 
Psalms24:6 People like that come to God. They want to please him. They like to pray to the God of Jacob. 
Psalms24:7 Old gates of the city, wake up! Open now, to leave a wide path! Then the great king of glory will come in." 
Psalms24:8 Who is this great king of glory? He is the Lord, who is strong and powerful. He is the Lord, who wins against his enemies." 
Psalms24:9 Be ready, you old gates of the city! Be ready to open wide! Then the great king of glory will come in." 
Psalms24:10 Who is this great king? He is the Almighty Lord. Yes, he is the great king of glory!" 
Psalms25:1 Lord, I offer myself to you." 
Psalms25:2 I trust in you, my God. Please do not let me be ashamed. Do not let my enemies laugh at me, because they are stronger than me." 
Psalms25:3 Surely nobody who trusts in you will become ashamed. But those who like to deceive others will become ashamed. 
Psalms25:4 Lord, help me to understand your ways. Teach me your good paths." 
Psalms25:5 Please be my guide and teach me your truth. I trust you every day, because you are the God who keeps me safe." 
Psalms25:6 Lord, please be kind to me and show me your faithful love. From long ago you have always been like that." 
Psalms25:7 Do not punish me for the sins that I did long ago. When I was young, I turned against you. Please be kind to me, because of your faithful love!" 
Psalms25:8 The Lord is good and he does what is right. Because of that, he teaches sinners the right way to live." 
Psalms25:9 He is a guide for humble people, so that they know what is right. He teaches them to follow his way." 
Psalms25:10 The Lord is kind to his people in everything that he does. He shows his faithful love to those who obey the rules of his covenant. 
Psalms25:11 Lord, my sins are very many. Please forgive me, and show that your name is great." 
Psalms25:12 If people respect the Lord, the Lord will show them the right way to live." 
Psalms25:13 People like that will enjoy a good life. The land will belong to their descendants. 
Psalms25:14 The Lord is a guide for those who serve him. He teaches them to obey his covenant. 
Psalms25:15 I always look to the Lord for help. He will keep me safe from my enemies, so that their traps do not catch me." 
Psalms25:16 Please turn towards me, Lord, and be kind to me. I feel very weak and lonely." 
Psalms25:17 I worry a lot about my troubles. Please rescue me from them! 
Psalms25:18 Look at all my pain! See how I am suffering! Forgive me for all my sins. 
Psalms25:19 Look at all my enemies! There are very many of them! They hate me so much that they want to hurt me. 
Psalms25:20 Keep my life safe and take care of me. I have come to you to keep me safe, so do not let me be ashamed." 
Psalms25:21 I trust you to save me. Please take care of me, because I am honest and good." 
Psalms25:22 God, save Israel's people from all their troubles!" 
Psalms26:1 Lord, I always try to do what is right. I continue to trust you all the time. So please show that I am not guilty of wrong things." 
Psalms26:2 Test me, Lord, and see how I have lived. Look into my mind and see my thoughts." 
Psalms26:3 I always know that I can trust you, because of your faithful love. So I live to please you." 
Psalms26:4 I do not become a friend of people who tell lies. I do not join together with hypocrites. 
Psalms26:5 I keep away from any crowd of evil people. I do not become a friend of wicked people. 
Psalms26:6 I wash my hands as a sign that I live in a pure way. I will worship you at your altar, Lord." 
Psalms26:7 I will sing aloud to thank you. I will tell people about all the great things that you have done. 
Psalms26:8 Lord, I love the temple that is your house. It is the place where we clearly see your glory." 
Psalms26:9 Do not take away my life, as you do with sinners. Do not punish me with death, as you do with murderers." 
Psalms26:10 Those wicked people are always ready to do evil things. They use bribes to get what they want. 
Psalms26:11 But I always try to do what is right. Please save me and be kind to me! 
Psalms26:12 Now I stand in a safe place. I will praise the Lord when his people meet together to worship him. 
Psalms27:1 The Lord gives me light in my life and he makes me safe. So I am not afraid of anyone! The Lord takes care of my life. Nobody can make me afraid! 
Psalms27:2 Evil men are ready to attack me. They want to destroy my body. But when my enemies attack me, they trip and fall to the ground!" 
Psalms27:3 Even if a whole army comes to fight against me, I will not be afraid. Even if they attack me in war, I will continue to trust God." 
Psalms27:4 There is one thing that I have asked the Lord to do for me. This is the one thing that I really want: I want to live in the Lord 's house all the days of my life. Then I will clearly see the Lord 's beauty. I will pray to him in his temple. 
Psalms27:5 When danger is near, the Lord will keep me safe. He will take care of me in his home. He will put me in a safe place, on the top of a high rock." 
Psalms27:6 My enemies may be all around me, but I will win against them. They will see how strong I am! I will offer sacrifices to the Lord in his temple. I will be so happy that I will shout aloud! Yes, I will sing to praise the Lord." 
Psalms27:7 Lord, hear me when I call to you for help. Be kind to me and answer me." 
Psalms27:8 I have heard you say to me, ‘Come near to me.’ So I will come and pray to you, Lord." 
Psalms27:9 Please do not turn away from me. I am your servant, so do not be angry with me. You have always helped me in the past, so do not push me away now. You are the one who saves me, God, so do not turn away from me." 
Psalms27:10 Even if my father and my mother leave me, the Lord will still take care of me." 
Psalms27:11 Lord, please teach me to follow your way. Lead me along a good, safe path, because my enemies are waiting to attack me." 
Psalms27:12 Do not give me to my enemies. They say false things against me, so that they can destroy me." 
Psalms27:13 But I surely believe that I will see how good the Lord is! I will know his love while I still live in this world. 
Psalms27:14 Wait for the Lord to help you! Be strong and brave. Yes, wait for the Lord to help you!" 
Psalms28:1 Lord, I am calling to you for help. You are my safe place, like a high rock. Please do not refuse to listen to me. If you do not answer me, I will be like those people who are going down into the deep hole of death." 
Psalms28:2 Hear me when I call to you for help. Please be kind to me. I am lifting up my hands towards your Most Holy Place. 
Psalms28:3 Do not pull me away to punishment, as you do with wicked people. Those people do evil things. They say kind words to their friends, but really they want to hurt them." 
Psalms28:4 Punish them for their sins. Give back to them what they deserve! Yes, punish them! That is the fair thing to do." 
Psalms28:5 They do not want to understand the things that the Lord has done, or the things that he has made. The Lord will punish them, and he will remove them for ever." 
Psalms28:6 We should praise the Lord! He has been kind to me, when I called to him for help." 
Psalms28:7 The Lord makes me strong. He keeps me safe like a shield. I trust in him completely, and he has helped me. I am very happy, and I will thank him with my songs." 
Psalms28:8 The Lord makes his people strong. He protects the king that he has chosen. The Lord keeps him safe. 
Psalms28:9 Lord, save your people! Bless the people who belong to you! Take care of your people like a shepherd. Always carry them in your arms!" 
Psalms29:1 All you angels in heaven, say how great the Lord is! Say that the Lord rules with power!" 
Psalms29:2 Say that the Lord 's name is great! Worship the Lord for who he is, and remember that he is holy." 
Psalms29:3 The voice of the Lord shouts over the water. The God of glory makes the noise of thunder. The Lord appears over the great sea. 
Psalms29:4 The Lord 's voice is powerful. It has the authority of a king! 
Psalms29:5 When the Lord shouts, even the cedar trees break! Yes! The Lord breaks the great cedar trees of Lebanon." 
Psalms29:6 He makes Lebanon jump like a calf. He causes Mount Hermon to shake like a young bull jumps. 
Psalms29:7 The Lord 's voice makes fire like lightning in the sky. 
Psalms29:8 When the Lord shouts, the desert shakes! He makes the Kadesh desert shake!" 
Psalms29:9 When the Lord shouts, the big oak trees bend over. The leaves fall off all the trees in the forests. But in his temple, everyone says, ‘He is great!’" 
Psalms29:10 The Lord rules as king over the deep waters. The Lord will be king for ever. 
Psalms29:11 The Lord makes his people strong. The Lord blesses his people with peace. 
Psalms30:1 Lord, I will praise you, because you have saved me from death. You have not let my enemies laugh at me." 
Psalms30:2 Lord, my God, I called to you for help, and you have made me well again." 
Psalms30:3 Lord, you pulled me up out of the deep hole of death. You saved me from among those who are going into the grave." 
Psalms30:4 Sing to the Lord, you people who trust him. Praise his holy name." 
Psalms30:5 His anger continues only for a moment, but he is kind to us for our whole life. We may be sad all through the night, but in the morning, we will be happy again." 
Psalms30:6 When I was feeling safe and strong, I thought, ‘Nothing can ever destroy me.’" 
Psalms30:7 But Lord, it was you who made me safe and strong, because you are so kind. But then you turned away from me, and I became very afraid." 
Psalms30:8 I called to you for help, Lord. I asked you to be kind to me, my Lord." 
Psalms30:9 If I go down into the deep hole of death, that will not help you! My dead body cannot praise you there! It cannot tell people to trust you!" 
Psalms30:10 Lord, please listen to me! Please be kind to me! Lord, please be my helper!" 
Psalms30:11 Now you have helped me to stop crying, so that I dance instead! I do not need to wear sackcloth, because I am no longer sad. Instead, you have made me very happy." 
Psalms30:12 So I am truly happy and I will sing to praise you. I cannot stay quiet! Lord, my God, I will continue to thank you for ever." 
Psalms31:1 Lord, I have come to you to keep me safe. Never let me become ashamed. You always do what is right so please save me!" 
Psalms31:2 Listen to me, and quickly send help to me. Lord, be a rock where I can hide. Be a strong place to keep me safe." 
Psalms31:3 You are my high rock and my strong place. Because of your good name, lead me and be my guide." 
Psalms31:4 You are the place where I can hide, so keep me safe. Do not let my enemies' traps catch me." 
Psalms31:5 I put my life into your hands. I know that you will save me, Lord, because you are a God that I can trust." 
Psalms31:6 I hate people who worship useless idols. As for me, I trust in the Lord." 
Psalms31:7 I am very happy because of your faithful love. You see when I suffer. You know when I am very upset. 
Psalms31:8 You have not put me under the power of my enemies. You have led me into an open place, where I can be safe." 
Psalms31:9 Lord, I am very upset, so please be kind to me. My eyes are tired because I weep so much. My whole body has become very weak." 
Psalms31:10 I am near to death, because I am so sad. I am crying with pain as my life comes to an end. I have so much trouble that I have no strength at all. Even my bones have become weak." 
Psalms31:11 All my enemies hate me. My friends insult me. Everyone who knows me is afraid of me. They run away when they see me in the street. 
Psalms31:12 Everybody has forgotten about me. I might as well be dead! They think that I am useless, like a broken plate." 
Psalms31:13 I hear what many people are saying against me. News from everywhere makes me afraid. They are deciding together how to kill me. 
Psalms31:14 But I continue to trust you, Lord. I say, ‘You are my God!’" 
Psalms31:15 You are the one who decides what will happen to me in my whole life. Save me from the power of my enemies and from the people who want to catch me. 
Psalms31:16 Turn towards me, your servant, and be kind to me! Keep me safe, because of your faithful love." 
Psalms31:17 Lord, do not let me become ashamed. I call to you for help. Cause evil people to become ashamed. Let them go to their graves and be silent there!" 
Psalms31:18 May people that tell lies become silent. They insult righteous people. They are proud people, and they boast about themselves." 
Psalms31:19 You have prepared many good things to bless people who respect you. While everyone watches, you bless those who come to you for help." 
Psalms31:20 You give them a place to hide, and you keep them safe with you. When their enemies want to attack them, you take care of them. When their enemies speak lies against them, they are safe with you." 
Psalms31:21 Praise the Lord! When my enemies were all around me, he showed me that his faithful love is very great." 
Psalms31:22 When I was afraid, I said, ‘God is no longer with me!’ But I spoke too soon. When I called to you for help, you heard me and you answered me." 
Psalms31:23 Love the Lord, all you people who belong to him! If people serve the Lord faithfully, he keeps them safe. But if people are proud, he punishes them as they deserve." 
Psalms31:24 All you people who trust the Lord to help you, you should be strong and very brave!" 
Psalms32:1 When God has forgiven a person's sins, they are truly happy! They may have turned against God, but when God forgives them, they are happy." 
Psalms32:2 They may have done something bad, but when the Lord says, ‘Not guilty!’, he has truly blessed them. They are happy because they no longer try to hide their sins." 
Psalms32:3 When I said nothing to God about my sins, my whole body became weak. I cried all the time because of the pain." 
Psalms32:4 In the day and in the night, you punished me very much. It seemed as if the hot sun was taking away my strength." 
Psalms32:5 Then I told you about my sins. I did not try to hide the bad things that I had done. I said, ‘I will tell the Lord about the things that I have done against him.’ When I did that, you forgave my sins." 
Psalms32:6 Because of this, all your own people should pray to you. They should pray while they still have the chance. Then, when danger comes to them like a river of deep water, they will still be safe." 
Psalms32:7 Yes, Lord, you are a place where I can hide. You will keep me safe from trouble. I will hear happy songs around me, because you save your people from danger." 
Psalms32:8 The Lord says, ‘I will teach you the right way to live. I will be your guide and I will take care of you." 
Psalms32:9 Do not be like a silly horse, or a mule. You have to use a rope to show them which way to go. Without that, they will not obey you.’" 
Psalms32:10 Wicked people will receive a lot of trouble and pain. But if anyone trusts in the Lord, they will know that God always loves them." 
Psalms32:11 So all you people who belong to the Lord, praise him and be happy! All of you who are good, honest people, shout aloud because you are so happy!" 
Psalms33:1 You righteous people, sing to praise the Lord and be happy! It is right for good, honest people to do that." 
Psalms33:2 Pick up your harp and praise the Lord! Make music for him with a lyre that has ten strings. 
Psalms33:3 Sing to him a new song! Make beautiful music, as you sing aloud to praise him." 
Psalms33:4 Yes! The Lord 's message is true. We can always trust him to do what is right. 
Psalms33:5 He loves everything that is right and fair. The world is full of his faithful love. 
Psalms33:6 The word of the Lord made the heavens. When he spoke, all the stars in the sky appeared." 
Psalms33:7 He put the water of the sea together in a heap. He made the deep seas stay in their right places. 
Psalms33:8 Everyone who lives on the earth should respect the Lord. They should bend down to worship him. 
Psalms33:9 The Lord spoke, and the world began! He spoke his command, and everything became fixed in its place." 
Psalms33:10 Nations decide what they want to do, but the Lord causes their plans to be useless." 
Psalms33:11 But whatever the Lord decides to do will always happen. His thoughts will not change from one century to the next. 
Psalms33:12 The Lord blesses the nation that has him as their God. He has chosen them to belong to him, as his own special people." 
Psalms33:13 The Lord looks down from heaven. He sees every man, woman and child." 
Psalms33:14 From the place where he lives, he watches everyone who lives on the earth." 
Psalms33:15 He made each person and all their thoughts. He knows everything that they do. 
Psalms33:16 A great army will not save their king. A soldier does not stay alive because he is strong. 
Psalms33:17 In war, do not hope that even a strong horse will help you to win. You cannot trust it to save you." 
Psalms33:18 Remember, the Lord takes care of people who respect him. Those people trust him for his faithful love." 
Psalms33:19 They trust him to keep them safe from death, and to keep them alive during a time of famine." 
Psalms33:20 Yes! We will trust the Lord to come and help us. He is our helper who keeps us safe, like a soldier's shield." 
Psalms33:21 We are happy because he loves us. We trust in his holy name. 
Psalms33:22 Lord, help us to know your faithful love, because we are trusting in you." 
Psalms34:1 I will always praise the Lord. All the time I will sing to praise him. 
Psalms34:2 I will boast that the Lord is great! I want weak people to hear that. I want them to be happy! 
Psalms34:3 Join with me to tell people that the Lord is great! Together we will make his name famous. 
Psalms34:4 I asked the Lord to help me, and he answered me. He saved me from everything that made me afraid." 
Psalms34:5 People who trust the Lord to help them will be very happy. Their faces will be bright, and they will not be ashamed." 
Psalms34:6 I was weak and helpless, so I called out to the Lord. The Lord heard me. He saved me from all my troubles." 
Psalms34:7 The Lord 's angel is very near to people who respect and obey the Lord. He keeps them safe from danger. 
Psalms34:8 Taste and see that the Lord is good! God blesses people who turn to him to keep them safe. 
Psalms34:9 All you people who belong to the Lord, continue to obey him. People who respect the Lord will have the things that they need." 
Psalms34:10 Even young lions may become hungry because they have no food to eat. But people who trust the Lord for help will receive every good thing that they need. 
Psalms34:11 Children, come and listen to me! I will teach you how to respect the Lord." 
Psalms34:12 Do you want to live a good life? Do you want your life to be long and happy? 
Psalms34:13 If so, do not speak any evil words. Do not tell lies to deceive people." 
Psalms34:14 Do good things instead of evil things. Try to bring peace into people's lives. 
Psalms34:15 The Lord watches over righteous people. He hears them when they call to him for help. 
Psalms34:16 But the Lord turns against people who do evil things. He causes everybody to forget that they ever lived. 
Psalms34:17 The Lord hears his righteous people when they call for help. He saves them from all their troubles. 
Psalms34:18 When people are upset, the Lord is near to them. He saves those who feel very weak and upset." 
Psalms34:19 Righteous people may often have troubles, but the Lord saves them from every danger." 
Psalms34:20 The Lord keeps them safe. Not even one of their bones is broken! 
Psalms34:21 Evil things will return to kill wicked people. Whoever hates God's own people will receive punishment. 
Psalms34:22 The Lord saves the lives of his servants. Everyone who trusts the Lord to keep them safe will not receive punishment as a guilty person. 
Psalms35:1 Lord, please attack those people who are attacking me. Fight against those people who are fighting against me." 
Psalms35:2 Yes, pick up your shield and put on your armour! Prepare to fight and give me help!" 
Psalms35:3 Take your spears and throw them, to stop the people who are chasing me. Say to me, ‘I will make you safe.’" 
Psalms35:4 Cause the people who want to kill me to become ashamed. Chase away the people who want to hurt me, so that they become confused." 
Psalms35:5 Make them become like chaff that the wind blows away. Send the angel of the to chase them away. 
Psalms35:6 Make their way dark and dangerous, as the angel of the Lord chases after them." 
Psalms35:7 They hid a trap to catch me. There was no reason for them to do that. They even dug a deep hole for me to fall into. 
Psalms35:8 Surprise my enemies and destroy them! Let their own traps catch them! Let them fall to their own death! 
Psalms35:9 Then I will be happy because of what the Lord has done. I will be happy because he has made me safe. 
Psalms35:10 I will shout with all my strength, ‘ Lord, who is like you? When strong people try to hurt weak people, you are the one who keeps the weak people safe. You also keep poor people safe when robbers try to take things from them.’" 
Psalms35:11 Cruel people tell lies against me in court. I do not even know what they are talking about. 
Psalms35:12 When I do good things to help them, they pay me back with evil things! That makes me feel very sad and upset." 
Psalms35:13 When they were ill, I wore sackcloth and I stopped eating food. I bent down my head as I prayed for them." 
Psalms35:14 I wept as I prayed, as I would weep when a friend or my mother had died." 
Psalms35:15 But when I had troubles myself, they were happy. They stood together and they laughed at me. Even strangers attacked me and they would not stop." 
Psalms35:16 Useless people like that insulted me, and they showed their teeth to frighten me." 
Psalms35:17 My Lord, how long will you continue to watch as they do this to me? Keep me safe when they attack me. They attack me like hungry lions. Do not let them kill me!" 
Psalms35:18 Then I will praise you in the great meeting of your people. I will thank you so that everyone hears me. 
Psalms35:19 My enemies tell lies about me. Do not let them win against me and be happy. They have no reason to hate me, so do not let them laugh at me." 
Psalms35:20 They do not talk in a way that brings peace. Instead, they try to deceive people who want to live quietly." 
Psalms35:21 They open their mouths wide to speak against me. They say, ‘Aha! We have seen the bad things that you do!’" 
Psalms35:22 Lord, you have seen all this. Do not remain silent, my Lord. Do not stay far away from me." 
Psalms35:23 Wake up! Get ready to give me justice! Show that I am not guilty, my Lord and my God." 
Psalms35:24 Lord, you are righteous. Give me justice, my God. Do not let them laugh at me." 
Psalms35:25 Do not let them say, ‘Aha! This is what we wanted.’ Do not let them say, ‘We have eaten him alive!’" 
Psalms35:26 Punish those people who are happy when I have troubles. Make them ashamed and confused! Bring down those proud people who laugh at me. Make them feel very ashamed. 
Psalms35:27 But for those who want justice for me, let them shout aloud in their pleasure. May they always say, ‘The Lord is great! He is happy when his servant is safe and well!’" 
Psalms35:28 Then I will tell everyone that you are righteous. I will praise you all the time. 
Psalms36:1 A wicked person turns against God completely. He does not respect God. 
Psalms36:2 He is too proud to think about his sin, so he does not hate it." 
Psalms36:3 He speaks wicked things to deceive people. He does nothing that is wise or good. 
Psalms36:4 While he lies on his bed at night, he thinks about bad things that he can do. He lives only to do things that are not good. He does not turn away from evil things." 
Psalms36:5 Lord your faithful love reaches as high as the sky. People can trust you beyond the clouds. 
Psalms36:6 Your justice is like the strongest mountains. You always do what is right, as sure as the seas are deep. You take care of people and animals and you keep them safe." 
Psalms36:7 God, your faithful love has great value! You keep all humans safe, like birds protect their babies under their wings." 
Psalms36:8 You have plenty of food in your house, to give them all that they need. You give them water from your river, that makes them happy when they drink it." 
Psalms36:9 Yes, you are the one who gives us light and life." 
Psalms36:10 Continue to love those who serve you as your people. Let good, honest people receive your justice." 
Psalms36:11 Do not let proud people hurt me. Do not let evil people chase me away from home. 
Psalms36:12 Now I see that those wicked people have fallen down! They are lying there on the ground, and they cannot get up again!" 
Psalms37:1 Do not become upset because of what wicked people do. Do not be jealous of people that do bad things. 
Psalms37:2 Remember that they are soon gone! They are like grass that quickly becomes dry. They will soon die like green plants in hot weather. 
Psalms37:3 Trust in the Lord and do good things. Then you will live safely in the land. 
Psalms37:4 Be happy that the Lord is your friend. He will give you what you most want. 
Psalms37:5 Let the Lord be your guide into the future. Trust in him and he will help you. 
Psalms37:6 He will show everyone that you are righteous, as clear as the light at dawn. People will know that you are a good person, as clearly as the sun shines at noon." 
Psalms37:7 Be patient as you wait for the Lord to help you. Trust him to bring help. Do not become upset when bad people seem to do well, when their wicked ideas really happen." 
Psalms37:8 Do not become angry or upset! It will only bring trouble for you. 
Psalms37:9 Wicked people will disappear one day! But people who trust the Lord for help will live safely in the land that he has given to them. 
Psalms37:10 Evil people will soon be gone. You may look for them, but you will not find them." 
Psalms37:11 Humble people will live safely in the land. They will live in peace and they will enjoy good things. 
Psalms37:12 Wicked people think about how to hurt righteous people. They show their teeth to frighten good people. 
Psalms37:13 But the Lord God laughs at those wicked people, because he knows that their day of punishment will come." 
Psalms37:14 Wicked people get out their swords, and they prepare to shoot their arrows. They try to make poor and helpless people fall down. They try to destroy people who do what is right." 
Psalms37:15 But their swords will go into their own hearts. Their bows will break in pieces. 
Psalms37:16 It is better to do what is right and only have a few things, than to be wicked and have many things." 
Psalms37:17 The Lord keeps righteous people safe, but he will destroy the power of wicked people." 
Psalms37:18 If anyone lives a completely good life, the Lord takes care of them. His gifts to them will last for ever." 
Psalms37:19 When times of trouble come, they will still be safe. In a time of famine, they will have enough to eat." 
Psalms37:20 But wicked people will die. They are the Lord 's enemies. They will disappear like smoke from a fire that burns them up. 
Psalms37:21 If you lend money to a wicked person, he will not give it back to you. But righteous people are kind to others and they give things freely." 
Psalms37:22 Yes, people that the Lord blesses will live safely in the land. But people that he has cursed will disappear." 
Psalms37:23 If the way that somebody lives pleases the Lord, the Lord will lead him into good things." 
Psalms37:24 Even if that person trips, he will not fall. The Lord will hold his hand." 
Psalms37:25 I was young once and now I am old. But in all my life, I have never seen any good man that God has left alone. I have not seen his children so hungry that they ask people for food." 
Psalms37:26 Good people are always kind. They lend things to other people. Their children also bring God's blessing. 
Psalms37:27 Turn away from evil things! Do what is right! Then you will live safely in the land for ever. 
Psalms37:28 The Lord loves justice. He will never leave those who serve him. He will take care of them for ever, but he will remove the children of wicked people." 
Psalms37:29 God's righteous people will take the land for themselves. They will live there for ever. 
Psalms37:30 Righteous people speak wise words. What they say brings justice. 
Psalms37:31 They think about God's Law all the time, so that they do not make mistakes." 
Psalms37:32 Evil people wait for a chance to kill righteous people. 
Psalms37:33 But the Lord will not leave his righteous people, so that their enemies have power over them. When evil people accuse them, the Lord will show that they are not guilty." 
Psalms37:34 Be patient and continue to trust the Lord. Obey his commands. Then he will give you honour, so that you live safely in the land. You will see that the Lord destroys wicked people." 
Psalms37:35 I have seen cruel, wicked people become very powerful, like trees that grow well in good soil." 
Psalms37:36 But soon after that, I see that they have disappeared! I looked for them, but I could not find them anywhere." 
Psalms37:37 Think about people who are honest and who do what is right. Those who help people to live in peace will live to enjoy their children. 
Psalms37:38 But God will destroy people that turn against him. Evil people will not have descendants. 
Psalms37:39 The Lord will make righteous people safe. He will take care of them in times of trouble. 
Psalms37:40 The Lord helps them. He saves them from wicked people. He keeps them safe because they turn to him for help. 
Psalms38:1 Lord, when you are angry, please do not punish me." 
Psalms38:2 You have shot your arrows deep into me. Your hand has pushed me down to the ground. 
Psalms38:3 Your punishment has made my whole body ill. My health has gone, because of my sin." 
Psalms38:4 My sin is too much for me! It is like a heavy weight, that I cannot carry." 
Psalms38:5 My wounds make a bad smell, and they get worse. This is because I have done stupid things." 
Psalms38:6 My body is bent down to the ground. Every day I weep because I am so sad. 
Psalms38:7 My body is hot with fever. I am very ill. 
Psalms38:8 The pain has made me tired and weak. I am so upset that I cry aloud. 
Psalms38:9 My Lord, you already know what I want most. You hear me when I cry with pain." 
Psalms38:10 My heart beats very fast, and my strength is leaving me. Even my eyes can no longer see properly." 
Psalms38:11 My family and my friends stay away from me, because of my illness. They do not come near to me." 
Psalms38:12 My enemies prepare traps, because they want to kill me. They talk about ways to destroy me. They want to hurt me, so they talk about that all through the day." 
Psalms38:13 But I am like a deaf man who hears nothing. I am like a dumb man who says nothing. 
Psalms38:14 So I live like a man who cannot hear what people say. I refuse to argue against them. 
Psalms38:15 Yes, Lord, I wait for you to help me! I believe that you will answer me, my Lord and my God." 
Psalms38:16 I have prayed that my enemies will not laugh at me. If I slip and fall down, they will be very happy!" 
Psalms38:17 I will soon fall down to the ground. I have nothing but pain all the time. 
Psalms38:18 But I agree that I have done wrong things. I am sorry about my sins. 
Psalms38:19 I have many enemies and they are strong. They have no reason to hate me. 
Psalms38:20 When I do good things, they do evil things to me in return! I try to do what is right, but they turn against me." 
Psalms38:21 Lord, please do not leave me alone. Do not stay far away from me, my God!" 
Psalms38:22 My Lord God, you are the one who can save me. Please come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms39:1 I said, ‘I will be careful what I do, so that I do not sin. I will also be careful what I say. When wicked people are listening, I will not even open my mouth.’" 
Psalms39:2 So I did not speak a word, even about good things. That made me very upset." 
Psalms39:3 It was like a fire inside me! As I thought more about it, I just had to say something! So I said this:" 
Psalms39:4 ‘ Lord, tell me about my life. Tell me how long it will be, and when it will finish. I need to remember that life is only short." 
Psalms39:5 Yes, you have made the days of my life only a few! The time that I live seems very short to you. Human life is only a breath, even for those who seem to be strong." 
Psalms39:6 As we live, we are just like shadows. We work to get things for ourselves. We may get a lot of money, but we do not know who will have it after we die.’" 
Psalms39:7 So, Lord God, what help can I hope to get? You are the only one that I can really trust!" 
Psalms39:8 Save me from all my sins! Do not let fools insult me. 
Psalms39:9 I will not say anything more. I will not open my mouth, because it is you who have punished me!" 
Psalms39:10 Please do not hurt me any more! You have already punished me enough, so that I am almost dead!" 
Psalms39:11 When people do wrong things, you warn them and you punish them. You destroy their valuable things, like a moth eats cloth! Yes, our lives are only a breath!" 
Psalms39:12 Lord, please hear my prayer. Listen to me when I call to you for help. Do not turn away from me when I weep. I need your help, like a foreign person who lives away from his home. I live on earth as a stranger, as all my ancestors did." 
Psalms39:13 Stop being angry with me, so that I can be happy again before I die." 
Psalms40:1 I was patient while I waited for the Lord to help me. He turned towards me, and he heard me when I called to him." 
Psalms40:2 He lifted me out of the deep hole of danger. He picked me up out of the mud. He put my feet on a rock. Then I could walk and not slip. 
Psalms40:3 He gave me a new song to sing. It was a song to praise our God. Many people will see what God has done to help me. Then they will respect and obey him. Yes, they will trust in the Lord." 
Psalms40:4 If anyone trusts in the Lord he blesses them! They do not ask proud people to help them. They do not join with those people who worship false gods. 
Psalms40:5 Lord, my God, you have done many great things. You have done so many things for us that we cannot count them! There is nobody else like you! I want to talk about the things that you have done, but there are too many things to speak about!" 
Psalms40:6 You do not really want sacrifices and offerings. I understand that! You do not ask for burnt offerings or sin offerings. 
Psalms40:7 Then I said, ‘Here I am, my God! The message that is written in the book tells me what I must do." 
Psalms40:8 I want to do what pleases you. Your Law is always in my thoughts.’ 
Psalms40:9 In the great meeting of your people, I have told them about your justice. I cannot keep quiet! You know that this is true, Lord." 
Psalms40:10 I have not hidden the good news about your justice. I have told everyone that they can trust you, and that you have the power to save. I tell the great meeting of your people about your faithful love." 
Psalms40:11 You, Lord, will continue to be kind to me. Please continue to keep me safe, because of your faithful love." 
Psalms40:12 There are many dangerous troubles all around me. They are too many to count! My sins are too strong for me. They are more than all the hairs on my head! So I am weak and I cannot see clearly. 
Psalms40:13 Lord, please save me! Lord, come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms40:14 Chase away the people who want to kill me! Cause them to become ashamed and confused. Chase away the people who want to hurt me, so that they run away in shame." 
Psalms40:15 When people laugh at me, make them sorry for what they have done." 
Psalms40:16 Lord, may those who come to you be very happy, because you have saved them. They should never stop saying, ‘ Praise the Lord!’" 
Psalms40:17 But I am poor and weak, my Lord. Please continue to think about me. You are the one who helps me and who saves me. My God, come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms41:1 If someone is kind to poor people, God blesses him. When he is in trouble, the Lord rescues him." 
Psalms41:2 The Lord keeps him safe. The Lord saves his life! God causes him to be happy here in the land where he lives. The Lord will not put him under the power of his enemies. 
Psalms41:3 The Lord takes care of him when he is ill in bed. Whenever he is ill, you will make him completely well again." 
Psalms41:4 I said, ‘ Lord be kind to me and forgive me. I have done wrong things against you, but please make me well again.’" 
Psalms41:5 My enemies say cruel things about me. They say, ‘Surely he must die soon! Then everyone will forget about him.’" 
Psalms41:6 When they come to see me, they say that they are my friends. But they think of ways to make me ashamed. When they leave, they tell everyone bad things about me." 
Psalms41:7 All the people who hate me insult me to other people. My enemies make plans to hurt me. 
Psalms41:8 They say, ‘He has a dangerous disease. He will never get up from his bed.’" 
Psalms41:9 Even my special friend has turned against me. He was someone that I trusted. I often ate food with him in my home. 
Psalms41:10 But you, Lord, please be kind to me. Raise me up to be well again. Then I can punish my enemies as they have punished me." 
Psalms41:11 They have not won against me, so I know that you are pleased with me!" 
Psalms41:12 You continue to help me because I do what is right. You will let me stay near to you for ever. 
Psalms41:13 Let us all praise the Lord, who is Israel's great God! Yes, praise him now and for ever! Amen! Amen!" 
Psalms42:1 God, I need you so much! I look for you, as a thirsty deer looks for streams of water." 
Psalms42:2 Deep down inside me, I am very thirsty! You are the God who lives for ever, and I need life from you! When will I be able to come near to you and see you face to face?" 
Psalms42:3 All day and all night I weep. My tears are my only food! My enemies say to me all the time, ‘Where is your God?’" 
Psalms42:4 When I remember how my life was before, I am very upset and I weep. Once I walked with a big crowd of people to worship God in his temple. I led the people there as we sang happy songs, and we praised God with loud voices." 
Psalms42:5 Now I ask myself, ‘Why am I so sad and upset?’ I must wait patiently for God to help me. Then I will praise my God once again, because he is the one who saves me." 
Psalms42:6 Deep inside me I am very sad. So I will turn to you, my God, from where I am. I will pray to you from Mount Hermon where the Jordan River begins, and from Mount Mizar." 
Psalms42:7 I hear your waterfalls make a noise like thunder. It seems like all that water is pouring over me and it knocks me down to the ground. 
Psalms42:8 Each day, the Lord shows me that he loves me with his faithful love. Each night he gives me a song to sing, as I pray to the God who lives for ever." 
Psalms42:9 I say to God, who is my high rock, ‘Why have you forgotten me? Why must I be so sad? How much longer must I continue to weep, because my enemies do cruel things to me?’" 
Psalms42:10 My enemies laugh at me all the time. It is like they are breaking all my bones. They are always saying, ‘Where is your God?’" 
Psalms42:11 I ask myself again, ‘Why am I so sad and upset?’ I must wait patiently for God to help me. Then I will praise my God once again, because he is the one who saves me." 
Psalms43:1 My God, show that I am not guilty. Make the people who do not respect you know that I am right. Rescue me from the evil people who tell lies about me." 
Psalms43:2 God, you are the safe place where I can hide. Why have you pushed me away? How much longer must I continue to weep, because my enemies do cruel things to me?" 
Psalms43:3 Send me your light and your truth. Let them be my guide. They will take me back to your holy mountain and to the house where you live. 
Psalms43:4 Then I will go to your altar, to worship you, the God who makes me happy. You are my God and I will praise you there. I will sing to thank you, God, with my harp." 
Psalms43:5 I ask myself, ‘Why am I so sad and upset?’ I must wait patiently for God to help me. Then I will praise my God once again, because he is the one who saves me." 
Psalms44:1 God, we have heard it for ourselves. Our ancestors told us what you did for them a long time ago." 
Psalms44:2 You used your power to chase away the other nations, so that our ancestors could live in their land. You punished those nations, and you helped our ancestors to be free." 
Psalms44:3 Our ancestors did not get the land by the power of their own swords. Their own strength did not win the fight. It was your own power and strength that did it! You were kind to them because you were their friend. 
Psalms44:4 God, you are my king! You command that Jacob's descendants must win against their enemies." 
Psalms44:5 Because of your power, we can knock down our enemies. Because of your strength, we can beat them down." 
Psalms44:6 I do not trust in my bow and arrows. It is not my sword that has helped me to win the fight. 
Psalms44:7 It is you who rescues us from our enemies. You bring shame on the people that hate us. 
Psalms44:8 Every day, we praise you, God. We will always thank you, all the time, because you are great!" 
Psalms44:9 But now you have turned against us. You have made us ashamed. When our armies go out to fight a battle, you no longer go with us." 
Psalms44:10 You make us run away from our enemies. The people who hate us take from us whatever they like! 
Psalms44:11 You have put us under their power, so that they kill us like sheep for them to eat. You have sent us away to live in foreign countries." 
Psalms44:12 You have sold your own people, for almost nothing! They had no value to you." 
Psalms44:13 The people who live in countries near us do not respect us. They laugh at us and they insult us. 
Psalms44:14 The other nations tell jokes about us. They think that we are nothing. 
Psalms44:15 I feel ashamed all the time. I do not know where to look. 
Psalms44:16 This is because my cruel enemies are always insulting me. All they want is to hurt me. 
Psalms44:17 We have not turned away from you, but all this has happened to us. We have not turned against the covenant that you made with us." 
Psalms44:18 We have continued to serve you and we obey your commands. 
Psalms44:19 But you have broken us in pieces. You have left us for wild dogs to eat. You have covered us with the darkness of death. 
Psalms44:20 We might have turned against you, our God, and worshipped a strange god." 
Psalms44:21 But if we had done that, you would have discovered it. You know even our most secret thoughts." 
Psalms44:22 But, because we are your people, God, people try to kill us all the time. They think that we are like sheep and we are ready to be killed." 
Psalms44:23 Lord God, do something! Why are you sleeping? Wake up! Do not turn away from us for ever!" 
Psalms44:24 Why do you turn away from us? Why do you forget our troubles and the way that people hurt us? 
Psalms44:25 We have fallen down in the dirt. Our bodies are lying on the ground. 
Psalms44:26 Wake up and help us! Save us, because of your faithful love." 
Psalms45:1 This beautiful song makes me feel very happy. I will sing this special poem to the king. My voice is ready to sing it very well, as well as a clever writer can write with a pen." 
Psalms45:2 You are the most handsome man that there is. You speak kind words in a beautiful way. And so God will continue to bless you for ever. 
Psalms45:3 Brave soldier, wear your sword at your side! Show what a great king you are!" 
Psalms45:4 Wear your royal clothes and ride out to win against your enemies! Win the war on behalf of truth and justice! Then your strong right hand will do powerful things! 
Psalms45:5 Your sharp arrows will cut into the heart of your enemies. Nations will fall down under your feet. 
Psalms45:6 God, you will continue to rule as king always and for ever. Your authority as king will bring justice to your people." 
Psalms45:7 You love people to do what is right, and you hate anything that is evil. So God, your God, has chosen you to be greater than all your people. He has put special olive oil on you, to make you happy." 
Psalms45:8 All your clothes have the beautiful smell of myrrh, aloes and cassia. You hear the music of stringed instruments that comes from beautiful palaces. That makes you happy." 
Psalms45:9 The daughters of kings are among your visitors. Your royal bride stands at your right side. She is wearing gold from Ophir. 
Psalms45:10 Royal daughter, listen to me. Listen carefully to what I am saying. Forget your people and your family." 
Psalms45:11 You are very beautiful and the king will want to be with you. He is your master, so respect him." 
Psalms45:12 Rich people will come from Tyre to give gifts to you. They will want you to help them. 
Psalms45:13 The king's bride is in the palace, and she looks beautiful! Her dress has gold threads in it." 
Psalms45:14 Now they lead her to the king in her beautiful clothes. Her young friends follow after her to bring her to the king. 
Psalms45:15 They are very happy as they walk together and they go into the king's palace. 
Psalms45:16 You will have sons to become kings, as your ancestors were kings before you. You will make them princes to rule over all the land." 
Psalms45:17 In future years I will cause people to remember that you are a great king! Then people will praise you always and for ever. 
Psalms46:1 God is a strong place for us, where he will keep us safe. He will always help us when troubles come." 
Psalms46:2 So even when the earth shakes, we will not be afraid. And when the mountains fall down into the deep sea, we will still not be afraid." 
Psalms46:3 If the sea makes dangerous waves, so that even the mountains shake, we will never be afraid." 
Psalms46:4 There is a river that runs into the city of God, and it brings God's blessing to the people there. That is the holy place where the Most High God lives." 
Psalms46:5 God is in that city. Nothing can ever destroy it. When dawn comes, God will be there to help it." 
Psalms46:6 Nations are shouting with anger! Kingdoms shake and fall. God calls in a loud voice, and even the earth melts!" 
Psalms46:7 The Lord Almighty is there to help us. The God of Jacob keeps us safe. 
Psalms46:8 Come and see the great things that the Lord has done. He has brought terrible things to the earth! 
Psalms46:9 All over the world, he causes wars to finish. He destroys the soldiers' bows and spears. He burns their shields in a fire." 
Psalms46:10 He says, ‘Be still and quiet! Understand that I am God. I rule as king over the nations. All the world will say that I am great.’"

Psalms47:2 The Lord, the Most High God, is wonderful. He is the great King who rules over the whole earth." 
Psalms47:3 He has won the battle against our enemies, and those nations are now under our power." 
Psalms47:4 He chose a special land to belong to us. Jacob's descendants are proud of that land. They are his own people that he loves. 
Psalms47:5 God has gone up to sit on his throne, while the people shout aloud. The Lord has gone up there, while the trumpets make a loud noise."

Psalms47:8 God rules over the nations. God is sitting on his holy throne.

Psalms48:1 The Lord is great. He deserves people to praise him in the city of our God, on his holy mountain." 
Psalms48:2 That place is high up and it is beautiful. It brings joy to the whole world. Mount Zion is the city of the great King. It is where the true God lives. 
Psalms48:3 God is there in its strong places and he keeps the city safe. 
Psalms48:4 Look! There are kings of other nations who join together to attack our city. 
Psalms48:5 But when they come near and they see it, they are very surprised. It frightens them so much that they quickly run away!" 
Psalms48:6 They cannot stop themselves shaking, like a woman who is giving birth to a baby!" 
Psalms48:7 You push them away, like an east wind that destroys great ships." 
Psalms48:8 We had heard about the great things that God has done. Now we have seen them for ourselves, here in the city of the Lord Almighty, the city of our God. It is God who keeps the city safe for ever!" 
Psalms48:9 When we are in your temple, God, we think about your faithful love." 
Psalms48:10 You are famous all over the world, so that people praise you as you deserve. You are powerful and you rule with justice." 
Psalms48:11 Because you judge people in a right way, Mount Zion and all the towns of Judah will be happy." 
Psalms48:12 Walk all around Zion and look at it. Count the towers. 
Psalms48:13 Look at its strong buildings and its strong walls. Then you will be able to describe it to your children. 
Psalms48:14 Yes, this is our God, who is with us for ever! He will always be our guide." 
Psalms49:1 Listen to this, people from every nation. Yes, listen carefully, everyone who lives in this world!" 
Psalms49:2 All people must listen, whoever you are, whether you are rich people or poor people." 
Psalms49:3 I will speak wise words to you. When you know my thoughts, you will understand things better." 
Psalms49:4 I have studied proverbs. I explain what they mean as I make music on my harp. 
Psalms49:5 When trouble comes, why am I afraid? When cruel people are all around me, and they want to hurt me, why am I afraid?" 
Psalms49:6 Those people think that their money will keep them safe. They are proud about their riches. 
Psalms49:7 Nobody can pay the price to save another person. Nobody can pay God the price for their life. 
Psalms49:8 A human life costs too much to buy it back. Nobody could ever pay enough. 
Psalms49:9 So nobody can live for ever. Everyone goes to their grave one day. 
Psalms49:10 Surely you see that even wise people die, in the way that fools and stupid people also die. Then their money will belong to someone else." 
Psalms49:11 Their graves will be their homes for ever. They had their own land, which was called by their name," 
Psalms49:12 but people do not live for ever, even if they are very rich. They will die, just like the animals." 
Psalms49:13 The same thing will happen to fools, and to those who copy their way of life." 
Psalms49:14 They will follow one another like sheep. Death will be their shepherd that leads them into their graves. But when the day arrives, righteous people will rule over them. They will no longer live in their great houses. Death will destroy their bodies in their graves." 
Psalms49:15 But God will save my life from the power of death. He will take me to a safe place with him. 
Psalms49:16 If somebody becomes rich and he has more and more things, do not be afraid!" 
Psalms49:17 He cannot take anything with him when he dies. His riches cannot go with him into death. 
Psalms49:18 While he lived, he was happy with his life. He thought, ‘People will praise me, because I am rich.’" 
Psalms49:19 But he will join his ancestors in death. He will never see the light of day again. 
Psalms49:20 Rich people do not always understand the truth. They will die, just like the animals." 
Psalms50:1 God, the great God, the Lord, is speaking. He is calling everybody on the earth, from where the sun rises in the east, to where it goes down in the west." 
Psalms50:2 God is shining out from Zion, that most beautiful place." 
Psalms50:3 Our God is coming, and he does not come quietly! A fire burns up everything that is in front of him. There is a great storm all around him." 
Psalms50:4 He calls to the skies above, and to the earth below. They must watch while he judges his people." 
Psalms50:5 He says, ‘Bring my people to me. They are the people who offered sacrifices to me, to agree our covenant.’" 
Psalms50:6 The skies above show that God is righteous, and God himself is the judge." 
Psalms50:7 He says, ‘Listen to me, my people, and I will speak to you. I have something to say against you, Israel. I am God, your God." 
Psalms50:8 You offer sacrifices and burnt offerings to me all the time but that is not why I speak against you. 
Psalms50:9 I do not need a bull from your farm, or goats from your fields." 
Psalms50:10 All the wild animals of the forest belong to me! All the cows on a thousand hills belong to me! 
Psalms50:11 I know every bird in the mountains, as well as all the insects." 
Psalms50:12 Even if I did become hungry, I would not tell you. All the world belongs to me, and everything that is in it." 
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the meat of bulls? No! Do I drink the blood of goats? No! 
Psalms50:14 Say “thank you” to me as your offering. Do whatever you have promised to do for me, the Most High God." 
Psalms50:15 Pray to me when you are in trouble. Then I will rescue you, and you will praise me.’" 
Psalms50:16 But God says this to the wicked people: ‘It is useless for you to speak about my commands, or about my covenant." 
Psalms50:17 For you hate my teaching, and you refuse to accept my message." 
Psalms50:18 If you see a robber, you run to help him! You become friends with adulterers." 
Psalms50:19 You speak about evil things, and you are always telling lies." 
Psalms50:20 You say bad things about your own brother, and you speak against your own family!" 
Psalms50:21 You did all these things, and I said nothing. So you thought that I was as bad as you are. But now I am warning you! I will tell you clearly what you have done wrong." 
Psalms50:22 So think carefully about this, all you people who have turned away from me. If not, I will tear you into pieces, and nobody will be able to save you." 
Psalms50:23 If anyone wants to praise me, they should say “thank you” to me as their offering. If anyone obeys my commands, I will show them my power to save people.’" 
Psalms51:1 Please forgive me, God, because of your faithful love. Because you are so kind, please clean away my sins." 
Psalms51:2 I have done bad things, so wash me to make me clean again." 
Psalms51:3 I know that I have not obeyed you. I cannot forget that I am guilty. 
Psalms51:4 It is you that I have done bad things against, only you! I have done things that are evil, as you know. And so you are fair when you speak against me. You are right when you say that I am guilty." 
Psalms51:5 Yes, even from the day I was born I have done bad things. I have always been guilty of sin." 
Psalms51:6 But you want me to be completely good and honest. You want to teach me to be wise in the way that I live. 
Psalms51:7 Splash water over me with a hyssop plant, and I will be really clean. Wash me and I will be whiter than snow." 
Psalms51:8 Please forgive me so that I can be happy. Then my whole body will feel well again. 
Psalms51:9 Hide your face from my sins. Clean away all the bad things that I have done. 
Psalms51:10 God, please make me completely pure, deep inside me. Make my spirit strong and true." 
Psalms51:11 Do not send me away from you. Do not take your Holy Spirit away from me. 
Psalms51:12 Give me joy again, because you have made me safe. Make me strong and ready to obey you." 
Psalms51:13 Then I will teach your ways to people who have turned against you. Then sinners will turn back and they will obey you. 
Psalms51:14 God, I am guilty of murder. Please forgive me! You are the one who saves me. When you rescue me, I will sing aloud about your righteousness." 
Psalms51:15 Lord God, help me to speak, and then I will praise you." 
Psalms51:16 I would be happy to offer sacrifices to you, but that is not what you really want. You do not want a burnt offering from me." 
Psalms51:17 You want me to be humble and sorry. That is the sacrifice that you want. When people are humble and they turn away from their sins, you will not refuse them." 
Psalms51:18 God, please be kind and help Zion. Make the walls of Jerusalem strong again." 
Psalms51:19 Then you will accept our proper sacrifices. Our burnt offerings and sacrifices of whole animals will make you happy. Once again, people will offer bulls to you, on your altar." 
Psalms52:1 Strong man, why are you so proud about the evil things that you like to do? Remember that God's faithful love keeps me safe." 
Psalms52:2 Your tongue is like a sharp knife! You talk about how to hurt other people, You speak nothing but lies." 
Psalms52:3 You love to do evil things, not good things. You love to tell lies more than the truth." 
Psalms52:4 You love to say cruel words. You speak to deceive others. 
Psalms52:5 But God will knock you down, and he will break you into pieces. He will take you away from your home. He will remove you from this world where people live." 
Psalms52:6 Righteous people will see what has happened, and they will be afraid. Then they will laugh at the wicked person, and they will say," 
Psalms52:7 ‘Look! This man did not trust God to keep him safe. Instead, he trusted in his riches. He thought that he could be cruel, and that would keep him safe.’" 
Psalms52:8 But I am like a strong olive tree that grows in the house of God. I will always trust in God's faithful love for his people. 
Psalms52:9 I will always praise you, God, for what you have done. I will trust you to help me, together with all those who serve you." 
Psalms53:1 A fool thinks that there is no God. People like that are bad. They do evil things. None of them does anything that is good. 
Psalms53:2 God looks down from heaven at the people on earth. He wants to see if there is anyone who is really wise and who wants to know God. 
Psalms53:3 But they have all turned away. They have all become evil. Nobody does anything that is good, not even one person." 
Psalms53:4 People who do wicked things understand nothing. They destroy my people as easily as they eat bread. They never pray to God. 
Psalms53:5 But now they are very afraid when there is nothing to be afraid of! God has completely won against his enemies, the people that attack you, his people. God has turned against them, so you are able to make them ashamed!" 
Psalms53:6 God, you are the one who saves your people, Israel! Leave your home in Zion and make your people strong again! When God blesses his people, Jacob's descendants will sing! Yes, Israel's people will be very happy!" 
Psalms54:1 God, you are great, so please help me! Use your power to show that I am right." 
Psalms54:2 God, hear my prayer, and listen carefully to what I am saying." 
Psalms54:3 Strangers are attacking me! Cruel people want to kill me. They do not respect you, God." 
Psalms54:4 But I know that God is the one who gives me help. My Lord God takes care of me. 
Psalms54:5 Pay back my enemies with their own evil. Please do what you have promised and destroy them. 
Psalms54:6 I choose to offer a sacrifice to thank you. Lord, you are always good, so I want to praise your name." 
Psalms54:7 Yes, God has saved me from all my trouble. When I looked at my enemies, I see that I have won against them." 
Psalms55:1 God, listen to my prayer. Do not turn away when I ask you to help me." 
Psalms55:2 Listen carefully and answer me. I am very upset because of all my troubles, which are too many for me." 
Psalms55:3 I am afraid because of what my enemies say. Those wicked people give me much pain. They are so angry that they hate me and they give me nothing but trouble. 
Psalms55:4 My heart beats fast inside me. Death seems very near, and I am very afraid." 
Psalms55:5 I am shaking with fear! My troubles are too much for me. 
Psalms55:6 So I say, ‘If I had wings to fly like a dove, I would fly away to a safe place." 
Psalms55:7 Yes! I would go far away, and I would live in the wilderness." 
Psalms55:8 I would go quickly to a safe place. The strong winds and storms of my enemies would not reach me there!’ 
Psalms55:9 My Lord God, make my enemies confused! Stop them from doing what they want to do. Everywhere that I look in the city, I see that they do cruel things to attack people." 
Psalms55:10 They walk on the city's walls in the day and at night. Inside the city they do evil things that bring trouble everywhere. 
Psalms55:11 They destroy everything! In all the city's streets, they move freely. They hurt people and they cheat them." 
Psalms55:12 But it is not my enemies that have insulted me. That would not have hurt me. If my enemy laughs at me, I can always hide from him!" 
Psalms55:13 But it is you, my best friend! We did things as men together, and I trusted you!" 
Psalms55:14 We enjoyed our time together. We often went to God's temple, and we joined with the crowd of people there." 
Psalms55:15 Please let death suddenly destroy my enemies! May they go down into their graves alive! They are at home with evil, and that is all that they think about." 
Psalms55:16 But I will call to God for help. The Lord will make me safe. 
Psalms55:17 I will weep and cry to him, morning, noon and night. He will listen to what I say." 
Psalms55:18 I have very many enemies who attack me all the time. But God will rescue me and keep me safe. 
Psalms55:19 God has always ruled everything as king. He will listen to me, and he will punish my enemies. They refuse to change their thoughts, and they do not respect God." 
Psalms55:20 But my friend has turned against his own friends. He has not been a true friend, as he promised to be." 
Psalms55:21 He speaks a message of peace, but he is really thinking about war. His words are easy to listen to, but they hurt like sharp knives." 
Psalms55:22 Take your problems to the Lord. He will take care of you. He will never let his own people fall. 
Psalms55:23 And as for wicked people, you, God, will send them to their graves. They will go down into the deep hole of death while they are still young. That is what will happen to those cruel people, who kill and cheat others. But I will trust in you, God."

Psalms56:2 They chase me all the time! Those proud people want to catch me, and there are many of them!" 
Psalms56:3 But when I am afraid, I will trust in you." 
Psalms56:4 By God's help, I will praise his word. By God's help, I will trust in him. I will not be afraid. People can do nothing to hurt me!" 
Psalms56:5 All the time, they bring trouble on me. They are always thinking of ways to hurt me." 
Psalms56:6 They hide and they watch me. They watch me wherever I go, and they hope to kill me." 
Psalms56:7 God, they want to do wicked things, so punish them! Destroy these people, because you are angry with them." 
Psalms56:8 Every time that I am upset, you know about it. Put all my tears in your bottle! Yes, write down every tear in your book." 
Psalms56:9 When I call to you for help, my enemies will turn back. I know that God is with me. He will fight for me." 
Psalms56:10 By God's help, I will praise his promise. Yes, by the Lord 's help, I will praise his word." 
Psalms56:11 I am not afraid, because I trust in God. People can do nothing to hurt me."

Psalms57:1 God, please be kind to me and help me! Please help me! I have come to you to keep me safe. Until the danger has gone, keep me near to you, as birds protect their babies under their wings." 
Psalms57:2 I will call to the Most High God to help me. He is the God who stands beside me. 
Psalms57:3 He will send help from heaven and he will save me. He will punish the people who attack me. God will show me his faithful love, and he will do what he has promised." 
Psalms57:4 My enemies are all around me, like hungry lions! I must lie down among wild animals that want to eat me! They are men who use spears and arrows to bite like teeth. They use their tongues to hurt people, like sharp swords." 
Psalms57:5 God, show how great you are, high above the sky! Let everybody on the earth see your glory!" 
Psalms57:6 My enemies have prepared a trap to catch me. That makes me very upset. They have dug a deep hole on the path where I walk. But they have fallen into it themselves! 
Psalms57:7 God, I have decided to trust you always. Yes, I will trust you! I will sing songs to praise you!" 
Psalms57:8 I will wake up now! I will pick up my harp and my lyre. I will make music as the sun rises! 
Psalms57:9 My Lord God, I will thank you in front of all the people. I will sing to praise you, so that all nations hear." 
Psalms57:10 Your faithful love is great. It is higher than the skies. Your truth reaches beyond the clouds. 
Psalms57:11 God, show how great you are, high above the sky! Let everybody on the earth see your glory!" 
Psalms58:1 Do you rulers really say what is right? Do you judge people in a fair way? 
Psalms58:2 No! You do not! You like to think of evil things to do. You make cruel things happen everywhere in the land. 
Psalms58:3 Wicked people do wrong things from their birth. As soon as they are born, they start telling lies." 
Psalms58:4 Their words hurt people like a snake's poison. They listen to nobody, like a deaf cobra." 
Psalms58:5 It does not obey the voice of the magician, even if he sings very well!" 
Psalms58:6 God, break the teeth of my enemies! Yes, Lord, break into pieces the teeth of those lions!" 
Psalms58:7 Cause them to run away like water and disappear. Cause them to die like dry grass in hot weather. 
Psalms58:8 Cause them to be like a worm that melts as it moves along. Cause them to be like a child that is born dead and never sees the light of day. 
Psalms58:9 Remove them quickly, more quickly than pots become hot over a fire of thorn bushes." 
Psalms58:10 When God punishes those wicked people, righteous people will be very happy. They will wash their feet in the blood of the wicked people!" 
Psalms58:11 People who see it will say, ‘It is true! God helps good people! There is a God who judges people on earth in a fair way.’" 
Psalms59:1 My God, please save me from my enemies! They are attacking me, so please keep me safe from them." 
Psalms59:2 Rescue me from those dangerous men, who are doing evil things." 
Psalms59:3 Look at them! They are waiting to kill me! Cruel men are ready to attack me. Lord, I have not done anything against them, but they still want to kill me." 
Psalms59:4 I have done nothing that is wrong, but they are preparing to attack me. Do something to help me! Look at what they are doing!" 
Psalms59:5 For you are the Lord God Almighty, the God that Israel serves. Get up and punish all the foreign nations. Do not forgive any wicked people who have turned against us." 
Psalms59:6 Those wicked people come back to the city every evening. They go from place to place, and they cry out like dogs." 
Psalms59:7 They shout evil things, and their words hurt like swords. They think, ‘Nobody can hear what we say!’" 
Psalms59:8 But Lord, you laugh at them. You laugh at all the foreign nations." 
Psalms59:9 I will wait for you to help me, because you are the one who gives me strength. You, God, are the strong place where I will be safe." 
Psalms59:10 Yes God, you love me with a faithful love, and you will come to help me. God will let me see that he has won against my enemies." 
Psalms59:11 Do not kill them yet, or my people will soon forget. Use your power to send them away without homes. Then make them fall! You are our Lord God, who keeps us safe like a soldier's shield." 
Psalms59:12 Those wicked people speak evil words. Let their own pride catch them in a trap, because they curse people, and they tell lies." 
Psalms59:13 Because you are angry with them, destroy them! Destroy them completely, so that they are no more. Then everybody will know that God rules in Israel, and he rules everywhere in the earth." 
Psalms59:14 Those wicked people come back to the city every evening. They go from place to place, and they cry out like dogs." 
Psalms59:15 They go from place to place and they look for food to eat. They will not rest until they are full. 
Psalms59:16 But I will sing to praise you. I will sing about your strength! In the morning, I will praise you for your faithful love. Because you are the strong place where I can hide. When I am in trouble, you keep me safe." 
Psalms59:17 You are the one who gives me strength. I will sing songs to praise you. You, God, are my strong, safe place. You are the God who loves me with a faithful love."

Psalms60:2 You made the ground shake and you tore it open. Now mend the holes, or everything will fall down." 
Psalms60:3 You have given your people a time of great trouble. You have given us strong wine to drink, that has made us fall like drunk people." 
Psalms60:4 But you have lifted up a flag for the people who serve you. They can meet together there, to be safe in the battle." 
Psalms60:5 Use your power to rescue us! Answer our prayers! Then the people that you love will be safe. 
Psalms60:6 God has spoken from his holy place! He has promised: ‘I will show my power! I will divide Shechem into separate pieces. I will measure Succoth Valley. 
Psalms60:7 Gilead belongs to me, and so does Manasseh. Ephraim is like my helmet for war. Judah has the authority of my royal sceptre." 
Psalms60:8 But Moab is the place where I wash my hands. Edom is my servant. I can throw my shoes at him! I will shout loudly, because I have won against the Philistines.’" 
Psalms60:9 Who will lead me into the strong city? Who will take me to fight and win against Edom? 
Psalms60:10 God, have you really turned against us? Will you not lead our armies into battle?" 
Psalms60:11 Help us to fight against our enemies, because help from men is useless!" 
Psalms60:12 With God's help, we will win! He will beat down our enemies for us."

Psalms61:2 I will call out to you from far away, because I am sad and weak. Lead me to a strong, safe rock that is higher than I am." 
Psalms61:3 You are the safe place where I can hide. You are like a strong tower that keeps me safe against my enemies. 
Psalms61:4 I want to live in your house for ever. I want you to keep me safe, as birds protect their babies under their wings." 
Psalms61:5 You, God, have heard my promises to you. You have blessed me, as you bless all those people who serve you." 
Psalms61:6 Please give the king a long life. Let him live to see many descendants. 
Psalms61:7 Let him rule for ever with your authority. Cause your faithful love and your truth to keep him safe. 
Psalms61:8 Then I will always praise your name. Every day I will give you the offerings that I have promised.

Psalms62:2 He alone is the strong rock where I can hide and be safe. With him, nothing will make me fall." 
Psalms62:3 How long will you all continue to attack me? I am just one man, and you want to kill me! You want to knock me down, like a weak wall or a fence that is falling down." 
Psalms62:4 You only want to push me off my high place. You love to tell lies to deceive people. When you say words to bless people, you are cursing them in your thoughts." 
Psalms62:5 I must be patient as I wait for God to help me. He is the only one that I can trust for help. 
Psalms62:6 He alone is the strong rock where I can hide and be safe. With him, nothing will make me fall." 
Psalms62:7 It is God who has kept me safe, and he has made me great. He is the strong place where I can hide and be safe." 
Psalms62:8 Yes, my friends, you should always trust in God! Tell him about all the troubles in your thoughts. God is our safe place to hide!" 
Psalms62:9 People are only as light as a breath! Whoever they are, they are not important. If you try to weigh them, all together, they weigh less than a breath of air." 
Psalms62:10 Do not cheat people, or rob them to get money! That will not give you anything that you can trust to help you. Even if you become very rich, do not think it is important." 
Psalms62:11 I have heard God speak his truth once, no twice! God has great strength." 
Psalms62:12 And also, my Lord God, you have a faithful love. You pay back to everybody what they deserve for the things that they have done."

Psalms63:2 When I have gone into your temple, I have seen what you are like. I have seen your great power and your glory." 
Psalms63:3 Your faithful love for me is better than life itself. So I will speak aloud to praise you. 
Psalms63:4 Yes, I will thank you for as long as I live. I will lift up my hands and I will pray to you." 
Psalms63:5 You make me very happy, better than a feast of good food. I will sing happy songs to praise you." 
Psalms63:6 When I lie on my bed, I think about you all through the night." 
Psalms63:7 You are the one who keeps me safe, as a bird protects its babies under its wings. So I sing happy songs." 
Psalms63:8 I keep very near to you and your strong right hand keeps me safe. 
Psalms63:9 My enemies are trying to kill me, but I know that they themselves will die. They will go down into the deep hole of death."
