Psalms64:2 When evil men think of ways to hurt me, hide me in a safe place. Those people work together as a group to do wicked things." 
Psalms64:3 Their tongues are like sharp swords that hurt people. Their cruel words are like arrows that they shoot at people. 
Psalms64:4 They hide in secret places, and they quickly shoot lies at good people. They are not afraid that anyone will punish them." 
Psalms64:5 They help each other to do evil things. They decide where they will hide their traps, and they say, ‘Nobody will see them!’" 
Psalms64:6 They think of wicked ideas to hurt people. They say, ‘That is a great idea!’ Nobody really understands what people think deep inside themselves." 
Psalms64:7 But God will punish them! He will suddenly shoot an arrow at them and it will hurt them a lot. 
Psalms64:8 Their own evil words will turn against them and make them fall. Everyone who sees them will shake their heads. 
Psalms64:9 People will be afraid. They will say, ‘Look at what God has done!’ They will think carefully about it all." 
Psalms64:10 But people who serve the Lord should be happy because of what he has done. They should trust him to keep them safe. Good, honest people should praise God, because he is great!" 
Psalms65:1 God, it is right for us to praise you in Zion. There, we will offer to you the gifts that we have promised." 
Psalms65:2 You answer our prayers. All of us must come to you," 
Psalms65:3 because of our many sins. Our sins are too heavy for us to carry, but you forgive the bad things that we do against you." 
Psalms65:4 You have blessed each person that you have chosen. You let them live near to you in your home. The good things that are in your holy temple will make us very happy! 
Psalms65:5 God, you are the one who saves us! You answer our prayers, and you do great things to rescue us. People from everywhere on earth trust in you, even those who live far away across the seas." 
Psalms65:6 You used your power to make the mountains, and you showed how strong you are." 
Psalms65:7 You stop the seas from being angry, so that their waters make no noise. You do the same with the angry nations." 
Psalms65:8 People who live far away see the great things that you have done. It makes them afraid. Everywhere on earth, east and west, people sing happy songs to praise you." 
Psalms65:9 You take care of the land. You send rain to make the earth good for plants. Your rivers are full of water. You cause the earth to give good crops so that the people have grain to eat. 
Psalms65:10 When people plough their fields, you send rain to make the earth wet. You make the ground soft with the rain, so that crops grow there." 
Psalms65:11 Every year at harvest time, you bless people with lots of good things. Everywhere that you go, there is plenty of food." 
Psalms65:12 Fresh grass covers the fields in the wilderness. There are plenty of good things all over the hills, so that people are very happy." 
Psalms65:13 The fields are full of sheep. The valleys are covered with crops. Everywhere seems to shout and sing for joy! 
Psalms66:1 Everyone on earth, shout aloud to praise God, because you are very happy." 
Psalms66:2 Sing to praise his great name! Praise him in the way that he deserves. 
Psalms66:3 Say to God, ‘You do great things! Your great power makes your enemies afraid. They come to you and they bend down to the ground." 
Psalms66:4 The whole world bends down to worship you. Everyone sings to praise your name.’ 
Psalms66:5 Come and see the great things that God has done. He has done wonderful miracles for people! 
Psalms66:6 He caused the Red Sea to become dry land. His people walked across the river! We praise him for what he did there! 
Psalms66:7 He has the power to rule for ever. He watches everything that the nations do. Wicked people should not be proud and turn against him. 
Psalms66:8 You people from every nation, agree that our God is great! Praise him with loud voices, so that everyone can hear!" 
Psalms66:9 He keeps our lives safe. He does not let us fall to the ground. 
Psalms66:10 You have tested us, God. You wanted to make us pure, as people make silver pure in a hot fire." 
Psalms66:11 You caused a trap to catch us, so that we suffered very much." 
Psalms66:12 You gave people power to hurt us. We went through dangerous fire and water. But then you brought us into a safe, open place." 
Psalms66:13 I will bring burnt offerings to your Temple. I will bring you the gifts that I promised. 
Psalms66:14 When I was in trouble, I made those promises. I spoke them aloud." 
Psalms66:15 I will offer you gifts of fat animals, to burn them as sacrifices. I will also burn sheep as offerings, and their smell will please you. I will also offer bulls and goats to you." 
Psalms66:16 Come and listen, all you people who serve God. I will tell you about the things that God has done for me." 
Psalms66:17 I called to him for help, then I praised him aloud!" 
Psalms66:18 If I had kept wicked things in my mind, the Lord God would not have listened to me." 
Psalms66:19 But God did hear me. When I called to him, he listened to my prayer." 
Psalms66:20 So I praise God, as he deserves. He has not refused to listen to me. He has continued to love me with his faithful love." 
Psalms67:1 God, please be kind to us and bless us. Look at us with a smile on your face." 
Psalms67:2 Then people on the earth will know what pleases you. People from all nations will know that you have the power to save them. 
Psalms67:3 May people everywhere praise you, God. Yes, may they all praise you!" 
Psalms67:4 May the nations be so happy that they sing aloud with joy! Because you are fair when you judge people, and you are a guide for all the nations of the world." 
Psalms67:5 May people everywhere praise you, God. Yes, may they all praise you!" 
Psalms67:6 The land has given us a harvest of food, and God, our God, continues to bless us." 
Psalms67:7 God, please bless us! Then all people everywhere will give you honour." 
Psalms68:1 God will rise up and he will chase his enemies away! The people who hate him will quickly run away from him! 
Psalms68:2 God, blow them away, as wind blows smoke away. Destroy those wicked people, as wax melts near a fire!" 
Psalms68:3 But righteous people will be happy, when they come near to you. They will shout with joy, because they are so happy!" 
Psalms68:4 Sing songs to God! Sing to praise his name! He is the one who rides on the clouds, so praise him aloud! His name is the Lord! Shout with joy to worship him!" 
Psalms68:5 God is a father for those who have no father. He takes care of widows. He rules from his holy home. 
Psalms68:6 God gives homes and a family to lonely people. He makes prisoners go free so that they are happy. But people who turn against him have to live in a hot, dry land." 
Psalms68:7 God, you led your people out. You took them across the desert." 
Psalms68:8 Then the ground shook, and rain poured down from the sky. You showed your power at Mount Sinai. You are the God who rules at Sinai. You are the God that Israel's people serve." 
Psalms68:9 God, you caused much rain to fall on your people's land. You made it fresh again when it had become dry." 
Psalms68:10 Your own people came to live in it. God, because you are kind, you gave many good things to poor people." 
Psalms68:11 The Lord God gives a command! Very many women take his message to other places. 
Psalms68:12 Kings with large armies quickly run away. Yes, they run away! The women at home share the valuable things that the armies have left behind." 
Psalms68:13 Even those who stayed to take care of the sheep found valuable things, like a dove with silver wings and bright gold feathers." 
Psalms68:14 When the Almighty God chased away foreign kings, it was like snow that fell on Mount Zalmon." 
Psalms68:15 Bashan is a very great mountain. It has many high rocks. 
Psalms68:16 You high mountains, why are you jealous when you look at Mount Zion? That is the mountain where God has chosen to live. Yes, that is where the Lord will live for ever!" 
Psalms68:17 God has millions of chariots. The Lord God has come from Mount Sinai to his own holy place. 
Psalms68:18 When you went up to the highest place, you took many prisoners with you. You received gifts from men, even from the men who had fought against you. Yes, the Lord God lives there!" 
Psalms68:19 Praise the Lord God, as he deserves! Every day he helps us with our troubles. He is the God who keeps us safe!" 
Psalms68:20 Our God is the God who rescues people. The Almighty Lord saves us from death. 
Psalms68:21 He will knock down the heads of his enemies. He will destroy their skulls and their long hair, if they continue to turn against him." 
Psalms68:22 The Lord God says, ‘I will bring my enemies back from Bashan, even from deep down in the sea." 
Psalms68:23 Then you can wash your feet in their blood. Your dogs can have as much as they want!’ 
Psalms68:24 God, you are my God and my King. People will see the great crowd that goes with you, as you go to your holy temple." 
Psalms68:25 The singers will march in front. The musicians will follow them. Young women will be with them as they beat their tambourines. 
Psalms68:26 They all sing, ‘Praise God, all you crowd of people! All you people of Israel, praise the Lord!’" 
Psalms68:27 Benjamin, the smallest tribe, will lead them all. The leaders of Judah, with their big group, will follow. Then will come the leaders of Zebulun and Naphtali." 
Psalms68:28 God has commanded that you should be strong. God, please show us again how strong you are, as you have used your strength to help us in past times." 
Psalms68:29 As you live in your temple in Jerusalem, kings will bring gifts to you." 
Psalms68:30 Speak against your enemies! Some are like wild animals that live in the reeds. Some are proud nations, like bulls among their young cows. They must be humble. They must offer gifts of valuable silver things to you. Chase away those nations that like to fight wars." 
Psalms68:31 Egypt's leaders will come with gifts. People will come from Cush and they will agree to serve God. 
Psalms68:32 All you kingdoms of the earth, sing to God! Yes, sing to praise the Lord God!" 
Psalms68:33 He is the one who rides in the sky, as he has done from the beginning of time. Listen! He shouts with a powerful voice." 
Psalms68:34 Agree that God is very powerful! He is the King who rules over Israel. He shows his power in the skies. 
Psalms68:35 God, how great you are, as you come from your holy temple! He is the God of Israel. He gives power to his people, and he makes them strong. Praise God as he deserves!" 
Psalms69:1 Please rescue me, God, because the deep water has come up to my neck." 
Psalms69:2 I will soon sink into deep mud. There is no safe place to put my feet. I am now in deep water, and the floods pour over me." 
Psalms69:3 I have shouted so much for help that I am tired and weak. My mouth is so dry that it hurts. I have looked so long for help from you, my God, that my eyes have become weak." 
Psalms69:4 Very many people hate me with no good reason. They are more than the number of hairs on my head! My enemies are too strong for me. They want to destroy me with their lies. They make me give back to them things that I did not take from them! 
Psalms69:5 You know, God, that I do foolish things. I cannot hide my sins from you." 
Psalms69:6 My Lord, Almighty Lord, Israel's God, do not let anyone who trusts you become ashamed because of me. Do not let anyone who serves you become ashamed because of me." 
Psalms69:7 Because I belong to you, people insult me. They make me feel ashamed." 
Psalms69:8 I have become like a stranger to my brothers. I am like a foreigner to my family. 
Psalms69:9 My love for your house burns inside me like a fire. Anybody who insults you, is also insulting me!" 
Psalms69:10 When I weep and I stop eating food, it causes people to insult me." 
Psalms69:11 When I wear sackcloth to show that I am sad, people laugh at me." 
Psalms69:12 The leaders at the city gate talk about me. People who are drunk make songs to sing about me. 
Psalms69:13 But as for me, Lord, I continue to pray to you. Please answer me at the right time. Be kind to me, God, because of your faithful love. Save me, as you have promised to do." 
Psalms69:14 Rescue me out of the mud and do not let me sink down into it. Save me from the people that hate me. Save me from the deep water. 
Psalms69:15 Do not let the floods pour over me. Do not let the deep sea cause me to drown. Do not let me fall into the deep hole of death. 
Psalms69:16 Please answer me, Lord, because your faithful love is great. Turn to hear me, because you are so very kind." 
Psalms69:17 Do not turn away from me, your servant. I am in great trouble, so please answer me very soon." 
Psalms69:18 Come very near to me, and make me safe. Rescue me from my enemies." 
Psalms69:19 God, you know that people are insulting me. They laugh at me to make me feel ashamed. You can see what all my enemies are doing." 
Psalms69:20 When they insult me, it makes me very upset. I feel helpless. I hoped that someone would be kind and help me, but there was nobody. I wanted someone to comfort me, but I did not find anyone." 
Psalms69:21 Instead, my enemies put poison in my food. When I was thirsty, they gave me vinegar to drink." 
Psalms69:22 Please God, cause their feasts to become a trap for them. Let it bring trouble to them and their friends." 
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be in the dark so that they cannot see. Let their bodies become weak. 
Psalms69:24 Yes, punish them, because you are so angry with them!" 
Psalms69:25 Let their homes become empty, so that nobody lives in them any more." 
Psalms69:26 Do this, because they chase after the people that you have already punished. They talk about the pain of the people that you have hurt." 
Psalms69:27 Make a note of all their sins. Do not ever forgive them, so that they are righteous." 
Psalms69:28 Remove their names from the book of living people. Do not write their names on the list of righteous people. 
Psalms69:29 But I have pain in my body and trouble in my mind. God, rescue me and keep me safe!" 
Psalms69:30 I will sing to praise God's name! I will thank him because he is great! 
Psalms69:31 That will please the Lord more than if I offer him an ox, or a great bull with horns." 
Psalms69:32 When humble people see that God has rescued me, they will be very happy. People who want to serve God will become strong in their lives." 
Psalms69:33 For the Lord listens to the prayers of poor people. He does not forget to help his people when they are prisoners. 
Psalms69:34 Heaven and earth should praise him! The seas and everything that lives in them should praise him! 
Psalms69:35 For God will rescue Zion's people. He will build Judah's cities again. Then his people will live there. The land will belong to them again. 
Psalms69:36 Then the descendants of his servants will have the land for themselves. The people who love God will live there safely. 
Psalms70:1 God, please save me! Lord, come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms70:2 Stop the people who want to kill me, so that they become ashamed and confused. Chase the people who want to hurt me, so that they run away in shame." 
Psalms70:3 When people laugh at me, make them ashamed for what they have done." 
Psalms70:4 God, may those who want to know you be very happy. Everyone who loves you because you have saved them should never stop saying, ‘ Praise God.’" 
Psalms70:5 But I am poor and weak, God. Please hurry to give me help. You are the one who helps me and who saves me. Lord, come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms71:1 Lord, I have come to you to keep me safe. Never let me become ashamed." 
Psalms71:2 You always do what is right, so please rescue me! Listen to me, and come to save me!" 
Psalms71:3 Be a rock where I can hide. Be a strong place where I can be safe. You are my high rock and my strong, safe place." 
Psalms71:4 My God, save me from the power of wicked people, so that those cruel people cannot catch me." 
Psalms71:5 My Lord God, I trust you to help me. I have trusted in you, Lord, since I was young." 
Psalms71:6 Since I was born, you have been there to help me. You have taken care of me since my mother gave birth to me. So I will always praise you!" 
Psalms71:7 When people see me, they see trouble! But you are a strong place to keep me safe." 
Psalms71:8 I can never stop praising you! All the time, I speak about your glory!" 
Psalms71:9 Do not chase me away when I am old. When I am no longer strong, do not turn away from me." 
Psalms71:10 For my enemies say things against me. They think of ways that they can kill me. 
Psalms71:11 They say, ‘God has turned away from him. Run after him and catch him, for nobody will rescue him.’" 
Psalms71:12 God, do not stay far away from me. My God, come quickly to help me!" 
Psalms71:13 Make my enemies become completely ashamed. They speak against me and they want to hurt me. So let them fall with great shame! 
Psalms71:14 But as for me, I will always wait for you to help me. I will continue to praise you more and more." 
Psalms71:15 I will tell people that you always do what is right. I will always speak about your power to save people. But you have done more than I will ever know about! 
Psalms71:16 I will tell about the great things that you have done, Almighty Lord. I will tell everyone that you are righteous. You, only you, are righteous." 
Psalms71:17 God, you have taught me since I was young. I still tell people about the great things that you have done." 
Psalms71:18 And even when I am old and my hair is grey, do not turn away from me, God. I need to tell the people still to be born about your great strength. My descendants need to know about your power." 
Psalms71:19 Your justice reaches beyond the sky above! You have done such great things, God. There is nobody like you!" 
Psalms71:20 You have caused me to have many bad troubles. But you will make my life strong again. You will lift me back out from this deep hole in the earth. 
Psalms71:21 You will make me great again, so that people respect me. You will turn back to me, so that I feel strong." 
Psalms71:22 I will pick up my harp and I will sing to thank you. I will praise you, my God, because you do what you promise. I will sing to praise you, the Holy One of Israel, with the music of a lyre." 
Psalms71:23 I will shout aloud with joy, as I praise you with music! I will sing songs to praise you, because you have rescued me." 
Psalms71:24 I will talk about your justice all the time. The people who want to hurt me will become confused and ashamed. 
Psalms72:1 God, please help the king to judge people in a right way. Help the king's son to be honest and fair, as you are." 
Psalms72:2 Then he will rule your people in a good way, so that even poor people receive justice." 
Psalms72:3 The mountains and the hills will bring peace, so that the people can live honest lives." 
Psalms72:4 Please help the king to judge poor people fairly. May he save the children of weak people, and destroy those cruel people who hurt them." 
Psalms72:5 May people always respect your authority, God, through all time, as long as the sun and the moon remain in the sky." 
Psalms72:6 May the king be a blessing to his people, like rain that falls on grass that lies in the fields. May he be like rain that brings water to the soil." 
Psalms72:7 While he rules as king, those people who serve God will have success. There will be peace in all the land, for as long as the moon remains in the sky." 
Psalms72:8 May the king rule everywhere, from sea to sea, and from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth." 
Psalms72:9 Then people who live in the desert will bend down to respect him. His enemies will fall down with their faces in the dust. 
Psalms72:10 Kings from Spain and from other far places will pay taxes to him. Kings from Sheba and from Seba will bring gifts to him. 
Psalms72:11 All kings will bend down in front of him. People from all nations will serve him. 
Psalms72:12 He will rescue the poor person who calls out to him for help. He will save weak people who have nobody to help them. 
Psalms72:13 He will be kind to people who are poor and weak. He will save them from death. 
Psalms72:14 He will rescue them from cruel people who hurt them. Their lives are important to him. 
Psalms72:15 Long live the king! May people come from Sheba to give gold to him. May people always pray for him. May people praise him all the time. 
Psalms72:16 May there always be plenty of grain that grows in the land. May the crops grow well on the tops of the mountains. May the fruit trees give a lot of fruit, as they do in Lebanon. May all the crops grow well, like grass that grows in a field." 
Psalms72:17 May the king be famous for ever! May people remember him for as long as the sun continues to shine. May people from all nations use his name when they bless one another. May they all say that God has blessed the king! 
Psalms72:18 Praise the Lord, Israel's God, as he deserves. Only he does such wonderful things." 
Psalms72:19 Praise his great name for ever! May his glory fill the whole earth! Amen! May that all happen! 
Psalms72:20 This is the end of the prayers of Jesse's son, David." 
Psalms73:1 God is truly good to the people of Israel, to people who want to serve him honestly." 
Psalms73:2 But this is what happened to me: I nearly slipped and fell over. I could no longer stand properly. 
Psalms73:3 When I saw that wicked people had plenty of things, I was jealous of those proud people." 
Psalms73:4 Their bodies are fat and strong, and they feel no pain in their lives." 
Psalms73:5 They do not have trouble like other people. They seem to have no problems. 
Psalms73:6 Their pride is like their necklace. They like to be cruel, as they like to wear clothes." 
Psalms73:7 Their greedy eyes lead them into sin. Their minds think of evil things to do. 
Psalms73:8 They laugh at people and they say bad things about them. They proudly speak about cruel things that they want to do to people. 
Psalms73:9 They think that they rule in heaven, as well as on the earth!" 
Psalms73:10 As a result, God's people become confused, and they listen to them. They think that everything is true that those wicked people say." 
Psalms73:11 They say, ‘Does God really know what we do? Does the Most High God know everything?’" 
Psalms73:12 Take a good look, and see how wicked those people are! They have no troubles, and they become richer and richer." 
Psalms73:13 I decided that I had tried to do what is right, but it did not help me at all! I had refused to do anything wrong, for no good reason!" 
Psalms73:14 I had trouble each and every day. Punishment came to me every morning! 
Psalms73:15 If I had spoken aloud things like that, I would have turned against your own people." 
Psalms73:16 When I thought about all this, it was so hard for me to understand." 
Psalms73:17 But I went into your temple, God. Then I understood what would happen to wicked people in the end." 
Psalms73:18 Yes, I realized that they are in a dangerous place, where they will easily slip. You will cause them to fall down to their death." 
Psalms73:19 It will take just a moment to destroy them! Great fear will sweep them away! 
Psalms73:20 They will completely disappear, like a dream disappears after you wake up. Lord God, when you decide to do something, you will think that they are nothing!" 
Psalms73:21 I was very upset, and my thoughts were confused." 
Psalms73:22 I was stupid, and I did not understand you properly. I was no better than an animal." 
Psalms73:23 But I am always near to you. You hold my right hand, as my guide." 
Psalms73:24 You teach me the right way to go. Finally, you will receive me with honour." 
Psalms73:25 You are the only one in heaven or on earth that I need to help me. You are the only one that I want. 
Psalms73:26 My body and my mind may become weak, but God will always keep me safe and strong." 
Psalms73:27 Those people who stay far away from you will die. You destroy everybody who turns away from you. 
Psalms73:28 But as for me, it is good for me to stay near to God. I have gone to the Almighty Lord as the place where I can be safe. I will tell people about the great things that you have done." 
Psalms74:1 God, have you turned against us for ever? Why are you so angry with us? We belong to you, so please take care of us, like a shepherd takes care of his sheep." 
Psalms74:2 Remember that we are your people. Long ago you chose us for yourself. You rescued us from being slaves, so that we could be your special nation of people. So please think about us, as well as your home on Mount Zion." 
Psalms74:3 Come and look at our buildings! Your enemies have completely destroyed them. They have also destroyed everything that is in your temple. 
Psalms74:4 Your enemies shout loudly inside your special meeting place. They have put up their own flags there. 
Psalms74:5 They used axes to cut the temple into pieces, like people who cut down trees!" 
Psalms74:6 They used their hammers and axes to break the beautiful wooden boards. 
Psalms74:7 They completely burned your temple. They knocked it down to the ground! They caused your special place to become useless! 
Psalms74:8 They said to themselves, ‘We will completely destroy them all.’ So they burned down every place in the land where people meet to worship God." 
Psalms74:9 We no longer see any signs that God is with us. There are no more prophets among us. Nobody can tell us how long this will continue. 
Psalms74:10 God, how long will our enemies laugh at you? Will they insult your name for ever?" 
Psalms74:11 Why do you do nothing to help us? Use your power now to punish them! 
Psalms74:12 God, you have been my king from the beginning. You have done great things to rescue us all over the earth." 
Psalms74:13 You divided the sea into separate pieces, because you are so strong. You broke the heads of the monsters in the waters of the sea." 
Psalms74:14 You broke the heads of Leviathan. You gave his body as food for the animals that live in the desert. 
Psalms74:15 You caused springs and streams of water to appear, and you made large rivers become dry!" 
Psalms74:16 Days and nights both belong to you. You put the moon and the sun in their places. 
Psalms74:17 You said where the land must be among the seas. You made both summer and winter. 
Psalms74:18 Lord, think about your enemies. They are always laughing at you. Foolish people are insulting your name." 
Psalms74:19 We, your people, are like your own special dove. Do not let wild animals destroy us! We are weak and helpless, so do not forget about us!" 
Psalms74:20 Remember what you have promised to do for us. There are dark places everywhere, where cruel people hide." 
Psalms74:21 We are weak, and people want to hurt us. Do not let us become ashamed. Instead, let the poor, weak people praise your name!" 
Psalms74:22 Do something, God! Show everyone that you are good. Think how fools are laughing at you all the time." 
Psalms74:23 Do not forget what your enemies are saying. They shout things against you all the time! 
Psalms75:1 God, we thank you! Yes, we thank you! You are very near to us. People everywhere say what great things you have done." 
Psalms75:2 God says, ‘I choose the time when I will judge people, and I will be a fair judge." 
Psalms75:3 When the earth shakes and the people in it are afraid, I will keep its foundations strong.’" 
Psalms75:4 I say to people who are proud, ‘Do not be proud.’ I say to wicked people, ‘Do not boast about your power." 
Psalms75:5 Do not boast against God. Do not speak with such proud words.’ 
Psalms75:6 Who has the power to judge people? Nobody from the east, or from the west, or from the desert can be judge." 
Psalms75:7 God is the one who judges people. He decides who to bring down and who to lift up. 
Psalms75:8 The Lord holds a cup of punishment in his hand. It is full of wine, with spices in it. The Lord pours out his anger from this cup. All the wicked people in the world will have to drink it. They will drink all of it, so that none remains." 
Psalms75:9 But as for me, I will always thank you, God. I will sing to praise you, the God of Jacob." 
Psalms75:10 You say, ‘I will destroy the power of wicked people. I will give strength to my righteous people.’" 
Psalms76:1 People in Judah know who God is. He is famous in Israel. 
Psalms76:2 His home is in Salem. Yes, he lives in Zion." 
Psalms76:3 There he destroyed all the weapons of our enemy, their arrows, their shields, and their swords. He destroyed everything that they use to fight wars." 
Psalms76:4 God, you are the one who shines with glory! You are greater than the greatest mountains." 
Psalms76:5 You won against brave soldiers, so that we robbed them of everything. Now they are sleeping, and they will never wake up! They are completely helpless." 
Psalms76:6 God of Jacob, when your enemies heard you shout at them, both the horses and their riders fell down dead." 
Psalms76:7 Yes, everyone is afraid of you, our God! When you are angry, nobody can stand against you." 
Psalms76:8 From heaven you said how you would judge people. Everybody on earth became afraid. They could not say anything! 
Psalms76:9 This happened when you came to punish wicked people. You came to rescue the weak and poor people in the land. 
Psalms76:10 Everybody will praise you, even those who are angry against you. You will show them how angry you really are." 
Psalms76:11 Make promises to the Lord your God, and do what you have promised. Everyone who serves God should bring gifts to him. We must respect him with fear." 
Psalms76:12 He makes proud rulers become humble. All the kings of the world should be afraid of him. 
Psalms77:1 I will call out to God for help. I will call aloud to God, and he will listen to me." 
Psalms77:2 When trouble came to me, I looked for the Lord to help me. All through the night, I lifted my hands to him while I prayed. But I did not feel peace in myself." 
Psalms77:3 When I think about God, I am sad and upset. I do not know if he will help me." 
Psalms77:4 God, you do not let me close my eyes to sleep. I am so upset that I cannot even speak." 
Psalms77:5 I think about my life long ago, in the years that have passed." 
Psalms77:6 At night, I remember the songs that I sang before. I think carefully about what is happening." 
Psalms77:7 I ask myself, ‘Will the Lord God always turn against me? Will he never be kind to me again?" 
Psalms77:8 Does he no longer have a faithful love for his people? Will he never do what he has promised us? 
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be kind and help us? Is he so angry that he will not be kind to us any more?’ 
Psalms77:10 Then I said, ‘This makes me very upset! Has the Most High God stopped helping us?" 
Psalms77:11 I will remember the things that the Lord has done. Yes, I will remember the miracles that you did long ago." 
Psalms77:12 I will think about everything that you have done. I will think carefully about all those great things.’ 
Psalms77:13 God, your ways are holy. There is no god as great as our God." 
Psalms77:14 You are the God who does miracles. You show the nations that you are very powerful. 
Psalms77:15 You used your power to rescue your people. Yes, you saved the descendants of Jacob and Joseph." 
Psalms77:16 The waters of the sea saw you, God. When they saw you, they shook with fear! Even the deepest seas were afraid." 
Psalms77:17 Rain poured down from the clouds. The noise of thunder was in the skies. Your arrows of lightning shot everywhere. 
Psalms77:18 The sound of your thunder was in the storm. Your lightning made all the world bright. The earth shook. 
Psalms77:19 You went through the sea. Your path went through the deep water, but nobody saw where your feet had gone." 
Psalms77:20 You led your people, as a shepherd leads his sheep. Moses and Aaron did that for you." 
Psalms78:1 My people, listen to what I am teaching you. Listen to the words that I speak." 
Psalms78:2 I will tell you something to make you wise. Then you will understand things that happened long ago. 
Psalms78:3 We have heard about these things and we know them. Our ancestors have told them to us," 
Psalms78:4 and we will not hide them from our children. We will tell our children and our grandchildren why they should praise the Lord. We will tell them about his strength, and about the great things that he has done." 
Psalms78:5 He gave his laws to Jacob's descendants, the Israelite people. He commanded our ancestors to teach them to their children." 
Psalms78:6 Then the children would know about them, even the children still to be born. When the time came, they too would tell their children." 
Psalms78:7 Then their family would trust in God. They would not forget what God had done. They would obey his commands. 
Psalms78:8 So they would not be like their ancestors, who turned against God. Those people refused to obey God, and they did not trust him to be their guide." 
Psalms78:9 The soldiers of Ephraim had bows and arrows, but they ran away when the battle started!" 
Psalms78:10 They did not obey the covenant that God had made with them. They refused to obey his laws. 
Psalms78:11 They forgot about what he had done for them. They forgot the miracles that he had shown them. 
Psalms78:12 He did miracles for their ancestors to see, in the region of Egypt that is called Zoan." 
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea into two separate parts, and he led them through it. He made a path between two walls of water." 
Psalms78:14 He showed them the way with a cloud in the day and with light from a fire all night. 
Psalms78:15 He broke rocks into pieces in the wilderness so that his people had plenty of water. Springs of water came from deep in the earth. 
Psalms78:16 He caused streams to pour from the rocks so that the waters ran like rivers. 
Psalms78:17 But his people continued to do bad things against him. In the wilderness, they turned against the Most High God." 
Psalms78:18 They decided to test God, to see what he would do. They demanded to have the food that they liked best." 
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God, and they said, ‘Can God give us food to eat here in the wilderness?" 
Psalms78:20 Yes, water did come out when he hit the rock. The water poured out like a river. But can he also give us food to eat? Can he supply meat for his people?’" 
Psalms78:21 When the Lord heard this, he was very angry. So he sent fire to punish his people. He became very angry against the Israelites." 
Psalms78:22 They did not trust God, that he had the power to save them." 
Psalms78:23 So God commanded the clouds high above to give food to his people. He caused the skies to open like a door. 
Psalms78:24 He poured down manna like rain, so that they could eat grain that came from heaven." 
Psalms78:25 In that way, people ate the special bread of angels! God gave them all the food that they could eat." 
Psalms78:26 Then God caused a wind to blow from the east. He used his power to send a south wind too. 
Psalms78:27 He caused meat to pour down on them, like dust from the sky. The birds covered all the ground like sand on the shore of the sea." 
Psalms78:28 God made the birds fall near the tents where his people were living. 
Psalms78:29 They ate as much as they could eat. Yes, God gave them what they had demanded!" 
Psalms78:30 But God became very angry with them, even before they had finished eating. While the food was still in their mouths, he killed the strongest men among them. Many of the best young Israelite men died." 
Psalms78:31  
Psalms78:32 Even when this happened, the people continued to do bad things. When God had done these miracles, they still did not trust him to help them." 
Psalms78:33 So he made their lives finish quickly, like a breath. They became very afraid." 
Psalms78:34 When God killed some of them, the other people turned to God. They were sorry and they prayed to him." 
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their strong Rock. They remembered that the Most High God was their Redeemer. 
Psalms78:36 But they deceived him with their words. What they said to him was lies. 
Psalms78:37 They did not really trust him. They did not obey the covenant that he had made with them. 
Psalms78:38 But God was kind to them, and he forgave them for their sins. He did not destroy them. Many times he was patient, and he did not become angry with them." 
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were weak and human, like a wind that blows away and does not return." 
Psalms78:40 When the Israelite people were in the wilderness, they often turned against God. That caused God to be very sad." 
Psalms78:41 Many times they tested God, the Holy One of Israel, so that he was very upset." 
Psalms78:42 They forgot about his great power, and how he rescued them from their enemies." 
Psalms78:43 He did powerful miracles in Egypt, in the region of Zoan." 
Psalms78:44 He caused the Egyptians' rivers to become blood, so that they could not drink water from them." 
Psalms78:45 He sent large numbers of flies to bite them. He sent frogs that destroyed their land. 
Psalms78:46 He caused locusts to eat their crops, and everything that grew in their fields." 
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail. He destroyed their fig trees with frost. 
Psalms78:48 He sent hail to kill their cows. He sent lightning to kill their other animals. 
Psalms78:49 He was so angry with them, that he sent terrible things to punish them. Great trouble came to them, like an army of his angels that bring death." 
Psalms78:50 Nothing could stop his anger. He did not save them from death. He let bad diseases kill them. 
Psalms78:51 He killed all the firstborn sons in Egypt. The strongest men of Ham's descendants died. 
Psalms78:52 But God led his own people safely out of Egypt. He took them through the wilderness, like a shepherd leads his sheep." 
Psalms78:53 They were safe with him as their guide, and they were not afraid. But their enemies drowned in the sea." 
Psalms78:54 He brought his people to the edge of his holy land. It was land with the mountain that he had taken with his power. 
Psalms78:55 He chased away the nations that were living there. He gave to each tribe their own piece of land, so that Israel's tribes could live there." 
Psalms78:56 But the Israelites continued to test the Most High God, and they turned against him. They did not obey his commands." 
Psalms78:57 They turned away from him and they deceived him, as their ancestors had done. They were useless, like a bow that could not shoot straight!" 
Psalms78:58 The places where they worshipped their idols made God very angry. 
Psalms78:59 God heard what they were doing, and he became very angry. He turned against the Israelite people." 
Psalms78:60 He went away from his home at Shiloh, the tent where he lived among people." 
Psalms78:61 He let Israel's enemies take away the Covenant Box, that showed his power and his glory." 
Psalms78:62 He was so angry with his own people, he let their enemies kill them in battle." 
Psalms78:63 Fire killed their young men in war. There was nobody for the young women to marry. 
Psalms78:64 Their priests died in battle. Their widows had no chance to weep. 
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord God woke up, like someone had woken him from sleep. He shouted loudly, like a soldier who has drunk a lot of wine." 
Psalms78:66 He chased his enemies away. He caused them to be ashamed for ever. 
Psalms78:67 He chose not to live among Joseph's descendants. He did not choose the tribe of Ephraim. 
Psalms78:68 Instead, he chose the tribe of Judah. He chose Mount Zion, the place which he loves." 
Psalms78:69 There he built a temple for himself, to be like his home in heaven. He made it to be strong, like the earth itself, so that it would always be there." 
Psalms78:70 Also, he chose his servant, David. He took David away from the fields," 
Psalms78:71 so that he no longer took care of his father's sheep. He became like a shepherd, to lead his people, Jacob's descendants. He ruled the people who belonged to God, the Israelite people." 
Psalms78:72 David took care of them with an honest mind. He was wise in the way that he led them. 
Psalms79:1 God, foreign nations have attacked your own land. They have caused your holy temple to become unclean. They have destroyed the buildings in Jerusalem." 
Psalms79:2 They have let birds come down and eat the dead bodies of your people. Your servants have become food for wild animals to eat. 
Psalms79:3 The blood of your servants has poured out all over the streets of Jerusalem. There is nobody left there to bury their bodies. 
Psalms79:4 The nations that live near to us do not respect us. They laugh at us and they insult us. 
Psalms79:5 Lord, how long will this continue? Will you be angry with us for ever? Will you always be angry, like a fire that continues to burn?" 
Psalms79:6 Please punish those nations that do not respect you. Be angry with the kingdoms that do not pray to you. 
Psalms79:7 They have destroyed Jacob's descendants and the land where they live. 
Psalms79:8 Our ancestors did bad things, but do not say that we are guilty because of that. Be kind and send help to us quickly. We are in a lot of trouble!" 
Psalms79:9 God, help us! You are the one who can save us. Rescue us, so that people will respect your great name. Forgive our sins, so people know that you are good." 
Psalms79:10 Foreign nations are saying about us, ‘Their God cannot help them!’ That is not right! People of those nations have murdered your servants, so punish them for us to see." 
Psalms79:11 Listen to the prisoners who are in pain! They are calling out for help. Use your great power to make them free, before their enemies kill them." 
Psalms79:12 Lord, those countries near to us have insulted you. Punish them as they deserve, seven times over! Give them as much trouble as they have given to you." 
Psalms79:13 Then we will continue to thank you for ever. You take care of us, as the sheep in your field. We will tell our children and grandchildren to praise you, as you deserve." 
Psalms80:1 Shepherd of Israel, listen to us! You lead us, Joseph's family, as your sheep. God, you sit on your throne as king, above the cherubs. Show everyone your bright glory!" 
Psalms80:2 Shine on Ephraim, Benjamin and Manasseh. Show your great power! Come and rescue us!" 
Psalms80:3 God, turn us to come back to you. Look at us with a smile on your face! Then we will be safe." 
Psalms80:4 Lord God Almighty, we, your people, are praying to you. How long will you continue to be angry with us?" 
Psalms80:5 You have caused us to eat tears as our food. Buckets full of tears is what we drink. 
Psalms80:6 You have caused the people who live near us to laugh at us. Our enemies insult us. 
Psalms80:7 God Almighty, turn us to come back to you. Look at us with a smile on your face! Then we will be safe." 
Psalms80:8 You pulled us out from Egypt, like a vine that you dug out of the ground. You chased nations out of this land, and you planted us here." 
Psalms80:9 You prepared the ground ready for us. We put down roots like a vine. We grew well and we covered all the land. 
Psalms80:10 Our branches gave shade to the mountains, and to the big cedar trees." 
Psalms80:11 Our branches reached the Mediterranean Sea, and as far as the Euphrates River." 
Psalms80:12 Why have you knocked down the walls that protected us? Now, anyone who passes can rob our fruit." 
Psalms80:13 Wild pigs from the forest attack your vine. Other wild animals eat all the fruit. 
Psalms80:14 Come back to us, God Almighty! Look down from heaven and see what is happening! Take care of your special vine." 
Psalms80:15 You planted it with your own right hand. You caused it to grow, like a strong son." 
Psalms80:16 But now the enemy has cut down your vine, and they have burned it with fire. Show them that you are angry with them, and destroy them!" 
Psalms80:17 Give strength to the one who sits at your right hand. He is the man that you have chosen to serve you. 
Psalms80:18 Then we will never turn away from you. Give to us new life and strength, so that we may always worship you." 
Psalms80:19 Lord God Almighty, turn us to come back to you. Look at us with a smile on your face! Then we will be safe." 
Psalms81:1 Sing with joy to praise God! He is the one who makes us strong! Shout aloud to the God of Jacob. 
Psalms81:2 Start the music! Beat your tambourines! Make beautiful music on your harps and lyres. 
Psalms81:3 Start the New Moon Festival with the sound of the trumpet. Also do it on the day of the Full Moon. 
Psalms81:4 This is a rule for Israel's people. The God of Jacob commanded us to do it. 
Psalms81:5 When God punished the land of Egypt, he gave this rule to Joseph's family. I heard a voice that I did not know." 
Psalms81:6 It said: ‘I took the heavy load off your back. You no longer had to carry those heavy baskets. 
Psalms81:7 When you had trouble, you called to me for help and I made you safe. I answered your prayers from the middle of the dark storm. I tested you at Meribah, when you had no water in the wilderness." 
Psalms81:8 I said, “My people, listen to me! I warn you now. Listen carefully to me, Israel's people!" 
Psalms81:9 There must not be any strange god among you. You must never worship a foreign god. 
Psalms81:10 I am the Lord, your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it with many good things.”" 
Psalms81:11 But my people refused to listen to me. My people, Israel, did not obey me." 
Psalms81:12 So I let them follow their own proud ideas. They could do whatever they wanted to do. 
Psalms81:13 But my people should listen to me! Yes, Israel should obey my commands!" 
Psalms81:14 Then I would quickly win against their enemies. I would attack them all.’ 
Psalms81:15 (Then people who hate the Lord would fall down with fear in front of him. They would be ashamed for ever.) 
Psalms81:16 ‘But I would feed you, my people, with the best wheat. I would give you plenty of honey from the rock, for you to eat and be full.’"

Psalms82:2 He tells them, ‘You must no longer judge people in a way that is not fair. You must stop saying that wicked people are right." 
Psalms82:3 You must help poor people and those with no family to receive justice. Help those who are suffering to receive the help that they need. 
Psalms82:4 Rescue weak and helpless people, so that evil people cannot hurt them." 
Psalms82:5 The powerful leaders know nothing and they understand nothing. They walk about in a dark place. At the same time the foundation of the earth shakes! 
Psalms82:6 I said, “You are gods. You are all sons of the Most High God." 
Psalms82:7 But you will die, as all people die. You will come to an end, like all other rulers.” ’" 
Psalms82:8 God, now do something! Judge all the nations of the earth. For they all belong to you." 
Psalms83:1 God, do not continue to be silent! Do not remain quiet. Please do something!" 
Psalms83:2 Look! Your enemies are causing trouble. The people who hate you are ready to attack. 
Psalms83:3 They decide carefully how they will attack your people. They want to hurt the people that you love. 
Psalms83:4 They say, ‘Come now! We will destroy them. They will no longer be a nation. Nobody will remember the name of Israel any more.’" 
Psalms83:5 They agree together what they will do. They join one another to fight against you. 
Psalms83:6 Those enemies are Edomites and Ishmaelites, Moabites and Hagrites." 
Psalms83:7 They also come from Gebal, Ammon and Amalek, Philistia and Tyre." 
Psalms83:8 Even the Assyrian army has joined with them. They have given strength to Lot's descendants, the Moabites and the Ammonites." 
Psalms83:9 God, punish them as you punished Midian. Do what you did to Sisera and Jabin at the River Kishon." 
Psalms83:10 You destroyed them at Endor. Their bodies remained on the ground, like dirt." 
Psalms83:11 Do to their leaders what you did to Oreb and Zeeb. Let their rulers die, like Zebah and Zalmunna." 
Psalms83:12 Those people said, ‘Let us take God's valuable country for ourselves!’" 
Psalms83:13 God, blow them away like dust, like chaff that blows away in the wind!" 
Psalms83:14 Go after them, like a fire that burns both forest and mountains." 
Psalms83:15 Chase them away with your storms. Frighten them with your strong winds. 
Psalms83:16 Cause them to be very ashamed, Lord, so that they might turn to you." 
Psalms83:17 May they always be ashamed and frightened. Let them die with shame! 
Psalms83:18 Then they will understand that you are the Lord. They will know that you are the Most High God who rules over all the earth.

Psalms84:3 In your temple, even sparrows have their homes. Swallows build their nests there, to take care of their babies. They can be near to your altars, Lord Almighty, my king and my God." 
Psalms84:4 You have truly blessed those people who live in your temple. They can praise you all the time! 
Psalms84:5 You have blessed those people who come to you to make them strong. They love to travel to your temple in Zion.

Psalms84:7 The people become stronger as they travel, until each one meets with God in Zion." 
Psalms84:8 Lord God Almighty, please listen to my prayer! God of Jacob, hear me!" 
Psalms84:9 God, look at our king, the one who protects us like a shield! Take care of the king that you have chosen."

Psalms85:2 You forgave your people's sins. You took away their guilt. 
Psalms85:3 You stopped being so angry with them. You did not punish them any more. 
Psalms85:4 But now turn back to us, and make us strong again, God. You are the one who rescues us. Please stop being angry with us." 
Psalms85:5 Will you continue to be angry with us for ever? Will you be angry with our children too? 
Psalms85:6 Please turn back to us and give us new life! Then your people will be happy and they will praise you. 
Psalms85:7 Lord, show us again your faithful love! Please rescue us!"

Psalms85:9 Yes, God will soon rescue his people who serve him. Then we will see his glory again in our land." 
Psalms85:10 God will be faithful to us and he will love us with his faithful love. His justice and his peace will join together, like friends that hug each other."

Psalms85:12 Yes! The Lord will bless us with many good things. Plenty of food will grow in our land. 
Psalms85:13 Justice will go before our God, and it will make a way for him to walk on." 
Psalms86:1 Lord, please listen to me, and answer me! I am weak and I need help." 
Psalms86:2 I am your faithful servant, so keep my life safe! Please save me, my God, for I trust in you." 
Psalms86:3 My Lord, be kind to me. All through the day, I am calling to you for help." 
Psalms86:4 Please make me happy deep inside, for you are the one that I pray to, my Lord." 
Psalms86:5 Yes, my Lord, you are very kind, and you are ready to forgive people. When anyone calls to you for help, you love them with your faithful love." 
Psalms86:6 Lord, hear my prayer! Please listen when I call to you for help." 
Psalms86:7 Whenever trouble comes to me, I will pray to you. I know that you will answer me." 
Psalms86:8 There is no one like you, my Lord, among all the gods. Nobody can do the great things that you do." 
Psalms86:9 You have made all the nations of the world, and they will all come to worship you, my Lord. They will agree that your name is great." 
Psalms86:10 For you are great and you do great things. You alone are God. 
Psalms86:11 Lord, teach me how I should live. Then I will obey your truth. Teach me to serve you with all that I am, so that I respect you with fear." 
Psalms86:12 My Lord and my God, I will thank you with all my strength. I will praise your name for ever!" 
Psalms86:13 I know that you love me very much with your faithful love. You will continue to keep my life safe, so that I do not go down into the deep hole of death." 
Psalms86:14 God, proud people are attacking me. Cruel men have joined together to kill me. They do not respect you at all." 
Psalms86:15 But you, my Lord, are a kind God who forgives people. You do not quickly become angry, and your faithful love is very great. We can always trust you to do what you have promised." 
Psalms86:16 Turn to me and be kind to me. I am your servant, so make me strong. Please rescue your slave!" 
Psalms86:17 Help me, so that I know you are pleased with me. Then my enemies will see it, and they will be ashamed. They will know that you, Lord, have helped me and comforted me."

Psalms87:5 They say this about Zion: ‘Each one of these people was born there.’ It is the Most High God who makes that city strong.

Psalms88:1 Lord, you are the God who rescues me. I call out to you in the day, and I pray for help at night." 
Psalms88:2 Please listen to my prayer! I am calling to you for help! 
Psalms88:3 There is a lot of trouble in my life. I feel that I am near to death. 
Psalms88:4 People see me as if I am already in my grave. I feel completely helpless. 
Psalms88:5 I am the same as people who have died. Their bodies already lie in their graves. You do not remember them any more. You cannot use your power to help them. 
Psalms88:6 You have put me in the deep hole of death, far down in a very dark place." 
Psalms88:7 Your anger is very heavy on me. It is like the waves of the sea that knock me down. 
Psalms88:8 You have caused my best friends to keep away from me. They do not even want to look at me. A trap has caught me and I cannot escape. 
Psalms88:9 My eyes have become weak because I am so sad. Lord, every day I call to you for help. I lift my hands up to you as I pray." 
Psalms88:10 Your great miracles do not help dead people! Dead people cannot get up and praise you! 
Psalms88:11 People in their graves cannot talk about your faithful love! In the world of death, they cannot say how faithful you are." 
Psalms88:12 In those dark places, they do not see your miracles. In that place where people forget everything, they cannot know about the good things that you do." 
Psalms88:13 But as for me, Lord, I call to you for help. Every morning I pray to you." 
Psalms88:14 Lord, why do you throw me out? Why do you turn away from me?" 
Psalms88:15 Since I was young, I have been in pain. I have always been near to death. You have caused bad troubles to frighten me, so that I feel helpless." 
Psalms88:16 Your anger is too much for me! You are attacking me and destroying me! 
Psalms88:17 The troubles that you send are all around me, like a flood of deep water. They come very near to me, on every side." 
Psalms88:18 You cause my friends and my neighbours to stay away from me. All that I have near to me is darkness.

Psalms89:2 Yes! Your faithful love will always continue to be strong. You will always be faithful, as the skies will always be there." 
Psalms89:3 The Lord has said, ‘I have made a covenant with the king that I chose. I have made this strong promise to my servant David:" 
Psalms89:4 “Someone from your family will always be king. Your descendants will continue to rule for ever.” ’ 
Psalms89:5 Lord, everything in heaven praises you for the great things that you have done. Yes, your angels meet together to praise you, because you are always faithful." 
Psalms89:6 In all the heavens, there is no one like you, Lord! No angel in heaven is equal to you." 
Psalms89:7 When the angels meet together in heaven, they give you honour. All who meet round your throne in heaven respect you, as you deserve." 
Psalms89:8 Lord God Almighty, no one is as powerful as you! You are always faithful in all that you do!" 
Psalms89:9 You rule over the powerful sea. When the waves rise up, you make them quiet again." 
Psalms89:10 You caught the monster Rahab and you killed it! With your power, you chased away your enemies." 
Psalms89:11 The heavens belong to you and so does the earth. You made the world and everything that is in it. 
Psalms89:12 You made everything, from the north to the south. Tabor and Hermon are happy to praise your name." 
Psalms89:13 You have great strength, and you can do great things. You hold up your right hand, as you win against your enemies." 
Psalms89:14 As King, you rule with justice, and you always do what is right. You show your faithful love and you are faithful." 
Psalms89:15 You have truly blessed the people who know how to praise you, Lord. They know that you are pleased with them." 
Psalms89:16 They praise your name all the time. Your justice makes them happy. 
Psalms89:17 You give strength and glory to your people. Because you are kind to us, we win against our enemies." 
Psalms89:18 Yes! Our king belongs to the Lord, and he protects us like a shield. Our king belongs to the Holy One of Israel." 
Psalms89:19 A long time ago, Lord, you spoke to your servants. In a vision, you said to them, ‘I have helped a brave soldier to be strong. I have chosen as king a young man from among the people." 
Psalms89:20 I have found David, my servant. I have poured my special oil on him, to make him king." 
Psalms89:21 I will help him with my power, so that he is strong." 
Psalms89:22 No enemy will win against him. No cruel people will have power to hurt him. 
Psalms89:23 I will destroy his enemies in front of him. I will kill those who hate him. 
Psalms89:24 I will always be faithful and show him my faithful love. By my name he will win against his enemies. 
Psalms89:25 I will cause his kingdom to reach beyond the sea and the rivers. 
Psalms89:26 The king will say to me, “You are my Father. You are my God. You protect me and you rescue me.”" 
Psalms89:27 Also, I will make him my firstborn son. He will be the greatest of the kings of the earth." 
Psalms89:28 I will always love him with my faithful love. I will never change the covenant that I have made with him. 
Psalms89:29 Someone from his family will always be king. They will rule for ever, as long as there are skies above us." 
Psalms89:30 But I will punish his descendants, if they do not accept my laws. If they do not obey my teaching, my rules, or my commands," 
Psalms89:31  
Psalms89:32 I will punish them. Because of their sins, I will cause them to suffer." 
Psalms89:33 But I will not take my faithful love away from David. I will always be faithful, and I will do what I have promised to him." 
Psalms89:34 I will not spoil the covenant that I have made with him. I will always do what I have promised to do. 
Psalms89:35 I made a promise to David at one time, and that will not change. I promised him by my holy name and I will never deceive him." 
Psalms89:36 His descendants will always rule. His kingdom will continue to be there for ever, as long as the sun shines." 
Psalms89:37 As the moon is always there in the sky, to show that God is faithful, David's kingdom will also continue.’" 
Psalms89:38 But now you have turned against your king. You have become angry with the king that you have chosen. 
Psalms89:39 You have spoiled the covenant that you made with your servant. You have thrown his crown into the dirt. 
Psalms89:40 You have knocked down all the walls of his city. You have destroyed all his strong places. 
Psalms89:41 Every stranger robs him of his things. His neighbours laugh at him. 
Psalms89:42 You have made his enemies strong, so that they win against him and they are happy." 
Psalms89:43 You have made his weapons useless, so that he cannot win against his enemies in war." 
Psalms89:44 You have taken away his authority as king. You have knocked down his throne to the ground. 
Psalms89:45 He no longer has the strength of a young man. You have dressed him in clothes of shame. 
Psalms89:46 Lord, how long will this continue? Will you hide yourself from us for ever? Will your anger continue to burn like a fire?" 
Psalms89:47 Remember that my life is very short! People are weak and their lives seem useless. Why do you make people like that? 
Psalms89:48 Nobody can continue to live and never die. Nobody can escape from the power of death. 
Psalms89:49 My Lord, where is the faithful love that you showed to us in past times? Where are the blessings that you promised to David?" 
Psalms89:50 My Lord, look at how people are insulting me! People from all nations laugh at me, your servant." 
Psalms89:51 Lord, your enemies insult the king that you have chosen. Wherever I go, they insult me!" 
Psalms89:52 Praise the Lord as he deserves! Yes! Amen! We agree! 
Psalms90:1 Lord God, from long ago, you have always been a safe home for us." 
Psalms90:2 From before the mountains began, and before you made the world, you were already there, as God. You have no beginning and no end." 
Psalms90:3 People return to dust in the ground when they die. You say, ‘Become dust again, you people.’" 
Psalms90:4 For you, a thousand years is only a short time. They pass as quickly as a day for you, or just a few hours in the night." 
Psalms90:5 You cause people's lives to end, as a dream finishes when you wake up. People are like grass that appears in the morning." 
Psalms90:6 During the morning, it grows well. By the evening, it is dry and it dies." 
Psalms90:7 Your anger destroys us, and we become very afraid." 
Psalms90:8 All our sins are there for you to see. You even see the sins that we have tried to hide. 
Psalms90:9 Your anger gives us trouble all through our lives. Then our lives finish like a short breath. 
Psalms90:10 The number of days that we live make 70 years, or maybe 80 years if we are strong enough. But even in the best years of our lives, we have trouble and we suffer. Our lives quickly pass, and we hurry towards death." 
Psalms90:11 Lord, nobody knows all the power of your anger. It is your anger that causes people to be afraid of you." 
Psalms90:12 Teach us to count carefully the days of our short lives. Then we will be wise in how we live. 
Psalms90:13 Lord, return to help us! How long will you be angry with us? Please be kind to us, your servants!" 
Psalms90:14 Show your faithful love to us every morning. Then we will sing with joy. We will be happy for as long as we live. 
Psalms90:15 For many years you have caused us to suffer. Now, please make us happy for as many years as you have given us trouble." 
Psalms90:16 Do great things again, so that we, your servants, see them! Let our children see that you rule as King!" 
Psalms90:17 Yes, may the Lord our God bless us. May everything that we do have success. Yes, may everything go well!" 
Psalms91:1 If anyone lives in Almighty God's safe place, the Most High God protects them." 
Psalms91:2 I will say to the Lord, ‘You are the strong place where I will be safe. You are my God, and I trust in you.’" 
Psalms91:3 God will keep you safe, so that no trap will catch you. He will not let any illness kill you." 
Psalms91:4 He will keep you safe, like a bird keeps its babies safe under its wings. Because he is faithful, he will keep you safe like a shield and armour." 
Psalms91:5 Do not be afraid of danger that comes in the night, or of your enemy's arrows during the day." 
Psalms91:6 Do not be afraid of any illness that comes when it is dark, or any trouble that comes in the middle of the day." 
Psalms91:7 A thousand people may die near you. Even ten thousand people may die at your side. But nothing will hurt you. 
Psalms91:8 Your own eyes will see this happen. You will see wicked people receive the punishment that they deserve. 
Psalms91:9 You have come to the Lord to make you safe. The Most High God has become your home. 
Psalms91:10 No bad thing will happen to you. No illness will come to your home. 
Psalms91:11 For God will tell his angels to keep you safe. They will protect you wherever you go. 
Psalms91:12 They will hold you safely in their hands, so that you do not hurt your foot on a stone." 
Psalms91:13 You will win against lions and snakes. You will walk over them! 
Psalms91:14 The Lord says, ‘If someone loves me, I will keep him safe. Because he worships me, I will protect him." 
Psalms91:15 When he calls to me for help, I will answer him. When he has trouble, I will be with him. I will rescue him, and people will respect him." 
Psalms91:16 I will give him a long life, so that he is happy. He will see that I have the power to save people.’" 
Psalms92:1 It is good to thank the Lord! We sing to praise your name, Most High God." 
Psalms92:2 We sing in the morning to thank you for your great love. We sing at night to say that you are always faithful. 
Psalms92:3 We make beautiful music with lutes, harps and lyres." 
Psalms92:4 The things that you do, Lord, make me very happy. I sing with joy because of everything that your hands have made!" 
Psalms92:5 Yes, Lord, you have done great things! Your thoughts are more than we can understand!" 
Psalms92:6 This is something that stupid people do not know. Fools do not understand it. 
Psalms92:7 Wicked people may continue to grow, like green grass. Evil people may grow like weeds. But in the end you will destroy them and they will never grow again." 
Psalms92:8 You, Lord, will rule as King for ever!" 
Psalms92:9 Yes, Lord, look at your enemies! Your enemies will all die! Those people who do evil things will all disappear!" 
Psalms92:10 You have made me as strong as a wild bull. You have poured pure olive oil on me. 
Psalms92:11 With my own eyes, I have seen my enemies fall. With my own ears, I have heard them cry out in pain." 
Psalms92:12 Righteous people will do well, like a palm tree that makes new leaves. They will become strong, like a tall cedar tree in Lebanon." 
Psalms92:13 They are like trees that someone has planted in the Lord 's house. They grow well in the yard of our God's temple. 
Psalms92:14 When they are old, they will still give fruit! They will always be strong, with fresh, green leaves." 
Psalms92:15 Righteous people will tell everyone, ‘The Lord always does what is right. He is my strong, safe rock. There is nothing bad in him.’"

Psalms93:2 You, Lord, have always ruled as King, since before the beginning of time." 
Psalms93:3 The floods of water have risen up, Lord. They have risen with a loud noise. Their waves beat against the land." 
Psalms93:4 But the Lord rules as King high above everything! He rules over the powerful waves of the sea, with all their great noise!" 
Psalms93:5 Lord, your rules remain strong and true. Your house will always be holy, because you are holy."

Psalms94:3 How long will wicked people continue to be happy? Yes, Lord, how long?"

Psalms94:5 Lord, see how they hurt your own people. They are cruel to the people who belong to you." 
Psalms94:6 They kill widows and foreign people who live in our land. They murder children who have no family. 
Psalms94:7 They say, ‘The Lord does not see us. Israel's God does not see what we are doing.’" 
Psalms94:8 Think again, all you fools among the people! Stupid people, become wise!" 
Psalms94:9 God made our ears! Can he himself not hear? He made our eyes! Does he not see? 
Psalms94:10 He teaches the nations what is right. Will he not punish you, when you do what is wrong? He teaches people what they should know." 
Psalms94:11 The Lord knows what people think. He knows that their thoughts are empty. 
Psalms94:12 Lord, when you teach people what is right, you have really blessed them. You use your Law to teach them." 
Psalms94:13 When those people have troubles, you keep them safe. Then the wicked people fall into a deep hole that you have dug to catch them." 
Psalms94:14 The Lord will not leave his people alone. He will not forget to help the people who belong to him. 
Psalms94:15 Once again, judges will do what is right and fair, and good, honest people will agree."

Psalms94:19 When I have troubles in my mind, so that I am afraid, you help me to be happy again."

Psalms101:5 If anyone secretly speaks against his friend, I will destroy them. If anyone is proud and they boast about themselves, I will send them away from me."

Psalms102:1 Lord, please hear my prayer! Listen to me when I call to you for help." 
Psalms102:2 Now that I am in trouble, please do not turn away from me. Listen carefully to me. Now that I am calling to you for help, please answer me quickly." 
Psalms102:3 My life is quickly disappearing, like smoke from a fire. My bones hurt very much, as if they were burning in an oven." 
Psalms102:4 I am very weak and upset, like dry grass that will soon die. I even forget to eat my food." 
Psalms102:5 I cry aloud with pain, so that I am just skin and bones." 
Psalms102:6 I am like a wild bird in the desert. I am like an owl in the wilderness. 
Psalms102:7 I am awake in the night, like a bird that sits alone on the roof." 
Psalms102:8 All day my enemies insult me. They laugh at me and they use my name as a curse. 
Psalms102:9 Because you are so angry with me, I eat ashes as my food. My tears drop into the water that I drink. It seems that you have picked me up, and then you have thrown me away." 
Psalms102:10  
Psalms102:11 My days are disappearing, like a shadow in the evening. Like dry grass, I will soon die." 
Psalms102:12 But you, Lord, rule for ever as king. People will always remember you." 
Psalms102:13 You will soon be kind to Zion again. Now it is time to forgive her. Yes, the time is now right for you to do that." 
Psalms102:14 Her buildings have become broken stones and dust. But your servants still love your city. We are sad to see that it is broken down. 
Psalms102:15 The nations will respect the Lord 's name. All the kings of the world will praise your glory. 
Psalms102:16 That will happen when the Lord builds Zion again and he appears there in his glory. 
Psalms102:17 He will answer the prayers of poor people who have nothing. He will not refuse to help them. 
Psalms102:18 Write these things down for people who live in future times to read. Then they too will praise the Lord. 
Psalms102:19 Tell them that the Lord looked down from his home in heaven. He saw what was happening on the earth. 
Psalms102:20 He heard the prisoners call out in pain. He saved the lives of prisoners so they could go free before their enemies could kill them. 
Psalms102:21 As a result, people will shout the Lord 's name in Zion. Yes, they will praise him in Jerusalem!" 
Psalms102:22 At that time, people from all nations will meet together. The kingdoms of the world will come to serve the Lord." 
Psalms102:23 But God has made me weak before I have reached old age. He has made my life short. 
Psalms102:24 So I pray, ‘My God, please do not let me die now, in the middle of my life. The years of your life continue for ever!" 
Psalms102:25 Long ago, you built the world on a strong foundation. You created the heavens with your own hands." 
Psalms102:26 They will all disappear one day, but you will remain. They will become spoiled, like old clothes. Like clothes, you will remove them, so that they disappear." 
Psalms102:27 But you remain the same. The years of your life will never finish. 
Psalms102:28 As your servants, our children will live here safely. Their descendants too will be safe, as they live near to you.’"

Psalms103:4 He saves me from the deep hole of death's power. He always loves me and he is kind to me. That is how he blesses me so much! 
Psalms103:5 He gives me many good things for my life here. So I become strong again, with the strength of a young eagle." 
Psalms103:6 The Lord does what is right and fair. He helps poor people who suffer.

Psalms103:8 The Lord is kind to people and he forgives them. He is slow to become angry and his faithful love always continues. 
Psalms103:9 He will not always tell us that we are wrong. He will not always continue to be angry. 
Psalms103:10 We should receive much punishment for the bad things that we do. But the Lord does not punish us so much. 
Psalms103:11 For people who worship him, his faithful love is very great. How far is the sky above the earth? His love is greater than that!" 
Psalms103:12 How far is the east from the west? The Lord has taken our sins further away than that! He no longer says that we are guilty. 
Psalms103:13 As a father is kind to his children, the Lord is kind to us. He is kind to people who worship him." 
Psalms103:14 He knows that we are weak humans. He remembers that he used dust to make us. 
Psalms103:15 A person's life is like grass that quickly dies. He is strong only for a short time, like wild flowers in a field." 
Psalms103:16 A hot wind blows over flowers and they die! Nobody even remembers where they were growing. 
Psalms103:17 But the faithful love of the Lord will always be with people who worship him. He promises to be kind to their descendants for ever.
