Psalms104:1 I say to myself, ‘ Praise the Lord!’ Lord, my God, you are very great! You rule with authority as a great king." 
Psalms104:2 Light is all around you, like the clothes that you wear. You have put the skies as a roof over the earth." 
Psalms104:3 You have built the beams of your home on the clouds of rain. The clouds carry you as you travel, and you ride on the wind." 
Psalms104:4 You use the winds to carry your messages. Flames of fire are your servants. 
Psalms104:5 You, Lord, built the earth on its strong foundations. Nothing will ever shake it from its place." 
Psalms104:6 You covered the earth with deep water, like a coat. The water covered the tops of the mountains." 
Psalms104:7 But when you shouted, the water went back. It ran away, when you shouted like thunder." 
Psalms104:8 The water poured down from the mountains, and it went down into the valleys. It went to stay in the place that you had chosen for it." 
Psalms104:9 You made a border for the water so that it could not pass across. Never again will the water cover the earth. 
Psalms104:10 You cause springs of water to pour along the valleys. Streams of water run between the hills. 
Psalms104:11 They give water to all the wild animals. Wild donkeys drink from them. 
Psalms104:12 The birds make their homes beside the streams. They sing from the branches of the trees. 
Psalms104:13 You pour down rain on the mountains from your home above the sky. You cause many fruits to grow on the earth. 
Psalms104:14 You give grass for farm animals to eat. You give crops for people to plant. In that way, people get food from the ground." 
Psalms104:15 They grow grapes for wine, which makes them happy. They grow olive trees for oil, which makes their faces shine. They grow grain for bread, which makes them strong." 
Psalms104:16 The Lord 's own trees receive plenty of rain. They are the cedar trees that he planted in Lebanon. 
Psalms104:17 The birds make their nests there. Storks live there in the fir trees. 
Psalms104:18 The wild goats live high up in the mountains. Rock badgers hide among the rocks. 
Psalms104:19 You made the moon to show us the months and seasons. The sun also knows the right time for sunset. 
Psalms104:20 At night you make it dark, so that the forest animals come out." 
Psalms104:21 The lions roar while they look for animals to kill. They look for the food that God gives to them. 
Psalms104:22 At sunrise, they go back to their homes, and they lie down to rest." 
Psalms104:23 During the day, people go out to work. They work at their jobs until it is evening." 
Psalms104:24 Lord, you have made very many different things! You were very wise in the way that you have made them all. The earth is full of the living things that you have made." 
Psalms104:25 Look at the sea, which is deep and wide! It is full of living things, which are both large and small. They are more than anyone can count." 
Psalms104:26 Ships travel on the sea. You made the monster, Leviathan, to swim and to play in the sea." 
Psalms104:27 All these living things wait for you to give them their food when they need it. 
Psalms104:28 When you give it to them, they take it. You open your hand to feed them, and they have plenty of good things." 
Psalms104:29 When you turn away from them, they become very afraid. When you take away their breath, they die and they return to the dust." 
Psalms104:30 But when you breathe into anything, it receives life. In that way, you bring new life to the earth." 
Psalms104:31 I pray that the glory of the Lord will continue for ever! I pray that the Lord will be happy with everything that he has made! 
Psalms104:32 When he looks down at the earth, it shakes! When he touches the mountains, they pour out smoke!" 
Psalms104:33 All my life, I will sing to praise the Lord. I will always sing songs to praise my God, for as long as I live." 
Psalms104:34 I want my song to make him happy. I will be happy, because of what the Lord has done." 
Psalms104:35 I pray that wicked people will disappear from the earth. May they disappear for ever! I say to myself, ‘Praise the Lord! Hallelujah!’" 
Psalms105:1 Thank the Lord! Make his name famous! Tell people in all the nations what he has done. 
Psalms105:2 Sing songs and make music to praise him! Tell people about the miracles that he has done. 
Psalms105:3 Boast about his holy name. Everybody who wants to worship the Lord should be very happy! 
Psalms105:4 Ask the Lord to help you. Ask him to give you strength. Come near to him and worship him. 
Psalms105:5 Remember the great things that he has done. Remember his miracles, and the commands that he has spoken." 
Psalms105:6 Remember that you are descendants of God's servant, Abraham. You are Jacob's descendants, and God has chosen you to be his people." 
Psalms105:7 He is the Lord, our God. He rules the whole earth with justice." 
Psalms105:8 He will always remember the covenant that he made with us. He made that promise to continue for ever. 
Psalms105:9 That is the promise that he made to Abraham, and that he also made to Isaac." 
Psalms105:10 He repeated it to Jacob as a law. It was a covenant with Israel's people that would continue for ever. 
Psalms105:11 He promised Jacob, ‘I will give you the land of Canaan. It will belong to you, and to your descendants.’" 
Psalms105:12 At that time, God's people were only a few. They lived in Canaan as strangers." 
Psalms105:13 They travelled among different nations and different kingdoms. 
Psalms105:14 But the Lord did not let anyone hurt them. He punished kings to keep his people safe. 
Psalms105:15 He said, ‘Do not even touch the people that I have chosen to be mine. Do not hurt my prophets.’" 
Psalms105:16 The Lord sent a famine to the land of Canaan, so that his people had no food left to eat." 
Psalms105:17 But he sent Joseph to Egypt before Jacob's family went there to get food. Joseph's brothers had sold him as a slave. 
Psalms105:18 In Egypt the chains on his feet hurt him. He had a heavy piece of iron around his neck. 
Psalms105:19 Joseph remained in prison until what he said would happen really happened. Then the Lord showed that Joseph was right. 
Psalms105:20 The powerful king of Egypt let Joseph go free out of prison. 
Psalms105:21 The king made Joseph master of his palace. Joseph took care of everything that belonged to the king. 
Psalms105:22 The king gave him authority over his officers. Joseph could teach the king's officers, so that they would know what to do." 
Psalms105:23 Then Jacob's family came into Egypt. They lived as strangers in the land of Ham's descendants. 
Psalms105:24 The Lord gave many children to his people. They became more powerful than their enemies. 
Psalms105:25 So the Egyptians began to hate God's people. They did cruel things to the Lord 's people. 
Psalms105:26 The Lord sent his servant Moses to help his people in Egypt. He also chose Aaron to help them. 
Psalms105:27 Moses and Aaron did many miracles in Egypt, where Ham's descendants lived. They showed the Lord 's great power to the Egyptians." 
Psalms105:28 God made all the land become dark. But the Egyptians did not obey God's command. 
Psalms105:29 God caused their rivers to become blood, and he killed their fish." 
Psalms105:30 Frogs covered all their land. They even went into the bedrooms of the palace! 
Psalms105:31 The Lord commanded flies and gnats to cover everywhere that the Egyptians lived. 
Psalms105:32 He sent hail with the rain, and there was lightning everywhere." 
Psalms105:33 He destroyed the Egyptians' vines and fig trees. He knocked down the trees everywhere in their land. 
Psalms105:34 He commanded many locusts to come. There were too many locusts to count! 
Psalms105:35 They ate all the plants in their land, and all the crops in their fields." 
Psalms105:36 Then the Lord killed all the firstborn sons in Egypt. He killed the oldest son in each family. 
Psalms105:37 So the Lord led his people out from Egypt. They took with them valuable silver and gold things. Nobody among the Israelite people was too weak to go. 
Psalms105:38 The Egyptians were happy when they went, because they were afraid of the Israelites." 
Psalms105:39 The Lord made a cloud to cover his people and a fire to give them light at night. 
Psalms105:40 When they asked him for food, he gave them quails to eat. He fed them with bread from heaven." 
Psalms105:41 He broke a rock, so that water poured out from it. It ran as a river through the dry places." 
Psalms105:42 Yes, the Lord remembered the holy promise that he had made to his servant, Abraham." 
Psalms105:43 So he led his people out from Egypt, and they were very happy! They were the people that he had chosen for himself, and they shouted with joy!" 
Psalms105:44 The Lord gave them the land of other nations. They enjoyed the good things that other people had worked to get. 
Psalms105:45 Then God's people could obey his commands and his laws. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! Thank the Lord, because he is good. His faithful love will always be with us." 
Psalms106:2 The great things that the Lord has done are too many to tell! Nobody can praise him enough. 
Psalms106:3 The people who obey God's commands are truly happy. They always do what is right. 
Psalms106:4 Lord, when you help your people, please remember me! Do not forget to help me, when you come to rescue them." 
Psalms106:5 I want to enjoy the good things that you give to the people that you have chosen. I want to be happy together with the nation of your people. I want to join with the people who belong to you, and praise you as you deserve." 
Psalms106:6 We have done bad things, as our ancestors also did. We have done things that are wicked and evil." 
Psalms106:7 When our ancestors were in Egypt, they did not understand your miracles. They forgot the many ways that you showed them your faithful love. When they arrived at the Red Sea, they turned against you, Lord." 
Psalms106:8 But the Lord rescued them, to show that his name is great. He showed that he was very powerful." 
Psalms106:9 He shouted at the Red Sea, and it became dry. He led his people through the middle of the deep water, as if they walked in a desert!" 
Psalms106:10 He rescued them from the people who hated them. He saved them from their enemy's power. 
Psalms106:11 The water of the sea drowned their enemies, so that none of them remained alive." 
Psalms106:12 Then God's people believed his promises. They sang songs to praise him. 
Psalms106:13 But they soon forgot what the Lord had done to help them. They did not wait for him to tell them what to do. 
Psalms106:14 In the wilderness they wanted better food. They tested God to see what he would do. 
Psalms106:15 He gave them the food that they asked for, but he also sent a bad disease on them." 
Psalms106:16 The people became jealous of Moses, and Aaron, the Lord 's special priest." 
Psalms106:17 So God made the earth break open, so that Dathan fell into it. It also destroyed Abiram and his people." 
Psalms106:18 Fire burned all their group, and it killed those wicked people." 
Psalms106:19 At Horeb, the Israelites used gold to make a cow, and they worshipped it." 
Psalms106:20 They turned away from their great God. Instead, they worshipped the statue of a cow, an animal that eats grass!" 
Psalms106:21 They forgot the God who had saved them, when he did great miracles in Egypt." 
Psalms106:22 He had done powerful things in the land of Ham's descendants, and at the shore of the Red Sea. His people forgot about all that!" 
Psalms106:23 So God said that he would destroy them. But God's servant, Moses, spoke on their behalf. He asked God not to be angry with his people, and God agreed." 
Psalms106:24 Later, they did not believe God's promise to take them safely into the beautiful land of Canaan." 
Psalms106:25 They spoke against the Lord in their tents, and they did not obey him." 
Psalms106:26 So he made a strong promise that he would cause them to die in the desert. 
Psalms106:27 He promised that he would chase their descendants away, so that they died in foreign countries." 
Psalms106:28 After that, they started to worship the false god, Baal of Peor. They ate the food from sacrifices that people gave to dead idols." 
Psalms106:29 The Lord became angry because of what his people did. He sent a bad disease among them. 
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas punished the guilty people, so that the disease stopped killing people." 
Psalms106:31 So we remember that Phinehas was a righteous man, and that will be true for ever." 
Psalms106:32 At Meribah springs, the Israelites made God angry. What they did there caused Moses to have trouble." 
Psalms106:33 Moses was so upset that he spoke in a careless way. 
Psalms106:34 The Lord commanded his people to destroy the nations of Canaan, But they did not obey the Lord 's command." 
Psalms106:35 Instead, they mixed with those nations and they learned their way of life." 
Psalms106:36 They worshipped their idols which caught them in a trap. 
Psalms106:37 They even killed their sons and their daughters as sacrifices for those idols of demons! 
Psalms106:38 Their sons and their daughters did not deserve to die, but they killed them as sacrifices for the idols of Canaan. Those murders made the land unclean." 
Psalms106:39 The things that the Lord 's people did made them unclean. They were not faithful to the Lord, like a wife who is not faithful to her husband." 
Psalms106:40 So the Lord became angry with his people. They belonged to him but he turned away from them. 
Psalms106:41 He let other nations have power over them. Their enemies ruled over them. 
Psalms106:42 Their enemies had power to hurt them, and they were cruel to them." 
Psalms106:43 Many times, the Lord rescued his people, but they had decided not to obey him. Their sins made them weaker and weaker." 
Psalms106:44 But the Lord still saw when they were in trouble. He answered them when they called to him for help. 
Psalms106:45 He remembered the covenant that he had made with them. Because of his faithful love for his people, he stopped punishing them." 
Psalms106:46 He caused all their enemies to be kind to them. 
Psalms106:47 Lord, our God, please save us! Bring us safely home from among the other nations. Then we will thank you! We will shout aloud to praise your holy name." 
Psalms106:48 Praise the Lord, Israel's God, as he deserves! Praise him now and for ever! Let everybody say, ‘ Amen! We agree!’ Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!" 
Psalms107:1 Thank the Lord because he is good. His faithful love for his people continues for ever. 
Psalms107:2 Everyone that the Lord has rescued should tell others. Tell them how he has rescued you from the enemy's power. 
Psalms107:3 He brought his people from foreign countries. He brought them together from the east and the west, from the north, and from the south." 
Psalms107:4 Some of them travelled through the wilderness on a road that went nowhere. They did not find any city to live in. 
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty. They were very weak and nearly dead. 
Psalms107:6 Because of their trouble, they called to the Lord for help. He rescued them from their troubles." 
Psalms107:7 He led them along a straight road, to arrive at a city where they could live." 
Psalms107:8 So God's people should thank the Lord because of his faithful love. They should thank him for the great things that he does for people. 
Psalms107:9 He gives drink to people who are thirsty. He gives plenty of good things to hungry people. 
Psalms107:10 Some people sat in a completely dark place. Iron chains held them as prisoners, and they were very sad." 
Psalms107:11 They had refused to obey God's commands. They did not accept the teaching of the Most High God. 
Psalms107:12 Because of that, God caused them to become weak from very hard work. When they fell down, nobody would help them to stand up." 
Psalms107:13 Because of their trouble, they called to the Lord for help. He rescued them from their troubles." 
Psalms107:14 He took them out of the completely dark place. He broke away the chains that held them. 
Psalms107:15 So they should thank the Lord because of his faithful love. They should thank him for the great things that he does for people. 
Psalms107:16 He broke bronze gates into pieces and he cut through iron bars. 
Psalms107:17 Some people became fools and they turned against God. They suffered because of their sins. 
Psalms107:18 They did not want to eat any food, so they nearly died." 
Psalms107:19 Because of their trouble, they called to the Lord for help. He rescued them from their troubles." 
Psalms107:20 He sent his word to them and he made them well again. He rescued them from the deep hole of death. 
Psalms107:21 So they should thank the Lord because of his faithful love. They should thank him for the great things that he does for people. 
Psalms107:22 They should offer sacrifices to thank God. They should sing aloud to tell about what he has done. 
Psalms107:23 Some people were traders who travelled far across the sea in ships. 
Psalms107:24 They saw the great things that the Lord did, even in the middle of the deep seas." 
Psalms107:25 He would command a storm to come, so that the wind caused big waves to rise up." 
Psalms107:26 The waves threw the ships up high in the air, then dropped them deep down. The danger made the sailors afraid, and they thought that they would die." 
Psalms107:27 Like drunk people, they could not stand or walk properly. They did not know what to do!" 
Psalms107:28 Because of their trouble, they called to the Lord for help. He rescued them from their troubles." 
Psalms107:29 He caused the storm to stop, so that the waves became quiet." 
Psalms107:30 The sailors were happy when it became quiet. God led them safely to the port, where they wanted to go." 
Psalms107:31 So they should thank the Lord because of his faithful love. They should thank him for the great things that he does for people. 
Psalms107:32 They should praise the Lord when they meet together. They should praise him at the meeting of their leaders. 
Psalms107:33 In places where people are wicked, God makes the streams and rivers become dry, so that the land becomes a desert. He makes good land become a salty wilderness." 
Psalms107:34  
Psalms107:35 But for his people, he changes deserts to become pools of water. He makes springs of water in dry land." 
Psalms107:36 He lets hungry people live there safely. They can build a city for themselves. 
Psalms107:37 They plant crops in their fields, and they plant vines to grow grapes. They have plenty of food to eat." 
Psalms107:38 He blesses them, so that they have many children. Their animals also grow in number." 
Psalms107:39 But they may become weak and few in number, because of cruel enemies who give them trouble." 
Psalms107:40 Then God causes the enemy's leaders to become ashamed. He makes them travel on roads that go nowhere. 
Psalms107:41 But for people who are poor and weak, he rescues them from their trouble. He gives them many children, so that they have big families." 
Psalms107:42 Good people see what God has done, and it makes them happy. Wicked people have nothing to say!" 
Psalms107:43 Wise people should think about these things. They should understand the Lord 's faithful love for his people. 
Psalms108:1 God, I have decided to trust you always. I will sing songs to praise you, with all that I am." 
Psalms108:2 I will pick up my harp and my lyre. I will make music as the sun rises! 
Psalms108:3 Lord, I will thank you in front of all the people. I will sing to praise you, so that all nations hear." 
Psalms108:4 Your faithful love is great. It is higher than the skies. Your truth reaches beyond the clouds. 
Psalms108:5 God, show how great you are, high above the sky! Let everybody on the earth see your glory." 
Psalms108:6 Use your power to rescue us! Answer our prayers! Then the people that you love will be safe. 
Psalms108:7 God has spoken from his holy place! He has promised: ‘I will show my power! I will make Shechem into separate pieces. I will measure Succoth Valley. 
Psalms108:8 Gilead belongs to me, and so does Manasseh. Ephraim is like my helmet for war. Judah has the authority of my royal sceptre." 
Psalms108:9 But Moab is the place where I wash my hands. Edom is my servant. I can throw my shoes at him! I will shout aloud, because I have won against the Philistines.’" 
Psalms108:10 Who will lead me into the strong city? Who will take me to fight and win against Edom? 
Psalms108:11 God, have you really turned against us? Will you not lead our armies into battle?" 
Psalms108:12 Help us to fight against our enemies, because help from men is useless!" 
Psalms108:13 With God's help, we will win! He will beat down our enemies for us." 
Psalms109:1 You are the God that I praise. Let me hear you speak! 
Psalms109:2 Wicked people are saying bad things against me. They are telling lies about me. 
Psalms109:3 They are all around me, and they say cruel things. I have done nothing against them, but they still attack me." 
Psalms109:4 I have loved them, but they still accuse me. I will continue to pray for them." 
Psalms109:5 I have been kind to them, but they do bad things to me in return. I have loved them, but they hate me in return." 
Psalms109:6 Find an evil man to judge my enemy! Put somebody at his right side to accuse him! 
Psalms109:7 When they judge him, show that he is guilty. Show that even his prayers are a sin." 
Psalms109:8 Make his life very short! Let somebody else do the job which he did. 
Psalms109:9 May he soon die, so that his children have no father, and his wife has no husband." 
Psalms109:10 Cause his children to have no home, so that they go from place to place, and ask people to give them food." 
Psalms109:11 May the people who lent money to my enemy, take away everything that belonged to him. Let strangers take everything that he worked hard to get for himself." 
Psalms109:12 Do not let anyone be kind to him. Do not let anyone help his children, even when they have no father." 
Psalms109:13 Remove all the descendants in his family. When his children die, may nobody remember his family any more!" 
Psalms109:14 Lord, please remember his ancestors' sins. Please do not forgive his mother's sins." 
Psalms109:15 Lord, never forget his family's sins. May nobody on earth remember his family's name." 
Psalms109:16 My enemy was never kind to anyone. He was cruel to poor, weak and helpless people. He even caused them to die," 
Psalms109:17 He loved to curse people. Now may those curses happen to him! He never asked God to bless other people, so nobody will ask God to bless him!" 
Psalms109:18 He always cursed others, as he always put on clothes. So those curses have become like his food and drink. May they go deep inside him, like olive oil that goes into his bones." 
Psalms109:19 Then curses will truly be his clothes. They will be like the belt that he wears every day. 
Psalms109:20 Yes, Lord, please punish all my enemies like that, because they say evil things to accuse me!" 
Psalms109:21 You, Almighty Lord, must help me! Show that your name is great. Rescue me, because your faithful love is good." 
Psalms109:22 I am weak and helpless. My heart beats fast inside me. 
Psalms109:23 I will soon be gone, like a shadow that disappears in the evening. I am like an insect that the wind blows away." 
Psalms109:24 I am so hungry that my knees shake. I am so thin that my bones show. 
Psalms109:25 My enemies laugh at me! When they see me, they shake their heads at me." 
Psalms109:26 Lord my God, please help me! Please rescue me, because of your faithful love." 
Psalms109:27 Lord, cause my enemies to know that you are the one who has rescued me." 
Psalms109:28 They may curse me, but you will bless me. When they attack me, they will become ashamed. But I will be very happy, as your servant." 
Psalms109:29 Shame will come on my enemies. It will cover them like a coat. 
Psalms109:30 I will thank the Lord with a loud voice! I will praise him, in front of a big crowd of people." 
Psalms109:31 He stands beside poor people, and he is ready to help them. When cruel people accuse a weak person, the Lord saves that person's life. So I will praise the Lord." 
Psalms110:1 The Lord said to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right side until I win against your enemies. Then you will be able to put your feet on them.’" 
Psalms110:2 The Lord will give you great authority, as you rule from Zion. You will rule over all your enemies that are around you." 
Psalms110:3 When you go to fight your enemies, your people will be happy to fight beside you. As the sun rises on the day of battle, your young men will be there, on the hills round Zion." 
Psalms110:4 The Lord has made a strong promise. He will not change it. He has promised, ‘You will be a priest for ever, in the same way that Melchizedek was my priest.’" 
Psalms110:5 The Lord is standing at your right side. When he becomes angry, he will knock down kings!" 
Psalms110:6 He will punish the nations, so that dead bodies cover the ground. He will destroy kings everywhere on the earth." 
Psalms110:7 He will drink from a stream at the side of the road. With new strength, he will lift up his head."

Psalms111:2 The Lord does great things! People who enjoy them, think carefully about them." 
Psalms111:3 Everything that he does shows that he is a very great king! He will always do what is completely right. 
Psalms111:4 The Lord causes us to remember his miracles. He is kind and he forgives people. 
Psalms111:5 He gives food to the people who serve him. He always remembers the covenant that he made with his people. 
Psalms111:6 He said that he would use his great power to help his people. He has given to them the lands where other nations lived. 
Psalms111:7 He is always honest and fair in the things that he does. His rules help people to live well. 
Psalms111:8 They will continue to be right for all time. People should trust those rules and obey them. 
Psalms111:9 He rescued his people so that they became free. He made his covenant with them so that it would continue for ever. His name is holy, and people should respect him with fear." 
Psalms111:10 If you want to live in a wise way, respect and obey the Lord. Everyone who lives in the way that he teaches will understand what is right. People should praise the Lord for ever!" 
Psalms112:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! When someone respects and obeys the Lord, God has blessed that person! He is happy to obey the Lord 's commands." 
Psalms112:2 His children will become powerful in the land. God will bless their descendants who serve him. 
Psalms112:3 His family will have valuable things, and they will be rich. People will always remember the good things that they have done." 
Psalms112:4 God's light shines on those who serve him, even when they are in a dark place. God blesses those who are kind to others, and who do what is right and fair." 
Psalms112:5 People who are kind and lend money to others, and who work in an honest way, will enjoy a good life." 
Psalms112:6 Righteous people will never fail. People will always remember them. 
Psalms112:7 They are not afraid to hear bad news. They are brave, because they trust in the Lord." 
Psalms112:8 Their thoughts are strong and they will not be afraid. They know that they will win against their enemies. 
Psalms112:9 They give their things to help poor people. People will always remember the good things that they have done. They will be powerful and people will respect them. 
Psalms112:10 When wicked people see this, they will be angry. They will show their teeth in anger, but then they will disappear. Whatever wicked people want, they will never get it."

Psalms113:2 Everyone should praise the name of the Lord now and for ever! 
Psalms113:3 The Lord deserves that people praise him, from where the sun rises in the east, to where it goes down in the west." 
Psalms113:4 The Lord rules over all nations. His glory is great. It is higher than the heavens! 
Psalms113:5 There is nobody like the Lord our God. He sits on his throne high above us. 
Psalms113:6 He bends down to look below, at the sky and the earth." 
Psalms113:7 He lifts poor people out of the dirt. He lifts up weak people from the ashes. 
Psalms113:8 He gives them a seat with princes, the princes who rule his own people." 
Psalms113:9 He gives children to the woman who cannot give birth. She becomes a happy mother in her home. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!

Psalms114:2 Judah became God's special place for his people. There he ruled over Israel's people.

Psalms114:4 The mountains shook, like sheep that are jumping. The hills jumped like lambs."

Psalms114:6 And you mountains and hills, why did you jump like sheep?" 
Psalms114:7 Earth, shake with fear! The Lord, Jacob's God, has come to you, so be afraid!"

Psalms115:1 Lord, you are the one who deserves that people praise you. They should praise only you, not us! You love us with a faithful love, and you always do what you have promised." 
Psalms115:2 Why should other nations say about us, ‘Where is their God?’" 
Psalms115:3 We know that our God is in heaven. He does whatever he wants to do. 
Psalms115:4 The idols of those nations are made of silver and gold. Yes, human hands have made them!" 
Psalms115:5 Each idol has a mouth, but it cannot speak! It has eyes, but it cannot see!" 
Psalms115:6 It has ears, but it cannot hear! It has a nose, but it cannot smell!" 
Psalms115:7 It has hands, but it cannot feel anything! It has feet, but it cannot walk! It cannot make a sound!" 
Psalms115:8 The people that make those idols will become useless like them. The same is true for everyone who trusts their idols to help them.

Psalms116:6 The Lord takes care of helpless people. I was in danger of death, and he saved me!"

Psalms119:1 People who always do what is right are truly happy. They obey the Law of the Lord. 
Psalms119:2 They agree to live by his rules. They love to pray to him. God has blessed people like that. 
Psalms119:3 They do nothing that is wrong. They live in a way that pleases the Lord. 
Psalms119:4 You, Lord, have told us to obey your teaching carefully." 
Psalms119:5 I want to be able to obey your laws all the time. 
Psalms119:6 When I remember all your commands, I will not be ashamed." 
Psalms119:7 When I learn your good rules, I will do what is right and I will thank you." 
Psalms119:8 Yes, I will obey your laws. Please do not turn away from me!" 
Psalms119:9 How can a young man live a pure life? He must be careful to obey your message. 
Psalms119:10 I bring all my thoughts to you. Do not let me turn away from your commands. 
Psalms119:11 I always keep your message in my mind, so that I do not do anything bad against you." 
Psalms119:12 Lord, everyone should praise you! Please teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:13 You have taught us your rules. I will speak them aloud for others to hear. 
Psalms119:14 Your rules give me a happy life. When I obey them, it is better than being rich!" 
Psalms119:15 I want to think carefully about your teaching. You have shown me the best way to live. 
Psalms119:16 Your laws make me very happy. I will not forget what you have taught me. 
Psalms119:17 Please be kind to me, your servant. Then I will live and I will obey your message." 
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes wide! Then I will see great things in your Law. 
Psalms119:19 I live like a stranger here on the earth. Please do not hide your commands from me. 
Psalms119:20 I really want to obey your rules all the time. I want that more than anything else. 
Psalms119:21 You warn people who are proud. If they turn away from your commands, you will surely punish them." 
Psalms119:22 I obey your rules. So do not let people insult me, so that I become ashamed." 
Psalms119:23 Rulers may think of ways to hurt me. But I will think carefully about your rules. 
Psalms119:24 Yes, your laws make me really happy. They show me the right way to live." 
Psalms119:25 I have fallen into the dust on the ground. I am almost dead. Give me life again, as you have promised to do." 
Psalms119:26 I told you what my life is like, and you answered me. Please teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:27 Help me to understand what your teaching means. Then I will think carefully about the great things that you do. 
Psalms119:28 I am so sad that I feel very weak. Use your word to make me strong again. 
Psalms119:29 Stop me from living in a wrong way. Please be kind to me, so that I know your Law." 
Psalms119:30 I have chosen to live in the right way. I will continue to obey your rules. 
Psalms119:31 Yes, Lord, I will always respect your teaching. Please do not let me become ashamed." 
Psalms119:32 I want to obey your commands all the time. Help me to understand them more and more. 
Psalms119:33 Lord, help me to understand your teaching, then I will always obey it." 
Psalms119:34 Explain your Law to me, so that I will obey it. I will always love to do what it says." 
Psalms119:35 Help me to live in a way that respects your commands. That is what makes me very happy. 
Psalms119:36 Help me to love your rules. That is better for me than riches. 
Psalms119:37 Turn my eyes away from useless things. Use your word to help me to live. 
Psalms119:38 I am your servant. Please do what you have promised to do. You have promised to bless anyone who respects you. 
Psalms119:39 I am afraid when people insult me. Your rules are good, so do not let me be ashamed." 
Psalms119:40 I really want to obey your rules. Because you are righteous, please let me continue to live." 
Psalms119:41 Lord, I want your faithful love to be with me. Please keep me safe, as you have promised." 
Psalms119:42 Then I will be able to answer the people who insult me. I will show them that I trust in your word. 
Psalms119:43 Do not stop me from speaking your true word! I know that you will do what is right. 
Psalms119:44 I will always obey your Law, now and for ever." 
Psalms119:45 I will not be in danger as I live, because I try to obey your teaching." 
Psalms119:46 I will speak to kings about your rules. I will not be ashamed to do that. 
Psalms119:47 I love your commands and they make me very happy. 
Psalms119:48 I respect your commands, and I love them. I am always thinking about your laws." 
Psalms119:49 Remember the word that you have spoken to me, your servant. You have helped me to hope in it." 
Psalms119:50 When I have trouble, your promise gives me peace in my mind. It makes my life strong again." 
Psalms119:51 Proud people laugh at me all the time. But I do not turn away from your Law. 
Psalms119:52 Lord, I think about the rules that you made long ago. When I do that, I feel happy." 
Psalms119:53 When wicked people turn against your Law, I become very angry." 
Psalms119:54 Wherever I live, I sing songs about your rules." 
Psalms119:55 In the night, Lord, I think about who you are. I will continue to obey your Law." 
Psalms119:56 This is what I do every day: I obey your teaching. 
Psalms119:57 Lord, you are everything that I need. I promise to obey your rules." 
Psalms119:58 I ask you, ‘Please, please take care of me. Be kind and forgive me, as you have promised to do.’" 
Psalms119:59 I have thought about the way that I live. I promise to obey your teaching. 
Psalms119:60 I will be careful to obey your commands. I will not be slow to do that. 
Psalms119:61 Wicked people try to catch me in their traps, but I will not forget your Law." 
Psalms119:62 I get up in the middle of the night to praise you. I thank you for your fair rules. 
Psalms119:63 I am a friend of everyone who respects you, and who obeys your teaching." 
Psalms119:64 Lord, your faithful love is everywhere! Please teach me your commands." 
Psalms119:65 Lord, you have blessed me, your servant, as you have promised to do." 
Psalms119:66 Teach me to understand what is right, because I trust your commands." 
Psalms119:67 Before you showed me what was right, I made mistakes. But now I obey your teaching." 
Psalms119:68 You are good and you do good things. So please teach me your laws. 
Psalms119:69 Proud people have told many lies against me, but I love to obey your rules." 
Psalms119:70 Those people do not understand anything, but your Law makes me very happy." 
Psalms119:71 When I suffered, it was good for me. It helped me to learn your commands." 
Psalms119:72 The rules that you have taught me are very valuable. They are more valuable to me than thousands of pieces of gold and silver. 
Psalms119:73 You made me with your hands. You made me what I am. Help me to understand things well, so that I learn your commands." 
Psalms119:74 People who respect you will be happy when they see me. That is because I trust your word. 
Psalms119:75 Lord, I know that your rules are good and fair. When you sent trouble to me, it was because you truly loved me." 
Psalms119:76 Now, please use your faithful love to comfort me. That is what you promised to do for me, your servant." 
Psalms119:77 Be kind to me so that I will live! Your Law makes me really happy. 
Psalms119:78 Proud men should be ashamed! They have spoken lies against me. 
Psalms119:79 Let the people who respect you join with me. They will learn to obey your rules. 
Psalms119:80 Let me obey your commands completely. Then I will not be ashamed. 
Psalms119:81 As I wait for you to save me, I feel tired and weak. I trust in your word." 
Psalms119:82 As I wait for you to do what you have promised, my eyes are too tired to stay open. I ask, ‘When will you come to help me?’" 
Psalms119:83 I am like an old wineskin that has become useless. But I have not forgotten your rules. 
Psalms119:84 As your servant, how much longer must I be patient? Please punish the people who want to hurt me. Please do it soon." 
Psalms119:85 Proud people want to catch me in their traps. That is against your Law! 
Psalms119:86 I know that I can trust all your commands, but people still give me trouble with their lies. Quickly, help me!" 
Psalms119:87 People here on earth have almost destroyed me, but I continue to obey your teaching." 
Psalms119:88 Please use your faithful love to keep me alive. Then I can continue to obey the rules that you have taught me. 
Psalms119:89 Lord, your teaching will always be there. You keep it safe in heaven." 
Psalms119:90 You always show that people can trust you, from one century to the next. You have fixed the earth in its place, and it will remain." 
Psalms119:91 It is by your command that all things continue. You have put everything there to serve you. 
Psalms119:92 If your Law had not made me truly happy, I would have been sad until I died." 
Psalms119:93 I will never forget your rules, because you have used them to give me life." 
Psalms119:94 I belong to you. Please save me! I have respected your rules. 
Psalms119:95 Wicked people are ready to kill me, but I think carefully about your laws." 
Psalms119:96 I have learned that all things will come to an end, but your commands have no end." 
Psalms119:97 I love your Law very much. I think about it all through each day. 
Psalms119:98 Your commands make me wiser than my enemies. They are always in my thoughts. 
Psalms119:99 I understand more than all my teachers, because I think carefully about your rules." 
Psalms119:100 I understand more than people who are old, because I obey your teaching." 
Psalms119:101 I do not follow evil ways, so that I can obey your word." 
Psalms119:102 I do not turn away from your rules, because you are my teacher." 
Psalms119:103 Your words are very good. They taste sweeter than honey in my mouth! 
Psalms119:104 Your teaching helps me to understand things, so I hate anything that is false." 
Psalms119:105 Your word is like a lamp that shines as my guide. It shows me the right way to live. 
Psalms119:106 I have made a strong promise that I will always obey your righteous rules. 
Psalms119:107 I am suffering very much. Lord, please keep me safe, as you have promised to do."

Psalms119:110 Wicked people try to catch me in a trap, but I do not turn away from your teaching." 
Psalms119:111 Your laws will always be with me. They make me happy deep inside. 
Psalms119:112 I have decided to obey your commands always, until the end of my life." 
Psalms119:113 I hate people who are not faithful to you. I love your Law. 
Psalms119:114 You are the safe place where I can hide. You keep me safe like a soldier's shield. I know that I can trust your word. 
Psalms119:115 Go away from me, you evil people! I will obey my God's commands."

Psalms119:119 The wicked people in the world are like rubbish to you. So I choose to obey your rules. 
Psalms119:120 I respect your great strength, so that my body shakes with fear. The laws that you use to judge people make me afraid."

Psalms119:127 That is why I love your commands. They are more valuable to me than very pure gold. 
Psalms119:128 So I carefully obey your teaching. I hate everything that is false. 
Psalms119:129 Your laws are wonderful, Lord! So I obey them."

Psalms119:131 I want to know your commands, like a thirsty dog needs water to drink."

Psalms119:134 Keep me safe from cruel people, so that I can obey your teaching." 
Psalms119:135 Show a kind face to me, your servant. Teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:136 I weep with streams of tears, because people do not obey your Law." 
Psalms119:137 Lord, you are righteous and your laws are completely fair." 
Psalms119:138 The rules that you have given us are good and right. We can trust them completely. 
Psalms119:139 I am so angry that it makes me weak. My enemies do not accept your message. 
Psalms119:140 I know that your promises are completely true. I love them very much.

Psalms119:142 You will always do what is right and your law is completely true. 
Psalms119:143 I am suffering because of great trouble, but your commands make me happy." 
Psalms119:144 Your rules are always fair. Help me to understand them, so that I may find true life."

Psalms119:146 I say to you, ‘Save me, so that I can obey your rules.’"

Psalms119:148 I am awake during each night, and I think carefully about your message."

Psalms119:153 Look at my pain and trouble! Please save me, because I do not forget your Law."

Psalms119:163 I hate all kinds of lies, but I love your Law."

Psalms119:165 People who love your Law have peace in their minds. Nothing will cause them to fall down.

Psalms120:2 I prayed, ‘ Lord, keep me safe from people who tell lies. Save me from people who want to deceive me.’" 
Psalms120:3 How will God punish you, you people who deceive others? He will punish you as you deserve!" 
Psalms120:4 Yes, the sharp arrows of a soldier will hurt you. They will give you pain, like wood that burns with fire." 
Psalms120:5 I am so sad! I live in Meshech as a stranger. I live among the people of Kedar. 
Psalms120:6 I have lived too long with people who hate peace. 
Psalms120:7 I want to live here in peace. But when I speak about peace, they only want war!"

Psalms121:2 My help comes from the Lord. He made heaven and the earth. 
Psalms121:3 He will take care of you, so that you do not fall. The one who protects you will not sleep." 
Psalms121:4 Yes! The Lord keeps Israel's people safe. He never gets tired or sleeps at all! 
Psalms121:5 The Lord is the one who keeps you safe. He is always with you, to give you shade from the hot sun." 
Psalms121:6 The hot sun will not hurt you in the daytime. At night, the moon cannot hurt you." 
Psalms121:7 The Lord is the one who takes care of you in all danger. He keeps your life safe. 
Psalms121:8 Whatever you do and wherever you go, the Lord will always keep you safe, now and for ever."

Psalms124:6 We should praise the Lord! He did not let our enemies destroy us, like wild animals that kill their food and eat it."

Psalms126:3 Yes, the Lord has done great things for us. So we are very happy." 
Psalms126:4 Lord, please bless us as you did before. It will be like streams of water that return to the desert." 
Psalms126:5 When people went to plant seeds in their fields, they were crying. But they will be happy when they bring in the harvest!" 
Psalms126:6 People were weeping when they took their bags of seeds to plant. But when they carry their crops back home, they will shout because they are so happy!"

Psalms135:1 Hallelujah! Praise the name of the Lord! Praise him, you servants of the Lord!" 
Psalms135:2 You serve the Lord in his temple. You serve him in the temple yards. 
Psalms135:3 The Lord is good, so praise him! His name is good and great, so praise him with songs!" 
Psalms135:4 The Lord has chosen Jacob's family for himself. Yes, he has chosen Israel's people to belong to him." 
Psalms135:5 I know that the Lord is great. Our Lord is greater than any other god. 
Psalms135:6 He does whatever he decides to do. He rules the sky and the earth, and the deepest parts of the sea." 
Psalms135:7 The Lord causes clouds to rise up all over the earth. He sends lightning with the storms of rain. He sends out the wind from its place, so that it blows where he chooses." 
Psalms135:8 God destroyed the firstborn sons in Egypt, both men and animals." 
Psalms135:9 He did great miracles in Egypt. He warned Pharaoh and his officers that he would punish them. 
Psalms135:10 He destroyed many nations and he killed powerful kings. 
Psalms135:11 He killed Sihon, the king of the Amorites. He killed Og, the king of Bashan, and all the other kings of Canaan." 
Psalms135:12 He gave their land to his people, the Israelites. The land would belong to them and to their descendants." 
Psalms135:13 Lord, people will always remember your name. You will always be famous." 
Psalms135:14 The Lord shows that his people are right. He is kind to those who serve him. 
Psalms135:15 Other nations make idols with silver and gold. People worship things that they have made with their hands! 
Psalms135:16 Their idols have mouths, but they cannot speak! They have eyes, but they cannot see!" 
Psalms135:17 They have ears, but they cannot hear! They cannot even breathe!" 
Psalms135:18 The people who made them will become like them. Everyone who trusts in idols will become as useless as their idols. 
Psalms135:19 Israel's family, praise the Lord! Descendants of Aaron, praise the Lord!" 
Psalms135:20 Descendants of Levi, praise the Lord! Everyone who serves the Lord, praise him!" 
Psalms135:21 Everyone in Zion should praise the Lord. He is the one who has his home in Jerusalem. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Psalms136:1 Thank the Lord, because he is good! His faithful love will always continue." 
Psalms136:2 Thank God, who rules all gods. His faithful love will always continue." 
Psalms136:3 Thank the Lord God who rules all lords. His faithful love will always continue. 
Psalms136:4 The Lord alone does great miracles. He was wise when he made the skies. He put the earth across the water of the seas. He made the great lights to shine in the sky. He made the sun to rule over the day. He made the moon and the stars to rule the night. So thank the Lord! His faithful love will always continue.

Psalms136:7  
Psalms136:8  
Psalms136:9  
Psalms136:10 The Lord destroyed the firstborn sons in Egypt. He brought the Israelites out from among the Egyptians. He did this with his great power and strength. He cut the Red Sea into two separate parts. He led the Israelites through the middle of the sea. He threw Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea to drown. The Lord led his people through the wilderness. So thank the Lord! His faithful love will always continue.

Psalms136:12  
Psalms136:13  
Psalms136:14  
Psalms136:15  
Psalms136:16  
Psalms136:17 The Lord destroyed great kings. Yes, he killed powerful kings. He killed Sihon, the king of the Amorites. He killed Og, the king of Bashan. He gave their land to his people, the Israelites. He gave it to them as their own land. So thank the Lord! His faithful love will always continue."

Psalms137:1 When we sat down beside the rivers in Babylon, we were very upset. We thought about Zion that we had left behind, and we wept." 
Psalms137:2 We hung up our harps there, on the branches of the willow trees." 
Psalms137:3 Our enemies asked us to sing songs for them there. They laughed at us as their prisoners. They asked for a song to make them happy. They shouted, ‘Sing us a song about Zion!’" 
Psalms137:4 But we are in a foreign land, so we cannot sing a song to the Lord." 
Psalms137:5 Jerusalem, I never want to forget you. I would rather lose my right hand!" 
Psalms137:6 I would rather my tongue could no longer move! Yes, I will always remember you, Jerusalem. You are the most important thing that I think about, more than anything else that makes me happy." 
Psalms137:7 Lord, remember to punish the people of Edom. They were happy when Babylon's army won against Jerusalem. On that day, the Edomites said, ‘Knock down the city so that nothing still stands!’" 
Psalms137:8 People of Babylon, an army will soon destroy you! They will punish you in the same way that you punished us. May God bless whoever does that to you!" 
Psalms137:9 They will hit your babies against a rock. May God bless whoever does that to you! 
Psalms138:1 I will thank you, Lord with all that I am. I will sing and praise you in front of the gods." 
Psalms138:2 I look towards your holy temple, and I bend down to worship you. I thank you for who you are. You love your people with a faithful love and they can always trust you. You are more famous than anyone, because you always do what you promise." 
Psalms138:3 When I prayed to you, you answered me. You made me brave and strong." 
Psalms138:4 All the kings in the world will thank you, Lord, when they hear the promises that you have made." 
Psalms138:5 They will sing about the things that you have done, because they see that you are great, Lord." 
Psalms138:6 Yes, the Lord is high above the earth, but he takes care of humble people. He also knows what proud people are doing, even if they are far away." 
Psalms138:7 When there is danger all around me, you help me to be strong. When my enemy is angry, you fight against him. You save me with your strong right hand." 
Psalms138:8 The Lord will do what he has promised for me. Yes, Lord, your faithful love will always continue. You have made us who we are, so do not leave us now!" 
Psalms139:1 Lord, you look deep inside me, and you know all about me." 
Psalms139:2 You know when I sit down, and you know when I get up. Even when you are far away, you understand what I am thinking about." 
Psalms139:3 You see me when I go out, and you see me when I stay at home. You know everything that I do!" 
Psalms139:4 Yes Lord, before I open my mouth to speak, you know what I will say." 
Psalms139:5 You are all around me, in front of me and behind me. You put your hand on me to help me." 
Psalms139:6 You know so much about me, it is wonderful. I cannot understand it. My thoughts cannot reach as high as that!" 
Psalms139:7 Is there anywhere that I can go to run away from your Spirit? Anywhere that I go, you are already there!" 
Psalms139:8 If I go up to heaven, you would be there. If I dig deep down into the ground to reach Sheol, you would be there too." 
Psalms139:9 If I fly away to where the sun rises in the east, or to the other side of the sea in the west, you would be there." 
Psalms139:10 You would use your hand to lead me, even there. Your strong right hand would keep me safe." 
Psalms139:11 I might say, ‘I will hide myself in the dark, and the light around me will change into night.’" 
Psalms139:12 But it is never too dark for you to see. For you, the night has as much light as the day. Darkness and light are the same to you!" 
Psalms139:13 You made every part of me. You made me grow inside my mother before I was born. 
Psalms139:14 I thank you for the wonderful way that you have made me. Everything that you do is wonderful! I know all this is true. 
Psalms139:15 While you caused me to grow in a secret place, none of my body was hidden from you. You made me deep in the earth." 
Psalms139:16 Your eyes watched me before I was born. Before I had seen the light of day, you decided how many days I would live! You wrote it down in your book." 
Psalms139:17 You have so many thoughts about me, God, that I cannot understand them all." 
Psalms139:18 They are too many for me to count, more than the sand on the shore of the sea. Even if I finished counting them, you would still be there!" 
Psalms139:19 God, you should surely destroy the wicked people! Yes, you murderers, go away from me!" 
Psalms139:20 Those people say bad things against you, God. Your enemies always tell lies." 
Psalms139:21 Lord, I hate those people who hate you. I do not respect those who turn against you." 
Psalms139:22 Yes, I really hate them. Your enemies are my enemies too." 
Psalms139:23 God, please look deep inside me. See what is there in my thoughts." 
Psalms139:24 Show me if I am following any evil way. Lead me in the right way that has always been true. 
Psalms140:1 Lord, please save me from evil people. Rescue me from people who are violent." 
Psalms140:2 They think of evil ideas to hurt people. They only want to cause trouble! 
Psalms140:3 Their words hurt people, like the tongue of a snake. They bite with poison, like a dangerous snake." 
Psalms140:4 Lord, keep me safe from the power of wicked people. Protect me from violent people who want to attack me." 
Psalms140:5 Proud people have prepared a trap to catch me. They have put their nets across my path. 
Psalms140:6 Lord, I say to you, ‘You are my God.’ Lord, please hear my prayer for help!" 
Psalms140:7 Lord, my Lord, you are my strong helper. You protect my head when I fight in a battle." 
Psalms140:8 Lord, do not let wicked people do what they want to do. Stop them when they prepare to attack." 
Psalms140:9 Stop the people who are ready to attack me! Let the bad things that they say against me happen to them instead! 
Psalms140:10 Drop burning coals on them like rain! Throw those people into the fire! Let them fall into deep holes that they cannot get out of! 
Psalms140:11 People who tell cruel lies should not live in our land. Trouble will happen to violent people. Terrible trouble will knock them down! 
Psalms140:12 I know that the Lord helps poor people. He makes sure that they receive justice. 
Psalms140:13 Your righteous people will always praise you. Good, honest people will live with you." 
Psalms141:1 Lord, I am calling to you for help. Come to me quickly! Please listen to what I am saying." 
Psalms141:2 I offer my prayer to you like incense. Please accept it when I raise my hands to you. Accept it like a gift that I offer to you in the evening. 
Psalms141:3 Lord, keep my mouth safe, so that I do not speak bad things." 
Psalms141:4 Do not let my mind want evil things. Do not let me join with wicked people, to do the same bad things that they do. I do not even want to eat their good food." 
Psalms141:5 If a good man hits me because he loves me, he is being kind to me. If he shows me that I have done a wrong thing, that it is like olive oil on my head. I would not refuse it. I continue to pray against what wicked people do." 
Psalms141:6 Wicked people will throw their rulers down from high rocks. Then people will agree that my words were true. 
Psalms141:7 It will be like a farmer who ploughs his field, and breaks the earth into pieces. Just like that, they will break the bones of wicked people. They will throw their bones on the ground at the entrance to Sheol." 
Psalms141:8 But I look to you for help, Almighty Lord. I need you to keep me safe. Do not let wicked people kill me." 
Psalms141:9 They have prepared traps to catch me. Do not let me fall into the traps of those evil people. 
Psalms141:10 May those bad people fall into their own traps! Then I will be free from their power. 
Psalms142:1 I am calling to the Lord to help me. I ask him to be kind to me. 
Psalms142:2 I tell him about all my problems. I tell him about all my trouble. 
Psalms142:3 Even when I do not feel brave, you are still my guide, Lord. In the path where I walk, my enemies have prepared a trap to catch me." 
Psalms142:4 Look at me! I have no friend here to help me. There is no safe place where I can go. Nobody thinks about what is happening to me. 
Psalms142:5 Yes, Lord, I am calling to you to help me. You are my safe place where I can hide. You are all that I need in this world where I live." 
Psalms142:6 Please listen to me as I cry to you. I am very upset! My enemies are trying to catch me. Save me from their power! They are stronger than I am. 
Psalms142:7 Please take me out of this prison. Then I will praise your name. Your own good people will see how you have helped me. They will meet around me to praise you. 
Psalms143:1 Lord, I pray to you for help. Please listen to me. Answer me, because you do what is right. I know that I can trust you." 
Psalms143:2 Do not judge me, your servant. Nobody is righteous when they stand in front of you." 
Psalms143:3 My enemies are chasing me. They knock me down to the ground. They make me live in dark places, like people who have been dead for a long time." 
Psalms143:4 My strength is leaving me and I feel very upset. 
Psalms143:5 I remember what happened many years ago. I think carefully about all the good things that you have done. 
Psalms143:6 I raise my hands to pray to you. I need you to help me, like dry ground that needs rain." 
Psalms143:7 Please answer me soon, Lord. I am losing my strength. Do not turn away from me. If you leave me, I will join those people who are going down into the deep hole of death." 
Psalms143:8 Every morning, help me to remember your faithful love, because I am trusting in you. Show me how I should live to please you. I know that I belong to you." 
Psalms143:9 Lord, save me from my enemies. I run to you to keep me safe." 
Psalms143:10 Teach me to do what pleases you, because you are my God. Let your kind Spirit lead me, so that I can walk in a safe place." 
Psalms143:11 Your name is great, Lord, so please keep my life safe. You do what is right, so bring me out of all my trouble." 
Psalms143:12 I am your servant and you love me. Please show your faithful love to me and destroy my enemies. Yes, kill all those people who want to kill me!" 
Psalms144:1 Lord, I praise you! You are my great Rock! You teach me how to fight well. You show me how to use my weapons." 
Psalms144:2 You always love me. You are the strong place where I am safe. You rescue me from the power of my enemies. You are like a shield for me. I know that I am safe with you. You give me power over nations. 
Psalms144:3 Lord, why do you even think about men and women? Why do you take care of humans?" 
Psalms144:4 People soon pass away, just like a breath. Their life is like a shadow that soon disappears." 
Psalms144:5 Lord, open up the heavens! Leave your home and come down to earth! Touch the mountains so that they burn with smoke." 
Psalms144:6 Send bright lightning to frighten your enemies. Shoot your arrows at them so that they run away. 
Psalms144:7 Put your hand down from heaven, and lift me up from the deep water. Save me from the power of foreign people." 
Psalms144:8 Their mouths speak lies. Even when they make a promise, they are deceiving people." 
Psalms144:9 I will sing to you a new song, God. I will make music to you on my harp." 
Psalms144:10 You help kings to win against their enemies. You also save me, your servant David, from my enemies, when they try to kill me with their dangerous swords." 
Psalms144:11 Keep me safe from the power of foreign people. They speak lies. Even when they make a promise, it is false." 
Psalms144:12 When our sons are young, may they grow quickly, like strong plants. May our daughters become tall and beautiful, like the beautiful pillars in a king's house." 
Psalms144:13 May our homes have plenty of different kinds of food. May our sheep have thousands of lambs, so that there is no space in our fields." 
Psalms144:14 May our cows give birth to many babies. May our enemies never knock down our city's walls. May none of us become their prisoners. May nobody cry in our city's streets because they are afraid. 
Psalms144:15 When God does that for a nation, the people are truly happy! If people know that the Lord is their God, they are truly happy!" 
Psalms145:1 I will praise you, my God and my King. I will always say how great you are!" 
Psalms145:2 I will praise you every day! Yes, I will always say how great you are!" 
Psalms145:3 The Lord is great and we should always praise him! He is greater than anyone can understand! 
Psalms145:4 Parents will tell their children about you. They will all praise your powerful acts. 
Psalms145:5 They will speak about your great glory. I too will think carefully about your wonderful acts. 
Psalms145:6 People will talk about the powerful things that you have done. I too will say how great you are! 
Psalms145:7 Everyone will say how good and kind you are. They will sing with joy about your justice. 
Psalms145:8 Yes, the Lord is very kind and patient! He shows how great his faithful love is." 
Psalms145:9 The Lord is good to everyone. He takes care of everything that he has made. 
Psalms145:10 Everything that the Lord has made will thank him. Your own people will praise you, Lord!" 
Psalms145:11 They will say that your kingdom has great glory! They will speak about your royal power. 
Psalms145:12 Then everyone will agree that you do great things. They will know about the glory of your kingdom. 
Psalms145:13 Your kingdom will remain for ever. You will rule as king through every generation. The Lord always does what he promises to do. He is faithful in everything that he does. 
Psalms145:14 The Lord lifts up everyone who falls. He helps everyone who feels weak. 
Psalms145:15 Everyone looks to you for help. You provide food for everything at the right time. 
Psalms145:16 You are ready to give with an open hand. You give food to every living thing, as much as they need." 
Psalms145:17 The Lord is fair in everything that he does. He always shows his faithful love. 
Psalms145:18 The Lord is ready to help everyone, if they pray to him in an honest way." 
Psalms145:19 He helps those who respect and obey him. He saves them when they call to him for help. 
Psalms145:20 The Lord protects everyone who loves him, but he destroys all wicked people." 
Psalms145:21 I will speak aloud to praise the Lord! Everyone who lives should praise his holy name for ever! 
Psalms146:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! I say to myself, ‘Praise the Lord!’" 
Psalms146:2 I will praise the Lord through all my life. While I am alive, I will always sing to praise my God." 
Psalms146:3 Do not trust in human leaders. No human can save you. 
Psalms146:4 When they die, they return to the ground. On that day, their plans come to an end." 
Psalms146:5 The person who knows that the God of Jacob will help him is a happy person! If he trusts in the Lord as his God, he is happy." 
Psalms146:6 The Lord made the skies, the earth and the sea. He also made everything that is in them. He always does what he has promised to do." 
Psalms146:7 He helps poor people to receive justice. He gives food to the hungry. He makes people that are in prison go free. 
Psalms146:8 The Lord makes blind people see again. The Lord lifts up people who have fallen down. The Lord loves people who are righteous. 
Psalms146:9 The Lord takes care of strangers who live among us. He helps widows and children who have no family, but he stops wicked people doing what they want." 
Psalms146:10 The Lord will rule as king for ever! Zion's people, he will always be your God and your descendants' God. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!" 
Psalms147:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! It is good to sing to praise our God! Yes, it is right to praise him and it makes us happy." 
Psalms147:2 The Lord is building Jerusalem again. He is bringing home the Israelite exiles. 
Psalms147:3 He makes people who are very sad well again. He helps their wounds to get better. 
Psalms147:4 He decided how many stars there should be in the sky. He has given a name to each of them. 
Psalms147:5 Our Lord God is great and very powerful. He understands everything! 
Psalms147:6 The Lord gives help to poor people, but he knocks down wicked people to the ground." 
Psalms147:7 Offer a song to the Lord, to thank him for what he has done. Make music with a harp to praise our God." 
Psalms147:8 He fills the sky with clouds. He sends rain to make the earth wet. He causes grass to grow on the hills. 
Psalms147:9 He gives food for the animals to eat. He feeds the young ravens when they cry. 
Psalms147:10 If a horse is very strong, that does not please him. If a soldier is brave, that does not make him happy." 
Psalms147:11 Who does make the Lord happy? People who respect him and obey him. People who trust him because they know he loves them. 
Psalms147:12 Jerusalem's people, praise the Lord! Yes, Zion's people, praise your God!" 
Psalms147:13 Praise him because he makes your city's gates strong. He blesses the people who live there. 
Psalms147:14 He causes your borders to be safe from your enemies. He gives you lots of the best wheat to eat. 
Psalms147:15 He sends his commands to reach all the earth. Very soon the whole world knows what he wants. 
Psalms147:16 He covers the earth with snow, like a white blanket. He puts white frost everywhere, like ashes that the wind blows." 
Psalms147:17 He throws hail from the sky, like small stones. When he sends a cold wind, nobody can continue to stand." 
Psalms147:18 Then he commands it all to melt. He makes the warm wind blow, and the ice becomes streams of water." 
Psalms147:19 He sends his message to Jacob's descendants. He gives his rules and laws to Israel's people. 
Psalms147:20 He has not done that for any other nation. They do not know his laws. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Psalms148:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord from the sky above! Praise him high in the heavens. 
Psalms148:2 Praise him, all his angels. Praise him, all his armies in heaven." 
Psalms148:3 Praise him, sun and moon. Praise him, all bright stars." 
Psalms148:4 Praise him, skies above, and the water above the sky." 
Psalms148:5 They should all praise the name of the Lord. It was his command that created them. 
Psalms148:6 He has fixed them in their places for all time. Nothing can change the command that he has given. 
Psalms148:7 Praise the Lord from the earth! Praise him, sea monsters and all the deep seas." 
Psalms148:8 Praise him, fire and hail, snow and clouds. Praise him, wind and storm, as you obey his commands." 
Psalms148:9 Praise him, mountains and all hills. Praise him fruit trees and all trees in the forests." 
Psalms148:10 Praise him, wild animals and farm animals. Praise him, snakes and birds." 
Psalms148:11 Praise him, kings all over the world, and people of every nation. Praise him, princes and all rulers on earth." 
Psalms148:12 Praise him, young men and young women. Praise him, old men, as well as children." 
Psalms148:13 They should all praise the name of the Lord. Only the Lord 's name is great above all other names. He is the king who rules over heaven and earth. 
Psalms148:14 He has made his own people strong, so that other nations respect them. All the people who serve him should praise him. They are the people of Israel, who belong to him. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord!"

Psalms149:2 People of Israel, be very happy, because God made you. People of Zion, be very happy, because God is your king." 
Psalms149:3 Yes, dance to praise the name of the Lord. Take your harps and tambourines and make music to praise him with your songs." 
Psalms149:4 When the Lord 's people serve him, it makes him happy. He is happy to help humble people, so that people respect them." 
Psalms149:5 The people who belong to God should be very happy, because he has made them great. They should always sing with joy, even when they lie on their beds at night." 
Psalms149:6 They should shout aloud to praise God, while they hold sharp swords in their hands." 
Psalms149:7 That is how they must fight against other nations, to punish them for their sins." 
Psalms149:8 They must tie up the foreign kings with chains, and put the rulers in prison." 
Psalms149:9 They must do that to punish their enemies in the way that is right. That is how God will cause his people to become great. Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Psalms150:1 Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! Praise God in his temple. Praise him high in the sky, where he shows his great strength." 
Psalms150:2 Praise God for the great things that he has done. Praise him because he is great and powerful. 
Psalms150:3 Praise him with music! Yes! Use trumpets, harps and lyres to praise God!" 
Psalms150:4 Dance and hit drums to praise him! Make music with harps and flutes to praise him! 
Psalms150:5 Make music with big cymbals and small cymbals to praise him! 
Psalms150:6 Everything that is alive should praise the Lord! Hallelujah! Praise the Lord! 
Proverbs1:1 These are the proverbs of Solomon, David's son, king of Israel." 
Proverbs1:2 These are proverbs that will make you wise. They will help you to learn things and to understand good teaching. 
Proverbs1:3 They will help you to understand how to live a good life. They will teach you how to live as a person who is good, honest and fair." 
Proverbs1:4 They can cause even silly people to become clever. They teach young people what they should know, so that they can live well." 
Proverbs1:5 Wise people should also listen carefully, so that they learn something new. And people with clever minds can learn to live in a good way." 
Proverbs1:6 People will learn to understand what proverbs and picture stories mean. They will understand the teaching and clever messages of wise people. 
Proverbs1:7 If you want to know how to live in a good way, you must first learn to respect the Lord with fear. Fools refuse to listen to wise teaching." 
Proverbs1:8 Listen, my child, when your father shows you what is right. And do not forget what your mother has taught you." 
Proverbs1:9 Their teaching will bring you honour, like a beautiful crown or a valuable necklace." 
Proverbs1:10 My child, if sinners want you to join with them, do not agree!" 
Proverbs1:11 They may say, ‘Come with us. We will find someone to murder. We will attack any helpless person that we choose." 
Proverbs1:12 We will send them quickly to their graves. They may be strong and healthy, but they will soon be under the earth." 
Proverbs1:13 We will take all their valuable things for ourselves. We will fill our houses with lots of good things. 
Proverbs1:14 So come and join us! We will share with each other everything that we rob from other people.’ 
Proverbs1:15 My child, do not go along with people like that. Stay away from them." 
Proverbs1:16 They are in a hurry to do evil things. They cannot wait to murder someone! 
Proverbs1:17 Think about it! If you put a trap where the birds can see it, you will never catch anything." 
Proverbs1:18 These wicked men hide to attack someone. They are ready to kill someone, but they destroy their own lives!" 
Proverbs1:19 That is what happens to those who cheat other people to become rich themselves. The riches that they have taken from others will destroy their own lives. 
Proverbs1:20 Listen! Wisdom is like a woman who shouts in the streets. She speaks loudly in the market places. 
Proverbs1:21 She shouts above the noise in the city's streets. She calls aloud at its gates. This is what she says: 
Proverbs1:22 ‘Listen to me! Will you foolish people continue to be so silly? You people who laugh at God, will you always be like that? Will fools always refuse to learn anything?" 
Proverbs1:23 Listen to me as I warn you of danger! Then I will let you know my thoughts. I will let you hear my message. 
Proverbs1:24 I called out to you, but you refused to listen. I reached out to you, but you turned your backs to me." 
Proverbs1:25 You would not accept my teaching. When I warned you, you did nothing." 
Proverbs1:26 So, when trouble comes to you, I will laugh. I will laugh at you when you are afraid." 
Proverbs1:27 Fear may hit you like a strong wind. Trouble may knock you down, like a storm. Your problems may make you very afraid. But I will laugh at you." 
Proverbs1:28 Foolish people will call to me for help, but I will not answer. They will look for me everywhere, but they will not find me." 
Proverbs1:29 They have refused to learn what is right. They have refused to respect the Lord. 
Proverbs1:30 They have not agreed to my advice. When I warned them, they turned away." 
Proverbs1:31 So now they will receive the trouble that they deserve. Their own wicked ideas will make them sick. 
Proverbs1:32 Silly people have turned away from me, and that will kill them. Fools like an easy life, and that will destroy them." 
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me, Wisdom, will live safely. That person will have peace in his mind. He will not be afraid of trouble.’" 
Proverbs2:1 My child, listen to my teaching. Think about my commands." 
Proverbs2:2 Listen carefully, so that you understand what wisdom really is." 
Proverbs2:3 Yes, always try to learn what is right. Never stop trying to understand." 
Proverbs2:4 Look for wisdom as carefully as you would look for silver or valuable jewels. 
Proverbs2:5 Then you will understand how to respect the Lord with fear. You will learn all about God. 
Proverbs2:6 It is the Lord who gives wisdom to people. He teaches us to understand things, so that we know what is true." 
Proverbs2:7 He helps good people to be wise in how they live. And he keeps honest people safe. 
Proverbs2:8 He protects the lives of righteous people. He takes care of people who serve him faithfully. 
Proverbs2:9 So learn from God. Then you will understand what is right, true and fair. You will know the best way to live." 
Proverbs2:10 You will become wise in your thoughts. Your knowledge of what is right will make you happy. 
Proverbs2:11 You will know the right thing to do, and that will keep you safe." 
Proverbs2:12 You will not join with wicked people. You will keep away from those who speak false things. 
Proverbs2:13 Those people no longer follow honest ways. They live in dark places. 
Proverbs2:14 They enjoy doing wicked and evil things. 
Proverbs2:15 They only do wrong things. They are always deceiving others. 
Proverbs2:16 If you have wisdom, you will not listen to the sweet words of a prostitute. You will not let a woman like that deceive you. That kind of woman likes to have sex with many people." 
Proverbs2:17 She leaves her own husband that she married when she was young. Now she forgets what she promised to God at the time of her marriage. 
Proverbs2:18 Do not go into her house! If you do, you will be on the road to death. If you follow her, you will arrive at the home of the spirits of dead people." 
Proverbs2:19 Anybody who visits her will never return. He will never find the way back to a good life. 
Proverbs2:20 If you have wisdom, you will enjoy life, as good people do. You will live as righteous people live." 
Proverbs2:21 Good and honest people will continue to live in our land. 
Proverbs2:22 But God will remove wicked people from the land. He will throw out all those who are not faithful. 
Proverbs3:1 My child, do not forget what I teach you. Always think carefully about my commands." 
Proverbs3:2 If you obey them, you will live a long life. You will have peace in your mind and you will enjoy your life." 
Proverbs3:3 Keep hold of faithful love. That will be like a beautiful necklace that you always wear. Think about what it means to love other people. 
Proverbs3:4 If you do that, you will make God happy. People will also be pleased and they will respect you." 
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord completely. Do not think that you understand things well enough for yourself. 
Proverbs3:6 Whatever you are doing, remember that the Lord is with you. Then he will show you the right way to go." 
Proverbs3:7 Do not think that you are already wise enough. Obey the Lord and refuse to do evil things. 
Proverbs3:8 If you do that, it will make you healthy and strong." 
Proverbs3:9 Show that you respect the Lord in the way that you use your money. Offer the first part of the harvest from your farm to him. 
Proverbs3:10 If you do that, you will store more food than you need for yourself. You will have more wine than you can drink." 
Proverbs3:11 My child, if the Lord warns you that you have done something wrong, accept it. Do not be upset if he warns you." 
Proverbs3:12 He is doing it because he loves you. When a father loves his son very much, he warns him when he does something wrong." 
Proverbs3:13 Anyone who finds wisdom is in a happy place! So is the person who has learned to understand things. 
Proverbs3:14 Wisdom helps people better than silver or gold will ever help them. 
Proverbs3:15 Wisdom is more valuable than the most expensive jewels. Nothing that you might want for yourself is as good as wisdom. 
Proverbs3:16 Wisdom is ready to give you a long life. She will also give you riches and honour. 
Proverbs3:17 Wisdom will be your guide through a happy life. She will lead you in ways that keep you safe. 
Proverbs3:18 Wisdom is like a tree with fruits that give life to the people who pick them. The Lord blesses everyone who keeps hold of wisdom. 
Proverbs3:19 The Lord used wisdom to create the earth. He understood the right way to create the heavens. 
Proverbs3:20 He used his knowledge to pour water from under the earth into the rivers and seas. He caused the clouds to send down rain. 
Proverbs3:21 My child, always keep hold of wisdom and a good mind. Make sure that you keep them safe." 
Proverbs3:22 They will give you strength in life. You will enjoy life, like a beautiful necklace that you wear." 
Proverbs3:23 You will be safe as you travel through life. There will be no danger to make you fall down. 
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down to sleep, you will not be afraid. You will sleep well with peace in your mind." 
Proverbs3:25 You will not be frightened of any trouble that might surprise you. Trouble may destroy wicked people, but you will know that you are safe." 
Proverbs3:26 The Lord is the one who will take care of you. He will protect you, so that no danger can hurt you." 
Proverbs3:27 Do not refuse to help those people who need help. If you are able to help them, do it!" 
Proverbs3:28 If you can give your neighbour the help that he needs, do not say to him, ‘Go away! Come back tomorrow, and then I will help you.’" 
Proverbs3:29 Do not think of ways to hurt your neighbour. You should help him to trust you. 
Proverbs3:30 Do not quarrel with other people if they have done nothing to hurt you. You have no reason to accuse them of anything wrong. 
Proverbs3:31 Do not be jealous of cruel people. Do not try to copy the things that they do. 
Proverbs3:32 The Lord hates wicked people, but he makes righteous people his friends." 
Proverbs3:33 The Lord curses the homes of wicked people, but he blesses the homes of righteous people." 
Proverbs3:34 The Lord laughs at proud people who laugh at other people, but he is kind to humble people." 
Proverbs3:35 Wise people will receive honour, But fools will only have shame." 
Proverbs4:1 My children, listen to what your father teaches you. Listen carefully so that you understand what is right." 
Proverbs4:2 My teaching will help you, so do not refuse it." 
Proverbs4:3 My father taught me when I was a little boy. My mother loved me as her only child. My father said to me, ‘Think carefully about what I tell you. If you obey my commands, you will live well." 
Proverbs4:4  
Proverbs4:5 Learn to be wise and to understand things properly. Do not forget my teaching or turn away from it.
