Zechariah7:1 In the fourth year that Darius was king, the Lord spoke to Zechariah. It happened on the fourth day of the ninth month that is called Kislev." 
Zechariah7:2 At that time, the people of Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regem-Melech and their men to Jerusalem. They came to ask the Lord to bless them." 
Zechariah7:3 They came to the temple of the Lord Almighty to speak to the priests and to the prophets. They asked, ‘Should we continue to weep and to fast in the fifth month each year? We have done this for many years.’" 
Zechariah7:4 The Lord Almighty then gave this message to me. 
Zechariah7:5 He said, ‘Speak to all the people in this land and to the priests. Tell them, “You were prisoners in another country for 70 years. At that time, you were sad and you fasted in the fifth month and in the seventh month of each year. But you did not really do this to give me honour." 
Zechariah7:6 Also, when you eat and you drink at special times, you do that to please yourselves.” ’" 
Zechariah7:7 This is the same message from the Lord that the prophets spoke in past years. At that time, many people lived in Jerusalem and they were happy. Many people also lived in the towns around Jerusalem, in the Negev region and in the low hills in the west." 
Zechariah7:8 The Lord spoke again to Zechariah. 
Zechariah7:9 He said, ‘This is what the Lord Almighty says to the people: “Be sure that you do what is right and fair. Be kind and help each other." 
Zechariah7:10 Do not be cruel to widows or to children who have no family. Do not cheat foreigners or poor people. Do not think of ways to do bad things to each other.” ’ 
Zechariah7:11 But the people did not obey the Lord 's commands. They turned away from him and they refused to listen to him. 
Zechariah7:12 They were too proud to accept his laws. They would not obey the messages that the Lord Almighty used his prophets to speak to them in past times. It was God's Spirit that spoke those messages through the prophets. Because of that, the Lord Almighty was very angry with them." 
Zechariah7:13 The Lord Almighty says, ‘When I called out to my people, they refused to listen to me. So when they called out to me, I refused to listen to them." 
Zechariah7:14 Instead, like a strong wind, I blew them away! I sent them to live among other nations that they did not know. The land that they left became an empty place where nobody lived. They caused a beautiful land to become like a desert.’" 
Zechariah8:1 The Lord Almighty gave this message to me: 
Zechariah8:2 ‘I love Zion very much because it is my special place. I am very angry with anyone who hurts my people who live there.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Zechariah8:3 The Lord says, ‘I will return to Zion and I will live in Jerusalem. Jerusalem will be called “City of Faithful People”. The mountain of the Lord Almighty will be called “God's Holy Mountain”.’" 
Zechariah8:4 The Lord Almighty also says, ‘Old men and old women will walk in Jerusalem's streets with their walking sticks. They will sit and talk together in the public places." 
Zechariah8:5 Many boys and girls will play in the city's streets.’ 
Zechariah8:6 The Lord Almighty says, ‘For the few people who remain in those days, this may seem an impossible thing to happen. But it is not a difficult thing for me to do.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Zechariah8:7 The Lord Almighty says, ‘I will rescue my people from the countries in the east and in the west." 
Zechariah8:8 I will bring them back to live in Jerusalem. They will be my people and I will be their God. I will rule them in a way that is faithful and right.’ 
Zechariah8:9 The Lord Almighty also says, ‘You have heard the message that the prophets spoke when people were building the temple's foundation. So now be strong and brave! Finish building the house of the Lord Almighty." 
Zechariah8:10 Before that time, there was not enough money to pay for work that men or animals did. People could not travel safely anywhere because of their enemies. I caused people to hate each other." 
Zechariah8:11 But I will no longer do that to my people who remain. I will act differently from how I did in past times. 
Zechariah8:12 My people will be able to plant seeds safely in their fields. Their vines will provide plenty of grapes. Crops will grow well in their fields. There will be plenty of rain. My people who remain will enjoy all these things for themselves. 
Zechariah8:13 People of Judah and Israel, in past times other nations used your names as a curse. So now I will save you. You will provide a blessing to other nations instead of a curse. Do not be afraid! Be brave and strong.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Zechariah8:14 The Lord Almighty says, ‘When your ancestors made me angry, I decided to punish you. I would not change my mind about that." 
Zechariah8:15 But now I have decided to be kind to the people of Jerusalem and Judah. So do not be afraid! 
Zechariah8:16 These are the things that you must do. Each of you must speak true words to each other. In your courts, do what is right and fair." 
Zechariah8:17 Do not think of evil things that you can do to other people. Do not make false promises. All these things make me very angry.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Zechariah8:18 The Lord Almighty gave me another message. 
Zechariah8:19 He said, ‘There are special times each year when you fast. Those times are in the fourth, fifth, seventh and tenth months. Those times will now become happy feasts that the people of Judah should enjoy. So you must love truth and peace.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Zechariah8:20 The Lord Almighty also says, ‘One day, foreign people who live in many different cities will come to Jerusalem." 
Zechariah8:21 The people of one city will go and speak to the people of another city. They will say, “Come with us to Jerusalem to ask the Lord to bless us. We will go and worship the Lord Almighty. I am going there, so come with me.”" 
Zechariah8:22 People from many places and from powerful nations will come to Jerusalem to worship the Lord Almighty. They will ask him to bless them.’ 
Zechariah8:23 The Lord Almighty says, ‘At that time, people who speak many different languages will come from all other nations. Ten of them will go to one Jew and they will take hold of his coat. They will say to him, “People have told us that God is with you. So please let us go with you to worship him.” ’" 
Zechariah9:1 This is a message from the Lord against the land of Hadrach. It speaks about the city of Damascus. Everyone looks to see what the Lord will do, including all the tribes of Israel." 
Zechariah9:2 The people of Hamath, which is near Damascus, also wait to see what the Lord will do. The people in the cities of Tyre and Sidon also do this, even though they are very wise." 
Zechariah9:3 Tyre's people have built strong walls around their city to keep it safe. They have stored a lot of silver and gold, as much as the dirt and mud in their streets!" 
Zechariah9:4 But now the Lord God will take away all Tyre's valuable things. He will throw all its power into the sea! Fire will destroy the whole city. 
Zechariah9:5 The people in Ashkelon city will see what has happened to Tyre. They will be afraid. The people in Gaza city will shake with fear. The people in Ekron city will also be afraid. They will no longer hope for good things. The people of Gaza will no longer have a king. Ashkelon will become an empty place. 
Zechariah9:6 Foreign people will make Ashdod city their home. I, the Lord, will cause the proud Philistine people to be ashamed." 
Zechariah9:7 I will remove from their mouths the meat of sacrifices that they have offered to their idols. Those Philistines who remain will become a group of people who belong to me. They will be like a clan in the tribe of Judah. The Philistines of Ekron will join the people of Judah, as the Jebusites did long ago." 
Zechariah9:8 Then I will keep my temple safe. I will protect it like a guard. I will not allow an enemy to attack my people any more. Now I am watching carefully and I will take care of them. 
Zechariah9:9 Be happy and sing, people of Zion! Shout with joy, people of Jerusalem! Look! Your king is coming to you. He is righteous and he has won against his enemies. He is humble and he is riding on a donkey. Yes, he is riding on a young donkey." 
Zechariah9:10 I will remove the chariots from Ephraim. I will remove the soldiers' horses from Jerusalem, and I will destroy their bows and their arrows. The king will tell the nations that they will live in peace. He will rule as king from the sea in the west to the sea in the east. He will rule everywhere, from the Euphrates River to the furthest places on the earth." 
Zechariah9:11 I made a covenant with you, my people. The blood of sacrifices showed that you agreed with that covenant. Now some of you are prisoners. I will rescue those prisoners from dark places where there is no water." 
Zechariah9:12 Yes, you prisoners who now have hope of better things, return home where you will be safe. I tell you this today: I will give back to you twice as much as you have lost." 
Zechariah9:13 I will use Judah as my bow and Ephraim as my arrow to attack my enemies! I will use you, young men of Zion, to attack the soldiers of Greece. I will cause Zion to be like a soldier's sword in my hand." 
Zechariah9:14 Then the Lord will appear above his people. He will shoot his arrows like lightning in the sky. The Almighty Lord will make a loud noise with his trumpet. He will march into battle like a storm from the south. 
Zechariah9:15 The Lord Almighty will protect his people. They will attack their enemies with stones from their slings and they will win the battle. They will drink and they will shout loudly, like men who are drunk. The blood of their enemies will make them full, as a priest's bowl is full of blood when he pours it on the corners of the altar." 
Zechariah9:16 On that day, the Lord their God will rescue his people, as a shepherd rescues his sheep. They will live in his land, like bright jewels that shine in a crown." 
Zechariah9:17 It will all be very good and beautiful! Grain from the land will make the young men strong. The young women will become strong as they drink new wine. 
Zechariah10:1 Ask the Lord for rain in spring. It is the Lord who makes the clouds that give us rain. He will provide rain for everyone, so that crops grow in the fields." 
Zechariah10:2 When people ask their idols for help, the idols deceive them. People who use magic to know what will happen see false visions. People who explain the meaning of dreams tell lies. They cannot help anyone to be strong. As a result, people have nobody to show them the right way. They move around like sheep that have no shepherd to take care of them." 
Zechariah10:3 The Lord says, ‘I am very angry with the shepherds of my people. I will punish the leaders.’ Like a shepherd, the Lord Almighty takes care of his people. He will cause Judah's people to become strong, like horses that are ready to fight in a battle." 
Zechariah10:4 From among Judah's descendants a strong leader will appear. He is like the most important stone at the corner of a building. He is like a strong peg in a wall. He is like a bow that is ready to shoot arrows. Every strong ruler will come from Judah's descendants. 
Zechariah10:5 Together they will be like brave soldiers who stamp on their enemies in the mud of the streets. The Lord is with them as they fight. They will pull down the enemy soldiers who ride on horses. 
Zechariah10:6 The Lord says, ‘I will make Judah's people strong. I will rescue Israel's people. I will bring them back home, because I am sorry for them. It will seem as if I had never turned away from them. I am the Lord, their God, so I will answer their prayers." 
Zechariah10:7 Israel's people will be like brave soldiers. They will be as happy as men that have drunk wine. Their children will also see this and they will be happy too. They will sing with joy because of the things that the Lord has done. 
Zechariah10:8 I will call out to my people to come home. I have done what is necessary to rescue them. They will grow in number to be as many people as they were before. 
Zechariah10:9 I have sent them away to live among other nations. But they will remember me in those countries far away. They and their children will remain alive. They will return home together. 
Zechariah10:10 I will bring my people back from Egypt and from Assyria. I will bring them to live in Gilead and in Lebanon. Their own land will not be big enough for all of them. 
Zechariah10:11 When my people need to go across the sea of many troubles, I will cause its waves to stop. I will cause the deep water of the River Nile to become dry. Proud Assyria will become ashamed. Egypt will no longer have the power to rule other nations." 
Zechariah10:12 I will use my power to make my people strong. They will give me honour wherever they go.’ That is what the Lord says. 
Zechariah11:1 Open the gates of your land, Lebanon, so that the fire can destroy your cedar trees." 
Zechariah11:2 Cry aloud, pine trees, because the cedar trees have fallen. The great trees are completely spoiled. Cry aloud, Bashan's oak trees. Enemies have cut down the whole forest." 
Zechariah11:3 Listen to the shepherds as they cry aloud! Enemies have destroyed the fields where their sheep feed. Listen to the young lions as they roar! The trees of the Jordan Valley are completely spoiled. 
Zechariah11:4 The Lord my God said this: ‘You must take care of my people, as a shepherd takes care of his sheep. My people are like sheep that people will soon kill." 
Zechariah11:5 The people who buy the sheep will kill them. But nobody will punish them for this. The people who sell the sheep are happy. They say, “ Praise the Lord! Now I am rich.” Even the shepherds do not feel sorry for their own sheep.’" 
Zechariah11:6 The Lord says, ‘I myself will no longer feel sorry for the people of this land. I will put them under the power of other people. I will put them under the power of their own king. Those powerful people will destroy the land, and I will not rescue the people.’" 
Zechariah11:7 So I became a shepherd for the sheep that people would soon kill. I took good care of the weakest sheep. Then I took two shepherd's sticks. I called one stick ‘Kind’ and I called the other stick ‘Together’. I took good care of the sheep. 
Zechariah11:8 In one month, I removed the three other shepherds. I could not be patient with them and they hated me." 
Zechariah11:9 I said, ‘I will not be a shepherd for you any longer. If sheep are dying, I will allow them to die. If sheep are becoming lost, I will allow them to be lost. As for the sheep that still remain, they can destroy each other.’" 
Zechariah11:10 I took hold of my stick that was called ‘Kind’. I broke it into two pieces. This showed that I had caused my covenant to finish. That was the covenant that I had agreed with all the people. 
Zechariah11:11 It was on that day that the covenant finished. The men who bought and sold sheep saw what I was doing. So they knew that it was a message from the Lord. 
Zechariah11:12 I said to them, ‘If you think it is right, you should pay me for my work. But do not pay me if you do not think it is right.’ So they decided to pay me 30 silver coins." 
Zechariah11:13 The Lord said to me, ‘Throw the money to the potter! They think that I am worth only 30 silver coins!’ So I took the money and I threw them to the potter at the Lord 's temple." 
Zechariah11:14 Then I broke the second stick into two pieces. That was the stick called ‘Together’. This showed that the kingdom of Judah would become separate from the kingdom of Israel. 
Zechariah11:15 Then the Lord said to me, ‘Take again the things that a foolish shepherd would use." 
Zechariah11:16 I will now bring another shepherd to rule in this land. He will not help the sheep that will soon die. He will not look for the sheep that are becoming lost. He will not help the sick sheep to become well again. He will not even feed the sheep that are healthy. Instead, he will eat the meat of the fat sheep. He will even pull off their feet for himself." 
Zechariah11:17 Terrible things will happen to the useless shepherd who leaves his sheep. His enemy's sword will cut his arm and his right eye. His arm will become weak and useless. His right eye will become completely blind.’ 
Zechariah12:1 This is the Lord 's message that he spoke about Israel. He is the Lord who made all the heavens and who created the whole earth. He is the one who gives life to men and women. He says: 
Zechariah12:2 ‘Look! I will cause Jerusalem to become like a bowl that is full of wine! The nations that are near will drink from it and they will become drunk. They will attack Jerusalem and the other cities of Judah. 
Zechariah12:3 On that day, I will cause Jerusalem to be like a heavy rock. Any army that tries to move it will hurt themselves very much. But all the nations of the earth will come together to attack Jerusalem.’" 
Zechariah12:4 The Lord says, ‘On that day, I will cause every horse to become confused. I will cause their riders to be crazy. I will take care of Judah's people. But I will make the horses of their enemies blind." 
Zechariah12:5 Then the leaders of Judah will say to themselves, “Jerusalem's people are strong because the Lord Almighty is their God.”" 
Zechariah12:6 On that day, I will make Judah's leaders like a flame that starts a fire in a heap of wood. They will be like a fire in a field of dry grass. They will destroy all the nations that are everywhere around them. Then the people of Jerusalem will live safely in the city." 
Zechariah12:7 I, the Lord, will save the homes of Judah first, before I rescue Jerusalem's people. As a result, the descendants of King David and the people of Jerusalem will not receive greater honour than other places in Judah." 
Zechariah12:8 On that day, the Lord will save the people who live in Jerusalem. Even the people who are very weak will become strong again, like great King David. King David's descendants will rule the people, as if the angel of the himself is leading them." 
Zechariah12:9 On that day, I will begin to destroy all the nations that attack Jerusalem." 
Zechariah12:10 I, the Lord, will pour a new spirit on David's descendants and on the people who live in Jerusalem. That spirit will cause them to be kind and to pray for help. So they will look towards me. They will see the one that they have pushed a spear into. They will be sad and weep, as parents weep after the death of their only son. They will weep loudly, as if their firstborn son has died." 
Zechariah12:11 On that day, the people in Jerusalem will weep very much. They will be as sad as the people were at Hadad-Rimmon, in the fields of Megiddo." 
Zechariah12:12 All the people in the land of Judah will weep in separate groups. One group will be the clan of David's descendants. Their wives will weep in another group. The clan of Nathan's descendants will weep together as a group. Their wives will weep in a separate group. 
Zechariah12:13 The clan of Levi's descendants will also weep as a group. And their wives will weep as a group. The clan of Shimei's descendants will weep as a group. And their wives will weep as a group. 
Zechariah12:14 All the other clans of Judah that still remain will also weep in groups, as well as their wives in separate groups.’" 
Zechariah13:1 ‘On that day, water will start to pour from a fountain for King David's descendants and for Jerusalem's people. It will make them clean from their sins and the things that have made them unclean.’" 
Zechariah13:2 The Lord Almighty says, ‘On that day, I will remove the names of idols from the land. Nobody will remember them any more. I will also remove from the land the false prophets and the bad spirit that deceives them." 
Zechariah13:3 Because of that, if anyone continues to prophesy, his own father and mother will say to him, “You must die. You say that you speak with the Lord 's authority, but you tell lies.” Then his own parents will use a sword to kill him as he prophesies lies." 
Zechariah13:4 On that day, every prophet will be ashamed of his visions. He will no longer wear a prophet's rough clothes to pretend that he is a prophet." 
Zechariah13:5 Instead, he will say, “Do not think that I am a prophet. No, I am a farmer. From the time that I was a boy, I have always worked on the land.”" 
Zechariah13:6 People may ask him, “What caused those wounds on your body?” He will tell them, “I received those wounds when I was at the house of my friends.” ’" 
Zechariah13:7 The Lord Almighty says, ‘Wake up, my sword! Attack my shepherd, the man who is near to me. Kill the shepherd and the sheep will run away. I will attack the little sheep.’" 
Zechariah13:8 The Lord says, ‘In the whole land, only one third of the people will remain alive. Two-thirds of the people will die." 
Zechariah13:9 I will use fire to test those people who remain. I will make them pure, as men use fire to make gold and silver pure. They will call to me for help and I will answer them. I will say, “These are my people.” They will say, “The Lord is our God.” ’" 
Zechariah14:1 Listen! A day will soon come when the Lord will judge people. At that time, Jerusalem's enemies will take your valuable things. They will share your things among themselves as you watch." 
Zechariah14:2 I will bring all the other nations together to attack Jerusalem. They will take the city for themselves. They will rob the houses. They will take hold of the women to have sex with them. They will take half the people away to other countries as their prisoners. They will allow the other people to remain in Jerusalem. 
Zechariah14:3 Then the Lord will prepare for battle. He will fight against the people of those nations, as he has fought battles in past times." 
Zechariah14:4 On that day, the Lord 's feet will stand on the Mount of Olives that is on the east side of Jerusalem. The Mount of Olives will break in two pieces. Half of the mountain will move north and half of the mountain will move south. There will be a great valley in the middle that goes from east to west." 
Zechariah14:5 The valley will reach as far as Azel. You will escape through the valley in the middle of the mountains. It will be like the time of the earthquake when your ancestors escaped. That happened when Uzziah was king of Judah. Then the Lord my God will appear. He will bring his holy angels with him. 
Zechariah14:6 On that day, there will be no light, as the sun will not shine in the day." 
Zechariah14:7 It will happen in one special day, a day that only the Lord knows. There will not be a normal day and a night. Instead there will be light at evening time." 
Zechariah14:8 On that day, fresh water will pour out from Jerusalem. Half of this water will go towards the sea in the east. Half of it will go towards the sea in the west. This will happen during both summer and winter." 
Zechariah14:9 The Lord will then be king to rule over all the earth. On that day, there will be only one Lord. Everyone will know him by that one name." 
Zechariah14:10 All the land will change. From Geba to Rimmon, south of Jerusalem, the land will become flat. But Jerusalem itself will rise up high above the land around it. It will remain in its own place, from the Benjamin Gate to the First Gate and on to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hananel as far as the royal winepresses." 
Zechariah14:11 People will make their homes in the city. They will never be afraid of God's punishment again. Jerusalem will be a safe place to live. 
Zechariah14:12 But the Lord will punish the all nations that fought against Jerusalem. He will cause them to become very ill. Even as the people stand on their feet, their bodies will become weak and useless. Their eyes will become useless in their heads. Their tongues will become useless in their mouths." 
Zechariah14:13 On that day, the Lord will cause them to be afraid and confused. They will take hold of each other and fight against each other." 
Zechariah14:14 Judah's people will also go to Jerusalem to fight there. They will take for themselves the valuable things of all the nations around them. They will take gold and silver and lots of beautiful clothes. 
Zechariah14:15 The animals that belong to those other nations will also become ill, like their people. Their horses, their mules, their camels, their donkeys and all their other animals will be very ill in the places where they have their camps." 
Zechariah14:16 After that, the people who are still alive from the nations that attacked Jerusalem will return there. They will go to Jerusalem each year during the Festival of Huts. They will go to worship the King, the Lord Almighty." 
Zechariah14:17 If the people of any nation on the earth refuse to go to Jerusalem, their land will have no rain. They must go there to worship the King, the Lord Almighty." 
Zechariah14:18 If the Egyptian people refuse to go, their land will have no rain. Instead, the Lord will cause them to become very ill. That is how he will punish the people of any nation who refuse to go to Jerusalem for the Festival of Huts." 
Zechariah14:19 They must all go to worship the Lord during the Festival of Huts. The Egyptian people and the people of all the other nations will receive the same punishment if they do not do this. 
Zechariah14:20 On that day, the words, ‘Holy for the Lord ’ will be written on the bells of the horses. All the pots in the Lord 's temple will be as holy as the bowls in front of the altar." 
Zechariah14:21 All the pots in Jerusalem and in the whole of Judah will be holy things for the Lord Almighty. The people who come to offer sacrifices may use any of the pots to cook the meat for their sacrifices. On that day, there will no longer be any traders who buy and sell things in the temple of the Lord Almighty." 
Malachi1:1 This is the message that the Lord spoke to Malachi. Malachi knew that he must tell this message to the Israelites. 
Malachi1:2 The Lord says, ‘I have loved you.’ But you ask, ‘How have you loved us?’ The Lord says, ‘I chose to love Jacob and his descendants. Esau was Jacob's brother." 
Malachi1:3 But I have not loved Esau and his descendants. Instead, I have caused trouble for Esau. I caused Esau's mountains to become a wilderness. Nobody uses that land any longer. Now there are only jackals in the land that belongs to Esau.’" 
Malachi1:4 Esau's descendants in Edom may say, ‘They have destroyed our towns, but we will build our houses again.’ But the Lord Almighty says, ‘They may build again, but I will knock everything down. People will call Edom, “The Evil Land”. They will call them, “The nation that the Lord is angry with for ever”." 
Malachi1:5 You will see with your own eyes what I will do to them. Then you will say, “The Lord has great power, even beyond Israel's borders.” ’" 
Malachi1:6 The Lord Almighty says this to the priests: ‘A son respects his father. A servant respects his master. If I am really your father and your master, why do you not respect me? You are my priests, but you insult my name. But then you ask, “What have we done to insult your name?”" 
Malachi1:7 You insult my name when you offer unclean sacrifices to me on my altar. But you ask, “How have we offered unclean sacrifices to you?” You have shown that you do not respect the Lord 's altar as important." 
Malachi1:8 You offer blind animals as a sacrifice to me. You know that is wrong. You offer to me animals that cannot walk or animals that are ill. That is also a wrong thing to do. You would not give gifts like that to your city's ruler. If you did, he would surely not accept them. He would not be happy with your gifts.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi1:9 ‘Now you ask me to be kind to you. You want me to forgive you. But when you offer unclean animals as gifts to me, I will not be happy with your gifts.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi1:10 ‘It would be better if one of you shut the temple doors! Then you would not be able to offer useless sacrifices on my altar. I am not happy with you. I will not accept any more gifts from you.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Malachi1:11 ‘People in every nation of the whole world will respect my name. People everywhere will offer incense and pure sacrifices to worship me. They will all know that I am great.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Malachi1:12 ‘But you priests insult my name. You say that the Lord's altar is not special. You think that the food which you offer to me has no value. 
Malachi1:13 You also say, “It is too much trouble to serve the Lord.” You think that you are too important to serve me.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. ‘You offer to me animals that cannot walk or that are ill. You have even robbed other people to get some of these animals. Do you think that I will accept these unclean animals from you? Will I be happy with them?’ That is what the Lord says." 
Malachi1:14 ‘You may promise to offer to me a valuable male animal from among your sheep. But then you change your mind. Instead, you offer to me an animal that has no value. I will surely punish you when you cheat me like that. Remember that I am a great king. People of all nations in the world respect my name with fear.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi2:1 The Lord Almighty says this to the priests: 
Malachi2:2 ‘Listen carefully to what I say to you. You must respect my name properly. If not, I will punish you. I will no longer bless you but I will curse you. I am already doing that because you do not respect my name as you should." 
Malachi2:3 I will punish your descendants. When you offer an animal as a sacrifice at your festivals, I will cover your faces with its dirty bits. Then they will throw you away with all the other dirty bits. Those are the bits that I cannot accept as clean.’" 
Malachi2:4 The Lord Almighty says, ‘When I punish you like that, you will know that I have warned you. Then the covenant that I made with Levi and his descendants, the priests, can continue." 
Malachi2:5 I promised to give them a good life with many blessings. Levi received what I promised to him. He served me and he respected me, as a priest should do." 
Malachi2:6 He taught my true message. He did not teach people anything that was bad. He lived in a good and honest way as he served me. He helped many people to stop doing wrong things. 
Malachi2:7 Priests should help people to know the truth about God. People should ask the priests to teach them, because the Lord Almighty has chosen priests to speak his message." 
Malachi2:8 But you priests do not serve me in the right way. What you teach causes many people to do wrong things. In that way, you have spoiled the covenant that I made with Levi.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi2:9 ‘So I have caused all the people to insult you. They no longer respect you, because you do not obey my rules. When you teach my people, you are kind to some of them but you cheat others.’" 
Malachi2:10 You know that we all have one Father. It is God himself who made all of us. But now we turn against each other. We do not respect the covenant that God made with our ancestors. 
Malachi2:11 Judah's people have turned against God. As a result, people have done disgusting things in Jerusalem and in all Israel. You have spoiled the holy place, the temple that the Lord loves. You have turned away from the Lord and you worship foreign gods." 
Malachi2:12 I pray that the Lord will remove all people like that from among his people. Not one of them will continue to belong to the family of Jacob. They will no longer be able to offer gifts to the Lord Almighty. 
Malachi2:13 Also, you weep so much that your tears cover the Lord 's altar! You cry because the Lord no longer accepts your gifts. He is not happy with the gifts that you offer to him." 
Malachi2:14 You ask, ‘Why is the Lord not happy with our gifts?’ The Lord knows that you men have not done the right thing with your wives. When you married as a young man, you promised to take care of your wife always. But you have sent her away, when the Law says that you must stay with her as your wife." 
Malachi2:15 The Lord has joined you together, as a man with his wife. Your body and your spirit belong to him. Why did God put you together? He wanted you to have children who would belong to him. So think carefully about how you live. Do not leave the wife that you married when you were a young man." 
Malachi2:16 The Lord God of Israel says this: ‘If a man sends his wife away from him, I hate that! He has been cruel to his wife when he should take care of her. So be very careful! Remember your promise to take care of your wife.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi2:17 The Lord has become tired as he listens to your words. You ask, ‘Why is he tired? What have we done to cause that?’ You say, ‘The Lord thinks that all people who do evil things are good! He does not punish them but he is happy with them.’ You also say, ‘God is not here to bring justice!’" 
Malachi3:1 The Lord Almighty says, ‘Look! I will send someone to speak my message. He will prepare the way before me. Then the Lord that you are waiting for will quickly return to his temple. The one who brings the message about my covenant will certainly come. He is the one that you really want to see." 
Malachi3:2 But when he comes, he will be like a hot fire that makes metal pure. He will be like strong soap that makes clothes completely clean. Nobody will be able to stand in front of him when he appears. On that day, nobody will remain the same." 
Malachi3:3 He will work like a man who uses a hot fire to make gold and silver pure. He will make the Levites clean and pure. He will remove their dirt. Then they can offer proper sacrifices to the Lord. 
Malachi3:4 The Lord will accept the gifts that the people of Judah and Jerusalem offer to him. He will be happy with them as he was in earlier times. 
Malachi3:5 At that time, I will come among you as your judge. I will speak against those who have done wrong things. I will punish the magicians, the adulterers and those who tell lies against other people. I will punish those people who are cruel to their workers and those who deceive widows or children who have no family. And I will punish those who do not help foreigners who live among them. People who do those things show that they do not respect me.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi3:6 ‘I am the Lord and I do not change. Because of my promises to my people, I have not destroyed you, descendants of Jacob." 
Malachi3:7 I have told you my commands. But you do not listen to me any longer. Since the time of your ancestors, you have not obeyed me. Now, return to me! Then I will return to bless you.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. ‘But you ask, “How do we return to you?”" 
Malachi3:8 I say to you, “A man should not rob God. But you are robbing me.” But you ask, “How do we rob you?” I say, “You do not give to me the tithes and the offerings that you should give.”" 
Malachi3:9 Yes, all of you are robbing me. The whole nation is guilty. So you will receive the punishment of a curse." 
Malachi3:10 Now you must bring the whole tithe that belongs to me. Store it in my temple, so that there will be food in my house. Obey me and then see what I will do.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. ‘You will see how much I will bless you! You will not have enough room to store all the good things that I will give to you. I will pour blessings out of the sky like rain." 
Malachi3:11 I will stop insects from eating your crops. The grapes will not fall from your vines before they are ready to eat. 
Malachi3:12 You will be happy to live in your land. The people of all the other nations will see that God has blessed you.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Malachi3:13 The Lord says, ‘You have spoken bad words against me. But you ask, “What have we said against you?”" 
Malachi3:14 You have said, “We serve God, but that has not helped us at all. We are careful to do what he tells us to do. When we do wrong things, we have sad faces to show the Lord Almighty that we are sorry." 
Malachi3:15 But it is proud people that God blesses! Yes, it is the people who do evil things who become rich! God does not even punish people who like to test him. That is what we think.” ’" 
Malachi3:16 Then the people who respected the Lord talked to each other. The Lord listened to what they said. While the Lord watched, someone wrote names on a scroll. Those were the names of the people who respected the Lord and who gave honour to his name. They wrote down their names so that people would remember them." 
Malachi3:17 The Lord Almighty says, ‘These people will belong to me. One day I will come to judge everyone. At that time I will bring together all my own special people. I will not punish them. I will take care of them, like a father takes care of his son who serves him." 
Malachi3:18 Because of that, you will see again that I am kind to righteous people, but I punish wicked people. I show the difference between people who serve me faithfully and those who do not.’" 
Malachi4:1 ‘The great day when I come to judge people will certainly happen. Punishment will come like the great fire of a hot oven. It will destroy all the proud people who do evil things. It will completely destroy them, like a fire that destroys dry grass. Nothing will remain.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says." 
Malachi4:2 ‘But it will be different for you who respect my name. On that day, I will come to make you well again, like the sun that rises to shine on you. I will show that you are right. When that happens, you will be very happy. You will jump up and down like young cows when someone lets them go free." 
Malachi4:3 Then you will walk over the wicked people. They will be like ashes under your feet. That will happen on the day when I do these things.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says. 
Malachi4:4 ‘Remember the laws that I gave to my servant Moses at Mount Sinai. Those rules and commands were for all the Israelites to obey. 
Malachi4:5 Look! I will send my prophet Elijah to you. He will come before that great day of the arrives. That will be a day that makes people afraid. 
Malachi4:6 He will speak my message, so that fathers and their children return to each other as friends. Then I will not have to come and bring punishment on the land.’" 
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz. Rahab was his mother. Boaz was the father of Obed. Ruth was his mother. Obed was the father of Jesse. 
Matthew2:17 In this way, these words that God's prophet Jeremiah spoke long ago became true:" 
Matthew3:3 God's prophet Isaiah spoke about this man John long ago. He said: ‘Somebody's voice is shouting in the wilderness, “The Lord will come soon, so prepare a way for him. Make the paths straight for him.” ’" 
Matthew4:14 This caused God's message to become true. Long ago God had said through his prophet Isaiah: 
Matthew8:17 Jesus did this so that the words of the prophet Isaiah would become true: ‘He took away everything that makes us weak. He carried away our diseases.’ 
Matthew12:17 This has made what the prophet Isaiah spoke long ago become true. Isaiah wrote what God said: 
Matthew12:39 Jesus replied, ‘The people who are alive today are very wicked. They do not obey God, but they want God to show them something powerful. But God will not do this for them. They will only see the same powerful thing that God did for his prophet Jonah." 
Matthew12:40 Jonah stayed inside a big fish for three days and three nights. In the same way, the Son of Man will remain in the ground for three days and three nights." 
Matthew12:41 When God judges everyone, the people who lived in Nineveh will be there. They will stand up and they will speak against you. They will show that the people who are alive today are bad. When Jonah spoke to the people in Nineveh long ago, they stopped doing bad things. But look! There is someone here now who is greater than Jonah was." 
Matthew13:14 Long ago, the prophet Isaiah spoke a message from God about those people. Now it has become true. God said: “You will listen and listen, but you will not understand. You will look and look, but you will not see anything." 
Matthew15:7 You are hypocrites! What God's prophet Isaiah wrote about you long ago is true: 
Matthew16:4 The people who are alive today are very wicked. They do not obey God. They want God to show them something powerful. But God will not do this for them. They will only see the same powerful thing that God did for Jonah.’ Then Jesus went away and left them. 
Matthew16:14 They replied, ‘Some people say that you are John the Baptist. Other people say that you are Elijah. And some other people say that you are Jeremiah, or another prophet of God.’" 
Matthew16:17 Jesus said to Simon Peter, ‘Simon, son of Jonah, God has blessed you! No person on earth taught you this. God, my Father in heaven, has shown you this." 
Matthew23:35 Because of all this, God will punish you for all the good people that you and your ancestors have killed. Long ago, Abel was the first of those good people. You also killed Zechariah, the son of Berekiah. He died in the yard of the temple near the altar." 
Matthew24:15 Long ago, God's prophet Daniel wrote in the Bible about something that would happen. He said that one day people would put a disgusting thing that causes trouble in the temple. You will see it in the holy place. (When you read this, you must understand what it means.)" 
Matthew27:9 So the words that the prophet Jeremiah wrote long ago now became true. He had said, ‘Then they took the 30 silver coins. It was the amount of money the people of Israel had agreed to pay for the man." 
Mark1:2 The prophet Isaiah wrote this message from God in his book: ‘Listen! I will send someone to prepare a way for you. He will tell people my message. 
Mark7:6 Jesus said to them, ‘You are hypocrites! What God's prophet Isaiah wrote about you long ago is true: God says, “These people say good things about me, but they do not really want to obey me." 
Luke1:5 There was a man whose name was Zechariah. He lived when Herod was king of Judea. Zechariah was a priest and he belonged to a group of priests from the family of Abijah. He had a wife called Elizabeth. She also was a descendant of Aaron. 
Luke1:6 Zechariah and Elizabeth obeyed all God's commands and rules. God saw that they did what was right. They did not do wrong things. 
Luke1:8 One day, Zechariah's group of priests was working at the temple. He had a special job to do there." 
Luke1:9 The priests used lots to choose one of their group to go into the holy place in the temple. This time they chose Zechariah. His job was to burn incense there. 
Luke1:10 While Zechariah was burning the incense, many people were outside in the yard. They were praying to God." 
Luke1:11 Then an angel of the Lord appeared to Zechariah. The angel was standing at the right side of the incense altar. 
Luke1:12 When Zechariah saw the angel, he was very surprised and very afraid." 
Luke1:13 The angel said to him, ‘Zechariah, do not be afraid. God has heard what you prayed. He will give you what you asked for. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to a baby boy. You will give him the name “John”." 
Luke1:18 Zechariah asked the angel, ‘How can I be sure about this? You know that I am an old man. My wife is also old.’" 
Luke1:21 While this was happening, the people outside were waiting for Zechariah. They were thinking, ‘Why has Zechariah stayed for such a long time in the holy place? Why has he not come out yet?’" 
Luke1:22 When Zechariah did come out, he tried to talk to them. But he could not speak. So they knew that God had showed him something special in the holy place. Zechariah was moving his head and his hands about, to tell them what had happened. But still he could not speak." 
Luke1:23 When Zechariah had finished his work in the temple, he returned home." 
Luke1:40 When she arrived at the home of Zechariah, she said ‘hello’ to Elizabeth." 
Luke1:59 When the child was eight days old, people met together to circumcise him. They wanted to call the child Zechariah. This was the same name that his father had." 
Luke1:63 Zechariah asked for something to write on. He wrote, ‘His name is John.’ Everyone was very surprised at what he wrote." 
Luke1:64 Immediately, Zechariah could speak again. He could speak easily. He began to praise God." 
Luke1:65 The people who lived near Zechariah and Elizabeth were all very surprised. They saw how great God was. Many people who lived in the hills of Judea also talked about all these things. 
Luke1:67 The Holy Spirit filled John's father, Zechariah, so that he spoke God's message:" 
Luke1:76 Zechariah then spoke to his son, John: ‘You, my child, will be called a prophet of the Most High God. You will go in front of the Lord to prepare a way for him." 
Luke1:80 Zechariah's child grew. God made him strong in his mind. He went into the wilderness and he lived there for many years. Later, God sent him to Israel's people, to teach them." 
Luke3:1 This report is about John, the son of Zechariah. It happened when Tiberius Caesar had been the Roman ruler for 15 years. Pontius Pilate was the Roman ruler of Judea. Herod had authority over the region of Galilee. Herod's brother Philip had authority over Iturea and Trachonitis. Lysanias had authority over Abilene."
